# Changelog

All notable changes to Finlynq are documented here.
Format: [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/)
Versioning: [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/)

## [Unreleased]

- **feat(accounts): opening balance backed by ONE linked `kind='opening_balance'` transaction + date picker (FINLYNQ-206, 2026-06-27, web).** The account "opening balance" is now a single-source-of-truth field backed ENTIRELY by one transaction marked `kind='opening_balance'` — there is **no `accounts.opening_balance` column**; the transaction is the only place the value lives, and `getAccountBalances` already SUMs it into the running balance (no read-side special-casing). **Integrity:** a partial unique index `transactions_one_opening_balance_per_account ON transactions (user_id, account_id) WHERE kind='opening_balance'` (migration `20260627_opening_balance_unique.sql`) keeps the field ↔ transaction 1:1. **Never deletes:** clearing/zeroing the field UPDATEs the linked row to amount 0 (row kept, `updated_at` bumped) — only the user deleting it from the Transactions UI removes the row (the no-programmatic-tx-delete invariant). **Date:** the create dialog + the **Edit Account dialog on the accounts list page** AND the Edit dialog Details tab on the account detail page (FINLYNQ-227) all expose an opening-balance **amount + date** (defaults today; hidden when "Investment account" is checked) so a back-dated opening balance keeps Net Worth / Balance-Over-Time history starting from the right point instead of spiking on today. **Category:** new opening-balance rows resolve a per-user neutral **"Opening Balance" category (`type='R'`)** (find-or-create, mirrors the FINLYNQ-131 Transfer-category resolver) so they stay OUT of income/expense reports (those filter `type IN ('I','E')`). **Scope (v1): cash accounts only** — the field is hidden for investment accounts (they value off `holdings.value` and refuse generic transactions; an opening balance there needs a different mechanism, deferred). **Domain:** the upsert lives in `src/lib/accounts/opening-balance.ts` (`getOpeningBalance`/`setOpeningBalance`, refuses investment accounts), surfaced by `GET/PUT /api/accounts/[id]/opening-balance` (GET = requireAuth, PUT = requireEncryption + `isReasonableAmount` guard). `createTransaction` gained an optional `kind?` passthrough (REST create/update still keep `kind` off their boundary; the opening-balance path is the only non-portfolio writer that sets it). **Existing-data reconciliation (owner decision):** every multi-row `opening_balance` account in prod/dev is an INVESTMENT account (the `20260603` backfill stamped one per holding, e.g. prod acct 612 = 15 rows), which the account-wide partial index can't filter out — so the migration re-tags ALL of a VIOLATING account's `opening_balance` rows to a new `kind='balance_adjustment'` (kind-only; NO deletes, amounts/quantity/cost-basis byte-identical, balances unchanged) so the index builds; accounts with exactly one such row keep it. `balance_adjustment` is added to the `transactions_kind_check` constraint and to `PAIRLESS_CANONICAL_KINDS` (+ the operations.ts / apply.ts mirrors) so the re-tagged pair-less rows stay canonical to the backfill planner/coverage and aren't re-proposed. **v1 limitation (per triage):** legacy payee-string "Opening Balance" rows (no `kind`, encrypted payee → not DEK-free identifiable in SQL) are left as-is and not recognized by the new field. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260627_opening_balance_unique.sql` (new), `src/lib/accounts/opening-balance.ts` (new), `src/app/api/accounts/[id]/opening-balance/route.ts` (new), `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/backfill/types.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/backfill/apply.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/_components/transaction-table.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **style(portfolio): magnitude-adaptive decimal places on All Holdings numeric columns (FINLYNQ-244, 2026-06-27, web).** The All Holdings table (and per-account expanded rows) now display numeric values with precision scaled to the magnitude of each value: `|value| > 10` → 0 decimals; `0.1 ≤ |value| ≤ 10` → 2 decimals; `|value| < 0.1` → 3 decimals. Applies to: Total Qty, Avg Cost, Price, Mkt Value, Total Cost, Day G/L $, Unrealized G/L $, Realized G/L (and the per-account expanded columns: Qty, Avg Cost, Mkt Value, Unrealized G/L, Realized G/L, Cost Basis, Dividends, Total Return). Two shared helpers added to `src/lib/currency.ts`: `magnitudeDecimals(value)` (pure — returns 0|2|3 from the absolute value) and `formatCurrencyAdaptive(value, currency)` (wraps `formatCurrency` with the magnitude-derived decimal count). The CSV export (`exportByHoldingToCSV`) is NOT changed — it stays at full precision. Files: `src/lib/currency.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-table.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(import): Download .csv + Copy-to-clipboard controls on the post-import warning panel (FINLYNQ-237, 2026-06-27, web).** The expandable `IssueDetails` warning/advisory panel in the Money Pro and Generic CSV connector tabs (Settings → Import → Migrate from another app) gains two affordances in its expanded footer: **Download .csv** (via `exportCsv` from `src/lib/csv-export.ts` — one "Warning" column per row, filename `import-warnings-<date>.csv`) and **Copy to clipboard** (plain-text, one warning per line, with a 2s "Copied!" confirmation). Both controls are rendered inside the `<details>` element so they only appear when the panel is open, using the same amber colour tokens as the warning text. The export uses the shared `exportCsv` / `serializeCsv` — no hand-rolled CSV serialization. Files: `src/app/(app)/import/components/moneypro-connector-tab.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/generic-csv-connector-tab.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(feedback): clipboard paste-to-attach in feedback dialog + reply boxes (FINLYNQ-236, 2026-06-27, web).** Adds a `paste` event handler to the message textarea in the feedback dialog (`src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx`) and to the reply textareas in the user thread (`src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx`) and admin thread (`src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`). When `clipboardData.items` contains a `kind==="file"` image (e.g. a screenshot pasted with Ctrl/Cmd+V), a synthetic `File` is created with a `screenshot-<ts>.<ext>` name and the blob's MIME type, then run through the same `validateFeedbackAttachment` client pre-check the picker uses (denylist + 5 MB cap — server-side enforcement is unchanged). On success the image populates the single-attachment slot and the existing image thumbnail preview renders. Non-image clipboard content (plain text, links, etc.) is not intercepted — `preventDefault` is never called so normal text paste is unaffected. Files: `src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(portfolio): populate Day G/L for metals (XAU/…) and foreign-currency cash in All Holdings (FINLYNQ-246, 2026-06-27, web).** Gold (XAU, "Metals") and foreign-currency cash (e.g. Cash USD shown in a CAD display) rendered **`--`** for Day G/L because the cash/metal valuation branch of `GET /api/portfolio/overview` computed a `price` but never set `change`/`changePct` — so the per-holding `dayChangeDisplay` (gated on `changePct !== null`) stayed null and nothing rolled into the All-Holdings Day G/L $ / % columns (split out by FINLYNQ-245). **Fix:** a new `prevFxRates` map fetches every currency/metal-symbol rate at the prior calendar day via `getRate(cur, displayCurrency, prevDate)` (which routes through `fetchYahooMetalRateToUsd` / the `<CCY>USD=X` chart — both bias their lookup window backwards so a weekend/holiday resolves to the prior **trading** day's close). **Metals:** day change = today's cross-rate price minus the prior-day cross-rate price, in the holding's own currency (`change`/`changePct` in `quoteCurrency`), so the existing `change × qty` display formula AND the native rollup pick it up. **Foreign-currency cash:** the day change is purely an FX-rate move vs the display currency — the NATIVE change is genuinely 0 (1 USD is still 1 USD), so `change` stays 0 (native rollup shows 0, never a fake FX figure) and the display contribution is set EXPLICITLY as `dayChangeDisplay = qty × (todayRate − prevRate)` with `changePct` = the FX day-move %. **Same-currency cash:** rate 1 today and prior → 0 → stays `--` (never fabricated). **Reconciliation:** the summary "Day Change" tile now SUMS the per-holding `dayChangeDisplay` (the single source of truth) instead of independently re-deriving `change × qty` — byte-identical for equities/crypto (per-row `dayChangeDisplay` already equalled the old summand) but additionally folds in metals + foreign cash, so the tile reconciles exactly with the column. **Top Movers:** metals now surface as real movers (they have a symbol + day change); cash sleeves are filtered out of the movers input (an FX wiggle on "Cash USD" isn't an investment mover). Equity/crypto day-change path untouched; no migration. Files: `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): sort by every column + split Day/Unrealized G/L into separate $ and % columns in All Holdings table (FINLYNQ-245, 2026-06-27, web).** The All Holdings table previously only sorted by 3 columns (Holding name, Mkt Value, Unrealized %). Every column is now sortable: Total Qty, Avg Cost, Price, Mkt Value, Total Cost, Day G/L $, Day %, Unrealized G/L $, Unrealized %, Realized G/L, and Accounts. The `SortField` union in `page.tsx` is widened to 12 values and the comparator switch handles each with null-safe numeric compares (null sorts smallest). The combined "value (%)" cells for Day G/L and Unrealized G/L are **split into separate columns** so amount and percentage can each be sorted independently — the table now has 13 columns. The expand-region `colSpan` and empty-state `colSpan` are updated accordingly. All new sortable headers carry `aria-sort`. Table stays deliberately custom (collapsible/nested per-account expand region; not migrated to the shared `DataTable`). `docs/ui-tables.md` updated. Files: `src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-table.tsx`, `pf-app/docs/ui-tables.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **refactor(settings): move "Rebuild balance history" card to Settings → Data; collapse reconcile hide/show accounts behind a subpage (FINLYNQ-241, 2026-06-27, web).** Two small Settings UX relocations. **Part 1 — rebuild card relocation + rename:** the "Rebuild investment history" card is moved FROM Settings → Investments (`/settings/investments`) TO **Settings → Data** (`/settings/data`) and renamed **"Rebuild balance history"** throughout — the rebuild is now authoritative for both cash AND investment snapshots (FINLYNQ-226's orphan-reap pass), so "investment history" was inaccurate. The `RebuildSnapshotsButton` default label was already updated by FINLYNQ-230; this commit updates the card title, the in-progress panel label ("Rebuilding investment history…" → "Rebuilding balance history…"), the net-worth chart empty-state copy, and the API status route JSDoc. The button component is unchanged in behaviour — pure relocation. **Part 2 — reconcile dropdown visibility subpage:** `ReconcileHideAccountsCard` (the full per-account hide/show list) is moved off the main `/settings/import` page into a new subpage at `/settings/import/reconcile-visibility`. The main page now shows a compact card with a title, description, and a "N accounts hidden" badge that links to the subpage. The hidden count is fetched on mount from `GET /api/settings/reconcile-hidden-accounts` (same endpoint, no backend change). Hidden-is-dropdown-only semantics unchanged. Files: `src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/data/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/import/reconcile-visibility/page.tsx` (new), `src/components/portfolio/rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx`, `src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx`, `src/app/api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/status/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(feedback): auto-scroll feedback thread to the latest message on open (FINLYNQ-235, 2026-06-27, web).** The feedback reply thread (user thread at `/feedback` and admin thread at `/admin/feedback`) opened scrolled to the TOP, so the most recent message was off-screen and the user had to scroll manually. Fix: a bottom-sentinel `<div ref={bottomRef} />` is appended after the last message in the `max-h-[50vh] overflow-y-auto` scroll container, and a `useEffect` on `thread` calls `bottomRef.current?.scrollIntoView({ block:"end", behavior:"instant" })` — this fires both on initial thread load (when `thread` transitions from `null` to the fetched data) and after each new reply is appended via `setThread(t => ...)` (which creates a new object reference triggering the effect). `behavior:"instant"` avoids a visible scroll animation when the dialog opens. The admin thread mirrors the fix identically. Files: `src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(net-worth): reap stale LEADING cash snapshots dated before an account's own first tx (FINLYNQ-233, 2026-06-27, web).** The Balance Over Time chart (and the aggregate Net Worth Over Time chart) drew a flat line years before an account's first real transaction — e.g. prod account 601 ("Appartment") showed C$130,000 from **Jan 2020** even though its only tx is the Jan 1, 2022 opening balance. Root cause in [src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts): the UPSERT walk's `start` clamps UP to the **global** earliest cash delta and only ever rewrites `earliestDelta → today`, while `deleteOrphanCashSnapshots` only reaps accounts entirely absent from `byAccount` — so `source='cash'` rows dated before an account's own earliest tx (left over when an opening balance was back-dated to 2020 by a bulk import, then date-corrected) were never rewritten OR deleted while the account still had activity, and `buildNetWorthHistory` carried each one forward as a flat line. **Fix (per-account, unconditional on every rebuild):** (1) the grouping pass now tracks each account's OWN earliest delta (floored to `EARLIEST_CASH_DATE`); (2) a new `deleteLeadingCashSnapshots` pass deletes `source='cash'` rows where `snap_date < that account's own earliest delta` (owner-/account-scoped, never touches investment `source<>'cash'` rows); (3) the per-account walk `continue`s for grid days before the account's own earliest (the delta fold still runs to keep the running cumulative correct), so an account whose first tx is after the global start no longer accumulates leading rows; (4) the FINLYNQ-230 progress denominator now counts only the grid-days actually written (skipping each account's leading days) so the bar still reaches 100%. `deleteOrphanCashSnapshots` (fully-emptied accounts) is unchanged and still runs first. **No manual SQL needed:** `isCashStale` trips on every UTC-midnight rollover and on any cash tx change, so the cash self-heal re-runs `buildCashSnapshots` within ~1 day (or instantly on a tx edit) and the new delete pass clears the orphans automatically; existing charts correct themselves on the next self-heal tick. Snapshot VALUES for legitimate (at-or-after-first-tx) rows are byte-identical. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(net-worth): report cash-snapshot rebuild progress + relabel the button generically (FINLYNQ-230, 2026-06-27, web).** The "Rebuild investment history" progress bar (`RebuildSnapshotsButton`, fed by the `__pfRebuildProgress` registry) only tracked the **investment** day-walk; the **cash** rebuild (`rebuildCashSnapshots` → `buildCashSnapshots`) reported nothing. For a **no-holdings / cash-only** user, FINLYNQ-229's `holdingAccountIds.size === 0` branch skips the investment walk and jumps the bar straight to done while the (often slow, cold-cache historical-FX) cash walk runs silently — the chart stayed flat with zero feedback (repro: reseeded dev demo, HKD `BankAccount_01` built 2,929 daily snapshots over a noticeable delay with no progress shown). **Fix (reporting-only — snapshot VALUES are byte-identical):** (1) `RebuildProgress` gains a `phase: 'investment' | 'cash'`; `reportRebuildProgress` takes an optional `phase` (defaults `'investment'`, so the FINLYNQ-205 investment contract is unchanged). (2) An `onProgress(done, total)` callback is threaded through `rebuildCashSnapshots` → `buildCashSnapshots`, emitting per grid day (denominator = `gridDates.length × accounts`, published up front like the investment leg's leading emit). (3) `rebuildPortfolioSnapshots` passes that callback to its end-of-walk cash build tagged phase `'cash'`, so the manual button reflects cash progress. (4) The status endpoint returns `phase`; `RebuildSnapshotsButton` renders a phase-aware in-progress label ("Rebuilding cash balances…" vs "Rebuilding investment history…") with the same determinate "day X of Y" bar. (5) The chart-load cash self-heal (`kickCashSelfHeal`) now best-effort seeds + reports into the SAME registry (only when an investment rebuild doesn't already own the entry), so a background cash heal shows a bar too. (6) The default button label is now the generic **"Rebuild balance history"** (was "Rebuild investment history", wrong for cash-only users). The Settings → Investments card title is left to FINLYNQ-241 (which relocates/renames the card). Web only; cash builder stays DEK-free. Verify on dev: reseed → cash-only account → click Rebuild → continuous cash progress, no jump-to-done while the chart is flat. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/status/route.ts`, `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `src/components/portfolio/rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(admin): operator monitoring — Server Health + persistent Diagnostics log with operation attribution (2026-06-26, web, shipped dev → PROD).** Two new admin surfaces that answer "what is the CPU doing, and WHY", built after a prod CPU investigation traced the bursts to the net-worth `portfolio_snapshots` rebuild. **`/admin/system` (Server Health):** one read-only `GET /api/admin/system` (hand-rolled `requireAdmin` + postgres-dialect 403, no DEK) returns (1) OS+process CPU/load/mem/disk with a live in-memory sparkline AND a **durable 24h avg+peak CPU chart** (new `system_metrics_sample` table, sampler started at boot from `instrumentation.ts`, persists ~1/min, trimmed to ~7d), (2) live `pg_stat_activity` active/long-running queries + conn counts (self-monitoring SQL excluded), (3) a **"Top operations · 24h"** panel ranking routes/jobs by total wall-clock / calls / avg / slow / errors, (4) snapshot-rebuild activity (in-flight + recent from the FINLYNQ-205 registry via new cross-user readers `getAllRebuildProgress`/`getCashRebuildsInFlight`, stale `portfolio_snapshot_dirty` markers, heaviest users by snapshot rows), and (5) an outbound-API summary linking to `/admin/api-log`. **`/admin/diagnostics` (Diagnostics log):** a PERSISTENT `diagnostics_log` table (survives restarts, unlike the in-memory api-log) capturing four kinds — **`slow_query`** (any DB query ≥ `PF_SLOW_QUERY_MS`, default 2000ms), **`db_error`** (SQLSTATE in `code`), **`api_error`** (route 5xx, via `logServerError`), **`outbound_error`** (Yahoo/CoinGecko timeout or provider 5xx, via `marketFetch`) — filterable by kind + min-ms with a per-kind total/last-24h summary + clear; trimmed to the newest `PF_DIAGNOSTICS_CAP` rows (default 5000). **Capture chokepoint = an OBSERVE-ONLY wrapper on the pg Pool + transaction clients** (`instrumentPool` in [src/lib/diagnostics/log.ts](src/lib/diagnostics/log.ts), installed in `PostgresAdapter.initialize` before `drizzle()` wraps the pool) — chains a `.then` for timing/error capture and NEVER alters the query/args/result/rejection; recursion-guarded (skips any SQL touching `diagnostics_log`) and fire-and-forget + never-throws. **Operation attribution (Phase 2):** an AsyncLocalStorage `withOp(label, fn)` ([op-context.ts](src/lib/diagnostics/op-context.ts)) tags every query/error with the operation that triggered it (`rebuild:investment`/`rebuild:cash`, `GET /api/dashboard`, …) — wired into `apiHandler` + the hand-rolled dashboard / portfolio-overview / net-worth-history GET routes + both snapshot rebuilds; each `withOp` completion feeds an in-memory per-`(op,hour)` rollup flushed to the new `op_rollup` table every ~30s (amortised, trimmed to ~7d). **Env labeling:** every `diagnostics_log` row stamps `env` (prod/dev from `APP_URL`); prod and dev write to SEPARATE DBs (`pf` / `pf_dev`) so logs/rollups/samples are isolated — never combined. Server Health labels per-panel scope (CPU/mem/disk = whole VPS; DB/snapshots/rebuilds/top-ops = this env). Free-text (SQL/errors/URLs) is run through the existing `scrubSensitive`; captured SQL is parameterized (no literal user data). The three new tables are global/plaintext, no user_id/DEK, NOT in the per-user wipe path. **Known v1 gap:** the daily crons aren't yet wrapped in `withOp` (they read `op=(none)`). Knobs: `PF_SLOW_QUERY_MS`, `PF_DIAGNOSTICS_CAP`. Migrations `20260626c_diagnostics_log.sql` + `20260626d_diagnostics_phase2.sql` (additive, idempotent). Verified end-to-end on dev + prod. Files: `src/lib/diagnostics/{log,op-context,op-rollup,env}.ts` (new), `src/lib/admin/system-metrics.ts` (new), `src/app/api/admin/{system,diagnostics}/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/admin/{system,diagnostics}/page.tsx` (new), `src/db/adapters/postgres.ts`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/server-logger.ts`, `src/lib/market-fetch.ts`, `src/lib/api-handler.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/{rebuild,cash-builder}.ts`, `src/app/api/{dashboard,portfolio/overview,net-worth-history}/route.ts`, `instrumentation.ts`, `src/components/nav.tsx`, `scripts/migrations/20260626c_*.sql` + `20260626d_*.sql`, `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(crypto): scope the login encryption-upgrade sweep for `transaction_splits` through the parent transaction FK (2026-06-26).** `transaction_splits.{note,description,tags}` are registered as user-DEK-encrypted, but the sweep scoped every table by a direct `WHERE user_id = $1` and `transaction_splits` has no `user_id` (it's owned via `transaction_id → transactions`) — so the sweep threw `column "user_id" does not exist` (SQLSTATE 42703) and silently skipped those columns (surfaced by the new `/admin/diagnostics` db_error capture). The registry now supports a per-table `userScope: {fkColumn, parentTable}` override so splits scope via a join to the parent; a registry/schema consistency test guards any future no-`user_id` table. Files: `src/lib/crypto/upgrade-user-fields.ts`, `src/lib/crypto/user-encrypted-registry.ts`, `tests/upgrade-user-fields.test.ts`.
- **feat(import): Generic CSV (full ledger) connector — a mapping-driven, format-tolerant importer for any multi-account export (2026-06-26, web).** A fourth provider under Settings → Import → "Migrate from another app" for the shape the single-account `/import` column mapper can't take: ONE CSV carrying **many accounts, transfers, and currencies** (e.g. `date, amount, currency, account, note, category, account_to`). **NOT header-locked like Money Pro:** the pure transform [packages/import-connectors/src/generic-csv/](packages/import-connectors/src/generic-csv/) (`parseGenericCsv`/`genericCsvRowsToRawTransactions`, zero-dep) uses `suggestGenericCsvMapping(headers)` to derive a `GenericCsvMapping` (logical field → header) via case-insensitive **alias** matching (`note`/`memo`/`description`/`payee`, `account_to`/`destination`/`to`, …) — order-independent, unknown columns ignored; `isGenericCsv` is true once the required trio `date`/`amount`/`account` auto-maps. A file that doesn't fully auto-map is NOT rejected — the preview returns `mappingComplete:false` and the UI's **Match columns** step lets the user re-point a column, then re-previews. **Semantics:** **signed** amount (no Type column); per-row `currency` ISO column (falls back to amount symbol → `defaultCurrency`); **`account_to` set → two `linkId`-paired transfer legs** (outflow on `account`, inflow on `account_to`, category "Transfer" resolved/created by the orchestrator); `(OPENING BALANCE)` → an `Opening Balance` tx (gated by `includeOpeningBalance`), `(AUDIT)` → `Adjustment` (both marker sets configurable); flexible dates via `parseFlexibleDate` (ISO + slash/dot `D/M/Y`, `M/D/Y` via `dateOrder`, optional trailing time, self-disambiguates) + `decimalComma`. **Cross-currency (FX) transfers (2026-06-26):** a transfer row that ALSO supplies `amount_received` + `currency_to` (the `amountTo`/`currencyTo` mapping fields, alias-detected like the rest) records the inflow leg FAITHFULLY in its own currency — `-5000 HKD` out, `+502.18 GBP` in — so both legs share a `linkId` and each matches its own account, and `executeImport` stores it natively (or via locked-at-entry FX when the chosen account currency differs). BOTH `amountTo` + `currencyTo` are required to treat a transfer as FX; either absent → same-currency mirror (legacy). `refuseCrossCurrencyTransfers` now refuses ONLY the AMBIGUOUS case — a same-currency-row transfer (no received amount) whose two legs resolve to accounts of different **modal** currency — and keeps transfers whose legs carry different currencies (the discriminator is leg-currency-difference, not the accounts' modal currencies). An account's currency is its **modal** currency across its rows (a mostly-CNY account with stray HKD rows is created CNY, off-currency rows stored via `entered_currency`+FX — NOT first-row-wins). Orchestrator [generic-csv-orchestrator.ts](src/lib/external-import/generic-csv-orchestrator.ts) (`summarizeGenericCsv` read-only preview / `executeGenericCsvImport` resolve-or-create accounts+categories via `buildNameFields`, rewrite onto Finlynq names, `executeImport` txSource `connector`). Routes `POST /api/import/connectors/generic-csv/{preview,execute}` (`requireEncryption`; `preview` takes an OPTIONAL `mapping`+`defaultCurrency`+`includeOpeningBalance` and returns `{headers,sampleRows,mapping,missingRequired,mappingComplete,summary}`; `execute` requires a date/amount/account-complete `mapping` + per-account `choices`). UI `GenericCsvConnectorTab` (upload → Match columns + sample preview, incl. the optional Amount-received / Currency-received FX columns → map accounts → import); deep-link `/settings/import?tab=migrate&provider=generic-csv` (legacy `tab=connect` still accepted). Doubles as the inverse of Finlynq's own data export. **Rename (2026-06-26):** the provider/full-ledger flow is branded **"Migrate from another app"** (was "Import from another provider"), distinct from the bank-statement **import** at `/import`. Verified: 22 transform unit tests + the real multi-currency HSBC export (349 rows) parses to 565 transactions / 0 errors / 216 transfers, with the 3 HKD→GBP FX transfers recording `502.18 / 502.26 / 499.40 GBP` on their inflow legs; confirmed working end-to-end on dev. **Deferred:** per-row-id→`fitId` idempotent re-import, MCP/mobile parity. Files: `packages/import-connectors/src/generic-csv/{transform,index,transform.test}.ts` (new), `packages/import-connectors/src/index.ts`, `packages/import-connectors/package.json`, `src/lib/external-import/generic-csv-orchestrator.ts` (new), `src/app/api/import/connectors/generic-csv/{preview,execute}/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/import/components/generic-csv-connector-tab.tsx` (new), `src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx`, `docs/import-connectors.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(feedback): attachments v2 — durable storage + any file type (ex-dangerous) + per-message (FINLYNQ-228, 2026-06-26, web).** Follow-up to FINLYNQ-226. **(A) Durable storage (fixes a deploy-wipe DATA-LOSS bug):** v1 stored at `process.cwd()/uploads`, but on the VPS pf-dev/pf run from `WorkingDirectory=.next/standalone` and `deploy.sh` does `mv .next .next.old && rm -rf .next.old` every deploy — so `.next/standalone/uploads/` was permanently deleted on each redeploy. New shared `getUploadsBaseDir()` ([src/lib/uploads-dir.ts](src/lib/uploads-dir.ts)) resolves OUTSIDE `.next`: `PF_UPLOADS_DIR` env override → else when cwd ends with `/.next/standalone` climb to `<root>/uploads` (sibling of `.next`) → else `<cwd>/uploads`. Both feedback attachments AND `mcp_uploads` (the single clean write site in `src/app/api/mcp/upload/route.ts`) now use it; `/uploads/` added to `.gitignore`. Docker/self-hosted should mount a persistent volume at `PF_UPLOADS_DIR`. **(B) Any file type EXCEPT dangerous:** the image-only allowlist is replaced with a DENYLIST (`FEEDBACK_DANGEROUS_EXTENSIONS` + `FEEDBACK_DANGEROUS_MIME` in [attachment.ts](src/lib/feedback/attachment.ts)) — executables/scripts (exe, dll, bat, ps1, sh, js, jar, apk, dmg, …) + active web content (html, htm, xhtml, svg). `validateFeedbackAttachment({filename,mime,size})` now derives a sanitized alnum lowercased `ext` from the original filename (empty → `bin`), rejects dangerous ext OR MIME (415), empty (400), oversize (>5 MB, 413). Files land on disk as `<uuid>.<ext>` (no user path component); original name only in the DB pointer. Serve routes (shared `serveFeedbackAttachment` in [attachment-io.ts](src/lib/feedback/attachment-io.ts)) stream inline ONLY for safe images (png/jpeg/webp/gif); everything else is `Content-Disposition: attachment` + `application/octet-stream` + always `nosniff`. The dialog shows an image thumbnail or a file chip. **(C) Attachment per message:** additive `attachment_{path,filename,mime,size}` on `feedback_messages` (migration `scripts/migrations/20260626b_feedback_message_attachment.sql`); both reply endpoints (`/api/feedback/[id]/reply` user-owner-scoped + `/api/admin/feedback/[id]/reply` admin) accept multipart with one optional attachment (JSON path unchanged for back-compat/mobile). A new owner-scoped `GET /api/feedback/[id]/attachment?messageId=` serves the user thread; the admin serve route gained the same `?messageId=`. Both thread UIs ((app)/feedback + admin/feedback) render the seed + per-message attachments (shared `FeedbackAttachmentView` component) and have a reply attachment picker. All messages store under the THREAD OWNER's uploads dir; authorship is the existing `author_role`. **Authorship-aware wipe:** `unlinkUserUploadFiles` unlinks the seed + the user's OWN message files (`author_role='user'`); admin-authored reply attachments SURVIVE (maintainer-owned). `deleteAllUserDataTx` nulls the seed + user-authored message pointers, leaving admin pointers intact (mirrors v1 retention). Web only; mobile deferred. Files: `src/lib/uploads-dir.ts` (new), `src/lib/feedback/attachment.ts`, `src/lib/feedback/attachment-io.ts` (new), `src/components/feedback/attachment-view.tsx` (new), `scripts/migrations/20260626b_feedback_message_attachment.sql` (new), `src/app/api/feedback/[id]/attachment/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/feedback/route.ts`, `src/app/api/feedback/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/feedback/[id]/reply/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/reply/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/attachment/route.ts`, `src/app/api/mcp/upload/route.ts`, `src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/feedback/thread.ts`, `src/lib/auth/queries.ts`, `shared/types.ts`, `.gitignore`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(accounts): account detail page — move config cards into the Edit dialog + add transaction quick-actions (FINLYNQ-227, 2026-06-26, web).** The `/accounts/[id]` page ([page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx)) is decluttered: the **Reconciliation mode** (`ModePicker`), **Import preferences** (`ImportPrefsPicker`), and **Cash sleeves** cards no longer stack on the main view — they moved INTO the **Edit dialog** as base-ui `Tabs` (**Details / Reconciliation / Import / Cash sleeves**). The dialog widened to `sm:max-w-xl` (prefixed per FINLYNQ-144, never bare `max-w-*`) keeping the phone gutter + internal scroll. **Opportunistic cleanups while relocating:** the currency picker now uses `useActiveCurrencies(ensure)` (#291, was hardcoded CAD/USD/EUR/GBP), the Group field is the free-text `GroupField` + suggestion chips (FINLYNQ-179, was the `ACCOUNT_GROUPS` dropdown), and the cash-sleeve delete uses the shared `ConfirmDialog` (was `window.confirm`). **Deep-links preserved:** the `/inbox` lens-chip gear still points at `/accounts/[id]#reconciliation-mode`; the page reads `window.location.hash` on mount and **auto-opens the Edit dialog on the matching tab** (`#reconciliation-mode`→Reconciliation, `#import-prefs`→Import), so the linker is untouched. **Quick-actions (header):** a **normal account** gets a **New transaction** button (embeds the shared `TransactionDialog` seeded `kind:"transaction-prefill"` with THIS account pre-selected) + an **Investment transaction** dropdown limited to **Deposit / Withdrawal** (brokerage cash move between this account and a chosen investment account). An **investment account** gets an **Investment transaction** dropdown with **all 8** ops (Buy, Sell, Swap, In-kind transfer, Deposit, Withdrawal, Income/expense, FX conversion) and NO generic New transaction (per the investment-accounts-hidden-from-generic-dialog invariant). Each op deep-links to `/portfolio/new?op=<key>&account=<id>` (op keys are hyphenated: `income-expense`/`fx-conversion`). **`?account=` pre-select:** the new-entry parallel to `?editId=` is the pure `useAccountParam()` + one-shot `useSeedAccountFromParam()` ([src/lib/hooks/useAccountParam.ts](src/lib/hooks/useAccountParam.ts), [useSeedAccountFromParam.ts](src/lib/hooks/useSeedAccountFromParam.ts)), wired into all 8 portfolio forms — single-account forms seed `accountId`; the two-account Deposit/Withdrawal/Transfer forms choose the side via `&accountField=source|dest` (deposit→investment side is `dest`, withdrawal→normal side is `dest`, else `source`). It seeds ONCE (ref-guarded), only in new-entry mode (edit data wins), and only when the id is valid for that field's account list — absent param = byte-identical prior behavior. Same-currency Deposit/Withdrawal still refuses cross-currency via the existing `CurrencyMismatchError` path. Web only; MCP + mobile unchanged. Files: `src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/lib/hooks/useAccountParam.ts` (new), `src/lib/hooks/useSeedAccountFromParam.ts` (new), the 8 `src/components/portfolio/forms/*Form.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(settings): user-selectable UI font with FOUC-safe persistence (FINLYNQ-225, 2026-06-26, web).** Settings → General → Display Preferences gains a **Font** selector with 5 options: **Geist** (default, unchanged), **Inter**, **IBM Plex Sans**, **Atkinson Hyperlegible** (purpose-built for legibility), and **System UI** (native stack, no web-font download). Choice persists **per browser via localStorage** — the UI says explicitly "Applies to this browser only." Numerics/tabular figures always stay Geist Mono regardless of the choice (the `--font-mono` token is not overridden). **FOUC prevention:** a nonce-aware inline `<script>` in the root layout sets `data-font` on `<html>` before first paint, mirroring the `next-themes` pattern (same `x-nonce` CSP header). The CSS switches `--font-sans` off a `[data-font]` attribute; default (no attribute) is Geist — byte-identical to today. The circular `--font-sans: var(--font-sans)` in `globals.css` is replaced with a concrete `var(--font-geist-sans)` default. The 4 alternate fonts load via `next/font/google`; System UI needs no load. Files: `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/app/globals.css`, `src/components/font-provider.tsx` (new), `src/app/(app)/layout.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/general/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(feedback): attach an image/screenshot on the in-app feedback form (FINLYNQ-226, 2026-06-26, web).** The "Send feedback" dialog ([src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx](src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx)) gains an optional single-image picker (PNG/JPEG/WebP/GIF) with a thumbnail preview + remove control and a strengthened "no sensitive financial details" warning near the picker. **Storage = ON DISK, PLAINTEXT** — mirrors `mcp_uploads`: the file lands at `uploads/feedback/<userId>/<uuid>.<ext>` (sibling of `uploads/mcp/`, repo-root so it survives a rebuild) and a plaintext pointer (`attachment_path`/`attachment_filename`/`attachment_mime`/`attachment_size`, additive nullable columns on `feedback` via migration `scripts/migrations/20260626_feedback_attachment.sql`) is stored on the seed row. The attachment is **admin-readable, NOT DEK-encrypted** — feedback is deliberately plaintext because the maintainer has no per-user DEK, so the screenshot is kept OUT of the envelope-encryption path. **`POST /api/feedback`** now accepts `multipart/form-data` (JSON path unchanged for the no-attachment / mobile clients) and enforces the **MIME allowlist + 5 MB cap SERVER-side** (mirrors the import pipeline's `MAX_BYTES`) via the pure `validateFeedbackAttachment` ([src/lib/feedback/attachment.ts](src/lib/feedback/attachment.ts)) — a bad type → 415, oversize → 413, and NO row or file is persisted (the file is written before the row INSERT; a failed INSERT unlinks it). The admin serves the bytes from the new admin-gated `GET /api/admin/feedback/[id]/attachment` (`requireAdmin` + managed-mode guard, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, only known image content-types echoed), rendered inline + download link under the seed bubble at `/admin/feedback`. **Wipe:** `unlinkUserUploadFiles` (the same pre-DB-transaction unlink that handles `mcp_uploads`) now also unlinks feedback attachment files BEFORE `deleteAllUserDataTx`; feedback rows are maintainer-owned and intentionally survive a user wipe, so the in-tx step nulls the (now-dangling) attachment pointer columns rather than deleting the row (text feedback is retained, the privacy-sensitive image is gone). v1 = initial submission only; attach-on-replies + mobile parity deferred. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260626_feedback_attachment.sql` (new), `src/lib/feedback/attachment.ts` (new), `src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/attachment/route.ts` (new), `src/components/feedback-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/api/feedback/route.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/auth/queries.ts`, `shared/types.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `get_balance_anchors` / `upsert_balance_anchor` — read+write per-account balance anchors (FINLYNQ-217 / R-03, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** Two reconcile tools that let Claude READ, CORRECT, or CREATE a `bank_daily_balances` anchor (the bank's reported balance per date the reconcile engine validates the ledger against) outside the staged-import flow. **Anchors have NO synthetic id** — PK is `(user_id, account_id, date)`, so rows are identified by `(accountId, date)` (the proposed `id` was dropped from the contract). `get_balance_anchors(accountId, dateMin?, dateMax?)` → `Array<{ accountId, date, amount, currency, source, createdAt }>` ordered **date DESC** with inclusive `[dateMin,dateMax]` bounds (`amount` ⇐ the `balance` column; `createdAt` ⇐ `first_seen_at`); read-only (`get_` prefix), a cross-user/nonexistent `accountId` → `[]`. `upsert_balance_anchor(accountId, date, amount, currency)` → `{ accountId, date, amount, currency, created }` — `INSERT … ON CONFLICT (user_id, account_id, date) DO UPDATE` (newer balance wins); **`created` is derived from the `xmax = 0` system column** (insert vs update in one round-trip, no pre-existence SELECT). **Stamps `source='mcp_manual'`** — a NEW anchor source added to the `ANCHOR_SOURCES` tuple in [src/lib/bank-ledger-balance.ts](src/lib/bank-ledger-balance.ts) AND the `bank_daily_balances_source_check` DB CHECK via the ADDITIVE migration `scripts/migrations/20260625b_balance_anchor_mcp_source.sql` (WIDENS the allowed set — every existing row already satisfies it, so it auto-applies on deploy). A manual anchor writes an EMPTY `source_filenames` (`ARRAY[]::text[]`, no statement file → no encryption needed). **The upsert immediately affects the reconcile balance check** — `computeAccountBalanceSummary` → `getLatestBankAnchor` reads the latest anchor LIVE from the DB (no caching), so the new value flows into `get_reconcile_suggestions` / `get_reconciliation_summary` `balanceDelta` on the very next call. **`note` is dropped from v1** (no column; deferred follow-up). `currency` uses `supportedCurrencyEnum`. **DRY:** both tools call focused helpers (`listBankAnchorsInRange` / `upsertManualBankAnchor`) in `bank-ledger-balance.ts`, not inline SQL in `register-tools-pg.ts`. `upsert_balance_anchor` passes explicit `{ idempotentHint:true, destructiveHint:false }` annotations (the name escapes the auto-annotation prefixes). HTTP-only (reconcile cohort; anchors are plaintext so a `pf_` API key would also work, but the cohort is gated to HTTP for consistency). Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 115→117 (two tools); no stdio change. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260625b_balance_anchor_mcp_source.sql` (new), `src/lib/bank-ledger-balance.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **docs: reconcile skill document — data model, field glossary, and tool decision tree (FINLYNQ-219, 2026-06-25).** Added `pf-app/docs/reconcile-skill.md`, a ~700-word skill document covering the seven §6 sections: App overview (bookkeeping ledger, not a bank connection), Data model (`accounts`, `transactions`, `bank_transactions`, `transaction_bank_links`, `bank_daily_balances`), Reconciliation flow (upload → staged → bank ledger → reconcile → linked), Key field glossary (`seen_count` as a same-row re-import counter NOT a duplicate signal; `import_hash` over plaintext payee; balance anchor PK `(user_id, account_id, date)` with no synthetic id; `link_type`, `duplicateOfTransactionId`), Account-type taxonomy (investment accounts out of scope), Common patterns + root causes (overlapping imports, manual vs imported, timing lag), and a Tool decision tree mapping each reconciliation finding to the shipped MCP tool. References all 13 shipped reconcile tools. No code changes, no migration, no tool-count change.

- **feat(mcp): `accept_reconcile_suggestions` (bulk) — link many bank↔tx pairs per call (FINLYNQ-216 / R-01, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** The batch sibling of the singular `accept_reconcile_suggestion`, removing one MCP round-trip per pending suggestion (10-20 a session). `accept_reconcile_suggestions(pairs: Array<{ bankTransactionId[uuid], transactionId[int], linkType?:'primary'|'extra' }>)` returns an array **POSITIONAL with the input** (`result[i] ↔ pairs[i]`): `{ bankTransactionId, transactionId, linkId, setPrimaryFk, alreadyLinked, error? }`. **`linkType` defaults to `'primary'`** (the singular tool defaults to `'extra'`; bulk-accept wants the lineage FK set). **Partial commit:** each pair runs through the new `linkTransactionsToBank` ([src/lib/reconcile/links.ts](src/lib/reconcile/links.ts)), which loops the extracted `linkTransactionToBankCore` (the transactional core of `linkTransactionToBank`) inside a per-pair try/catch — each pair is its OWN `db.transaction` (a natural per-pair "savepoint"), so a bad / cross-account / unknown id rolls back ONLY its own tx and carries an `error` while the rest still commit (1 bad id out of 10 → 9 land). **DRY:** the bulk helper WRAPS the same core the singular tool uses; `linkTransactionToBank` is now a thin `core + invalidateUser` wrapper with a byte-identical external contract (no signature/return change). **Idempotent** — a re-submitted already-linked pair returns `alreadyLinked:true`, no error, no duplicate insert (inherited verbatim from the core). **`invalidateUser(userId)` fires EXACTLY ONCE** after the whole batch (never per-pair). The cross-account guard (FINLYNQ-211) surfaces as that element's `error` ("…belong to different accounts…"), not a batch rollback. Owner-scoped (the core re-asserts user ownership of both rows). `idempotentHint` is passed as an EXPLICIT 4th-arg annotation (the name escapes the auto-annotation prefixes). HTTP-only (reconcile cohort). Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 114→115; no stdio change, no migration. Test: `tests/reconcile-bulk-link.test.ts` (new — tc-1 ten-positional / tc-2 partial-commit + cross-account / tc-3 idempotent / tc-4 invalidate-once). Files: `src/lib/reconcile/links.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `tests/reconcile-bulk-link.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `get_reconciliation_summary` — portfolio-wide reconcile health in one call (FINLYNQ-215 / R-04, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** A read-only tool that collapses the session-startup "one `get_reconcile_suggestions` per account" into a SINGLE call. `get_reconciliation_summary(accountIds?:integer[], lookbackDays?=90)` returns `Array<{ accountId, accountName, linked, suggestions, bankOnly, txOnly, balanceMismatch, balanceDelta?, lastAnchorDate?, currency, isInvestment }>`, one row per in-scope account. **Omit `accountIds` ⇒ ALL non-investment accounts** (investment reconcile is out of scope); pass `accountIds` to scope it verbatim (still owner-scoped). The four reconcile-state counts **reuse `computeReconcileForAccount`** — the exact engine `get_reconcile_suggestions` and the `/import` page use, so `linked`/`suggestions`/`bankOnly`/`txOnly` are the result-array lengths and byte-identical to the per-account detail view — under a `lookbackDays`-derived `dateMin` (default 90, mirrors `get_reconcile_suggestions`). **`balanceDelta` reuses the SAME calc the `/import` reconcile header shows:** the bank-vs-system balance math was DRY-extracted from `src/app/api/reconcile/balance-summary/route.ts` into the new shared `computeAccountBalanceSummary` ([src/lib/reconcile/balance-summary.ts](src/lib/reconcile/balance-summary.ts)) — BOTH the route AND this tool call it, so `balanceDelta` EQUALS the header's `delta` verbatim (`= systemSideLatest − bankSideLatest`; positive ⇒ ledger says MORE than the statement; `null` when the account has no `bank_daily_balances` anchor). `balanceMismatch` = `status==='mismatch'`; `lastAnchorDate` = the latest anchor's date. The richer aggregator `getReconciliationSummary` lives in [src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts](src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts) (alongside the thin FINLYNQ-147 `getReconcileSummary`) and returns name-free rows; the MCP tool decrypts `accountName` (alias-then-name via `decryptNameish`) at the boundary, and the holdings-value map is pre-fetched ONCE then passed into each per-account balance summary so investment rows don't recompute it. Read-only (`readOnlyHint` inferred from the `get_` prefix); HTTP-only / DEK-required (payees decrypted for the fuzzy counts + names decrypted). The `/import` `balance-summary` route was refactored onto the shared helper — response shape byte-identical. Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 113→114; no stdio change, no migration. Files: `src/lib/reconcile/balance-summary.ts` (new), `src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts`, `src/app/api/reconcile/balance-summary/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(reconcile): bank-side delete now cascades a transfer's link-id siblings instead of orphaning the other leg (FINLYNQ-222, 2026-06-25, web).** `DELETE /api/bank-transactions/[bankId]` with `{deleteLinkedTransactions:true}` deleted EXACTLY the transactions primary-linked to that bank row and never their `link_id` / `trade_link_id` / `swap_link_id` siblings — so deleting one leg of a transfer/trade/swap left the OTHER leg ORPHANED (link_id set, no sibling, no bank link): a phantom in/outflow that distorts the other account's balance, violating the "Cascade delete on `link_id` / `trade_link_id` siblings" invariant the canonical `DELETE /api/transactions?id=N` already honors. Reproduced on dev (deleting a transfer leg's bank row left the sibling orphaned) and matched a real prod orphan. **Fix = cascade reuse (DRY):** the sibling-expansion that was inline in the canonical transactions-delete route was extracted into the shared, single-source helper [src/lib/transactions/link-siblings.ts](src/lib/transactions/link-siblings.ts) (`expandLinkSiblings(userId, seedIds, db?)` — owner-scoped, expands seeds to the full link-id closure including swaps' nested trade-link pairs; a non-link seed expands to just itself so a single non-transfer tx is NOT over-deleted). The canonical route was refactored to call it (behavior byte-identical — it replaced its own inline logic) and the bank route now expands `linkedTransactionIds` through the same helper before its owner-scoped bulk delete (still one DB transaction), so both routes cascade identically. The `deletedTransactionIds` in the response reflects the full expanded set. This is a user-initiated delete (the caller chose `deleteLinkedTransactions`), so cascading siblings is user-initiated, not a side-effect delete. Prod orphan cleanup (leg 52814) stays user-initiated via the Transactions UI — out of scope here. Test plan (tc-1 no-orphan / tc-2 canonical-still-cascades / tc-3 single-tx-unaffected) is DB-bound integration (real transfer pairs + bank links); CI/manual-verified. Files: `src/lib/transactions/link-siblings.ts` (new), `src/app/api/bank-transactions/[bankId]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/transactions/route.ts`, `pf-app/docs/architecture/bank-ledger.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `upload_statement` — stage a statement file via MCP (base64), no browser session (FINLYNQ-221 / R-08, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** Closes the fully-autonomous import loop: Claude can stage a CSV/OFX/QFX statement over the already-authenticated MCP connection without a browser session (the prior blocker — uploading via the Cloudflare-protected `POST /api/mcp/upload` returned `401 Authentication required` from a bash environment). `upload_statement(fileContent[base64 string], fileName, accountId, mimeType?)` returns `{ stagedImportId, rowCount, duplicateCount, newCount, dateStart, dateEnd, statementBalance, statementBalanceDate, statementCurrency, detectedFormat, counts, rowErrors }`. **Critical divergence (do NOT wrap `/api/mcp/upload`):** that legacy endpoint writes the WRONG artifact — a file on disk + an `mcp_uploads` row consumed by `preview_import`/`execute_import`, NOT a `staged_imports` row. `upload_statement` runs the STAGING pipeline (the path behind `/api/import/staging/upload`), so the returned `stagedImportId` is a real `staged_imports.id` that `send_to_bank_ledger` (R-07) + `approve_staged_rows` consume unchanged. **DRY chokepoint:** the parse step (`parseStatement`) and the classify → dedup → INSERT staged-write core (`writeStagedImport`) were lifted out of the inline body of `src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts` into the new [src/lib/import/stage-statement-file.ts](src/lib/import/stage-statement-file.ts) (`stageStatementFile` = parse + write); BOTH the web route's detailed-staging tail AND the MCP tool call it, so the staged-write logic lives in ONE place (the route keeps its confirm-mapping gates + simplified-bank-ledger path, which run BEFORE delegating; the MCP path is autonomous = always detailed-staging with auto-detected mapping). The route shed ~460 lines of now-shared code. The tool decodes base64 server-side → a `File`, enforces the **5 MB cap on the DECODED bytes** (mirrors the browser `MAX_BYTES`; base64 inflates ~33% so the MCP message carrying `fileContent` is ~6.7 MB — documented in the tool description), and keeps the `findUnreasonableAmountError` guard (FINLYNQ-159) the staging path needs since it bypasses `previewImport`. Owner-scoped (`accountId` must belong to the user; OFX/QFX require it). Parse failure / unsupported extension → descriptive error with `detectedFormat:'unrecognised'` and NO staged import created. Re-uploading the same file creates a NEW staged import (dedup is ROW-level not file-level — known rows are flagged `existing`/`skipped_duplicate`). Staged rows land at `encryption_tier='user'` (v1: under the DEK) → HTTP-only / DEK-required. No `invalidateUser` (writes only `staged_imports`/`staged_transactions`, never `transactions`). Explicit non-destructive + non-idempotent annotations (the name carries no `delete_`/`set_` token). Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 112→113; no stdio change, no migration. Files: `src/lib/import/stage-statement-file.ts` (new), `src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `delete_bank_transaction` — remove a bank-ledger row by id (FINLYNQ-214 / R-02, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** A destructive tool that deletes a single `bank_transactions` row — the companion to `find_duplicate_bank_rows` (surface the duplicate rows, then delete the extras). `delete_bank_transaction(bankTransactionId, dryRun?=false)` returns `{ deleted, unlinkedTransactionIds: integer[], dryRun }`. **Cascade is DB-level, not manual:** `transaction_bank_links.bank_transaction_id` is `ON DELETE CASCADE` and `transactions.bank_transaction_id` is `ON DELETE SET NULL` (migrations `20260523_transaction-bank-links.sql` / `20260522_bank-transactions-ledger.sql` — the FK constraint is migration-defined; the Drizzle `transactions.bankTransactionId` column has no `.references()`), so the commit is ONE owner-scoped `DELETE FROM bank_transactions WHERE id=? AND user_id=?` and the link-row removal + FK-nulling happen automatically — the `transactions` rows themselves SURVIVE (lineage cleared, ledger intact). `unlinkedTransactionIds` is pre-computed as the UNION of the join-table links (`transaction_bank_links`) and the lineage FK (`transactions.bank_transaction_id`) pointing at the row; `dryRun:true` returns it with ZERO DB writes (no DELETE, no `invalidateUser`). `invalidateUser(userId)` fires exactly ONCE after a real (non-dryRun) delete (the FK on `transactions` changed). Owner-scoped — a non-existent or cross-user `bankTransactionId` returns a "Not found" error and changes nothing. **Balance anchors (`bank_daily_balances`) are independent of bank rows** (they can exist on days with no row), so deletion never orphans an anchor — the doc's "refuses if sole anchor" clause was dropped. `destructiveHint` is inferred from the `delete_` name prefix by `withAutoAnnotations` (no explicit annotations arg). No DEK is decrypted (the ownership DELETE is plaintext-keyed), but it stays HTTP-only as part of the reconcile cohort. Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 111→112; no stdio change, no migration. Files: `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `find_duplicate_bank_rows` — surface duplicate bank-ledger rows (FINLYNQ-213 / R-06, 2026-06-25, HTTP-only).** A read-only tool that lists groups of duplicate `bank_transactions` rows for one account so Claude can pick a canonical to keep — duplicates arise when overlapping statement files are imported. `find_duplicate_bank_rows(accountId, lookbackDays?=180)` returns `Array<{ canonicalId, duplicateIds[], date, amount, payee, seenCount, linkedTransactionId? }>` (empty array when none). **Load-bearing correction:** `seen_count` is NOT the duplicate signal — re-importing the SAME row (identical `import_hash`) bumps `seen_count` on the existing single row rather than creating a second one; TRUE duplicate ROWS appear when overlapping imports produce DIFFERENT `import_hash`es for one event, so the tool groups DISTINCT row ids that share `(date, amount, normalized payee)`. `canonicalId` = oldest by `first_seen_at` (deterministic id tie-break); `duplicateIds` = the rest; `linkedTransactionId` is resolved from `transaction_bank_links` (primary link first) else the `transactions.bank_transaction_id` FK and populated when any row in the group is already linked. Owner-scoped (`user_id`); a cross-user/nonexistent `accountId` returns `[]`. `payee` is encrypted per `encryption_tier`, decrypted with the SAME tier-aware branch as `src/lib/reconcile/bank-ledger-pool.ts` (`decryptStaged()` for service tier, `tryDecryptField(dek)` for user tier; null-not-ciphertext on failure) — hence HTTP-only / DEK-required. **DRY:** the grouping logic is the pure, unit-testable `findDuplicateBankRows(rows)` in the new [src/lib/reconcile/find-duplicate-bank-rows.ts](src/lib/reconcile/find-duplicate-bank-rows.ts); the MCP tool is the thin adapter (load + tier-decrypt + resolve link → call helper). `readOnly` annotation is inferred by `withAutoAnnotations` (the `find_` prefix). Reachable via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 110→111; no stdio change (needs a DEK), no migration. Test: `tests/find-duplicate-bank-rows.test.ts` (new). Files: `src/lib/reconcile/find-duplicate-bank-rows.ts` (new), `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `tests/find-duplicate-bank-rows.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(reconcile): a transfer's peer leg can no longer read as "linked" via another account's bank row (FINLYNQ-211, 2026-06-25, web + MCP).** Reported + reproduced on prod and dev: when a transfer was reconciled from ONE account's statement, the OTHER leg (in the destination account, statement not yet imported) showed the green "linked" badge in that account's reconcile view, so a half-reconciled transfer read as already-done. Two-part root cause + fix: **(1) write-side same-account guard** — `linkTransactionToBank` ([src/lib/reconcile/links.ts](src/lib/reconcile/links.ts)) verified user ownership of the (tx, bank) pair but NOT that `transactions.account_id === bank_transactions.account_id`, so the raw `POST /api/reconcile/links`, `/links/bulk`, and MCP `accept_reconcile_suggestion` paths accepted a cross-account link (the match engine only ever SUGGESTS same-account, so this guards the manual/bulk/MCP entry points). It now throws `LinkError('cross_account')` → the single-link route returns **400** (clear "different accounts" message), the bulk route skips the pair (non-fatal, like a race miss), and the MCP tool returns a descriptive error. **(2) read-side bank-account filter** — `loadJoinRows` ([src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts)) derived "linked" by joining `transaction_bank_links → transactions` and filtering on `transactions.account_id = accountId` ONLY, never the bank row's account; it now also `INNER JOIN bank_transactions` and requires `bank_transactions.account_id = accountId`, so a leg linked to another account's bank row is hidden from this account's "linked" set — this also masks any PRE-EXISTING cross-account links (the prod row) with NO data migration. Net: reconciling one leg of a transfer never marks the opposite leg linked; the peer leg stays in `txOnly`/pending until its OWN statement is imported and the match engine suggests linking it (FINLYNQ-222 covers the separate sibling-cascade-on-delete bug). Test: `tests/reconcile-link-same-account-guard.test.ts` (new). Mobile reconcile inbox derives "linked" the same per-account way and is unaffected (separate `pf-mobile` worktree; verified by inspection, no code change here). Files: `src/lib/reconcile/links.ts`, `src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts`, `src/app/api/reconcile/links/route.ts`, `src/app/api/reconcile/links/bulk/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `tests/reconcile-link-same-account-guard.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): `send_to_bank_ledger` — promote staged rows to the BANK LEDGER ONLY, no `transactions` write (FINLYNQ-220 / R-07, 2026-06-24, HTTP-only).** Fixes a data-integrity bug found in a live session: the MCP `approve_staged_rows` tool still wrapped `executeImport` and materialized staged rows into the live `transactions` ledger, so running it on an account that already has manual/imported transactions for the period DUPLICATED them (e.g. a $4,501.07 RBC payment showing as both a transfer and a "PAYMENT, THANK YOU"). The web "Send to bank ledger" button was migrated to a bank-only promote on 2026-05-25 (import-modes Phase 3); the MCP tool never was. New HTTP-only tool `send_to_bank_ledger(stagedImportId, rowIds?, skipExistingMatches?=true)` promotes staged rows into `bank_transactions` ONLY (never `transactions`; the one bank↔tx link it can write is the legacy `reconcile_state='linked'` branch, which points at a PRE-EXISTING tx) + loads the statement balance anchor, returning `{loaded, skipped, skippedExisting, anchorLoaded, anchorDate, anchorAmount, batchId, …}`. No confirmation token (lower-risk than ledger creation; `upsertBankTransaction` is idempotent). `skipExistingMatches` (default true) drops `dedup_status='existing'` rows up front. **DRY:** the bank-only promote logic was extracted from `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/approve` into the shared chokepoint [src/lib/import/send-to-bank-ledger.ts](src/lib/import/send-to-bank-ledger.ts) (`sendStagedRowsToBankLedger`) — mirrors the FINLYNQ-150 `materializeBankRowAsTransaction` pattern — and BOTH the web route and the MCP tool call it; the route passes `skipExistingMatches:false` to stay byte-identical. `approve_staged_rows` behavior is unchanged; its description now makes the "creates ledger transactions" semantics explicit and points first-time-import users at it while routing the normal reconcile workflow to `send_to_bank_ledger`. Surfaced via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")`. `MCP_TOOL_COUNTS.http` 109→110. HTTP-only (needs the DEK to decrypt payee/note + re-encrypt at tier); no migration, no stdio change. Files: `src/lib/import/send-to-bank-ledger.ts` (new), `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(website): public release-notes pages at /releases + /releases/<tag> (2026-06-24, web).** A crawlable, SEO- and AI-citation-friendly changelog on the marketing site. Content is single-sourced in [src/lib/seo/releases.ts](src/lib/seo/releases.ts) (the `RELEASES` array of `{version, slug, name, date, current, tagline, summary, sections, githubUrl}` + `RELEASE_SLUGS` / `getRelease` / `RELEASES_BY_DATE` / `LATEST_RELEASE`), mirroring the glossary module so a new entry flows automatically into the sitemap, the `/releases` index, and `llms-full.txt`. The index ([src/app/releases/page.tsx](src/app/releases/page.tsx)) lists releases newest-first with a "Latest" badge; the detail page ([src/app/releases/[version]/page.tsx](src/app/releases/[version]/page.tsx)) uses `generateStaticParams` / `generateMetadata` and emits `articleSchema` + `breadcrumbSchema` JSON-LD via `<JsonLd>`, plus a GitHub-release link and the App Store / Google Play `StoreBadges`. Slug == GitHub release tag (`v3.3.0`, `v3.2.0`). Wired into `STATIC_ROUTES` ([site.ts](src/lib/seo/site.ts)), `sitemap.ts`, the `isWebsite` analytics/CSP gate ([middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts)), `public/llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`, and the landing-page footer. Seeded with v3.3.0 (full notes) + v3.2.0. Files: `src/lib/seo/releases.ts` (new), `src/app/releases/page.tsx` (new), `src/app/releases/[version]/page.tsx` (new), `src/lib/seo/site.ts`, `src/app/sitemap.ts`, `src/app/llms-full.txt/route.ts`, `public/llms.txt`, `src/middleware.ts`, `src/components/landing/landing-client.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(price): money-market funds value historically + stop the rebuild re-fetch storm (2026-06-23, web).** A balance/snapshot rebuild made repeated Yahoo calls even on a fully warm `price_cache` (caught via the new /admin/api-log: one symbol re-fetched once PER DAY of the walk — e.g. 65 calls for `FYIXX`). Root cause: money-market funds are quote-only on Yahoo (`instrumentType: "MONEYMARKET"` — `meta.regularMarketPrice` ≈ $1 but an EMPTY `close[]`), so `fetchQuoteAtDate` ([price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts)) found no closes, returned null, cached NOTHING, and didn't negative-cache → the day-by-day rebuild re-fetched the symbol for every historical date (and never valued it). Fixed: `fetchQuoteAtDate` now (1) skips an `isQuoteNegativelyCached` symbol up front, (2) on no-closes-but-`MONEYMARKET`+meta-price, caches that pegged price as a FLAT forward window via `cacheHistoricalWindow` (synthetic single bar) AND returns it — so the fund is valued at ~$1 historically AND the next ~2 months are cache hits, (3) otherwise `markQuoteMiss` so a genuinely hopeless ticker isn't re-hit daily either. Net: MM-fund holdings (FYIXX/SPAXX/FDRXX/…) now value correctly across history and a warm-cache rebuild makes ZERO calls for them. Files: `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(admin): outbound market-data API log at /admin/api-log (2026-06-23, web).** A diagnostic, admin-only view of every outbound call to the market-data providers (Yahoo Finance, CoinGecko), so an operator can see EXACTLY which upstream APIs an operation (e.g. a snapshot rebuild) hits — even when the local cache is warm. A drop-in `marketFetch` wrapper ([src/lib/market-fetch.ts](src/lib/market-fetch.ts)) records `{url, provider, method, status, ms, error, at}` for each call into an HMR-safe in-memory ring buffer on `globalThis` (last 1000, NO DB write, cleared on restart); the 5 provider `fetch(` sites in price-service / fx-service / crypto-service were swapped to it. `GET /api/admin/api-log` lists the buffer (newest-first, with provider/error counts); `DELETE` clears it. The page ([/admin/api-log](src/app/(app)/admin/api-log/page.tsx)) is the shared sortable `DataTable` + provider filter + URL filter + Clear; admin-gated (`requireAdmin` + managed-mode), no DEK. Workflow: Clear → reproduce an op → see which APIs were called. This is what surfaced the FYIXX money-market re-fetch storm above. Files: `src/lib/market-fetch.ts` (new), `src/app/api/admin/api-log/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/admin/api-log/page.tsx` (new), `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `src/components/nav.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(admin): market-data cache inspector at /admin/price-cache (Rate Cache) + row delete (2026-06-23, web).** An admin-only, read+delete view of the two server-side market-data caches — `price_cache` (stock/ETF/crypto daily closes) and `fx_rates` (USD-anchored FX). Two tabs, server-filtered + paginated (price_cache is ~60k rows), sortable via the shared `DataTable`, with a summary header (row/symbol counts, date range, today + stale-today counts). Each row carries a derived freshness state from the SAME `isPriceCacheRowStale` the read path uses (FINLYNQ-204): today-rows past the 30-min TTL show "Stale", historical rows "Cached" (immutable). `DELETE /api/admin/price-cache` removes rows — per-row (`?id`) or all-matching-a-filter (`?all=1`, refuses without a filter so it can't wipe the whole table) — handy for clearing garbage rows (e.g. currency-code symbols mis-served into price_cache); safe because real prices self-heal on next read. `GET/DELETE /api/admin/price-cache?table=price|fx`; `requireAdmin` + managed-mode, no DEK (both caches are plaintext). Files: `src/app/api/admin/price-cache/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/admin/price-cache/page.tsx` (new), `src/components/nav.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(fx): price precious metals from Yahoo futures — stooq endpoints are dead (2026-06-23, web).** stooq.com (the metals price source) changed its anti-bot posture: the `/q/l/` CSV quote endpoint now 404s for EVERY symbol (incl. equities) and `/q/d/l/` (history) sits behind a JS proof-of-work bot-challenge a server `fetch` can't pass. So XAU/XAG/XPT/XPD silently fell back to a stale hardcoded fallback (gold $4700 vs real ~$4145). Repointed metals to Yahoo front-month futures — `XAU→GC=F`, `XAG→SI=F`, `XPT→PL=F`, `XPD→PA=F` — via the new `fetchYahooMetalRateToUsd`/`METAL_YAHOO_TICKER` ([fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts)), which shares the existing Yahoo chart fetch core (live + full daily history, same reliable source as fiat); the dead `fetchStooqMetalRateToUsd` was removed and the stale fallback rates refreshed. A front-month future tracks spot within ~1%. Files: `src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(securities): unpriceable-ticker advisory + one-click ticker change (2026-06-23, web).** Some tickers our providers can't price (e.g. `POL` — Polygon migrated MATIC→POL 1:1, but Yahoo has zero `POL-USD` history; the price lives under `MATIC-USD`). A small curated registry ([src/lib/securities/ticker-advisories.ts](src/lib/securities/ticker-advisories.ts), `getTickerAdvisory`, seeded `POL→MATIC`) drives an amber advisory banner + per-row warning on the `/settings/investments` Securities tab with a one-click **"Change to <suggested>"** button (behind a `ConfirmDialog`). The securities `PATCH /api/securities` ([route](src/app/api/securities/route.ts)) now accepts `symbol` — a RE-CLUSTER mirroring the per-holding edit path: find-or-create the target security for the new ticker (`resolveOrCreateSecurity`), re-point every member position's symbol + `security_id`, then `gcOrphanSecurity` the old one if orphaned (positions/lots/transactions untouched; an existing target = legitimate merge). Symbol-change is handled before — and exclusive of — the name/assetType path. Files: `src/lib/securities/ticker-advisories.ts` (new), `src/app/api/securities/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): record income received AS SHARES — single-leg DRIP on the Income/Expense card (2026-06-23, web).** A dividend/income received as shares (a DRIP) previously needed TWO entries — income → cash, then a Buy. The portfolio Income/Expense card ([IncomeExpenseForm.tsx](src/components/portfolio/forms/IncomeExpenseForm.tsx)) gained a **"Settle into" toggle** (income-only + create-only — hidden for expenses and in edit mode): **Cash sleeve** (legacy, byte-identical) or **Holding (shares)**. In shares mode the income books ONE transaction directly on the chosen stock holding (`portfolio_holding_id`=the holding, `quantity`=shares, `amount`=$ value, `currency`=holding currency, `kind='portfolio_income'`, ANY category the user picks) and opens a cost-basis lot at `value/qty` via `openLotForBuyHook` — **no cash leg, no cash-sleeve movement, no FX at record time**. New helper `recordReinvestedIncomeInShares` in [operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) (sanctioned origin per audit #8; `portfolio_income` is not a paired-op kind); the route branches on `settleAs:"shares"` (+ required `holdingId`,`quantity`) at [/api/portfolio/operations/income-expense](src/app/api/portfolio/operations/income-expense/route.ts), cash branch still requires `currency`. **Zero read-side changes** — the dividends report already flags `qty>0` rows as reinvested ([dividends.ts](src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts)) and the lot origin becomes `reinvest_div` only when the category is Dividends (cosmetic; cost basis is `value/qty` either way). End state == the old two-entry workaround. Deferred: MCP `portfolio_income_expense`, mobile, expense-paid-in-shares, in-form edit of a shares row. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/operations/income-expense/route.ts`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/IncomeExpenseForm.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(rules): investment-op income rules can settle dividends as shares (DRIP) + pick ANY category (2026-06-23, web).** Two additions to the FINLYNQ-208 `record_investment_op` rule action ([rules/schema.ts](src/lib/rules/schema.ts)). **(1) Settle-as-shares (DRIP):** an optional `settleAs: "cash" | "shares"` (absent/`"cash"` = legacy). When `"shares"` (valid ONLY for `op: dividend | interest` — `validateInvestmentOpAction` returns `shares_unsupported_op` otherwise), the executor's `runHoldingCash` dispatches to a new `runIncomeInShares` ([materialize-portfolio-op.ts](src/lib/reconcile/materialize-portfolio-op.ts)) — resolves a REQUIRED destination position (`holdingId` or `useRowTicker`), derives shares+value from the qty/total bindings via the SAME `resolveTrade` core as a buy, and records ONE row via `recordReinvestedIncomeInShares` (qty=shares, opens a lot at value/qty, no cash sleeve / no cash leg). The rule editor gained a "Settle as: Cash / Shares" toggle on dividend/interest that swaps the Amount binding for qty + value bindings; `suggestedInvestmentOp` labels it `"<op> (shares)"` and the preview dialog notes the DRIP. **(2) Any category:** an optional `categoryId` on the action — the dividend/interest/fee ops no longer hardcode the canonical category. The executor prefers `action.categoryId`, falling back to `resolveOrCreateInvestmentIncomeCategory` (Dividends / Interest / Investment Fees) ONLY when unset; `collectActionFKs` adds it so ownership is verified at `POST/PUT /api/rules`. The editor shows a **Category** picker for dividend/interest/fee (default `Auto — <canonical>`), in both cash and shares modes. Rides Auto-pilot upload + MCP `apply_rules_to_bank_rows` + the manual preview-then-apply path for free (same executor). `fee`-as-shares + swap/in-kind/FX deferred; MCP rule-creation + mobile unchanged. Files: `src/lib/rules/schema.ts`, `src/lib/reconcile/materialize-portfolio-op.ts`, `src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(import/reconcile): widen the bank-ledger pane in investment mode so all columns fit (2026-06-23, web).** The investment reconcile bank ledger carries extra Ticker / Security / Qty columns (FINLYNQ-207) that overflowed the forced 50/50 two-pane split, clipping the Amount + Actions columns off the right edge. `TwoPaneLayout` ([two-pane-layout.tsx](src/components/import/reconcile/two-pane-layout.tsx)) gained an opt-in `rightWide` prop (`lg:grid-cols-[2fr_3fr]` → bank pane ~60%) passed only when the account is an investment account ([inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx)); the bank pane's widest columns were tightened (Actions `w-48→w-28`, Payee/Security `max-w 220/200→150`) so the full set fits ([bank-pane.tsx](src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx)). Cash reconcile stays 50/50 and byte-identical; the pane keeps `overflow-auto` as a no-clip fallback on narrow screens. Files: `src/components/import/reconcile/two-pane-layout.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx`, `src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(email): outbound transactional email now actually sends — Resend HTTP API transport (2026-06-22).** Discovered while verifying the feedback-notification feature on dev: outbound email had NEVER been deliverable on dev OR prod — `getTransport()` ([email.ts](src/lib/email.ts)) only supported nodemailer SMTP, and `SMTP_HOST` was unset in both environments, so every `sendEmail()` (verify, password reset, welcome, budget alerts, feedback) threw "Email transport not configured: SMTP_HOST is required in production" (register/reset swallowed it with `.catch(()=>{})`; only the feedback path logged it). `RESEND_API_KEY` was set on both boxes but the code only used Resend for INBOUND attachment-fetch. Added a native **Resend HTTP-API transport** (`POST https://api.resend.com/emails`, `From:` = `EMAIL_FROM` default `Finlynq <noreply@finlynq.com>` on the verified `finlynq.com` domain) and made `getTransport()` prefer it: **Resend (`RESEND_API_KEY`) → SMTP (`SMTP_HOST`, self-host fallback) → console (dev only)**; prod still refuses to silently console-log tokens when neither is set. No new secret (reuses the already-provisioned key). Fixes ALL outbound mail, not just feedback. Files: `src/lib/email.ts`, `docs/email-auth-spf-dmarc.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(settings): in-app change password + change email on /settings/account (2026-06-22, web, managed-mode only).** The Account & Security page never had a way to change your password or recovery email — only "Sign out" + the forgot-password reset (which WIPES data). Added two cards plus two routes. **`POST /api/settings/change-password`** ([route](src/app/api/settings/change-password/route.ts)) is a **non-destructive re-wrap** (distinct from the reset): it verifies the current password, unwraps the per-user DEK with the old password-derived KEK, then re-wraps the SAME DEK under a fresh salt + new-password KEK at the row's EXISTING `pepper_version` (`deriveKEK`/`wrapDEK` + `updateUserPasswordAndWrap`; pepper_version is preserved — login's lazy rotation owns target migration). DEK unchanged ⇒ all encrypted data stays readable and every active session stays valid (no force-logout). **`POST /api/settings/change-email`** ([route](src/app/api/settings/change-email/route.ts)) requires the current password, enforces the same same-column + cross-column (`isIdentifierClaimed`) uniqueness as registration, stores the new address UNVERIFIED with a fresh SHA-256 verify-token hash (`updateUserEmail`, Finding #10), and best-effort mails `emailVerificationEmail` (reuses the existing `/api/auth/verify-email` flow). Both are `getDialect()==='postgres'`-gated, `requireAuth`, per-user rate-limited 5/15min, hand-rolled (NOT `apiHandler`) to match the auth-route style. The `/settings/account` cards ([page](src/app/(app)/settings/account/page.tsx)) are gated on `/api/user/me` returning a non-null `username`, so self-hosted/passphrase mode (no user row) hides them. Self-hosted + MCP + mobile unchanged. Files: `src/app/api/settings/change-password/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/settings/change-email/route.ts` (new), `src/lib/auth/queries.ts` (`updateUserEmail`), `src/app/(app)/settings/account/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/email-auth-spf-dmarc.md`.

- **feat(feedback): email the admin on every feedback submission AND thread reply, with no hardcoded recipient (2026-06-22, web).** New feedback already emailed a maintainer, but to a hardcoded `FEEDBACK_EMAIL || "feedback@finlynq.com"`; user replies on a thread sent nothing. Now both fire a best-effort (fire-and-forget, never blocks/500s the request) notification to the admin recipient(s) resolved at send time via `getFeedbackNotificationRecipients()` ([src/lib/feedback/notify.ts](src/lib/feedback/notify.ts)) — the union of DB admin-account emails (`listAdminEmails()`, `users.role='admin'`) and the OPTIONAL `FEEDBACK_EMAIL` override, deduped, **NO hardcoded fallback address** (empty ⇒ no email). `feedbackNotificationEmail` no longer computes its own `to` (takes the resolved recipient); new sibling template `feedbackReplyNotificationEmail` ([email.ts](src/lib/email.ts)) covers replies (escapes the user body, links to `/admin/feedback`). Wired into `POST /api/feedback` ([route](src/app/api/feedback/route.ts)) and `POST /api/feedback/[id]/reply` ([route](src/app/api/feedback/[id]/reply/route.ts)).
