Files: `src/lib/feedback/notify.ts` (new), `src/lib/email.ts`, `src/lib/auth/queries.ts` (`listAdminEmails`), `src/app/api/feedback/route.ts`, `src/app/api/feedback/[id]/reply/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/email-auth-spf-dmarc.md`.

- **feat(currency): dropdowns scope to selected currencies + type-to-lookup add (Yahoo supported vs custom) (#291 follow-up, 2026-06-22, web).** Two changes so the "Currencies you use" setting is the single source of truth for which currencies the app offers. **(1)** `useActiveCurrencies` ([src/lib/hooks/useActiveCurrencies.ts](src/lib/hooks/useActiveCurrencies.ts)) now returns **only** the user's `active_currencies` set (no longer unioned with the full built-in fiat list), so the Transaction dialog, FX-rates picker, Account / Goals / Loans forms, and the Holding-edit symbol datalist show exactly the user's selected currencies (`ensure` still force-includes a form's current value for edit-mode + initial-fetch safety). The FX-rates picker also dropped its unconditional crypto union (just active currencies + the "+ Custom currency code…" escape). **(2)** Settings → "Currencies you use" ([active-currencies-section.tsx](src/components/active-currencies-section.tsx)) replaces the fixed ~32-item scrollable list with a **type-to-lookup**: the user types any 3-4 letter code and a debounced `GET /api/fx/preview?from=<code>&to=USD` probe reports whether it's **supported** (a live Yahoo / CoinGecko / Stooq rate exists — shows the rate + source) or **custom** (no market rate — "add it, then set a custom rate below"), mirroring the investment-ticker lookup so the user can reach the full supported set (~150 currencies) and clearly see supported-vs-custom. A short "Common" quick-add row covers the majors. `supported = source !== "fallback" && !needsOverride`. No new endpoint (reuses `/api/fx/preview`), no migration; MCP + mobile unchanged.

- **fix(currency): `formatCurrency` no longer crashes on a custom / non-ISO-4217 currency code (#291 follow-up, 2026-06-22, web).** `formatCurrency` ([src/lib/currency.ts](src/lib/currency.ts)) called `Intl.NumberFormat({ style: "currency", currency })` for any code outside the dollar-symbol map. Users can add arbitrary 3-4 letter codes (e.g. `TEST`) via Settings → "Currencies you use", and `Intl.NumberFormat` throws `RangeError: Invalid currency code` for codes that aren't well-formed ISO 4217 — so a single account/transaction denominated in such a code **crashed the entire Transactions page** (and any other view rendering that amount), and it kept crashing even after the code was removed from settings because existing rows still carried it. Now wrapped in try/catch: on `RangeError` it falls back to a plain decimal with the code as a prefix (`TEST 1,234.56`, sign preserved), so the UI degrades gracefully and the user can still see/edit/delete those rows. Regression test added in [tests/currency.test.ts](tests/currency.test.ts). The mobile mirror ([mobile/src/lib/format.ts](mobile/src/lib/format.ts)) already carries the identical try/catch guard (verified on `mobile-dev`) — no mobile change needed.

- **fix(ui): shared `Combobox` `onValueChange` always yields a string, not the selected item object (#291 follow-up, 2026-06-22, web).** base-ui's `Combobox` can hand back the full selected item object (`{value,label}`) instead of the raw string id (observed on type-ahead auto-select). The high-level wrapper ([combobox.tsx](src/components/ui/combobox.tsx)) passed it straight through as `(next ?? "")`, so a consumer that renders the chosen value directly as JSX text — the Settings → "Custom exchange rates" Rate row (`1 <currency> = …`) — received an object and threw **React error #31** ("objects are not valid as a React child"), crashing the page. `handleChange` now normalizes: extract `.value` when an object comes through, else `String(next)`. **No-op for the normal string path** (every existing consumer keeps its exact behavior); only the previously-broken object path changes. Surfaced while testing a custom-currency FX rate (`TEST`) after the picker fix below made custom codes selectable. Verified on dev: adding `1 TEST = 0.5 USD` now selects + saves without crashing.

- **fix(accounts): currency dropdowns honor "Currencies you use" setting (#291, 2026-06-22, web).** The Add/Edit Account currency dropdown was hardcoded to `CAD/USD/EUR/GBP` and ignored the user's Settings → "Currencies you use" (`active_currencies`) selection — so a currency enabled in Settings never appeared when creating/editing an account ([#291](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/issues/291)). New shared client hook **`useActiveCurrencies(ensure?)`** ([src/lib/hooks/useActiveCurrencies.ts](src/lib/hooks/useActiveCurrencies.ts)) returns the built-in `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES` **UNION** the user's `active_currencies` setting (so custom / regional / metal ISO codes added in Settings show up), always a **superset** of the built-in list (cold/failed fetch degrades to today's behavior), with `ensure` keeping a form's current value present even if deselected (edit-mode safety). This is the same union the Settings → Dropdown Ordering page already used. Wired into the Account create + edit dialogs ([accounts/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx)) and, for consistency, the Transaction dialog ([transaction-dialog.tsx](src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx)), Goals form ([goals/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx)), Loans form ([loans/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/loans/page.tsx)), and Holding edit form's symbol-suggestion datalist ([holding-edit-form.tsx](src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx)) — all of which previously showed only the 32 built-in fiat codes and never the user's custom currencies. **Also fixed the Settings → "Custom exchange rates" (FX override) currency picker** ([fx-overrides-section.tsx](src/components/fx-overrides-section.tsx)), which had the same gap: an already-active custom code (e.g. `TEST`) returned "No matches" and forced the user down the "+ Custom currency code…" re-type path — it now lists active currencies directly (union kept with `SUPPORTED_CRYPTO_CURRENCIES`, `USD` filtered as the anchor, and the "+ Custom currency code…" escape hatch retained). Custom-currency FX rates were already supported by the existing `fx_overrides` table + Settings UI; this only fixes the pickers. MCP + mobile unchanged.

- **feat(rules): investment reconciliation rules — user-authored portfolio ops from imported bank rows (FINLYNQ-208, 2026-06-20, dev, editor dev-gated).** Extends the reconciliation-rules system so a user-authored rule turns a matched **investment** bank row into a real lot-aware portfolio op — no auto-inference; the user picks the op type + binds the row's captured variables. **Conditions:** new `ticker` / `security_name` (string ops, encrypted at rest like payee via `rules/crypto.ts`) + `quantity` (numeric, mirrors `amount`); matched via the extended `TransactionInput` ([auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts)) with the bank-row probe in [match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts) now passing decoded ticker/securityName/quantity. **Action `record_investment_op`** ([rules/schema.ts](src/lib/rules/schema.ts)) — one discriminated kind: `op ∈ {buy,sell,dividend,interest,fee,deposit,withdrawal}`, `investmentAccountId`, optional `counterpartyAccountId` (deposit/withdrawal — user supplies the bank counterparty so both directions work), `holdingId` or `useRowTicker`, and **variable bindings** `qty`/`total`/`price` (`VarSource` = row field or fixed) with **any-two-of-three** derivation (pure [investment-op-binding.ts](src/lib/rules/investment-op-binding.ts), zero-guarded). Side-effect action; per-op validity via `validateInvestmentOpAction` (400 at `POST/PUT /api/rules`, re-checked in the executor). **Executor `materializeBankRowAsPortfolioOp`** ([reconcile/materialize-portfolio-op.ts](src/lib/reconcile/materialize-portfolio-op.ts)) lifts the investment-account guard the cash `materializeBankRowAsTransaction` keeps refusing (only after resolving/creating the position + cash sleeve via shared [portfolio/resolve-position.ts](src/lib/portfolio/resolve-position.ts), dual-writing `security_id` + `holding_accounts`), then calls [operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) (never INSERTs op rows itself), stamps bank lineage via `linkTransactionToBank`, and marks snapshots dirty. **Lot allocation is always automatic FIFO** — never rule-specified. Wired into `applyRulesToBankRows` (Auto-pilot upload + MCP `apply_rules_to_bank_rows` + on-demand); `computeReconcileForAccount` surfaces a `suggestedInvestmentOp` preview. Swap / in-kind transfer / FX conversion ops + MCP rule-creation parity deferred. **Applied in reconciliation (2026-06-20/21 follow-up):** un-gated the editor surface (no longer behind `useDevMode`); dropped `set_portfolio_holding` ("Set holding") from the new-rule action menu (schema/executor retained for legacy rules). **Unified with the cash workflow (no separate investment workflow):** import captures only; rules apply through the SAME engine + `applyRulesToBankRows` chokepoint as cash. Auto-pilot auto-applies investment-op rules at upload like cash rules; **manual reconcile = per-row suggestion + apply** — `computeReconcileForAccount` surfaces `suggestedInvestmentOp`, rendered as a per-row "→ buy" chip in [bank-pane.tsx](src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx), and the row's **Create** action applies the matched `record_investment_op` rule (replacing the blank-`/portfolio/new` redirect) via `POST /api/reconcile/apply-rules` (single-row adapter over `applyRulesToBankRows`, web parity of MCP `apply_rules_to_bank_rows`). A short-lived bulk "Apply rules" button was removed — it broke the per-row reconcile pattern. **Preview-before-record + investment account modes (2026-06-21 follow-up):** the reconcile per-row **Create** for an investment row now opens a **preview dialog** ([investment-op-preview-dialog.tsx](src/components/reconcile/investment-op-preview-dialog.tsx)) summarizing the op (operation / security / qty / amount / account) and only records on confirm — mirrors normal bank reconciliation (no auto-record on Create). The [lens-chip](src/components/inbox/lens-chip.tsx) un-grays **Auto-pilot** for investment accounts (works via the upload `applyRulesToBankRows` path); **Manual** works; **Approve-each stays disabled** for investment until its `/approve` commit flow is wired for investment ops. The Auto-pilot lens's **to-categorize tab** now records a matched investment-op row via the same preview-then-record path as the reconcile tab (previously it showed a dead "not supported in Auto-pilot" message — the "Approve/Create button does nothing" bug). **Multi-action rules (2026-06-21):** the reconcile/Auto-pilot materialize path (`applyRulesToBankRows`) now applies a rule's full `computePureActionPatch` (category + tags + payee + entered-currency) on the cash INSERT — previously only the primary action ran — and the investment executor accepts an `overrides:{payee,tags}` so an investment rule's rename_payee/set_tags ride onto the recorded op. Consistent with the staged/uncategorized paths. **Multiple investment ops per rule (2026-06-22):** a rule with several `record_investment_op` actions (e.g. buy + deposit) now executes EVERY one — `applyRulesToBankRows` loops all investment-op actions instead of running only the first; `suggestedInvestmentOp` joins all op names for the reconcile chip/preview. Tests: `tests/investment-op-rules.test.ts`. Files: `src/lib/rules/{schema,crypto,investment-op-binding,execute}.ts`, `src/lib/auto-categorize.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/resolve-position.ts`, `src/lib/reconcile/{materialize-portfolio-op,match-engine}.ts`, `src/app/api/reconcile/apply-rules/route.ts`, `src/app/api/rules/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx`, `src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx`, `tests/{investment-op-rules,rules-factory-and-crypto-boundary}.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `plan/finlynq-208-materialize-investment-imports.md`.
- **feat(crypto): Yahoo `-USD` historical tier for crypto dates beyond CoinGecko's 365-day window (2026-06-19, web).** Builds on the USD normalization: historical crypto valuations for dates older than CoinGecko's free ~365-day window previously degraded to today's spot price (inaccurate — 2024 net-worth points used 2026 prices). Now `getCryptoPricesAtDate` ([crypto-service.ts](src/lib/crypto-service.ts)) tiers by age — **within 365d → CoinGecko** (`vs_currency=usd`); **older → Yahoo `<SYM>-USD` daily history** (Yahoo serves crypto back to ~2014 via the SAME chart endpoint + negative-cache machinery the stock path uses, no API key), caching real USD historical prices under `CRYPTO:<SYM>`. New `fetchYahooDailyCloses(symbol, fromDate, today)` ([price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts)) fetches one `[fromDate, now]` chart call → `{date,close}[]` strictly before today, **negative-cache aware** (a ticker Yahoo can't serve is skipped for 10 min so a multi-year rebuild doesn't re-hit a hopeless symbol per old date). New pure `cryptoSymbolToYahooTicker(symbol)` resolver (`<SYM>-USD` default + override map; unit-tested incl. the MATIC/POL rebrand pair). Crypto trades 24/7 so Yahoo returns a close for every calendar day (no forward-fill needed); one chart call backfills a coin's whole history on the oldest-first walk (~1 call/coin), idempotent insert-missing. Anything neither tier can fill still degrades to the cache-first spot price. Net effect: historical crypto net-worth is now valued at real per-date prices AND the residual CoinGecko/spot traffic from old dates is eliminated. No schema change. Files: `src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `tests/crypto-yahoo-ticker.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **refactor(crypto): value crypto in a USD base + currency-aware reads (was CAD) (2026-06-19, web).** Crypto was the only asset class priced/cached in CAD (`vs_currency=cad`, `price_cache` rows `currency='CAD'`) with the valuation path **hardcoding** `priceCurrency = "CAD"` ([holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts)) — a legacy artifact from before the USD default. Everything else is USD-anchored (`fx_rates.rate_to_usd`; stocks carry their native `quote.currency`), so CAD-base forced an FX hop for the now-default USD users and triangulation for everyone else, and `-USD` market data has broader coverage than `-CAD`. Normalized to **USD**: `getCryptoPrice`/`getCryptoPrices`/`fetchCryptoHistoryToCache`/`getCryptoHistory` now use `vs_currency=usd` and cache `currency='USD'`; `CryptoPrice` gained a required `currency` field that every constructor populates (cache hits carry the **row's own** currency); `holdings-value` + `/api/portfolio/overview` use `cp.currency` instead of `"CAD"` and convert to the display currency via the USD-anchored FX path (overview also seeds each crypto price's currency into its FX-rate set). **Backward-compatible / no migration required:** reads carry each row's currency, so any legacy `CRYPTO:` CAD rows still convert correctly during the transition; today-rows flip to USD on the next 30-min refresh. Also fixes a latent bug in `fetchCryptoRateToUsd` ([fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts)) that returned a CAD price as if it were a USD rate. No schema change. (Dev: the existing CAD `CRYPTO:` rows are being dropped so they re-seed as USD.) Files: `src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, `src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/crypto/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **perf(crypto): don't call CoinGecko for historical dates older than its free ~365-day window (2026-06-19, web).** During a multi-year snapshot rebuild, `getCryptoPricesAtDate` ([src/lib/crypto-service.ts](src/lib/crypto-service.ts)) called `fetchCryptoHistoryToCache` for EVERY missing `(coin, date)` — but CoinGecko's free tier only serves ~365 days of daily history, so for any date older than that the request can never cover the date (it just re-returns the same trailing 365-day window, caches nothing new) and the code falls back to spot anyway. With 19 crypto holdings going back ~2.5 years, that was one wasted CoinGecko request per old date per coin — the dominant outbound traffic observed during a rebuild (sniffed: CoinGecko/Cloudflare was the heaviest talker). Fix: gate the fetch behind the new pure `isWithinCryptoHistoryWindow(date, today)` (exported, unit-tested; `CRYPTO_FREE_HISTORY_DAYS = 365`, strict `<` to stay off the boundary) — out-of-window dates skip straight to the cache-first spot approximation (step 3), which reads today's cached row first so it's ~1 call per coin, not one per day. In-window behavior is byte-identical. No schema change. (Separately notable: historical crypto on out-of-window dates is valued at ~spot — a pre-existing accuracy approximation, unchanged here.) Files: `src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `tests/crypto-history-window.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(portfolio/snapshots): dirty marker never cleared → rebuild re-ran on EVERY chart load (microsecond-vs-millisecond bug) (2026-06-19, web).** Root cause of "it keeps rebuilding / network traffic every time I open the page." `clearDirtyIfUnchanged` ([src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/dirty.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/dirty.ts)) deleted the `portfolio_snapshot_dirty` row with `WHERE marked_at <= ${markedAt}`, but `markedAt` is captured via `listDirtySnapshotUsers` → JS `Date` → `.toISOString()` (**millisecond** precision, e.g. `…45.010Z`), while the stored `marked_at` is `timestamptz` with **microsecond** precision (`…45.010582+00`). So `.010582 <= .010000` evaluated **FALSE**, the DELETE matched nothing, and the marker survived — so `kickSelfHeal` re-fired a full-history rebuild on every Net Worth chart load forever (the dev marker from 2026-06-17 had survived dozens of runs). Verified directly on the DB: `marked_at <= '…010Z'` → `f`; `date_trunc('milliseconds', marked_at) <= '…010Z'` → `t`. Fix: truncate the stored value to milliseconds before comparing (`date_trunc('milliseconds', marked_at) <= ${markedAt}::timestamptz`) so it matches the captured precision; a genuine mid-rebuild re-stamp lands in a LATER millisecond and still correctly survives (no lost edits). No schema change. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/dirty.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(portfolio/snapshots): chart-load self-heal now reports progress into the shared registry (no more phantom "starting…" on page open) (2026-06-19, web).** Opening the Net Worth chart fires the DEK-bearing self-heal (`kickSelfHeal` in [src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts](src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts)) when a dirty marker / missing-snapshots condition is set. It called `tryBeginRebuild(userId)` — which seeds the SHARED `globalThis` rebuild registry `running:true` — but invoked `rebuildPortfolioSnapshots(...)` **without an `onProgress` callback**, so `totalDays`/`daysProcessed` never updated. The `RebuildSnapshotsButton` polls that same registry on mount, so it showed **"starting…" before the user clicked anything** and stayed there for the whole (correctly-progressing) walk. Fix: the self-heal now threads `reportRebuildProgress(userId, done, total)` (mirroring the manual POST route) and calls `endRebuild(userId, { result | error })` so the button shows a real determinate "day X of Y" bar and a terminal "Rebuilt N days" / error state. Combined with the `onProgress(0, totalDays)`-before-loop change, a self-heal that's running on page open now renders an accurate determinate bar from the first poll. No behavior change to the rebuild computation; the in-flight guard + dirty-clear are unchanged. Files: `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **perf(portfolio/snapshots): one multi-row INSERT per rebuild day + drop a dead per-day query (2026-06-19, web, follows the windowed-cache fix below).** Measured a running rebuild on dev: ~13% CPU, zero live Yahoo/FX fetch chatter, no slow PG queries — i.e. the walk is **I/O-latency bound on serialized per-day DB round-trips**, not compute or network. `buildDailySnapshot` ([src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/builder.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/builder.ts)) previously issued one `db.execute(INSERT … ON CONFLICT)` PER investment account in a loop **plus** a separate aggregate INSERT (~N+1 round-trips/day) **and** awaited `computeNetContributions(...)` every day only to DISCARD the result (`void todaysFlows` — "for parity / future MWRR"). Now: the per-account rows + the whole-portfolio aggregate are collected and written in a **single multi-row INSERT** (built with `sql.join`, same COALESCE(account_id,-1) ON CONFLICT target; per-account ids are distinct and the aggregate is NULL→-1 so no intra-statement conflict), and the dead `computeNetContributions` call (a pure read, no side effects) + its import are removed. Drops per-day round-trips from ~N+2 to ~2 → materially faster multi-year rebuilds. **Numeric results identical**; the only behavior change is `gaps_filled` is now the day-level final FX-gap flag applied uniformly to all of the day's rows (was incidentally per-account by insert order; the aggregate already used the final value). No schema change. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/builder.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(portfolio/snapshots): "Rebuild investment history" no longer looks stuck on "starting…" + windowed historical price-cache fill (2026-06-19, web, follows FINLYNQ-205/204).** Two changes. **(1) Progress denominator published up front:** `rebuildPortfolioSnapshots` now emits `onProgress(0, totalDays)` BEFORE the day-by-day walk, not only after each day. The status bar's "starting…" state shows when `running && totalDays === 0`; previously `totalDays` was first reported only after day 1's `buildDailySnapshot` returned, so on a large cold-cache account (e.g. 53 holdings × ~1,267 days of as-of-date pricing) the bar sat on the indeterminate "starting…" for the entire multi-minute first day and a healthy-but-slow run looked hung. Now it shows a determinate "day 0 of N" immediately. **(2) Efficient quote pulls — cache the whole window:** `fetchQuoteAtDate` (the historical as-of-date path used by snapshot rebuilds + per-holding history) now fetches a FORWARD window (`-7d` to `+60d`, capped at now) and persists the WHOLE window — one `price_cache` row per CALENDAR day, weekends/holidays forward-filled to the most recent prior close — instead of only the single target date. Historical rows are immutable so over-caching is safe; this turns a sequential rebuild walk from ~1 Yahoo call PER (symbol, day) into ~1 call per ~2-month window per symbol (the root cause of the slow cold-dev-cache rebuild). New pure exported `buildForwardFilledRows(...)` (unit-tested: weekend fill, today/future exclusion, pre-first-bar skip); the DB writer `cacheHistoricalWindow` reads existing dates in range ONCE and inserts only the missing dates in a single multi-row INSERT (never touches an existing row — `(symbol,date)` is non-unique — and never writes today/future, which stays TTL-managed by the live path). `writePriceCache` (today-row path) unchanged; `previous_close` stays null on these historical bars. No schema change, no migration. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts`, `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `tests/rebuild-progress-registry.test.ts`, `tests/price-window-forward-fill.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(import/reconcile): surface captured investment fields (ticker / security name / quantity) in the reconcile bank pane (FINLYNQ-207, 2026-06-19, web).** Display-only follow-up to FINLYNQ-195, which captured ticker/security_name/quantity into `staged_transactions` + `bank_transactions` (ticker/security_name encrypted-in-place per the two-tier scheme; quantity numeric) but never threaded them past capture — so an investment statement import landed fields the user could never see at `/import`. Now, when the reconcile account `isInvestment`, the **Bank ledger** pane renders three gated columns — **Ticker**, **Security**, and **Qty** — populated with the per-row-decrypted values. **No portfolio-op materialization (FINLYNQ-208) and no reconcile rules (FINLYNQ-209) — display/fields only.** **Threading:** `BankCandidateRow` ([src/lib/reconcile/bank-ledger-pool.ts](src/lib/reconcile/bank-ledger-pool.ts)) gains `tickerPlain`/`securityNamePlain`/`quantity`; the pool selects the three columns and decrypts ticker + security_name with the SAME per-`encryption_tier` branch it already uses for payee — `service`-tier → `decryptStaged()`, `user`-tier → `tryDecryptField(dek)` — via a generalized `decryptBankField(tier,dek,value,label)` (was `decryptBankPayee`). `tryDecryptField` returns **null on auth-tag failure / no DEK** (load-bearing — never the raw ciphertext); the UI then renders the em-dash placeholder. `quantity` is plain numeric (no decrypt). **Snapshot shape (tc-3):** `ReconcileBankSnapshot` ([match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts)) gains OPTIONAL `ticker?`/`securityName?`/`quantity?` — attached ONLY when a row actually captured an investment field (investment-account import); cash rows leave all three NULL so the keys stay **OFF** the snapshot and the cash reconcile view + `GET /api/reconcile/suggestions` shape are byte-identical to today (the route spreads `result`, so no route change). **UI (tc-1/tc-2/tc-4):** `BankPane` ([src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx](src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx)) gains an `isInvestment` prop (default false → cash byte-identical) that gates the Ticker/Security/Qty `<TableHead>`s + cells; quantity renders via a pure `formatQuantity` as a **share/unit count with NO currency symbol** (`formatCurrency` is NOT used for it). `InboxReconcileTab` ([inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx)) derives `isInvestmentAccount` from the already-threaded `accounts[]`/`accountId`, threads the three fields onto each `BankRow`, and passes `isInvestment` to `BankPane`. `TransactionsPane` is unchanged — the system-side `transactions` table carries no investment-capture fields (FINLYNQ-195 never materializes into `transactions`). The reconcile-summary `DataTable` is account-grained (not per-bank-row) so it gets no per-row investment columns. 7 new unit tests: `tests/reconcile-investment-fields-pool.test.ts` (per-tier decrypt with REAL crypto — user-tier via DEK, service-tier via staging key, wrong-DEK → null-not-ciphertext, cash → null; + quantity passthrough) and `tests/reconcile-investment-fields-snapshot.test.ts` (investment row carries the 3 keys, cash row OMITS them, partial qty-only row). `tsc` + `audit:invariants` (15/15) clean. No migration; web only — MCP read tools + mobile unchanged. Files: `src/lib/reconcile/bank-ledger-pool.ts`, `src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts`, `src/components/reconcile/bank-pane.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx`, `tests/reconcile-investment-fields-pool.test.ts` (new), `tests/reconcile-investment-fields-snapshot.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): "Rebuild investment history" now shows server-tracked progress that survives reload (FINLYNQ-205, 2026-06-19, web).** The Settings → Investments "Rebuild investment history" control (also mounted in the net-worth chart empty-state) used to fire a single blocking `POST` and show only a small spinning icon — no progress, and a reload mid-rebuild reverted the button to idle even though the ~minute-long DEK-bearing day-by-day re-materialize was still running server-side. **Approach (migration-free, async + server-side progress registry):** `POST /api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild` now captures the session DEK, kicks the walk off **fire-and-forget**, and returns **202** immediately; progress is tracked in an HMR-safe per-user registry on `globalThis` (`__pfRebuildProgress`, the same pattern as the DB adapter / MCP tx cache — survives Next HMR AND browser reloads on the single-instance deploy, NO DB migration). The old `Set<string>` in-flight guard was upgraded in place to a `Map<string, RebuildProgress>` keeping `{running, daysProcessed, totalDays, lastResult, error}`; `tryBeginRebuild`/`endRebuild`/`isRebuildInFlight` keep their signatures (the net-worth-history self-heal caller is unchanged), and `endRebuild` now optionally records the terminal `{result}`/`{error}`. **`rebuildPortfolioSnapshots` gained an optional `onProgress(daysProcessed, totalDays)` callback** emitted once per day — the snapshot COMPUTATION is byte-identical (only instrumentation added); `totalDays` comes from a new pure `dayspanInclusive(from,to)`. New read-only `GET /api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/status` (`requireAuth`, no DEK) returns the registry snapshot. **`RebuildSnapshotsButton`** polls the status route on mount (re-attaching to an in-flight rebuild after a reload) and every 1.5s while running, renders an unmistakable in-progress panel with a determinate "Processing day X of Y" count + progress bar (indeterminate pulse until the first day lands), and clears to "Rebuilt N days" on completion. Because all UI logic lives in the shared button, **both mount points behave identically**. Acceptance bullets (a) unmistakable indicator, (b) progress conveyed, (c) reload-survival, (d) both-mounts: all met. 12 new unit tests (`tests/rebuild-progress-registry.test.ts`): registry lifecycle (seed/refuse-concurrent/linger-after-complete/restart), copy-on-read isolation, success vs error terminal state, `dayspanInclusive` edge cases, and `onProgress` per-day emission through `rebuildPortfolioSnapshots`. `tsc` + `lint` + `audit:invariants` (15/15) all clean. No migration; web only (MCP/mobile out of scope). Files: `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/route.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/status/route.ts` (new), `src/components/portfolio/rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx`, `tests/rebuild-progress-registry.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(prices): intraday 30-min TTL on today's price_cache rows so Portfolio day-change isn't frozen (FINLYNQ-204, 2026-06-19, web).** A stock/ETF (and crypto valuation) price was frozen at the FIRST cache fill of the UTC calendar day — `price_cache` is keyed `(symbol, date=todayISO())` and the read path was cache-first with no expiry, so the displayed day-change (`current − previous_close`) drifted hard intraday and after the close (verified on prod: VTI showed −1.24% vs an actual −0.10% EOD). Owner-approved Option A: give "today's" row a **30-minute TTL**, lazy refresh on read, no new cron; historical rows (`date != today`) stay immutable forever. New additive migration `scripts/migrations/20260625_price_cache_fetched_at.sql` (`ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS fetched_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`, auto-applied by `deploy.sh`; existing rows backfill to `now()`) + Drizzle `priceCache.fetchedAt`. Shared `PRICE_CACHE_TODAY_TTL_MS = 30*60*1000` + pure null-safe `isPriceCacheRowStale(rowDate, fetchedAt, today)` in `price-service.ts`, imported by `crypto-service.ts`. `readPriceCache`/`readPriceCacheBulk` now report per-row staleness; `fetchQuote`/`fetchMultipleQuotes` re-fetch the union of (missing ∪ stale-today) symbols and **UPDATE … WHERE symbol=? AND date=?** in place (NOT `ON CONFLICT` — the `(symbol,date)` index is non-unique and prod has duplicate rows), stamping `fetched_at=now()` + `previous_close`. **Retain-on-failure (load-bearing):** a failed live re-fetch of a stale row keeps serving the last-known price (its `fetched_at` untouched → retries next read) instead of blanking the holding; the existing 4s `AbortSignal.timeout` + 10-min negative cache are untouched. Crypto parity in `getCryptoSpotPrices` (same TTL, same retain-on-failure, `fetched_at` stamped on the crypto cache write) so dashboard / net-worth crypto valuations refresh every 30 min. 16 new unit tests (`tests/price-cache-ttl.test.ts`, `tests/crypto-cache-ttl.test.ts`): fresh<30m hit (no fetch), stale>30m refresh+recompute, stale+fetch-fail retain, historical never-refresh, duplicate-row UPDATE-in-place, crypto parity. `tsc` + `lint` + `audit:invariants` all clean. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260625_price_cache_fetched_at.sql` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `tests/price-cache-ttl.test.ts` (new), `tests/crypto-cache-ttl.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(reports): Last 12 Months preset now uses complete calendar months (FINLYNQ-203, 2026-06-18, web).** The "Last 12 Months" date preset on `/reports` was using a rolling `today − 1 year → today` window, producing partial months at both ends and double-counting boundary months. Fixed to use the **12 most recently completed calendar months**: `end` = last day of the prior (most recently completed) month; `start` = first day of the month 12 months before the current month. The in-progress current month is excluded, consistent with how `last-month` and `last-quarter` already work. Also: gave the `default` switch branch its own fallback (YTD, same as `"ytd"`), so no preset can ever produce a NaN range; refactored `getPresetRange` to accept an optional `now: Date` parameter (defaulting to `new Date()`) and exported it for unit-testing. Date strings built from local date components (`getFullYear()`/`getMonth()`/`getDate()`) — no `toISOString()` TZ drift. Unit tests (`tests/reports-preset-range.test.ts`) assert all 4 cases (tc-1 standard, tc-2 year boundary, tc-3 Feb non-leap, tc-4 Feb leap) pass deterministically. Initial `useState` seeds (`startDate`/`endDate`/`datePreset`) still agree on first render (ytd). mtd/qtd/ytd/last-month/last-quarter/last-year unchanged. `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking). Files: `src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx`, `tests/reports-preset-range.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **refactor(portfolio): classify ETF via Yahoo quoteType + user-settable asset type; drop hardcoded ETF literals (FINLYNQ-201, 2026-06-18, web + mobile-dev).** The ETF-vs-stock badge on `/portfolio` was derived at display time from `getEtfRegionBreakdown(symbol) !== null` — a lookup into a hardcoded ~80-entry `ETF_REGIONS` literal baked into the bundle. Any ETF not in the list (e.g. VDE) defaulted to the "Stocks" badge, and adding one meant a code change + redeploy. **Owner decision (chat 2026-06-18, OVERRIDING the title's "DB table"): NO new table** — classify from Yahoo's `quoteType`/`instrumentType` ('ETF', already returned by the quote layer) PLUS a user-settable asset type at security create/edit. **Classification mechanism + resolution order (durable, stable across warm/cold `price_cache`):** (1) user-set / persisted `securities.asset_type === 'etf'` (the user override always wins) → (2) live Yahoo `quoteType === 'ETF'` on this fetch → (3) fallback `stock`. **quoteType durability:** `price_cache` stores no quoteType, so a warm-cache hit carries `quoteType = null` — to keep the badge from flipping on cache warmth, `/api/portfolio/overview` **persists a freshly-resolved live Yahoo 'ETF' onto `securities.asset_type`** (fire-and-forget, best-effort, DEK-gated), but ONLY promotes a security currently typed `"stock"` (the eq-bucket default) → `"etf"`; a non-`"stock"` stored type (a user override or already-`"etf"`) is left untouched, so once set the badge is durable and the user override is never clobbered. **`QuoteResult.quoteType` + pure `isEtfQuoteType()`** added to [src/lib/price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts) (threaded from `fetchQuoteLive`'s `meta.instrumentType`). **NO RE-CLUSTER (tc-4, load-bearing):** stock + ETF share the `eq:` cluster bucket; `securities.cluster_key` derives from the SYMBOL (`eq:<symbol_lookup>` HMAC), NOT `asset_type` ([canonical.ts](src/lib/securities/canonical.ts) — `asset_type` is cosmetic via `KIND_TO_ASSET_TYPE`). Setting/persisting `asset_type='etf'` keeps the same `security_id`/`cluster_key`, so net-worth + by-holding totals stay byte-identical — `tests/securities-parity.test.ts` 6/6 + `audit:invariants` `securities-dual-write`/`securities-dual-write-on-edit` both green. **User control:** `PATCH /api/securities` ([route.ts](src/app/api/securities/route.ts)) accepts an optional `assetType` (cosmetic, never re-clusters); the `/settings/investments` Securities tab "Edit" dialog (formerly "Rename") gains a Stock/ETF asset-type selector for tradable (non-cash, non-crypto) securities. **Dropped:** `ETF_REGIONS`/`ETF_SECTORS`/`ETF_TOP_HOLDINGS` + the US/SP500/INTL/CA constituent arrays + `getHardcodedEtf*` + the dead `autoSeedEtfIfMissing` no-op + the stale "ETF database" comments (`rg "ETF_REGIONS|ETF_SECTORS|ETF_TOP_HOLDINGS|autoSeedEtfIfMissing" src/lib/price-service.ts` → 0, tc-3). **X-Ray graceful-empty:** `getEtfRegionBreakdown`/`getEtfSectorBreakdown`/`getEtfTopHoldings`/`getAvailableEtfSymbols`/`aggregatePortfolioExposure` are kept as stubs returning null/empty, so the ETF X-Ray region/sector/top-holdings breakdown (`/api/portfolio/overview` + `/api/portfolio/etf-breakdown` + `/api/prices`) degrades to an EMPTY breakdown with NO crash — the breakdown DATA source is repopulated separately in **FINLYNQ-202** (deferred; feature unreleased). **Mobile mirror (mobile-dev worktree):** the literal removal + Yahoo-`quoteType` classification are mirrored in `pf-mobile/src/lib/price-service.ts` + `pf-mobile/src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` (mobile stays on the legacy read path with no `securities` join, so it's Yahoo-`quoteType`-only — no user-override layer / no persist). `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking, `✓ Compiled successfully`) · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `securities-parity` 6/6 · mobile `tsc --noEmit` exit 0. No migration (`securities.asset_type` already exists). MCP read tools unchanged. Files: `src/lib/price-service.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `src/app/api/securities/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx`, `pf-mobile/src/lib/price-service.ts` (mobile-dev), `pf-mobile/src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` (mobile-dev), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **refactor(portfolio): retire vestigial per-holding name lock + login canonicalizer — superseded by securities master (FINLYNQ-198, 2026-06-18, web).** The securities-master read-flip (`3c6db8b`) + copy-on-rename made display names come from the `securities` row, leaving the OLD per-position name machinery as dead weight that **actively reverted** security-level renames on next login. Retired four pieces: **(1)** `isCanonicalHolding` predicate + its docstring ([src/lib/schemas/holding.ts](src/lib/schemas/holding.ts)); **(2)** the PUT "name is auto-managed" 400 rejection on the holding update ([src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts)) — a per-position Name edit is now a legitimate write (the `currentName`/`nextName`/`nextSymbol` derivations stay; they feed the securities-master edit-time re-resolve); **(3)** the edit-form Name lock — `nameAutoManaged` derivation + the `disabled` prop + the "Name is auto-managed…" hint ([src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx](src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx)). `HoldingEditForm` is still mounted at `/portfolio` (per-row edit-in-place), so per the body's decision the Name field is **unlocked** (edit path kept), not removed; **(4)** the login canonicalizer — `canonicalizePortfolioNamesIfReady`/`enqueueCanonicalizePortfolioNames` **fully removed** ([src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts](src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts) deleted) along with all 3 login call sites + imports (`login/route.ts`, `mfa/verify/route.ts`, `try-demo/route.ts`) and the stale `audit-invariants.ts` baseline exception keyed on the deleted file. **Full removal (not conservative no-op) chosen because** the canonicalizer was no longer subsumed-but-harmless — it overwrote `name_ct` back to `symbol`/"Cash <CCY>" on every login, REVERTING security-level renames that copy-on-rename had propagated. The login-time `backfillSecuritiesForUser` (wired at the EXACT same 3 sites, same DEK-sample-decrypt precondition) is the parity successor for identity management. **KEPT (per spec):** the `users.portfolio_names_canonicalized_at` column (no destructive migration — the historical schema-prep SQL gains a "retired" header note) AND the legacy `canonicalKey` display fallback (overview route / canonical.ts) so flag-off / un-backfilled / no-DEK rows still display in prod (read-flip is default OFF on prod; FINLYNQ-200 flips it). **tc-4 coverage confirmation:** transactions ledger ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) LEFT JOINs `securities`, Phase 2) + `/api/portfolio/overview` (FINLYNQ-194 `resolveSecurityName`) read from `securities` with a `?? canonicalKey(h).name` fallback; `isCanonicalHolding` played NO role in any display path — only the 400 + the field lock — so its removal touches no read surface or the canonicalKey fallback. `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking, `✓ Compiled successfully`) · `audit:invariants` 15/15 (incl. `securities-dual-write` 12 + `securities-dual-write-on-edit` 7 — write-side untouched) · `rg "isCanonicalHolding"` → only the historical-note doc comment, 0 live consumers. The `holding-edit-form.test.tsx` "disables Name on canonical row" case was flipped to assert the field stays ENABLED + the hint is gone. No migration; web only — MCP read tools + mobile unaffected (writes already dual-write `security_id`). Files: `src/lib/schemas/holding.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/route.ts`, `src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx`, `src/lib/crypto/stream-d-canonicalize-portfolio.ts` (deleted), `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts`, `src/app/api/auth/mfa/verify/route.ts`, `src/app/try-demo/route.ts`, `scripts/audit-invariants.ts`, `scripts/migrate-portfolio-canonical-names.sql`, `src/lib/securities/backfill.ts`, `src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts`, `tests/components/holding-edit-form.test.tsx`, `tests/auth/mfa-verify-rate-limit.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(import): ticker / security-name / quantity column mapping for investment accounts — staging capture v1 (FINLYNQ-195, 2026-06-18, web).** When the `/import` target is an **investment account**, the column-mapping dialog now offers three extra mappings — **Ticker / Symbol**, **Security name**, and **Quantity** — so a brokerage/holdings CSV export can be brought in through the import surface (previously cash-account-centric). **v1 is deliberately STAGING-CAPTURE ONLY** (locked by the user decision 2026-06-18): the mapped values thread through preview → staging (`staged_transactions`) → `bank_transactions`; v1 does **NOT** materialize lot-aware portfolio operations (no +stock/−cash legs, no `resolveOrCreateSecurity`, no write into `transactions` for an investment account — which would violate the `is_investment ⇒ portfolio_holdings` constraint). Materialization is a deferred follow-up. **UI gating:** the three fields render in their own "Investment columns" section in `ColumnMappingDialog` ([src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx)) + as live-sample columns, ONLY when the bound account `isInvestment` (threaded `/import/page.tsx` → `UploadDrawer` → dialog); a non-investment import is byte-identical (investment keys are stripped at confirm). **Schema (additive migration `scripts/migrations/20260624_import_investment_fields.sql`):** new nullable `ticker` + `security_name` TEXT columns on BOTH `bank_transactions` and `staged_transactions` (`quantity` already existed on both). **Encryption (load-bearing):** `ticker` + `security_name` are SENSITIVE free-text, so they're encrypted-in-place under the row's two-tier scheme (v1: user-DEK / sv1: PF_STAGING_KEY) exactly like `payee`/`note` — write via `encryptField` in `buildStagedRow` + `upsertBankTransaction` ([src/lib/bank-ledger.ts](src/lib/bank-ledger.ts)); read paths decrypt per-row on `encryption_tier` (staged detail GET, data export); the login-time service→user sweep ([upgrade-staging-encryption.ts](src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts)) re-encrypts them; backup export/restore round-trip them as plaintext↔re-encrypt (`BANK_TX_FIELDS` / `BANK_TX_ENC_FIELDS`). **Quantity guard:** the staging-upload route ([src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts)) — which doesn't route through `previewImport` — now calls `findUnreasonableAmountError(rows)` before any row is staged, so a garbage qty (e.g. `1e29`) is rejected with HTTP 400 (mirrors the OFX/IBKR direct-emit branches; `previewImport` already covered quantity on the CSV/PDF path). **Plumbing:** `ColumnMapping` gains typed `quantity`/`portfolioHolding`/`ticker` keys (templates round-trip them in the JSON blob, no migration); `RawTransaction` gains `ticker`; the CSV mapper parses `mapping["ticker"]`. **Formats:** generic CSV template / column-mapper path only (the surface with a mappable-column UI). OFX/QFX/IBKR direct-emit branches deferred. No `transactions`/portfolio-op writes; web only; MCP + mobile unchanged. `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking) · `audit:invariants` 15/15 (incl. #12 staging-metadata-encrypted, #13 source-filenames-encrypted). Files: `scripts/migrations/20260624_import_investment_fields.sql` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/import-templates.ts`, `src/lib/import-pipeline.ts`, `src/lib/csv-parser.ts`, `src/lib/bank-ledger.ts`, `src/lib/import/simplified-upload.ts`, `src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `src/app/api/data/export/route.ts`, `src/app/api/data/import/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/edit-template-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/save-template-dialog.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): Realized gains report gains a "hide zero-gain rows" toggle + group-by (holding / account / both) (FINLYNQ-193, 2026-06-18, web).** Two additive, component-state view options on the Portfolio → Realized gains report ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx)) — both implemented **client-side** over the already-fetched `data.rows` (FINLYNQ-129/172 toggle precedent); the `GET /api/portfolio/realized-gains` route, `listRealizedGainClosures`, `realizedGainsToCsv`, the MCP `get_realized_gains` tool, and mobile all stay on the legacy flat shape (unchanged). **(A) Hide zero-gain rows** — a checkbox (mirrors the existing "Show in <ccy>" styling) that drops closures whose **currently-displayed** realized gain is `0` — the native `realizedGain` in native view, the unified `realizedGainInBase` in the "Show in <ccy>" view — so the row showing `0` on screen is exactly the one hidden (cleanly removes the `fx_conversion` 0-gain noise in native view). **(B) Group by** — an Off / By holding / By account / Holding + account selector that collapses the flat list into rolled-up rows summing `qtyClosed` + realized gain, with a closure count + a close-date range (per-share / open / close columns drop — not meaningful aggregated). **LOAD-BEARING mixed-currency rule (documented choice):** group-by is **UNIFIED-DISPLAY-VIEW-ONLY** — every non-Off mode is **disabled in native view** (with an inline hint to enable "Show in <ccy>"), and an effect resets a stale group mode to Off if the user turns the unified toggle back off. The unified view converts every closure into the single display currency, so summing realized gain across a group is always one-currency-correct; **no cross-currency native sum is ever rendered** (CLAUDE.md "never SUM across mixed currencies under one label"). Summary badges + the closed-lot count reflect the **visible (filtered/grouped)** set, recomputed client-side. **CSV honors the active state** — the export button switched from a server `?format=csv` link to a client-side download via the shared `exportCsv` ([src/lib/csv-export.ts](src/lib/csv-export.ts)) over the SAME filtered/grouped rows + totals shown on screen (flat CSV mirrors the server columns + an extra unified column when active; grouped CSV emits the rollup columns). Tax-year / term / native-vs-unified filters unchanged. No migration; web only. `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking) · `lint` 0 errors (192 pre-existing warnings). Files: `src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(ui): base-ui Select triggers now show human-readable labels instead of raw ids/enum tokens — sweep of all confirmed-buggy sites (FINLYNQ-197, 2026-06-18, web).** base-ui v4 `Select.Value` renders the raw `value` string unless the `<Select>` Root receives an `items: Record<string,string>` value→label map. 13 files had id-valued or enum-token-valued selects (Account, Holding, Category, Type, Priority) whose triggers showed values like `"42"`, `"A"`, `"1"`, `"savings"`. All are display-only bugs — selection and submission were always correct. **Fix pattern:** build a `useMemo`-derived `Record<string,string>` from the same source array feeding the `<SelectItem>`s, pass it as `items` prop on the `<Select>` Root. Module-level constants used for static enums (no reactive dependency). **Strict scope:** `src/components/ui/select.tsx` is untouched; the reference implementation (`/settings/investments/page.tsx`) and three existing custom `render`-prop workarounds (`inbox-email-tab.tsx`, `import/page.tsx`, `connector-mapping-dialog.tsx`, `email-rule-dialog.tsx`) are untouched per spec. **Sites fixed (13 files, ~20 selects):** Account type A/L→Asset/Liability (`accounts/page.tsx`); Goal type + priority (`goals/page.tsx`); Account select (`holding-edit-form.tsx`); Account + Holding (`BuyForm`, `SellForm`); Account only (`FxConversionForm`); Source/Dest accounts + cash sleeve (`DepositForm`, `WithdrawalForm`); Source/Dest accounts + Holding (`TransferForm`); Account + Source/Dest holdings (`SwapForm`); Account + Holding + Category + Direction + incomeType (`IncomeExpenseForm`); Multi-account picker (`account-selector.tsx`, `useMemo` moved before early returns to satisfy Rules of Hooks); Target account + portfolio holding including `__none__` sentinels (`staged-row-editor.tsx`). **Secondary enum selects** (Direction `income`/`expense`, incomeType `dividend`/`interest`/`fee`/`other`, Type `savings`/`debt_payoff`) folded in where cheap. **Skipped:** currency-code selects where `value === display string` (CAD, USD, etc.). `tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors (191 pre-existing warnings). No migration; web only. Files: `src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/goals/page.tsx`, `src/components/holdings/holding-edit-form.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/BuyForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/SellForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/FxConversionForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/DepositForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/WithdrawalForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/TransferForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/SwapForm.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/IncomeExpenseForm.tsx`, `src/components/import/reconcile/account-selector.tsx`, `src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(ui): shared sortable DataTable + reconcile summary Current balance column (FINLYNQ-196, 2026-06-18, web).** Two things, one foundation. **(1) The standard:** extracted ONE centrally-managed, descriptor-driven, sortable (+ optionally filterable / column-hideable) flat-table layout — `DataTable<T>` ([src/components/ui/data-table.tsx](src/components/ui/data-table.tsx)) — that **composes** the presentational `ui/table.tsx` primitives (does NOT replace them). Before this, three near-identical hand-rolled sort state machines lived in the admin-users, securities, and portfolio-holdings tables. Configured by a `DataTableColumn<T>[]` array (`key`/`header`/`accessor`/`render?`/`sortable?`(default true)/`align?`/`filter?`(`text`\|`select`)/`hideable?`/`defaultHidden?`/`className?`), mirroring `exportCsv`'s `{header,accessor}` descriptor (FINLYNQ-144). **Sort = 2-state** (starts unsorted; first click asc, then toggles asc↔desc — matches the pre-existing reference tables), **client-side** (tiny row counts), with `aria-sort` on every sortable header and a chevron indicator. The exported pure `compareValues` is **null-safe** (string via `(a??"").localeCompare(b??"")`, numeric where null sorts smallest) per the load-bearing decrypted-name-defense invariant — sorting a column whose accessor returns null never throws. Per-column header filters + column show/hide are built as opt-in props now, wired on demand (the reconcile panel needs only sort). Sort/filter/visibility are internal (uncontrolled) by default; optional `sort`+`onSortChange` opt into controlled sort. A **feature audit** of the app's ~26 table files drove the prop surface, captured in a new maintained **Table Artifact Registry** ([docs/ui-tables.md](docs/ui-tables.md)) that classifies every table artifact centrally-managed vs custom (collapsible / nested / two-pane / reconcile-flow / static-marketing tables are deliberately custom with one-line reasons) — linked from CLAUDE.md UI conventions + docs/ui-design.md; every future table PR updates it. **(2) The feature (first consumer / dogfood):** the `/import` Reconciliation summary panel ([reconcile-summary-panel.tsx](src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx), FINLYNQ-147/184) — a bespoke raw `<table>` — now renders via `DataTable`: columns Account / **Current balance** (new) / Last import / Last reconciled / Pending are all click-sortable, with a non-sortable Open action column. **Current balance** is added to `GET /api/reconcile/summary` ([route](src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts)) by feeding `getAccountBalances()`'s raw ledger `COALESCE(SUM(amount),0)` through `applyInvestmentMarketOverlay` (FINLYNQ-151, the same overlay the MCP balance tools use) so **investment accounts show MARKET value** per the "account with holdings = `holdings.value`" invariant — NEVER a naive `SUM(transactions.amount)` (which for investment accounts is net contributions). This is a web-session route (`requireAuth`, DEK present) so the overlay can price holdings; a DEK-null caller degrades to the ledger balance per the overlay's own guard. Each balance displays in the account's OWN currency via `formatCurrency(balance, currency)` (no cross-currency conversion), right-aligned + `tabular-nums`. **No regression to FINLYNQ-184** (archived + `reconcile_hidden_accounts` accounts still excluded server-side), the lazy-load-on-first-expand, or the Refresh control. **Phase 3 deferred** — migrating the securities / admin-users / portfolio-holdings hand-rolled sort onto `DataTable` is a follow-up (registry rows marked "central-path candidate — not yet migrated"); suggest its own ticket. No migration; web only (mobile has its own table patterns). `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking) · `audit:invariants` 15/15. **Implementation note:** the per-account balance is computed at the route boundary (reusing the existing `getAccountBalances()` + the overlay) rather than inside `getReconcileSummary` — the summary core stays DEK-free per its existing design, so `src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts` is unchanged. Files: `src/components/ui/data-table.tsx` (new), `docs/ui-tables.md` (new), `src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx`, `src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts`, `docs/ui-design.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(portfolio): resolve All Holdings + Top Movers names from the securities table — one source of display names (FINLYNQ-194, 2026-06-18, web).** On `/portfolio`, **Holdings by Account** reflected security renames/additions but **All Holdings** and **Top Gainers/Losers** did not — they showed stale, symbol-synthesized names and never picked up a name set in the Securities catalog. All three render off `/api/portfolio/overview` but read DIFFERENT name fields: By Account used the per-position `name` (kept in sync by copy-on-rename ✅), while All Holdings (`byHolding`) + Top Movers read `canonicalKey(h).displayName` — synthesized from the SYMBOL/currency (`eq:`/`crypto:`/`metal:` → SYMBOL, `cash:` → "Cash <CCY>", only `custom:` reads the stored name) plus a Yahoo `quoteName` description that is null on a warm `price_cache` hit ❌. Even with the read-flip ON (rows bucketing by `security_id`), the route never SELECTed `securities.name_ct`. **Fix — single source of names:** [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) now **LEFT JOINs `securities` via `portfolio_holdings.security_id`** (aliased) and decrypts `securities.name_ct` through the existing per-user DEK `decryptNamedRows` path into a new `securityName` field on each holding. A pure resolver `resolveSecurityName(h)` returns the trimmed security name ONLY when `useSecurityGrouping` (`securitiesReadEnabledForUser`) is ON **and** `securityId != null` **and** the name is non-empty; otherwise `null`. The `byHolding` bucket's display `name` (and its `description`, so a user rename WINS over Yahoo `quoteName` in the client `holdingDescription` resolver) is set from `resolveSecurityName(h) ?? canonicalKey(h).name`. **Top Movers inherit it for free** — `aggregateMovers`'s `displayOf` callback returns `resolveSecurityName(h) ?? ck.name` (no name logic duplicated in `top-movers.ts`); the shared `moverBucketKey` grouping key is **untouched** (display-name only, GROUPING unchanged). **By Account converged** — [holdings-by-account.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-by-account.tsx) prefers `securityName` over Yahoo `quoteName`/per-position name for symbol-bearing rows (cash/custom fallback unchanged). **Parity (load-bearing, tc-3):** flag-off / un-backfilled (`security_id` null) / no-DEK rows get `securityName = null` ⇒ the legacy `canonicalKey` name + null-then-quoteName `description` path ⇒ All Holdings names AND every `byHolding` aggregate total stay **byte-identical to today**. New required `EnrichedHolding.securityName` field ([_types.ts](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts)) — server always sets it; no client constructs an `EnrichedHolding` literal. Phase-3-adjacent to plan/securities-display-consistency-plan.md (the Transactions ledger already single-sources from `securities` when the flag is on). No migration; web/REST only — MCP read tools + per-holding Performance chart + mobile stay on the legacy path. `npm run build` exit 0 (tsc + lint, both blocking) · `audit:invariants` 15/15 (read-side change, no new write-site). Files: `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-by-account.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(charts): unify Net Worth "By account" tooltip with colored dots + zero line + distinct palette (FINLYNQ-192, 2026-06-18, web).** Four cohesive polish changes to the dashboard **Net Worth Over Time** chart (and the account-detail **Balance Over Time** chart — same `NetWorthHistoryChart` component). **(1) Unified tooltip:** the stacked "By account" branch used recharts' DEFAULT `<Tooltip>` (the inconsistent `Name : value` text); it now renders a new SHARED `StackedAreaTooltip` (`src/components/chart-stack-tooltip.tsx`) built from the stack `legend` (`{key,name,color}` from `buildStackedSeries`) + the hovered payload — one row per account with a colored dot matching the band + legend chip, full names, and a date + total heading. The component is DRY-extracted from `PerformanceChart`'s former local `StackTooltip`, so Net Worth + Performance now share ONE stacked-area tooltip code path. **(2) Additive color dot:** `TooltipBreakdownList` (`src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx`) gains an OPTIONAL `color` field on `BreakdownRow` (renders a dot only when supplied, via a CSP-safe ref-callback) plus a `wide` lever (drops the `max-w-[140px]` name truncation); the dashboard sparkline + Income-vs-Expenses consumers pass neither and stay byte-identical (no dot, capped). **(3) Visible zero line:** a recharts `<ReferenceLine y={0} stroke="#888" />` is drawn always in the stacked branch (FINLYNQ-187 sign-split puts liabilities below the axis, so it always spans zero when liabilities exist) and conditionally in the aggregate branch via the now-destructured `spansZero` flag from `prepareTimeSeries` (mirrors `PerformanceChart`). **(4) More distinct colors:** `CHART_COLORS.categories` (`src/lib/chart-colors.ts`) expanded 8→12 colors, hue-ordered so adjacent bands differ and the two purples (indigo idx0, violet idx6) are far apart — a ~10-band stack no longer blends to mostly-purple; Net Worth stacked `fillOpacity` raised 0.55→0.7. Colors flow from the single source through bands, legend (`StackedChartLegend`), and tooltip dots. **FINLYNQ-187 sign-split semantics preserved** (band values stay signed, positives above / liabilities below, reconciled net == aggregate line); the Income/Expenses + Performance literal-stackId stacks are unaffected by the palette/opacity changes. `npm run build` (tsc + lint, both blocking) passed. Files: `src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx`, `src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx`, `src/components/chart-stack-tooltip.tsx` (new), `src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx`, `src/lib/chart-colors.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(reports): Reports Weekly period now renders data — replaced SQLite STRFTIME with native Postgres ISO-week expression (FINLYNQ-191, 2026-06-18).** The Reports page Weekly period granularity returned empty charts and an empty breakdown table. Root cause: `periodExpr()` in `src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts` (weekly `case`) used the SQLite-only `STRFTIME('%j', date)` function, which does not exist in PostgreSQL; the query threw, `/api/reports/trends?period=weekly` resolved null, and the page rendered no data. The daily/monthly/quarterly cases used plain `SUBSTR(...)` (Postgres-accepted) and worked. This route runs Drizzle `sql` against the main pg pool — NOT the MCP pg-compat shim — so nothing rewrote the expression. **Fix:** replaced the SQLite expression with the native Postgres ISO-week equivalent `to_char(${schema.transactions.date}::date, 'IYYY"-W"IW')` (transactions.date is TEXT 'YYYY-MM-DD', cast to date). The resulting `YYYY-Www` key format (e.g. `2026-W24`) matches what `formatPeriodLabel`'s weekly passthrough at line 33 already expects — no client-side changes needed. Validated against the live dev DB: `SELECT DISTINCT to_char(date::date, 'IYYY"-W"IW') AS wk FROM transactions ORDER BY wk LIMIT 5` returned `2022-W52 … 2023-W04` with exit 0. No other period cases touched. `tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `rg -F STRFTIME src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts` → 0 matches. Files: `src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **chore(mcp): Anthropic directory-submission hardening — bookkeeping framing + annotation accuracy + tool-count reconciliation (2026-06-18).** Pre-emptive fixes ahead of the Connectors Directory review (submission queued 2026-06-17), from a multi-agent self-audit against Anthropic's [review criteria](https://claude.com/docs/connectors/building/review-criteria) + [directory policy](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/13145358-anthropic-software-directory-policy). No blockers found; the two decisive gates (comprehensive privacy policy + auto-injected annotations) already pass. **(1) "Bookkeeping only" framing on money-verb tools** — the directory PROHIBITS software that "transfers money/crypto or executes financial transactions on behalf of users." Finlynq is a tracking app (no payment rails, no brokerage/bank connection — it only writes to the user's own ledger DB), but `portfolio_buy/sell/swap/transfer/deposit/withdrawal/income_expense/fx_conversion` + `record_transfer`/`record_transaction` read like real execution on a reviewer skim. Each now leads with a one-clause ledger disclaimer ("Bookkeeping only: records an entry in the user's own Finlynq ledger; never connects to a bank/brokerage or moves real money/crypto.") on BOTH transports (`register-tools-pg.ts` HTTP + `register-core-tools.ts` stdio; the 8 `portfolio_*` are HTTP-only), plus the server `description` (route.ts + index.ts) and `finlynq_help` (renamed the misleading `trade_tools` help-bucket → `transfer_tools`, reworded the "Buy/Sell shares" cribs, added a top-level ledger line + a `https://finlynq.com/mcp-guide` docs link). **(2) Annotation accuracy** — `replace_splits` (hard-DELETEs splits) and `cancel_import` (`fs.unlink`s the upload) were name-inferred as non-destructive; both now pass an explicit `{ destructiveHint: true }` annotation object (the `withAutoAnnotations` patch honors a caller-supplied annotations arg). And `inferAnnotations` ([mcp-server/auto-annotations.ts](mcp-server/auto-annotations.ts)) now gates `destructiveHint` behind `!isReadOnly` so `preview_bulk_delete`/`preview_delete_category` (pure reads whose names embed a `_delete` token) no longer carry a contradictory read-only-AND-destructive pair; `openWorldHint` is set for the two external-price reads (`get_fx_rate`, `convert_amount`). **(3) Tool-count reconciliation** — the live HTTP server already advertised the correct 109/v3.3.0, but `/mcp-guide`, `/about`, the `/vs/*` comparison pages, `/roadmap`, the landing page, the integrations settings page, a blog post, and the glossary still said "102 HTTP / v3.2.0"; all reconciled to **109 HTTP / 93 stdio / v3.3.0** behind a new single-source-of-truth [src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts](src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts) (`MCP_TOOL_COUNTS`/`MCP_SERVER_VERSION`), and stdio `index.ts` fixed from its stale "86". **(4) Nice-to-haves** — widened two over-narrow `z.enum(["CAD","USD","all"])` currency filters (`get_account_balances`/`get_net_worth`) to any ISO code (CLAUDE.md invariant), de-indexed the deprecated `get_loans` from `finlynq_help`, and disambiguated `get_portfolio_performance_v2`'s description. Privacy page: "all 22 tables" → "every table scoped to your user_id" (drift-proof) + Last-updated bumped to 2026-06-18. **Deferred (own focused changes, flagged in the audit):** `err()`/`sqliteErr()` `isError:true` (touches every error path on all 109 tools — wants isolated testing), the `pf_`-API-key ciphertext-redaction sentinel (cross-effect with `detect_subscriptions`'s `v1:` filter), and full removal of `get_loans` + renaming `_v2` (both breaking API changes for existing integrations — did the non-breaking variants). Full report: `Research/mcp-directory-review-audit-2026-06-18.md` (private repo). `tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `generate:mcp-catalog` → 109 HTTP tools. Files: `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`, `mcp-server/auto-annotations.ts`, `mcp-server/index.ts`, `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`, `src/lib/mcp/tool-counts.ts` (new), `src/app/{mcp-guide/layout,about/page,roadmap/page,privacy/page}.tsx`, `src/app/vs/*/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/integrations/page.tsx`, `src/app/blog/how-finlynq-encrypts-your-money/page.tsx`, `src/lib/seo/glossary.ts`, `src/components/landing/landing-client.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(reports): pivot Income/Expenses breakdown tables to per-period columns + 30-column cap (FINLYNQ-185, 2026-06-17, dev).** The Income and Expenses breakdown tables on the Reports → Income Statement tab now render **one column per period** (following the Period filter — daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly) with a trailing **Total** column, rather than showing a single collapsed total for the whole range. Per-period values come from `BreakdownItem.periods[key]` (already computed by the trends API); no data-layer changes. **30-column cap:** when `timeseries.length > 30`, only the last 30 periods are shown as columns; an inline amber notice ("Showing the most recent 30 of N periods — Total reflects the full range") appears only when truncated. **Total = full-range `item.total`** (not the sum of visible columns), ensuring the Total column always agrees with the "Total Income/Expenses" footer even when truncated. **Empty cells** render `--` (muted) instead of `$0.00` to avoid visual noise across 30 columns; a category with no activity in a period is blank. **Grouped sums:** `GroupedTable` (Group By = Category, collapsible) sums child `periods[key]` values per period for the group row; expanded children sum to the group row per column. **Horizontal overflow:** both `GroupedTable` and `FlatTable` are wrapped in `overflow-x-auto` with `min-w` column constraints so the wide table scrolls inside the card. **CSV export** migrated from the inline `exportCSV` to the shared `exportCsv` helper (`src/lib/csv-export.ts`) and now emits **ALL period columns (uncapped — CSV has no width limit)** plus Total, so exported data is never truncated by the 30-column visual cap. Balance Sheet / YoY / Cash Flow tabs are untouched. `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15. Files: `src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(import): hide archived and hidden accounts from the Reconciliation summary panel (FINLYNQ-184, 2026-06-17, dev).** The `/import` Reconciliation summary was showing archived accounts and accounts the user had hidden from the dropdown (via `reconcile_hidden_accounts`), cluttering the panel with accounts the user intentionally tucked away. This reverses a deliberate FINLYNQ-147 design decision: archived/hidden accounts are now **excluded** from the summary at the API route boundary (`GET /api/reconcile/summary`). Implementation: `getAccounts(userId)` (no `{ includeArchived: true }`) keeps archived accounts out of the pipeline; a `.filter((a) => !hiddenSet.has(a.id))` step removes hidden accounts before building the response rows. The now-dead `archived`/`hidden` fields are removed from `ReconcileSummaryApiRow` in `reconcile-summary-panel.tsx` and the archived/hidden badge JSX is cleaned up. CLAUDE.md FINLYNQ-147 bullet amended: hidden accounts are reachable via `/settings/import` and deep-links, NOT the summary panel. No migration. `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15. Files: `src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts`, `src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): manual lot reassignment from the lot inspector (FINLYNQ-178, 2026-06-17, dev).** Fast-follow to FINLYNQ-176's read-only inspector: the user can now **re-point a single SELL closure onto lots of their choosing** instead of accepting the automatic FIFO allocation. **Engine reuse (no re-derivation):** a new pure SPECIFIC variant `planLotReassignment(input)` in [src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts](src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts) (sibling of `planLotReallocation`) **drives the pick from `perLotQty:[{lotId,qty}]`** — mirroring `selectLotsToClose({strategy:"SPECIFIC"})` + the `closeLotsForSellHook` `perLotQty` branch — closing exactly the named qty per named lot (clamped at the lot's open qty) and routing overflow into **one short lot** at the proceeds price (FINLYNQ-162 precedent); transfer_out realizes 0. The DB orchestrator `reassignClosureLots(userId, {holdingId, closeTxId, perLotQty}, {dryRun, dek})` in [src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts](src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts) is **STRICT / all-or-nothing**: it validates **Σ `perLotQty.qty` == the closure's total qty** (mismatch → typed `qty_mismatch`, **writes NOTHING**), enforces **same-account-only** (`lot_not_in_scope`) and **sell-only** (`not_reassignable` — the commit path stamps `closeKind='sell'`), and on commit forces lot-hook strict mode ON, **reverses ONLY this one close-tx's closures** (`reverseLotsForDeleteHook`) then re-closes via the existing `closeLotsForSellHook` `perLotQty` path. **Load-bearing guarantees:** only the reassigned closure's realized gain restates — **sibling closures stay byte-identical**; position qty stays live `SUM(quantity)` (issue #128) — only lot/closure rows move; **NEVER DELETE+INSERTs a `transactions` row**; overflow→short happens within the same atomic unit. **API:** `POST /api/portfolio/holdings/[holdingId]/lots/reassign` ([route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/holdings/%5BholdingId%5D/lots/reassign/route.ts), `requireEncryption`) — body `{closeTxId, perLotQty, preview?}`; `preview:true` (default) dry-runs `{preview}`, `preview:false` STRICT-commits `{preview, committed:true}`; typed lib errors map to 400/404 with no write. **UI:** `LotInspectorDialog` ([lot-inspector-dialog.tsx](src/components/portfolio/lot-inspector-dialog.tsx)) gains an **"Edit allocation"** link per sell closure → a lot picker pre-seeded with the closure's current allocation → **Preview** (opened shorts + restated realized gain) → **Reassign** (preview-then-commit, mirroring FINLYNQ-176); transfer/swap/fx closures show no affordance. **Same-account, web/REST-only** (MCP parity deferred). No migration (lot/closure tables already exist from FINLYNQ-176). `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `vitest run tests/portfolio-lots-reassign.test.ts` 9/9 (tc-1 STRICT qty_mismatch zero-write + dry-run restates only that closure + commit touches ONLY lot/closure tables never `transactions`; tc-2 overflow→short atomic + non-sell `not_reassignable`). Files: `src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/holdings/[holdingId]/lots/reassign/route.ts` (new), `src/components/portfolio/lot-inspector-dialog.tsx`, `tests/portfolio-lots-reassign.test.ts` (new), `docs/architecture/lot-edits.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(portfolio): Top Movers dedupe a ticker held across multiple accounts into one row (FINLYNQ-190, 2026-06-17, dev).** The Portfolio "Top Movers" cards (Top Gainers / Top Losers) listed the SAME ticker N times when it was held across N accounts (VCN.TO seen 3×, one row per account position with its own day-change). All-Holdings already groups by security; Top Movers didn't. **Root cause:** `topGainers`/`topLosers` were built from the PER-POSITION `enrichedHoldings` array in [src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts) — `movers` filtered/sorted per-position then sliced top 5, with no aggregation by ticker. **Fix (aggregate-then-slice, REUSING the All-Holdings canonical-key rollup):** the route's `byHoldingMap` bucket-key logic was extracted into a single shared `moverBucketKey(h)` closure (`useSecurityGrouping && h.securityId != null ? sec:<id> : canonicalKey(h).key`) so the All-Holdings rollup AND Top Movers group on the EXACT same key — there is provably ONE grouping path (the `byHoldingMap` value is byte-identical; `securities-parity` test still 6/6). New pure helper `aggregateMovers(holdings, bucketKeyOf, displayOf)` in [src/lib/portfolio/top-movers.ts](src/lib/portfolio/top-movers.ts) consolidates per-position rows into one `Mover` per canonical key: **sums `dayChangeDisplay`** across accounts and derives a **value-weighted aggregate `changePct`** (Σ day-change ÷ Σ prior-day value × 100, mirroring the portfolio-total `dayChangePct` — NOT one account's percent), dropping positions with no real `symbol` or no live day-change (cash/metals/custom stay excluded). Ranking (by absolute display-currency day-change, tie-break symbol asc) + the top-5 cap are applied AFTER aggregation. **UI:** [src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx) maps one row per ticker keyed on the canonical security key (`m.key`, NOT the per-position `h.id`); the muted secondary name is hidden when it equals the symbol. New `Mover` type ([_types.ts](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts)) replaces `EnrichedHolding[]` for `topGainers`/`topLosers`. **No migration** (pure derived-display logic). All-Holdings table behaviour unchanged. `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `vitest run tests/top-movers.test.ts` 5/5 (tc-2: VCN.TO in 3 accounts +21.33/+14.04/+1.89 → consolidated dayChangeDisplay = 37.26; weighted % = 2.07; top-5 cap applied after aggregation) + `securities-parity` 6/6 (rollup unchanged). Files: `src/lib/portfolio/top-movers.ts` (new), `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx`, `tests/top-movers.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(email-import): email rules can record a TRANSFER, not just a category/expense (FINLYNQ-189, 2026-06-17, dev).** Email-import rules could only book a matched email as a single categorized income/expense, so internal-money-movement alerts ("You transferred $X to Savings", credit-card payment confirmations) got mis-booked as a categorized EXPENSE (wrong sign / double-counts in flow reports). A rule now carries an optional **transfer destination** — when set, a matched email records a TRANSFER from the rule's account (the OUTFLOW/source leg) → the destination (the inflow), mutually exclusive with the category. **Schema:** nullable `transfer_dest_account_id` on `email_import_rules` ([src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts), FK → `accounts.id` `ON DELETE SET NULL` — like the nullable `category_id`, NOT the NOT-NULL source `account_id`'s CASCADE, so deleting the dest clears the destination rather than the whole rule). **Migration:** [scripts/migrations/20260623_email_rule_transfer.sql](scripts/migrations/20260623_email_rule_transfer.sql) — **ADD COLUMN, additive/non-destructive/idempotent**, auto-applied by `deploy.sh` before build+restart; no backfill (existing rows read NULL → stay in category mode). **Transfer write — REUSE the canonical web path (FINLYNQ-131 + link_id invariants):** the record path delegates to the existing `createTransferPair` ([src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts)), so both legs land on the canonical "Transfer" category via `resolveTransferCategoryId` (never an arbitrary `type='R'` row) and share ONE server-generated `link_id` (NOT `trade_link_id`); the source-leg bank-ledger lineage is stamped on the outflow. **Same-currency guard (v1):** if source & dest currencies differ the record path REFUSES with a typed `{ status:"invalid", reason:"transfer_currency_mismatch" }` → HTTP 400, and writes NO rows (mirrors the web cross-currency transfer refusal);
