FX-conversion transfers are deferred. **`mode:"auto"` allowed** for transfer rules (the sweep auto-records a transfer rule with no category). **Threading:** `ActiveEmailRule.transferDestAccountId` ([src/lib/email-rules/load.ts](src/lib/email-rules/load.ts)) → both record paths (PATCH [src/app/api/import/email-inbox/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-inbox/%5Bid%5D/route.ts) + the sweep [src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts](src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts)) → CRUD ([src/app/api/email-rules/route.ts](src/app/api/email-rules/route.ts) + [[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/email-rules/%5Bid%5D/route.ts), with dest-account ownership + non-investment + same-account guards) → editor dialog ([src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx): a "Category" vs "Transfer to" toggle that clears/hides the Category picker in transfer mode, a destination select excluding investment accounts + the source account, and a client-side same-currency check) + the rules-manager list summary ([src/components/inbox/email-rules-manager.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rules-manager.tsx)). The category/expense path is unchanged; investment accounts stay excluded from both pickers (`isInvestment !== true`). **Web-only for v1** — MCP/mobile transfer support deferred; the new field is additive so existing email-rule API consumers are unaffected. `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `vitest run tests/email-rule-transfer.test.ts tests/transfer-category-canonical.test.ts` 4/4 (tc-2 cross-currency refusal writes no rows; tc-3 record path reuses `createTransferPair` + both legs resolve to the canonical Transfer category). Files: `scripts/migrations/20260623_email_rule_transfer.sql` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/email-rules/load.ts`, `src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts`, `src/app/api/import/email-inbox/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/api/email-rules/route.ts`, `src/app/api/email-rules/[id]/route.ts`, `src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/email-rules-manager.tsx`, `tests/email-rule-transfer.test.ts` (new), `tests/transfer-category-canonical.test.ts` (new), `tests/email-rules-match.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(dashboard): Quick Import carries the picked/dropped file into /import instead of discarding it (FINLYNQ-188, 2026-06-17, dev).** The dashboard Quick Import card ("Drop a CSV or OFX file to import") did nothing useful: `onFileInput` was `() => goToImport()` and `onDrop` never read `e.dataTransfer.files`, so the picked/dropped file was thrown away and the user landed on an EMPTY `/import` page. A secondary footgun: the file input's value was never reset, so re-selecting the same filename didn't re-fire `onChange`. **Fix (carry the File across the client-side navigation):** a `File` is not JSON-serializable, so it can't ride through `sessionStorage`/URL — but the JS module instance survives a Next.js `router.push` (no full reload). New tiny module-level **single-slot, clear-on-read** handoff store [src/lib/import/file-handoff.ts](src/lib/import/file-handoff.ts) (`setHandoffFile`/`takeHandoffFile`). **(1)** [quick-import.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/quick-import.tsx) now reads `e.target.files?.[0]` / `e.dataTransfer.files?.[0]`, stashes it via `setHandoffFile`, resets the input value (`e.target.value = ""` so re-selecting the same filename re-fires), then `router.push("/import")`; a fileless click/drop still falls back to a bare navigation. **(2)** [/import page](src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx) consumes the file ONCE on mount via `takeHandoffFile()` (clear-on-read means a refresh / repeat mount won't re-fire), opens its existing `UploadDrawer`, and passes the file as a new `initialFile` prop. **(3)** [UploadDrawer](src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx) gained an optional `initialFile` prop — when set + open, a guarded effect (a `useRef` prevents re-fire on re-render) auto-runs the SAME `submitUpload` pipeline the file picker already drives, with the default params the upload card produces (tolerance 3, no template, auto date-format), so the carried file lands directly in the existing preview/staging flow (including the OFX-confirm + column-mapping 422 branches). The import preview/dedup internals (`previewImport`, staging, `name_lookup`) are untouched — this only feeds the existing entry point. No migration; web only. `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `vitest run tests/import-file-handoff.test.ts` 4/4. Files: `src/lib/import/file-handoff.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/quick-import.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, `tests/import-file-handoff.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(charts): Net Worth Over Time "By account" stacks liabilities BELOW the zero axis (FINLYNQ-187, 2026-06-17, dev).** In the stacked "By account" view, liability accounts (Mortgage, Toyota Lease, etc.) rendered as positive bands ABOVE zero, so the stack height read as assets **+** liabilities and overstated net worth. The data layer was already correct — `buildNetWorthHistory` attaches signed per-account `members[]` (a liability contributes a NEGATIVE value); the bug was purely at the render/stacking layer. **Fix:** `buildStackedSeries` ([src/lib/chart-stack.ts](src/lib/chart-stack.ts)) gained an opt-in `signSplit?: boolean` (default OFF) that classifies each band into a positive (above-axis, `stackId="pos"`) or negative (below-axis, `stackId="neg"`) Recharts stack by the SIGN of its NET contribution across the window (so a member that briefly flips sign on one point still lands by its dominant sign; the "Other" residual is classified by its summed net sign too). Band VALUES are untouched (still signed), so the reconciled net — top of the positive stack minus bottom of the negative stack — still equals the aggregate `total`/Net Worth line at every point. The Net Worth chart ([src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx](src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx)) enables `signSplit:true` and renders `<Area stackId={b.stackId ?? "nw"}>`. **No-op for the all-same-sign Income/Expenses ("By category") + Performance ("By holding/account") stacks** — they don't pass `signSplit`, the new `StackLegendEntry.stackId` stays `undefined`, and they keep their own literal stackId, so their rows + legend are byte-identical (vitest `tc-2b` asserts this). No migration.

- **refactor(currency): unify currency model onto one display currency; drop `users.base_currency` (FINLYNQ-183, 2026-06-17, dev).** The app had THREE currency concepts but only two converged: a user could set Display Currency = CAD yet Portfolio → Realized gains still showed "Show in base currency (USD)" with no way to change it. **Root cause:** `users.base_currency` was a USD-locked orphan (`text("base_currency").notNull().default("USD")`, no UI / no API / no write path) read only by `getBaseCurrency()` from the realized-gains `?currency=base` augmentation. **Owner-resolved (2026-06-16): ONE user-facing currency** — `settings.display_currency` is now the single currency the whole app reports in AND the realized-gain accounting basis; there is no separate "base" concept. **(1)** `getBaseCurrency()` **removed** from [src/lib/fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts); its single caller (the realized-gains REST route) resolves `getDisplayCurrency()` instead, and the `?baseCurrency=` override is gone. **(2)** `GET /api/portfolio/realized-gains` ([route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/realized-gains/route.ts)) — the "unified" view (toggle) now converts every closure into the **display currency** at historical FX via the unchanged `augmentWithBaseCurrency(result, userId, displayCurrency)`; the toggle request param is renamed `?currency=base` → **`?unified=1`** (legacy `currency=base` still accepted for old links). **(3)** Realized-gains page ([portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx)) — reads `useDisplayCurrency()`; the toggle is a **dynamic "Show in `<ccy>`"** label (e.g. "Show in CAD") and the unified total badge/cells are labelled with the display currency. **(4)** MCP `get_realized_gains` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) — now augments its result into the user's display currency (resolved server-side via `resolveReportingCurrency(db, userId, null)`); the tool exposes **NO** base/reporting-currency override param (none was ever present — it now reports in display ccy with a `totalRealizedGainInBase` + per-row `realizedGainInBase`). No stdio variant of this tool exists. **(5)** Settings → General switch-currency confirm dialog ([settings/general/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/general/page.tsx)) copy **extended** to warn that **realized gains / tax figures will also re-base** to the new currency (no new dialog). **(6)** `users.base_currency` **dropped** from [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). **DESTRUCTIVE — LOOSE migration path:** the `ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS base_currency` lives at [scripts/migrate-drop-base-currency.sql](scripts/migrate-drop-base-currency.sql) (NOT `scripts/migrations/`, so deploy.sh does NOT auto-run it) and is applied MANUALLY by the operator AFTER the code release, code-first-then-SQL per [plan/migrations.md](plan/migrations.md). No data migration — `realizedGainInBase` is computed live per request from FX snapshots, never persisted, so the column carried no value. The schema is safe to deploy while the column still physically exists (Drizzle selects explicit columns; its NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD' covers any insert in the gap). **⚠️ Untouched:** the IBKR flexquery parser's unrelated broker `base_currency` ([external-import/parsers/ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts), [packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/*](packages/import-connectors/src/ibkr/)) is a broker-reported field, NOT the user field. **Grep gates:** `getBaseCurrency` → 0 callsites (only a comment), `users.base_currency` → 0 code refs (only the historical CREATE migration + a comment), remaining `baseCurrency` → only the realized-gains feature's internal per-row stamp + IBKR broker files. `npm run build` exit 0 (typecheck gate green — confirms nothing references the dropped field) · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15. Files: `src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/realized-gains/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/general/page.tsx`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `scripts/migrate-drop-base-currency.sql` (new), `plan/migrations.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(portfolio): Dividends report — reporting-currency toggle, currencies-as-columns pivot, date filters (FINLYNQ-182, 2026-06-17, dev).** The Portfolio → Dividend income report ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/dividends/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/dividends/page.tsx)) is reworked along four axes. **(1) Year/Quarter pivot** — `listDividendIncome` ([src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts](src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts)) used to bucket by `(period, currency)`, so a single year with mixed USD/CAD dividends rendered as TWO rows (2026 USD + 2026 CAD). A new **opt-in `pivot` flag** (sent by the web page as `?pivot=1`) buckets by PERIOD only and attaches a per-currency `byCurrency: {amount,rowCount,reinvestedCount,withholdingCount}` map to each group row; the page renders ONE row per period with a money column per currency. **(2) "Show in reporting currency" toggle** — a new **opt-in `reportingCurrency` flag** (`?reportingCurrency=1`) collapses all three views to a single reporting-currency Total. **Load-bearing (product-owner-locked + the prod FX reporting invariant):** reporting mode SELECTs and SUMs the STORED historical `transactions.reporting_amount` grouped by `reporting_currency` — it NEVER converts the raw `amount` at render time (no `convertReportingSlice`/`convertWithRateMap` in the dividends module). The route fires `selfHealReportingAmounts(userId, displayCurrency)` fire-and-forget on load (same pattern as the dashboard/flow reports) so stale rows re-rate in the background; rows whose reporting fields are still NULL are EXCLUDED from the total and surfaced as an `unratedCount` "re-rating in progress" hint, never on-the-fly converted. Display currency via `getDisplayCurrency()` (defaults USD). The top-of-card badges collapse to a single reporting-currency badge in reporting mode. **(3) Date filters** — a tax-year selector + from/to range wired to the existing API `from`/`to`/`taxYear` params, applied across ALL three views (year/quarter/holding); tax-year and explicit range are mutually exclusive. **(4) CSV** reflects the active mode (reporting = single reporting-currency total column; native pivot = one money column per currency; legacy = unchanged). **Backward-compat (CRITICAL):** both new behaviors are OPT-IN. Mobile (`mobile/src/screens/DividendsScreen.tsx`) and the MCP `get_dividend_income` tool send neither flag, so they keep the unchanged legacy native shape (flat per-`(period,currency)` group rows with `amount`/`currency`). New `byCurrency`/`unratedCount`/`mode`/`reportingCurrency` fields are additive-optional in both `src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts` and `shared/types.ts`. **FINLYNQ-173 attribution preserved** — `COALESCE(related_holding_id, portfolio_holding_id)` + `holdingId` filter matches EITHER the security or its cash sleeve, untouched. The DB-free aggregation core was extracted to the pure exported `aggregateDividendRows(...)` for unit-testability. Unit tests: [tests/dividends-aggregation.test.ts](tests/dividends-aggregation.test.ts) (7 cases — native pivot one-row-per-period, legacy-shape preserved, reporting sums stored fields not native amounts, NULL-laggard exclusion, attribution fold + source guards that the reporting path reads stored columns and never imports a render-time FX converter). NO migration (reporting columns already exist). `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `vitest` 7/7. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/dividends/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/dividends/page.tsx`, `shared/types.ts`, `tests/dividends-aggregation.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(portfolio): Performance chart tooltip auto-sizes to the full holdings list (FINLYNQ-181, 2026-06-17, dev).** On Portfolio → Performance → "By holding (value)", hovering a point opened a tooltip whose holdings list was capped at `max-h-40` with an inner vertical scrollbar, hiding holdings below the fold on large portfolios. **Fix (option a — the minimal fix per the report):** added an opt-in `uncapped?: boolean` prop to the shared `TooltipBreakdownList` ([src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx](src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx)) — default `false` PRESERVES the capped+scrollable behavior — and passed `uncapped` ONLY from the Performance `StackTooltip` ([src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx](src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx)) so it drops `max-h-40 overflow-y-auto` and grows to show every holding. **Scope (load-bearing):** the dashboard sparkline / Net Worth / Income-Expenses tooltips keep the cap (small-viewport safety) — they render the same component WITHOUT `uncapped`, so they are unchanged. `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `next build` green (exit 0). Files: `src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `../CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(dashboard): Weekly Recap anchors on last completed week, never future days (FINLYNQ-180, 2026-06-17, dev).** On a Tuesday the Weekly Recap widget showed the *current in-progress* week (e.g. 2026-06-14 → 2026-06-20), so the "Spent" figure covered only 2–3 elapsed days and the "↓ 97% vs previous week" delta stacked a partial week against a full one. **Root cause:** `getWeekBounds()` anchored on the week *containing* today (advancing to the upcoming Saturday), not the most recently completed one. **Fix (no-arg path only):** when called without an `endDate`, shift the anchor back by `(dayOfWeek + 1)` days to land on the most recent past Saturday — works for every day of the week (Sun=−1, Tue=−3, Sat=−7). `prevWeekStart/prevWeekEnd` continue to be derived from the new `weekEnd`, so the vs-previous comparison always pits two complete weeks. The explicit-`endDate` branch (used by the MCP `get_weekly_recap` tool) is untouched and still returns the week *containing* the given date. `getWeekBounds` is now exported for direct unit-testing. Six new test cases in `tests/api/recap.test.ts` (Tue/Sun/Sat clock, `weekEnd < today` guard, explicit-date Saturday/Sunday edge-cases). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · `next build` green · 9/9 tests pass. Files: `src/lib/weekly-recap.ts`, `tests/api/recap.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(accounts): user-defined account groups — free-text combobox + management UI (FINLYNQ-179, 2026-06-17, dev).** Account groups are no longer limited to the hard-coded per-type list. The create/edit Group field is now a **free-text input with suggestion chips** (`GroupField` in [src/app/(app)/accounts/_components/group-field.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/_components/group-field.tsx)) — suggestions = the seeded defaults UNION the user's existing group names for that account type (live-derived, no migration). A new **"Manage groups"** dialog ([manage-groups-dialog.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/_components/manage-groups-dialog.tsx)) lets the user **rename** a group across all its accounts (owner-scoped bulk `UPDATE accounts SET "group"=? WHERE lower("group")=lower(?) AND user_id=?` via `PATCH /api/accounts/groups`, `apiHandler` enveloped), **merge** a group into "Other" (same PATCH with `to:"Other"`, behind the shared `ConfirmDialog`), and **reorder** how group sections appear on the accounts page. Group ORDER is persisted per account type via a new settings key/value `account_group_order` (`{A:[],L:[]}`, NO migration — mirrors `reconcile_hidden_accounts`) behind `GET/PUT /api/settings/account-group-order`. The accounts-page section ordering now honours the saved order (with "Other" always last, alphabetical for the rest) via the pure `orderGroups`. The seeded defaults + all PURE helpers live in the client-safe single source of truth [src/lib/accounts/groups.ts](src/lib/accounts/groups.ts) (`ACCOUNT_GROUP_DEFAULTS` + `parseGroupOrder`/`groupSuggestions`/`orderGroups`/`dedupeGroups`/`normalizeGroupName`, never-throws); the **server-only** DB/settings/rename functions (`getAccountGroupOrder`/`setAccountGroupOrder`/`renameAccountGroup`, which import `@/db` → `pg`) live in the sibling [src/lib/accounts/groups-server.ts](src/lib/accounts/groups-server.ts), imported ONLY by the API routes — so the `"use client"` accounts page never drags `pg`/`dns` into the browser bundle (this split was the **cycle-2 fix** for the Turbopack `Can't resolve 'dns'` build failure on the cycle-1 commit). `accounts.group` was already free-text — no schema change; downstream group consumers (reports balance-sheet, dashboard balances, net-worth) already group by the string value, so custom names flow through automatically. Groups stay scoped per type (A=Asset vs L=Liability). `npm run build` exit 0 · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 15/15 · 12 new unit tests pass. Files: `src/lib/accounts/groups.ts`, `src/lib/accounts/groups-server.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/account-group-order/route.ts`, `src/app/api/accounts/groups/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/_components/group-field.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/_components/manage-groups-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx`, `tests/account-groups.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `../CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(dashboard): exclude archived accounts from Action Center low-balance alerts (FINLYNQ-175, 2026-06-15, dev).** The Dashboard Action Center (Spotlight engine) showed `low_balance` items for archived/deactivated accounts (e.g. a paid-off vehicle asset account at $0.00), inflating the "N items need attention" count. **Root cause:** `getLowBalances` in [src/lib/spotlight.ts](src/lib/spotlight.ts) filtered on `user_id + type='A'` only — `accounts.archived` (boolean, `notNull().default(false)`, exists since the initial schema) was never checked. **Fix:** added `eq(accounts.archived, false)` to the `getLowBalances` `where(and(...))` clause. **Audit of all six other Spotlight sources** confirmed the fix is confined: `getOverspentBudgets` / `getSpendingAnomalies` / `getUncategorizedTransactions` key off transactions (no account join); `getUpcomingLargeBills` / `getUpcomingSubscriptions` key off `subscriptions.status='active'` (no account join); `getGoalDeadlines` already gates on `goals.status='active'` and queries the linked account's transaction sum — an archived account with no new transactions would not inflate a goal's funded-%, so no leak. `getLowBalances` is the sole source of archived-account leakage. No schema change. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13. Files: `src/lib/spotlight.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(portfolio): All-Holdings ticker-description (Yahoo shortName) + single-line Holding cell (FINLYNQ-174, 2026-06-15, dev).** The All-Holdings table showed only the ticker code (symbol badge) plus the user-stored `name`, which for most equity rows IS the ticker, so there was no human-readable fund/company description anywhere, and the Holding cell stacked two lines (name on line 1; symbol + asset-type badges on line 2). **Decision: MERGE the description into the Holding cell, NOT a distinct column** — the table already carries 8 columns under an `overflow-x-auto` wrapper, and a single-line cell (image + symbol badge + description + asset-type badge + inline "Short" badge) satisfies both asks (description visible + more compact) at once, so **no `colSpan` change was needed** (detail row stays `colSpan={7}`, empty state stays `colSpan={8}`). **Data-layer plumb:** the descriptive long name lives only at the quote layer — `price-service.ts` reads Yahoo `meta.shortName` into the quote `name`, but ONLY on a live fetch (the `price_cache` table has no name column, so a warm-cache hit returns `name === symbol`; adding a cache column / changing the fetch contract was out of scope). `/api/portfolio/overview` ([route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)) now attaches **`quoteName`** to each enriched holding (the Yahoo `q.name` when it's a real description, i.e. ≠ symbol; null otherwise) and rolls the first non-null member `quoteName` up to a **`description`** field on each canonical `byHolding` row. New nullable fields `EnrichedHolding.quoteName` + `ByHoldingRow.description` ([_types.ts](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts)). **Graceful fallback (load-bearing):** the new pure, null-safe resolver `holdingDescription({ quoteName, name, symbol })` ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holding-description.ts](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holding-description.ts)) prefers the quote long name, falls back to the stored name, and returns `null` (→ rendered `--`) when neither is distinct from the ticker code — so cash sleeves, metals (XAU), and custom holdings render cleanly without layout breakage. Null-defense per CLAUDE.md ("String methods on decrypted-name fields must defend against null"): every input is treated as nullable, the function NEVER throws, and the sibling [holdings-by-account.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-by-account.tsx) view (mirrored for consistency) wraps the fallback in `safeName(...)`. The expandable drop-down (chevron → per-account breakdown + First Purchase / Days Held / % of Portfolio / Cost Basis / Dividends / Total Return grid) is UNCHANGED. CSV export ([csv.ts](src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/csv.ts) `exportByHoldingToCSV`) gained a "Description" column (17 cols; TOTAL row realigned). Unit test: [tests/portfolio/holding-description.test.ts](tests/portfolio/holding-description.test.ts) (9 cases — quote-wins, name-fallback, symbol-echo → null, cash/metal → null, all-null no-throw). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green · `vitest` 9/9. Files: `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holding-description.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_types.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-table.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-by-account.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/csv.ts`, `tests/portfolio/holding-description.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(transactions): first-class single-transaction id deep-link via buildTxDrillUrl (FINLYNQ-177, 2026-06-15, dev).** `/transactions` had no first-class filter for a single transaction id, so the currency-audit "Edit transaction" affordance ([src/app/(app)/transactions/audit/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/audit/page.tsx)) linked to `/transactions?id=<n>` — which the GET route IGNORED for LIST filtering (`id` was DELETE-only) — and silently rendered the full unfiltered list. Five portfolio-form dependent-row links + the delete-dialog dependent-row links used a different fuzzy `?search=%23<id>` text hack that matched the literal `#<id>` over decrypted payee/note (wrong rows or none). **Param chosen: `id`** — natural, collides with nothing on GET (DELETE uses `id` in a separate HTTP method and never routes through the query/drill helpers), and the existing audit link's shape is preserved so it works once migrated to the helper. **(1)** `GET /api/transactions` ([route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts)) parses `id` and applies it as an **owner-scoped** `WHERE transactions.id = ?` pushdown (combined with the always-present `eq(transactions.userId, userId)` in `buildTxFilterConditions`), so `?id=<another user's id>` and `?id=<nonexistent>` both return the empty state — never the full list; a present-but-non-positive/non-numeric `id` short-circuits to `{data:[],total:0}` rather than dropping the filter. **(2)** `TxSortFilter` ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)) + `TxFilters` ([build-query.ts](src/lib/transactions/build-query.ts)) gained the key (emitted first as `?id=<n>`). **(3)** `buildTxDrillUrl` ([drill-url.ts](src/lib/transactions/drill-url.ts), the FINLYNQ-130 single source of truth) added `id` to `ALLOWED_KEYS`. **(4)** The transactions page ([page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx)) reads `id` into its URL-driven `filters` state via the existing `useEffect([urlParams])` re-sync (a drill while mounted REPLACES, not merges), renders a clearable "Transaction: #N" chip, and includes it in the Clear-all gate. **(5) Migrated all 7 hand-rolled deep-links** to `buildTxDrillUrl({ id: String(id) })`: the audit "Edit transaction" link, the 5 portfolio forms (`Buy`/`Sell`/`Transfer`/`IncomeExpense`/`FxConversion`), and the delete-dialog dependent-row links on the transactions page. `git grep` shows zero remaining hand-rolled `/transactions?id=` / `?search=%23` page deep-link strings (the two `/api/transactions?id=` DELETE fetches are a different surface, untouched). Unit tests: new `id`-key cases in [tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts](tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts) + new [tests/lib/build-query-id.test.ts](tests/lib/build-query-id.test.ts) (pure, no DB) — 11/11 green. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/app/api/transactions/route.ts`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/lib/transactions/build-query.ts`, `src/lib/transactions/drill-url.ts`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/_hooks/use-transactions.ts`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/audit/page.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/{Buy,Sell,Transfer,IncomeExpense,FxConversion}Form.tsx`, `tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts`, `tests/lib/build-query-id.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(portfolio): lot edits warn-and-reallocate replaces hard 409 block (FINLYNQ-176, 2026-06-15, dev).** Previously editing or deleting a buy/transfer-in whose lot was already consumed by a later sell returned a dead-end HTTP 409 `portfolio_edit_blocked`. **Policy change (6 phases):** the 409 is preserved as the DEFAULT and now acts as an affordance — the client receives `blockingClosureTxIds` it can use to fetch a dry-run preview (`POST /api/transactions/lot-replan-preview`, `requireEncryption`) and then confirm the reallocation with a flag (`PUT` body `confirmReallocation: true` / `DELETE ?confirmReallocation=1`). The flag triggers `replanLotsAfterMutation` (Phase 2 DB orchestrator in [src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts](src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts)) which is STRICT/all-or-nothing: reverses dependent closures, reverses/re-applies the target row, then re-closes each dependent in chronological order via the existing FIFO close hook — **overflow auto-opens a short lot** (FINLYNQ-162 precedent). The pure re-plan core `planLotReallocation` ([src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts](src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts)) has no DB I/O and surfaces a `LotReallocationPreview` (proposed closures, opened shorts, restated realized-gain delta by calendar year). **UI (Phase 4):** `TransactionDialog` intercepts the PUT 409, fetches the preview, and offers "Reallocate & save" via `LotReallocationNotice` ([src/components/portfolio/lot-reallocation-notice.tsx](src/components/portfolio/lot-reallocation-notice.tsx)); the delete dialog adds a matching "Reallocate & delete" path. **Read-only lot inspector (Phase 5):** `GET /api/portfolio/holdings/[holdingId]/lots` (`requireAuth`, no DEK) returns every lot (qty, cost basis, side, status) plus the closures that consume each; `LotInspectorDialog` ([src/components/portfolio/lot-inspector-dialog.tsx](src/components/portfolio/lot-inspector-dialog.tsx)) opens from a "Lots" affordance on each per-account holding row in the All-Holdings table. Fast-follow FINLYNQ-178: manual lot reassignment + MCP edit parity. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/lots/replan.ts` (new), `src/lib/portfolio/lots/types.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts`, `src/app/api/transactions/lot-replan-preview/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/transactions/route.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/holdings/[holdingId]/lots/route.ts` (new), `src/components/portfolio/lot-reallocation-notice.tsx` (new), `src/components/portfolio/lot-inspector-dialog.tsx` (new), `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/holdings-table.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx`, `src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx`, `tests/portfolio-lots-replan.test.ts` (new), `tests/portfolio-lots-replan-orchestrator.test.ts` (new), `tests/api/portfolio-holding-lots-inspector.test.ts` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(portfolio): attribute per-holding dividends to the paying security, zero cash-sleeve dividends (FINLYNQ-173, 2026-06-15, dev).** On the per-holding / holding-detail view the **Cash CAD** sleeve showed a Dividends figure (e.g. C$1,052.83) and folded it into Total Return — cash pays no dividends. **Investigation (security-tie case):** a dividend cash inflow is recorded ON the brokerage cash sleeve (`transactions.portfolio_holding_id = USD_Cash`) but ALREADY carries `related_holding_id = the paying security` (stamped by `recordPortfolioIncomeOrExpense`, the `portfolio_income_expense` MCP tool, and the backfill pipeline — see the schema-pg.ts `related_holding_id` doc: "so reports can group dividends by source holding"). The bug was purely in the read/aggregation layer: every per-holding aggregator keyed dividends on `portfolio_holding_id`, so each ticker's dividend piled onto the Cash sleeve's card. **Fix (read layer only — no write-path / data change; classification stays category-id based):** dividends are now attributed to `COALESCE(related_holding_id, portfolio_holding_id)` so the SECURITY is credited and `is_cash=true` sleeves read **Dividends = 0** (and Total Return no longer folds them in); the fallback to `portfolio_holding_id` covers legacy rows + genuine cash interest with no security tie. The grand total is preserved (amount MOVED, never duplicated/dropped). **Single source of truth:** new pure `dividendAttributionHoldingId` + `dividendAttributionHoldingIdSql` in [src/lib/portfolio/aggregation-predicates.ts](src/lib/portfolio/aggregation-predicates.ts). **All four aggregators wired:** (1) REST `/api/portfolio/overview` ([route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)) — dividends pulled out of the cash-sleeve-keyed `fkAggRows` tally into a separate pass grouped on the attribution holding, FX-converted from the PAID currency into each target holding's quote currency, merged into `metricsByHoldingId` (the primary buggy surface — `EnrichedHolding.dividendsReceived` + `ByHoldingRow.dividendsDisplay`); (2) MCP `aggregateHoldings` ([register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts)) — `accumulate()` gained a `divTarget` (the related security + its name/currency, decrypted at the boundary) so the dividend branch credits the security's row, leaving the cash sleeve at 0 (also fixes `get_portfolio_performance` + `get_portfolio_analysis`, which consume it); (3) lots `loadMetricsForUser` ([lots/read.ts](src/lib/portfolio/lots/read.ts)) — dividend query keys on `COALESCE(related,portfolio)`, tagging the cell with the PAID currency (not the security's) so the metrics-layer FX hop stays correct; (4) `listDividendIncome` `groupBy:"holding"` ([dividends.ts](src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts)) — re-keyed + label/name pulled from the related holding, and the `holdingId` filter now matches EITHER the security or the cash sleeve so deep-links keep working. New regression cases (tc-7/8/9) in [tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts](tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts): cash-sleeve Dividends == 0 + security credited, grand total preserved, and no-related-holding rows stay put (Postgres-test-DB-gated; not runnable on this workstation). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors (touched files) · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/aggregation-predicates.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/lots/read.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/dividends.ts`, `tests/helpers/portfolio-fixtures.ts`, `tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(portfolio): Performance chart "By account" stacked view + compact, stack-ordered tooltip (FINLYNQ-172, 2026-06-15, dev).** Builds directly on FINLYNQ-129's stacked-member toggle, which shipped only "By holding (value)". With 70 holdings across 10 accounts the by-holding stack's tooltip listed every band, ran very tall off small viewports, and (in both the existing by-holding view AND the new by-account view) ordered its rows INVERTED relative to the visual stack. Three asks, all web-only and component-state-only (resets on reload, consistent with FINLYNQ-129). **(1) "By account" grouping** — the `GET /api/portfolio/performance/holdings` route ([src/app/api/portfolio/performance/holdings/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/performance/holdings/route.ts)) gained a **`groupBy=holding|account` param** (chosen over a near-duplicate `/accounts` route — both granularities share the same grid/FX/sampling scaffolding and the SAME per-holding pricing core, so only the member group-key + name resolution differ). For `groupBy=account` the per-day loop accumulates each holding's display-ccy market value by `accountId` instead of `holdingId`, so account bands sum to the IDENTICAL grand total as holding bands (tc-1 — Σ(bands incl. "Other") == aggregate line). Account display names are DEK-resolved at the API boundary via `decryptNamedRows` + `safeAccountName` (alias preferred); the pure pricing core stays name-free. Response now also echoes `groupBy`. Per-account value stays MARKET VALUE $ (never per-account TWRR), preserving the FINLYNQ-129 invariant. **(2) Compact tooltip** — `PerformanceChart` ([src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx](src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx)) replaces the stacked AreaChart's default Recharts `<Tooltip>` with a custom `StackTooltip` that renders the shared `TooltipBreakdownList` (FINLYNQ-128 — already `max-h-40 overflow-y-auto text-xs`, height-capped + scrollable so it never overflows a phone-width viewport, tc-2). **(3) Stack-ordered rows** — the stack draws `legend[0]` (largest, ranked desc by avg \|contribution\| in `buildStackedSeries`) at the BOTTOM, so `StackTooltip` reverses the legend to anchor the largest row at the bottom of the tooltip too; applies to BOTH by-holding and by-account modes (tc-3 — by-holding had the inverted order before this). **Component changes:** the boolean `stacked` state became a `GroupMode = "off" | "holding" | "account"`; two header toggles ("By holding (value)" + "By account"), the latter HIDDEN when the chart is scoped to one account (`accountId` prop set — it'd be a single band, tc-4) with a guard effect that resets a stale "account" mode if the scope changes; the lazy fetch passes `groupBy` and re-fetches/re-ranks on mode/period/account change; axis-unit label flips to "Market value by account (CCY)". `buildStackedSeries` + `StackedChartLegend` + `TooltipBreakdownList` reused UNCHANGED (all member-agnostic). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors (touched files) · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (route + 207 static pages). Files: `src/app/api/portfolio/performance/holdings/route.ts`, `src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(admin): OAuth-grants panel (active/dormant + last-used) + `oauth_access_tokens.last_used_at` (FINLYNQ-167, 2026-06-15, dev).** Source: audit #284 (H4 extension — operator-side OAuth-grants visibility), split out of FINLYNQ-154 during triage. The admin panel's headline columns are last-used + active/dormant, but `oauth_access_tokens` had no `last_used_at` column. **(1) Migration** `scripts/migrations/20260621_oauth_last_used.sql` — **additive, NON-destructive** `ALTER TABLE oauth_access_tokens ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ` (auto-applied by deploy.sh) + matching Drizzle column (`timestamp("last_used_at", { withTimezone: true })`) in [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). **(2) Throttled bump** — `validateOauthToken` ([src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts)) now also fires a fire-and-forget `bumpTokenLastUsed(tokenHash)` (private to oauth.ts), a **DB-side conditional UPDATE** `SET last_used_at = NOW() WHERE token = $1 AND revoked_at IS NULL AND (last_used_at IS NULL OR last_used_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes')` — the throttle lives in the WHERE clause (no read-then-write race, no write-per-request storm), keyed on the access-token hash so per-grant last-used reflects active-token use, try/catch-swallowed so it NEVER blocks or throws into token validation. Mirrors FINLYNQ-166's `bumpLastActive` pattern; new `LAST_USED_THROTTLE_MINUTES` (=15). Deliberately does NOT share a helper with `bumpLastActive` — that one keys on `userId`/`users`, this one on token-hash/`oauth_access_tokens`. **(3) Admin panel** — the `/admin` Activity tab ([src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx)) gains an **OAuth grants** card (alongside "Recent logins") listing every live grant across users: client name + client id, user label, scope, granted date, last-used date, and an **active/dormant badge** (dormant = `last_used_at` NULL or > 60d, reusing the pure `isDormant`/`DORMANT_DAYS` from [src/lib/auth/dormancy.ts](src/lib/auth/dormancy.ts) — same module FINLYNQ-166 introduced, no new dormancy math), plus a per-grant **Revoke** button. The grants fetch is non-fatal (a failed grants fetch leaves the panel empty rather than blanking the page). **(4) Helpers + route** — new in [src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts): `listAllGrants()` (cross-user; joins `oauth_clients` for the name + `users` for `displayName ?? username ?? email`; live = not revoked AND refresh not expired, mirroring `listConnectedApps`; ordered `last_used_at DESC NULLS LAST, created_at DESC`) and `revokeGrantByIdAdmin(id)` (the operator analogue of `revokeGrantById` — **NO `user_id` predicate** since an admin acts across users; flips `revoked_at`, killing access + refresh per the FINLYNQ-154 grant model). Surfaced via `GET/DELETE /api/admin/oauth-grants` ([src/app/api/admin/oauth-grants/route.ts](src/app/api/admin/oauth-grants/route.ts)) — **hand-rolls `requireAdmin` + the managed-mode `getDialect()!=='postgres'` 403 guard** (NOT apiHandler, per the other /api/admin/* routes); revocation is immediate because `validateOauthToken` already filters `revoked_at IS NULL`. Unit test: [tests/oauth-last-used.test.ts](tests/oauth-last-used.test.ts) (throttle UPDATE SQL shape via a mocked validate + the no-bump-on-unknown-token contract). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (`/api/admin/oauth-grants` registered) · `vitest` 20/20 (new suite + FINLYNQ-166's last-active/dormancy). Files: `scripts/migrations/20260621_oauth_last_used.sql` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/oauth.ts`, `src/app/api/admin/oauth-grants/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx`, `tests/oauth-last-used.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(dashboard): hover tooltips on stat-tile sparklines + Net Worth 12-month view (2026-06-15, dev).** The four dashboard stat-tile sparklines (Net Worth, Monthly Income, Monthly Expenses, Budgets) and the hero net-worth sparkline now show a **value + date tooltip on hover**, and the **Net Worth** sparkline now spans the last **12 months** (was 6). The shared `Sparkline` ([src/components/sparkline.tsx](src/components/sparkline.tsx)) gained two optional, backward-compatible props: `labels` (`"YYYY-MM"`, parallel to `data`) and `currency`. When `labels` is present it renders a Recharts `Tooltip` (date via `getMonthLabel`, value via `formatCurrency`) plus an `activeDot`; with no `labels` it is byte-identical to the previous non-interactive sparkline (the only two call sites are on the dashboard). **Clipping fix:** the chart is 30px tall and sits at the bottom of an `overflow-hidden` card, so the tooltip is steered ABOVE the cursor (`allowEscapeViewBox={{x:false,y:true}}` + `reverseDirection={{x:false,y:true}}`, `offset:8`) and kept inside the viewbox on X — verified un-clipped on all four edges in an isolated harness and live on dev (Net Worth `Jan 2026 · $35,306.85`, leftmost `Dec 2025`; Monthly Income `Mar 2026 · $4,700.37`). **The recharts `portal` prop is intentionally NOT used** — recharts 3.8 drops the positioning transform when a custom portal is supplied (`TooltipBoundingBox.js`: `hasPortalFromProps ? {} : …`), which renders the tooltip unplaced; a first attempt with `portal={document.body}` was caught via source review before pushing. `StatCard` ([src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/stat-card.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/stat-card.tsx)) forwards a new `sparkLabels` prop; [page.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx) builds `nwSparkLabels` (Net Worth, `slice(-12)` for hero + tile shared series) and `incExpSparkLabels` (shared by the income/expense/budget tiles, still 6-month). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors (touched files) · dev deploy green. Files: `src/components/sparkline.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/stat-card.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **fix(landing): correct the static `nginx/landing/self-hosted.html` self-host instructions (FINLYNQ-170, 2026-06-15, dev).** Closes the known gap FINLYNQ-141 explicitly left behind. The static landing page served by the dual-hosting nginx bundle at `/self-hosted` (`nginx.conf` maps `location = /self-hosted` → `/usr/share/nginx/landing/self-hosted.html`; distinct from the Next.js `src/app/self-hosted/page.tsx` route served on the live Caddy edge) contradicted the real self-host path end-to-end. **(1)** Wrong Docker image — it hardcoded `ghcr.io/nextsoftwareconsulting/pf:latest` (the last real `nextsoftwareconsulting` reference in tracked pf-app files; FINLYNQ-141 fixed `src/lib/oauth.ts` but left this asset). The bare `docker run` is removed entirely in favour of the compose flow, so the image now lives only inside the curl'd `docker-compose.yml` (`ghcr.io/finlynq/finlynq:latest`) and is no longer duplicated/stale in the HTML. **(2)** Removed-SQLite setup — the "What You Get" bullets claimed a "Local SQLite database", a `pf-data` Docker volume, and "fully offline capable"; SQLite was removed and Finlynq is PostgreSQL-only, so they now read `postgres_data` volume + "app plus PostgreSQL, nothing external" + envelope encryption + MCP + auto-migrations, mirroring the Next.js `/self-hosted` page. **(3)** The crash-looping bare `docker run` (no DB, no secrets) is replaced with the canonical README/compose flow: `curl -O …/docker-compose.yml` → write a sibling `.env` with the three required secrets (`PF_JWT_SECRET`, `PF_PEPPER` ≥32, `PF_STAGING_KEY` ≥32) each via `openssl rand -hex 32` → `docker compose up -d`, plus the `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` change-before-exposing note. Environment-variable list corrected (`PF_DATA_DIR` → `DATABASE_URL` + the three PF_* secrets + `PORT`). Re-branded PF → Finlynq (title, subtitle, footer). Static-asset-only change — no code, no build impact; `git grep -ni nextsoftwareconsulting` is now zero outside the CHANGELOG prose. Files: `nginx/landing/self-hosted.html`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(admin): per-user last_active_at tracking + sortable "Last active" column (FINLYNQ-166, 2026-06-14, admin/metrics).** Source: audit #284 dormancy follow-on. The only activity signal was `users.last_login_at` (web password logins only — misses OAuth/MCP + `pf_` API-key access), shown solely in the "Recent logins" card, so an MCP-heavy user looked falsely dormant. Adds a per-user **`users.last_active_at`** (nullable TIMESTAMPTZ) advanced on ANY authenticated access. **Additive, non-destructive migration** `scripts/migrations/20260620_user_last_active.sql` (`ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_active_at TIMESTAMPTZ`, auto-applied by deploy.sh) + matching Drizzle column (`timestamp("last_active_at", { withTimezone: true })`) in [src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts). **Throttled bump** single-sourced in `bumpLastActive(userId)` ([src/lib/auth/last-active.ts](src/lib/auth/last-active.ts)) — a **DB-side conditional UPDATE** `SET last_active_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1 AND (last_active_at IS NULL OR last_active_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes')`, so the throttle is in the WHERE clause (no read-then-write race, no write-per-request storm; a second authed request inside the 15-min window matches zero rows). Fire-and-forget + try/catch-swallowed so it NEVER blocks or throws into the auth path; it is a users-table metadata write, NOT a transactions write, so it does NOT call `invalidateUser`. Wired at TWO call sites covering all three auth modes: `requireAuth` ([src/lib/auth/require-auth.ts](src/lib/auth/require-auth.ts)) after a successful auth (covers BOTH the web-session AND `pf_` API-key strategies — both funnel through one dispatcher), and `validateOauthToken` ([src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts)) on every successful OAuth/MCP token validation (the path `last_login_at` misses entirely). Helper kept generic so FINLYNQ-167 (`oauth_access_tokens.last_used_at`) can mirror it. **Admin surface:** `/api/admin/users` returns `lastActiveAt` (added to `listUsers` select in [src/lib/auth/queries.ts](src/lib/auth/queries.ts); the route spreads it through), and the `/admin` Users table ([src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx)) gains a **sortable** "Last active" column between Txns and Joined, reusing FINLYNQ-149's existing sort mechanism. **Dormant indicator:** a row with `lastActiveAt` null OR older than `DORMANT_DAYS` (=60) renders muted (`text-muted-foreground`); null displays "Never" and sorts as least-recently-active. The dormancy/display/sort math is single-sourced in the pure, dependency-free [src/lib/auth/dormancy.ts](src/lib/auth/dormancy.ts) (`DORMANT_DAYS`, `isDormant`, `lastActiveAtMs`, `compareLastActive`) so it's safe in the client component and unit-testable. Unit tests: [tests/auth/last-active.test.ts](tests/auth/last-active.test.ts) (throttle SQL shape + no-op/no-throw contract) + [tests/auth/dormancy.test.ts](tests/auth/dormancy.test.ts) (null-sorts-dormant + threshold). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green · `vitest` 13/13. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260620_user_last_active.sql` (new), `src/lib/auth/last-active.ts` (new), `src/lib/auth/dormancy.ts` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/auth/require-auth.ts`, `src/lib/oauth.ts`, `src/lib/auth/queries.ts`, `src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx`, `tests/auth/last-active.test.ts` (new), `tests/auth/dormancy.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(oauth): log token issuance with unspecified scope (FINLYNQ-163, 2026-06-14, security/observability).** Source: audit #284 (H3). Behaviour is UNCHANGED — per the 2026-06-13 triage decision (option a), an omitted/empty/whitespace `scope` parameter still defaults to full access (`DEFAULT_SCOPE` = `mcp:read mcp:write`) because Claude/Cursor omit scope and rely on it; changing that (least-privilege options b/c) would break already-connected clients and is out of scope. This adds **observability only** so a future tightening is data-driven. New pure helper `scopeWasUnspecified(input)` in [src/lib/oauth-scopes.ts](src/lib/oauth-scopes.ts) is the single source of truth for "was the scope defaulted because the client omitted it?" — it mirrors the exact `input == null || input.trim().length === 0` branch in `normalizeRequestedScope` (which it now also calls, keeping them in lockstep). The log line is emitted in `POST /api/oauth/authorize` ([src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts)) — the auth grant is where the client's **raw** requested scope first arrives, so it's the only boundary where "unspecified" is observable (both `createAuthCode` and `createAccessToken` receive an already-defaulted scope from their callers, so logging there would never see the empty case). When `scopeWasUnspecified(rawScope)` is true it `console.warn`s `"[oauth] token issued with unspecified scope; defaulted to DEFAULT_SCOPE"` with `{ clientId, scope: DEFAULT_SCOPE }` (matching the existing `getIssuer` `console.warn` idiom; no new logging dependency). Fires once per grant; scope resolution, `normalizeRequestedScope`'s empty→DEFAULT_SCOPE mapping, and the refresh/validate paths are untouched. Unit test: new `scopeWasUnspecified` describe block in [tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts](tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts) (asserts it is true for exactly the inputs `normalizeRequestedScope` defaults, false for real/unknown scopes). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green · `vitest run tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts` 78/78. Files: `src/lib/oauth-scopes.ts`, `src/app/api/oauth/authorize/route.ts`, `tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(portfolio): confirm before an oversell opens a short position (FINLYNQ-162, 2026-06-14, ux).** Source: audit #284 (labeled "Critical oversell") — **NOT a vulnerability**: short positions are a documented, supported feature (`holding_lots.side`), and the ledger holds no real assets. Optional UX guard only. The web Sell form (`src/components/portfolio/forms/SellForm.tsx`, reached via `/portfolio/new?op=sell`) now intercepts submit when the entered sell quantity exceeds the current long position and surfaces the shared `ConfirmDialog` ("This will open a short position of N units — you hold M, selling Q. Continue?"). Confirming proceeds with the unchanged POST; cancelling stays on the form. **Warn-but-allow** — the sell is never blocked, a short lot still opens, and `operations.ts`/the lot engine/aggregators are untouched (no business-logic change). The long position comes from data the form already has (`selectedHolding.currentShares`, the same figure already shown as "Currently holding N shares"); `useAccountHoldingSelection` already excludes cash sleeves, so the guard never fires on a cash row. Edit mode and within-position sells are unchanged (no dialog). The predicate/short-units math is single-sourced in the pure `src/lib/portfolio/oversell.ts` (`isOversell` / `shortAmount`, both NaN/flat/already-short safe). MCP `portfolio_sell` note **deferred**: the tool's holding resolver returns only an id, so the pre-sell live position would need a new aggregator query (issue #128 paired cash-leg skip rules) — out of proportion for an advisory note; the web warning is the required deliverable. Unit test: `tests/portfolio/oversell.test.ts` (11 cases). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green.
- **feat(oauth): auto-expire inactive DCR clients (60d) + expiry disclaimer (FINLYNQ-160, 2026-06-14, security).** Source: audit #284 (M7). Open Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591) lets anyone register an `oauth_clients` row (rate-limited 10/hr/IP), so the table accumulates rows from one-shot connection attempts, abandoned setups, and leftover test clients. Adds a daily cron sweep `expireInactiveDcrClients()` in `src/lib/cron/expire-dcr-clients.ts` (registered in `instrumentation.ts` alongside the other PostgresAdapter-gated crons) that deletes a client once it has had no token activity for `DCR_INACTIVE_DAYS` (=60, a single named constant; flipping to 30 is one line) AND has no live (non-revoked, unexpired) token. "Activity" = the most recent `oauth_access_tokens.created_at` for the client, falling back to the client's own `created_at` so a freshly-registered-but-unused client gets the full window. "Live token" mirrors `listConnectedApps` (`revoked_at IS NULL AND refresh_expires_at > now`). Runs as one transaction: delete the reapable clients' stale authorization codes + dead token rows, then the client rows (there is NO FK from those tables to `oauth_clients` — `client_id` is plain text — so no cascade is blocked; we clean the dependents anyway to avoid dangling rows). DEK-free hard delete of plaintext metadata. The leftover `evil.*` audit test clients get reaped once their tokens lapse (supersedes archived FINLYNQ-164). The pure decision `isClientReapable(...)` is the testable single source of truth that mirrors the sweep SQL. Disclaimer text added in two spots: the `/oauth/authorize` consent footer and the Settings → Integrations "Connected apps" CardDescription, both stating "Connections you don't use are automatically removed after 60 days; you can also revoke them anytime." (FINLYNQ-155 banner + FINLYNQ-154 revoke logic untouched). The optional RFC 7592 `DELETE /api/oauth/client/{id}` is **deferred**: DCR registration issues no `registration_access_token`, so a spec-correct authenticated self-deregister isn't cleanly possible — the sweep + the existing revoke endpoint already cover the security need. Unit test: `tests/expire-dcr-clients.test.ts` (selection logic + SQL shape). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green.
- **feat(import): reject non-finite / out-of-range numeric amounts at preview (FINLYNQ-159, 2026-06-14, security).** Source: audit #284. Previously a malformed import file with an absurd value like `1e29` previewed cleanly (HTTP 200) and could land that figure in the ledger. Adds the pure bound `isReasonableAmount(n)` + `MAX_REASONABLE_AMOUNT` (=`1e12`) to `src/lib/utils/number.ts` (finite AND `|n| <= 1e12`; rejects NaN/±Infinity too). The CSV/PDF chokepoint `previewImport` now rejects out-of-range `amount`/`enteredAmount`/`quantity` per-row as a flagged preview error; the OFX/QFX/IBKR-FlexQuery preview branches — which return rows directly without `previewImport` — run the new exported pure scanner `findUnreasonableAmountError(rows)` and return HTTP 400 on a hit, naming the offending row + field. The zero-dep `@finlynq/import-connectors` package gets its own mirror of the bound (kept in sync) and its WealthPosition transform's amount guard now rejects out-of-range as well as non-numeric. Additive numeric validation only — no change to dedup (`name_lookup` HMAC), `import_hash`, or staging encryption. Unit tests: `tests/import-amount-bound.test.ts` + new cases in the WP transform suite.
- **docs(security): SPF record + DMARC p=reject rollout plan (FINLYNQ-158, 2026-06-14, ops).** DNS/ops item — no code changed. Adds `pf-app/docs/email-auth-spf-dmarc.md` documenting: (1) the current state (`finlynq.com` has no SPF record; DMARC is at `p=quarantine`; MX = AWS SES us-east-1); (2) the outbound sender inventory — two confirmed outbound paths: transactional mail via nodemailer/`SMTP_HOST` (password reset, email verification, welcome, budget alerts, feedback — `src/lib/email.ts`) and bounce notifications via the Resend API (`src/lib/email-import/bounce.ts`, gated on `RESEND_API_KEY`); inbound paths (DevManager self-smtp relay, Resend Inbound, SES MX) are NOT outbound senders; (3) the SPF record to publish: `v=spf1 include:amazonses.com include:_spf.resend.com ~all` on `finlynq.com` (SES-only variant omits the Resend include — operator must confirm which applies); (4) DKIM confirmation steps (SES CNAME via `dig CNAME <selector>._domainkey.finlynq.com`); (5) a staged DMARC `p=reject` plan: publish SPF → monitor RUA reports 4–8 weeks → confirm alignment → flip `_dmarc.finlynq.com` to `p=reject` with exact final record string; (6) an operator action checklist. The DNS publish itself and the eventual `p=reject` flip are operator DNS actions — item moved to `blocked_for_user_input`.

- **chore(security): drop X-Powered-By header; document Caddy Via strip (FINLYNQ-157, 2026-06-14, dev).** Sets `poweredByHeader: false` in `next.config.ts` to suppress the `X-Powered-By: Next.js` response header that disclosed the framework on every response. The `Via: 1.1 Caddy` header is added by the Caddy reverse proxy and cannot be removed from the app; the operator must add `header_down -Via` to the relevant Caddyfile site block(s) and reload Caddy (`systemctl reload caddy`) — see the FINLYNQ-157 DevManager comment for the exact snippet. CSP, HSTS, and all other security headers are unchanged. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `next.config.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(seo): publish `/.well-known/security.txt` (RFC 9116) (FINLYNQ-156, 2026-06-14, dev).** Adds the standard security contact file at `/.well-known/security.txt` so security researchers have a machine-readable, RFC-compliant disclosure path. Served via a **Next.js App Router route handler** (`src/app/.well-known/security.txt/route.ts`) rather than a static file under `public/` so the mandatory `Expires` field is computed dynamically (request-time `now + 6 months`, ISO 8601 with Z suffix) and never goes stale across deploys. Fields: `Contact: mailto:security@finlynq.com`, `Expires: <dynamic>`, `Policy: https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/blob/main/SECURITY.md` (links the checked-in `SECURITY.md` that already existed in the repo), `Canonical: <SITE_URL>/.well-known/security.txt` (from `src/lib/seo/site.ts` — self-hosters pointing `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` at their own domain get the correct Canonical automatically), `Preferred-Languages: en`. Response is `Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8` with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400`. Middleware and `isWebsite` are **unchanged** — the existing `matcher` already covers `.well-known/*` (it exempts only `_next/static`, `_next/image`, and `favicon.ico`), and `security.txt` is not a marketing/analytics content route so no `isWebsite` entry is needed. **`site.ts` not updated** — `security.txt` is not a sitemap/robots/llms content page and does not belong in `STATIC_ROUTES`. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (route registered). Files: `src/app/.well-known/security.txt/route.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(oauth): warn on unverified app / external redirect on consent screen (FINLYNQ-155, 2026-06-13, dev).** Closes the phishing-via-open-DCR residual from audit #284 (C1). The `/oauth/authorize` consent page already shows the full `redirect_uri` and refuses unknown `client_id`s. **What's new:** a red/amber warning banner rendered for the genuine phishing case — an arbitrary DCR client delivering the auth code to an external host. It shows when `knownClientName(clientId)` returns null (the client is not a well-known/verified app like Claude or Cursor) **AND** the `redirect_uri` hostname is not in the Finlynq allowlist (`finlynq.com`, `*.finlynq.com`, `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`). A well-known client legitimately redirects to its OWN non-finlynq host (claude.ai, cursor.com), so keying on `wellKnown` keeps that path banner-free; this matches the item's binding acceptance criteria ("Claude/Cursor well-known looks unchanged" = wellKnown → no banner). Safe because the redirect host is exact-match validated server-side per registered client (a well-known client can't be redirected to an attacker host) and an attacker can't make `knownClientName` truthy (hardcoded client ids). **Banner copy:** "This is not a verified Finlynq app. It will send an authorization code to `<host>`. Only continue if you started this connection." **Implementation:** two pure helpers added inline in `src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx` (consistent with the existing inline `knownClientName` pattern — the page is `"use client"` and cannot import from the server-only `oauth.ts`): `isFinlynqHost(host)` (checks the allowlist; returns false on null/empty, treating missing input as untrusted) and `redirectUriHost(uri)` (wraps `new URL(uri).hostname` in a try/catch, returns null on malformed input). `showUnverifiedBanner = !wellKnown && !isFinlynqHost(redirectHost)`. The existing full-URI display, read/write scope warning, unknown-client refusal, and all FINLYNQ-154 connected-apps plumbing are untouched. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(oauth): token revocation endpoint (RFC 7009) + Settings "Connected apps" + reset-revokes-grants (FINLYNQ-154, 2026-06-13, dev).** Closes audit #284 H4 (Medium): there was no way to cut off an issued OAuth grant short of rotating `DEPLOY_GENERATION` (nukes everyone). **Grant model:** one `oauth_access_tokens` row IS one grant — it holds both the access token (`token`) and its refresh token (`refresh_token`), so "kill the whole grant" = flip that single row's `revoked_at`; `validateOauthToken` already filters `revoked_at IS NULL` (no validator change), so revocation takes effect on the next call. Migration-free (`revoked_at` already exists). **(1) `POST /api/oauth/revoke` (RFC 7009)** ([src/app/api/oauth/revoke/route.ts](src/app/api/oauth/revoke/route.ts)) — accepts `token` (+ ignored `token_type_hint`) as JSON or form-encoded, `revokeGrant(token)` matches EITHER the access or refresh column and flips live rows to `revoked_at = now()` (a presented refresh token thus also kills its access token per §2.1). **Idempotent + non-leaking (§2.2): empty HTTP 200 for unknown / garbage / already-revoked tokens alike**; only 400 (`invalid_request`) for an unparseable body or missing `token`. CORS `*` + OPTIONS mirror the sibling `/api/oauth/{token,register}` routes (the token is the credential — no session/Bearer auth). **(2) Settings → Integrations → "Connected apps"** ([src/app/(app)/settings/integrations/connected-apps.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/integrations/connected-apps.tsx)) — lists the user's live grants (client name via `LEFT JOIN oauth_clients`, scope, connected-date; **no last-used** — split to FINLYNQ-167) with a Revoke button per row that kills the whole grant. Bespoke fetch/useState/useEffect (no SWR per CLAUDE.md), shared `ConfirmDialog` for the destructive confirm, `parseSaveError` for failed mutations, loading/error/empty states. Backed by `GET/DELETE /api/settings/connected-apps` ([route.ts](src/app/api/settings/connected-apps/route.ts), `requireAuth` — no DEK needed; DELETE is **owner-scoped** by `revokeGrantById(userId, id)` so a guessed row id from another user 404s without existence leak). **(3) Password-reset → revoke-all wiring** — `wipeUserDataAndRewrap` ([src/lib/auth/queries.ts](src/lib/auth/queries.ts), called from `password-reset/confirm`) now flips `revoked_at` on every live grant for the user INSIDE the wipe transaction, before the existing row DELETE — the DELETE already guaranteed a pre-reset token 401s (no row → `validateOauthToken` null), so this encodes the intent at the WIPE chokepoint and closes the in-tx window; `revokeAllForUser(userId)` is the standalone primitive a future password-CHANGE rewrap path can reuse (there is no in-app password-change route today, so that path is N/A here). New helpers in [src/lib/oauth.ts](src/lib/oauth.ts): `revokeGrant`, `revokeGrantById`, `revokeAllForUser`, `listConnectedApps` + the `ConnectedApp` type. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (both new routes registered). Files: `src/app/api/oauth/revoke/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/settings/connected-apps/route.ts` (new), `src/app/(app)/settings/integrations/connected-apps.tsx` (new), `src/lib/oauth.ts`, `src/lib/auth/queries.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/integrations/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(admin): make the admin Users table sortable by any column (FINLYNQ-149, 2026-06-12, dev).** On the `/admin` Users tab, every column header is now a clickable sort toggle (asc → desc → off) with a visible direction indicator (`ChevronUp`/`ChevronDown`/`ChevronsUpDown`). Seven sortable columns: **User** (display label = `displayName ?? username ?? email`), **Role**, **Plan**, **Verified**, **MFA**, **Txns**, **Joined**; **Actions** header stays non-sortable. Sort is **client-side** over the already-fetched list (the admin user list is not paginated). Default sort follows server order; state does not persist across reloads. **Null-safety:** the User comparator chains `?? ""` through every nullable display field and calls `.toLowerCase()` + `.localeCompare()` (never crashes on a null decrypted name); Role/Plan use `(a ?? "").localeCompare(...)`; Verified/MFA compare numeric boolean; Txns/Joined compare numeric. The `sortUsers` pure function and `SortIcon` component live in the same file as the page (no new files needed). Accessibility: each header button carries `aria-sort` (`ascending`/`descending`/`none`). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(mcp): value investment accounts at MARKET in get_account_balances + get_net_worth (FINLYNQ-151, MCP v3.3, 2026-06-12, dev).** Both HTTP MCP balance tools reported every account as raw `COALESCE(SUM(transactions.amount), 0)`. For investment accounts that figure is **net contributions, not market value** — a portfolio buy/sell writes a +stock / −cash pair summing to zero, and even a realized gain cancels (sell-stock −proceeds offsets the +proceeds cash leg) — so an AI client systematically understated investment accounts and diverged from the web dashboard's load-bearing invariant **"account with holdings = `holdings.value`"** (market, cash sleeve included). Both tools now overlay investment-account rows to market, matching the web. **Shared overlay** [mcp-server/investment-balance-overlay.ts](mcp-server/investment-balance-overlay.ts) (`applyInvestmentMarketOverlay(rows, dek, fetchHoldings)`) is dependency-injected so the two tools share ONE decision and tests need no module mocking; production call site is `() => getHoldingsValueByAccount(userId, dek)` (the same pricing path `get_goals` already uses for its `is_investment` branch). **Critical DEK gate:** with `dek == null` (a `pf_` API-key connection — OAuth/built-in-chat carry a DEK) holdings symbols decrypt to null and pricing returns garbage (qty×1, holdings-value.ts:319-326), so the overlay NEVER calls `fetchHoldings` when `dek == null` OR when there are no investment rows — API-key callers keep today's ledger numbers **byte-compatible with v3.2** plus a top-level `note`, and OAuth callers get market value. Investment rows take `balance = holdings.value ?? 0` (issue #204: a zero-priced investment account reports 0, never the tx-sum). **get_account_balances**: SQL adds `a.is_investment` + deterministic `ORDER BY a.type, a."group", a.id`; each row gains `isInvestment` + `balanceBasis` ('market'|'ledger'), and market rows also carry `costBasis` + `cashFlowBasis` (the net-contribution tx-sum, so the contribution figure stays reachable). **get_net_worth** current-totals branch restructured to per-account grain with the identical ordering + overlay, rolling up `byCurrency` with `+=`; Issue #210 parity (`total.net.amount === get_account_balances.totalReporting.amount`) is now **structural** (one item per account, same grain/order/amounts, holds in both dek-present and dek-null cases — argued in a code comment) + a top-level `basis`. The trend branch (priorMonths>0) is unchanged math but newly tags `basis: "ledger"` + a note (history is contribution-basis; marking it to market needs `portfolio_snapshots`, out of scope). No new tools — version stays **3.3.0** (set by FINLYNQ-150), tool count stays 109. Tool descriptions, `finlynq_help` (reworded get_net_worth, ADDED a get_account_balances entry), stale "marketValue not available in MCP" notes, the stdio get_net_worth description (HTTP-only contribution-basis note; stdio behavior untouched — it already refuses get_account_balances), `public/.well-known/mcp.json`, `src/app/(app)/api-docs/page.tsx`, and the regenerated `tools.generated.ts` catalog all reworded. New [tests/api/mcp-investment-balance-overlay.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-investment-balance-overlay.test.ts) — overlay units + handler parity (dek-present AND dek-null). Files: `mcp-server/investment-balance-overlay.ts` (new), `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`, `tests/api/mcp-investment-balance-overlay.test.ts` (new), `public/.well-known/mcp.json`, `src/app/(app)/api-docs/page.tsx`, `src/app/mcp-guide/tools/tools.generated.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(mcp): bank-ledger reconciliation + rule-application MCP tools + DRY-extracted materialize chokepoint (FINLYNQ-150, MCP v3.3, 2026-06-12, dev).** The HTTP MCP could review/approve *staged* statements and author rules, but had **zero coverage** of the `/import` page's bank-ledger **reconcile** layer (the three-layer match engine, materializing a bank-only row, accept/undo a link, the per-account pipeline mode, re-firing rules over a pending batch / Auto-pilot bulk). **(1) Load-bearing DRY extraction** — the category-materialize logic was inline in [src/app/api/reconcile/materialize/route.ts](src/app/api/reconcile/materialize/route.ts) (~lines 68–221) and embedded six invariants (sign-vs-category, verbatim `import_hash`, re-encrypt-under-DEK, audit-trio defaults, `invalidateUser`, investment-account refusal). Extracted verbatim into the shared chokepoint **[src/lib/reconcile/materialize-transaction.ts](src/lib/reconcile/materialize-transaction.ts)** (`materializeBankRowAsTransaction()`) returning a typed `{ok:false,code}` for the expected refusals; the route POST shrank to a thin adapter that maps those codes to its **byte-identical** HTTP statuses (`*_not_found`→404, `investment_account_unsupported`/`sign_category_mismatch`→400, success→`{success:true,data:{transactionId}}`). The web route AND the MCP tool now share one chokepoint (mirrors how `materializeBankRowAsTransfer` is shared by 3 callers). **(2) 7 new HTTP-only MCP tools** in [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts), each reusing the EXACT web lib in-process (no behavior drift), all needing a DEK so HTTP-only (stdio untouched): `get_reconcile_suggestions` (read → `computeReconcileForAccount`, replicating the route's per-user `reconcile_thresholds` load), `materialize_bank_row` (ONE tool, branches on `destAccountId` → transfer via `materializeBankRowAsTransfer` (outflow rows only) vs `categoryId` → category via the extracted lib; refuses both-set), `accept_reconcile_suggestion`/`unlink_reconcile` (→ `linkTransactionToBank`/`unlinkTransactionFromBank`, `LinkError`→`err("Not found")`), `set_account_mode` (inline owner-scoped `UPDATE accounts SET mode … WHERE id AND user_id RETURNING id, mode` — NOT a transactions write, so no `invalidateUser`), `apply_rules_to_staged_import` (→ `applyRulesToStagedBatch` over the Drizzle `@/db` proxy, pre-checked owner + `status='pending'`), and `apply_rules_to_bank_rows` (Auto-pilot bulk → `applyRulesToBankRows`; **two-step confirmation token** via `signConfirmationToken`/`verifyConfirmationToken`, precedent `approve_staged_rows` — the preview pass runs `autoMaterialize:false` and never writes; the token call commits with `autoMaterialize:true`). Cross-tenant ids resolve to `err("Not found")` (no existence leak); the wrapped libs already `invalidateUser` after any `transactions` write. Reachable via `finlynq_help(topic="reconcile")` + listed under the new `reconcile_tools` group. Reused unchanged: `match-engine.ts`, `links.ts`, `materialize-transfer.ts`, `rules/apply-to-staged-batch.ts`. **(3) Counts/version** — authoritative HTTP tool count settled by counting registered `server.tool(` calls (= 109 after +7) AND confirmed by `generate-mcp-tool-catalog.mjs` ("wrote 109 HTTP tools"); bumped every doc to match: `auto-annotations.ts` header (102→109), `index.ts` + `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts` serverInfo `version` 3.2.0→**3.3.0** + the route description (102→109 MCP tools), workspace `CLAUDE.md` (v3.2→v3.3, 102→109 HTTP, MCP bullet extended, the stale "PATCH … is the only write path" line corrected to name both write paths). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors (touched files) · `audit:invariants` 13/13 (the extracted lib's INSERT co-locates sign-vs-category + verbatim import_hash + re-encrypt-under-DEK + invalidateUser; the `invalidateUser-after-mcp-tx-mutation` audit stays green) · `npx vitest run reconcile` 10/10 incl. the new [tests/reconcile-materialize-transaction.test.ts](tests/reconcile-materialize-transaction.test.ts) (5 cases: tx+link in one db.transaction with `source='reconcile_link'` + verbatim `import_hash` + DEK re-encryption + `invalidateUser`; investment refusal; sign-vs-category refusal; bank/category not-found) and the existing reconcile route spec unchanged. Files: `src/lib/reconcile/materialize-transaction.ts` (new), `tests/reconcile-materialize-transaction.test.ts` (new), `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/app/api/reconcile/materialize/route.ts`, `mcp-server/auto-annotations.ts`, `mcp-server/index.ts`, `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(accounts): honour the Settings → Dropdown Ordering "account" sort order on the /accounts list (FINLYNQ-148, 2026-06-12, dev).** The account sort order configured in **Settings → Display & Ordering → Dropdown Ordering** (the per-user "account" pin list, stored in the `settings` key `dropdown_order` via `/api/settings/dropdown-order`) drove the account *pickers* but was **ignored by the main `/accounts` list** ([src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx)) — it rendered accounts in raw `/api/dashboard` order (grouped by type/group, then API order). **Root cause:** `page.tsx` already consumed `useDropdownOrder("currency")` for the currency combobox but never wired `useDropdownOrder("account")` into the rendered list. **Fix:** the per-group account list is now passed through the SAME pure `sortByUserOrder` helper the pickers use ([src/lib/dropdown-order.ts](src/lib/dropdown-order.ts)) inside the page's `groups()` helper — keyed on the numeric `accountId`, with a **null-safe** display-name fallback comparator `(a, b) => (a.accountName ?? "").localeCompare(b.accountName ?? "")` (account names are decrypted display values — defends against null per the `safeName` invariant). Pinned accounts lead in the saved order; the rest fall back to name order; a saved-order id for a since-deleted account is skipped silently. **No new storage / no migration** — reuses the existing `dropdown_order` settings key; the order persists across reloads because it's read from the same provider on every mount. The grouping (Assets/Liabilities → Group) and all account CRUD are unchanged. Scope: the **/accounts list** (the acceptance target) + every account picker/dropdown already honoured it; the dashboard account list (`/api/dashboard balances`) is a separate flat surface — noted as a follow-up. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · new vitest [tests/accounts-sort-order.test.ts](tests/accounts-sort-order.test.ts) (5 cases: name fallback when unset/empty, pinned-then-name order, null-safe display name, skip deleted ids) green. Files: `src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx`, `tests/accounts-sort-order.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(import): reconciliation summary panel + hide accounts from the reconcile dropdown (FINLYNQ-147, 2026-06-12, dev).** Two related improvements to the consolidated money-in surface. **(1) Reconciliation summary** — a collapsible "Reconciliation summary" panel on `/import` ([src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx](src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx)) lists every account with its **last import date**, **last reconciled date**, **pending (unreconciled) bank-row count**, and a quick "Open" link that selects that account. All figures are **DERIVED from existing tables — no new column** (per the item's lean): `lastImportAt = MAX(bank_upload_batches.uploaded_at)`, `lastReconciledAt = MAX(transactions.created_at WHERE bank_transaction_id IS NOT NULL)` (the materialize/reconcile lineage), `pendingCount = bank_transactions NOT EXISTS a referencing ledger transaction`. Pure DEK-free core in [src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts](src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts) (`getReconcileSummary`); names decrypted at the new read route `GET /api/reconcile/summary` ([src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts](src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts)) via `decryptNamedRows` + `safeAccountName` null-defense. Lazy-fetched (only on first expand), bespoke fetch/useState/useEffect (no SWR per FINLYNQ-118). **(2) Hide accounts from the reconcile dropdown** — a per-account "Hide / Show" control on `/settings/import` ([src/components/inbox/reconcile-hide-accounts-card.tsx](src/components/inbox/reconcile-hide-accounts-card.tsx)); hidden accounts drop out of the `/import` account picker. **Persistence: a per-user `settings` key `reconcile_hidden_accounts` (JSON array of account ids) — NO migration** (mirrors the `confirm_csv_mapping` / `email_retention_days` settings-key precedent). Pure normalizer `parseHiddenAccountIds` (never throws — malformed value degrades to "nothing hidden") + reader/writer in [src/lib/reconcile/hidden-accounts.ts](src/lib/reconcile/hidden-accounts.ts); GET/PUT at `/api/settings/reconcile-hidden-accounts` (bare shape + `requireAuth`, matching the sibling settings-key routes). **Hidden is a DROPDOWN-ONLY filter** — a hidden account stays fully reachable via `/settings/import`, via the summary panel, and via direct deep-links (`/import?account=<id>`, `/import/pending`); the `/import` dropdown also keeps the currently-selected account in its list so a hidden deep-linked account stays selectable in the trigger.
