No change to materialize, dedup, or any aggregate; archived accounts still filtered separately. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (both new routes compile) · new vitest `tests/reconcile-hidden-accounts.test.ts` (5 cases) green. Files: `src/lib/reconcile/summary.ts` (new), `src/lib/reconcile/hidden-accounts.ts` (new), `src/app/api/reconcile/summary/route.ts` (new), `src/app/api/settings/reconcile-hidden-accounts/route.ts` (new), `src/components/inbox/reconcile-summary-panel.tsx` (new), `src/components/inbox/reconcile-hide-accounts-card.tsx` (new), `src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx`, `tests/reconcile-hidden-accounts.test.ts` (new), `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **chore(lint/dry): ESLint teeth restoration + DRY burn-down — re-promote 3 rules, `^_` ignore contract, round2/todayISO consolidation, forecast N+1 (FINLYNQ-145, 2026-06-12, dev).** Cleanup pass that runs LAST among the 2026-06-11 audit cohort (depended on the reports/portfolio entity escapes from FINLYNQ-143/144, both landed). **(1) eslint.config.mjs** — re-promoted **3 rules to `error`** by removing them from the FINLYNQ-112 warn block (all zero-violation today): `@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports` (the lone `src/db/pg-shim.ts` `require()` — kept to dodge tree-shaking — now carries a justified file-scoped disable), `react/display-name` (never had a violation), `react/no-unescaped-entities` (last escapes landed in FINLYNQ-143/144). Gave `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` an **`^_` ignore contract** (`argsIgnorePattern`/`varsIgnorePattern`/`caughtErrorsIgnorePattern`) so intentionally-unused args/vars/caught-errors are silenced at source (~51 noise warnings gone); rewrote the stale `preserve-manual-memoization` comment (the 12 warnings are all in `src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, not the 3 components it named). **(2) round2 consolidation** — new canonical [src/lib/utils/number.ts](src/lib/utils/number.ts) exports `round2`; `currency-conversion.ts` now **re-exports** it (zero breakage — existing `@/lib/currency-conversion` importers untouched). Pointed the 8 local copies at it (`transfer.ts`, `fx/reporting-amount.ts`, `transactions/splits/route.ts`, `onboarding/sample-data/route.ts`, `mcp/duplicate-hints.ts`, `reconcile/match-engine.ts` + `external-import/duplicate-detect.ts` via `roundScore = round2`, `external-import/parsers/ibkr-flexquery-xml.ts`) plus the `reports/route.ts` inline copy. **`loan-calculator.ts` keeps its OWN private copy on purpose** (bundled into the MCP build — must stay dependency-free). **(3) todayISO sweep (~20 sites)** — deleted local `today`/`todayISO` wrappers + inline `new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0]` today-strings across `transfer.ts`, `spotlight.ts`, `chat-engine.ts`, `unrealized-pnl.ts`, `price-service.ts`, `crypto-service.ts` (4×), `cron/settle-future-fx.ts` (2×), `portfolio/lots/read.ts`, `fx-overrides-section.tsx`, and 8 API routes (`transactions`, `transactions/audit`, `fx/preview`, `scenarios`, `reports`, `reports/trends`, `rules`, `portfolio/overview`), and removed the shadowing `const todayISO` in `import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts` — all now import the shared `todayISO()` ([src/lib/utils/date.ts](src/lib/utils/date.ts)). Carved out per ticket: `transaction-dialog.tsx`/`reports/page.tsx`/`accounts/page.tsx` (already converted by 143/144), and `fx-service.ts:96` (intentionally inlined to dodge a documented TDZ on its arrow-`const` `todayISO`). Arbitrary-date `someDate.toISOString().split(...)` conversions (benchmarks, recurring-detector, weekly-recap window bounds, crypto-service history buckets, etc.) are NOT today-computations and were correctly left alone. **(4) forecast/route.ts N+1** — replaced the per-account `SUM` loop with one `GROUP BY account_id` query (`inArray(...)` = `account_id = ANY($)`), then `convertWithRateMap` per account row; swapped the inline rounding for `round2`. **FINLYNQ-123 currency split preserved EXACTLY** — still per-account NATIVE sum converted at the CURRENT rate; never `SUM(amount)` across mixed currencies. Iterating over `bankAccounts` (with `?? 0` for accounts with no rows) keeps the output numerically identical for a multi-account user. **(5) Targeted dead imports** — `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts` (dead fx + import-pipeline/duplicate-detect/duplicate-hints imports), `weekly-recap.ts` (`desc`/`accounts`), `age-of-money.ts` (`gte`/`lte`/`sql`), `portfolio/operations.ts` (`resolveCashLegForTx`), `cron/settle-future-fx.ts` (`gt`/`isNull`/`or`). `drizzle-pg-shim.d.ts`'s unused interface type-params can't be `_`-prefixed (renaming breaks Drizzle's declaration-merge → every `.all()/.get()` stops type-checking), so it carries a scoped `no-unused-vars` disable with a comment. Left SellForm `effectiveSellQty` + transactions `useDropdownOrder('holding')` alone (Tier-3). **(6) `eslint . --fix`** on the merged tree removed ~64 stale `eslint-disable` directives repo-wide (orphaned whitespace-only lines collapsed to empty). **Net:** lint warnings 351 → 184 (0 errors), 3 rules now gating at `error`. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (203/203 static pages). MCP files (`register-core-tools.ts`, `operations.ts`) type-check via the root `tsc` (which includes `mcp-server/**`); audit invariant #8 stays green. Files: `eslint.config.mjs`, `src/lib/utils/number.ts` (new), `src/lib/currency-conversion.ts`, `src/db/pg-shim.ts`, `src/drizzle-pg-shim.d.ts`, `src/app/api/forecast/route.ts`, the 8 round2 sites + `reports/route.ts`, the ~20 todayISO sites, the 5 dead-import files, ~40 files touched by `--fix`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(transactions/ui): dialog mobile responsiveness (base max-h + `sm:max-w` prefix sweep) + filtered-view CSV export (FINLYNQ-144, 2026-06-11, dev).** Two things: tall dialogs no longer overflow the viewport on phones, and the `/transactions` page can export its current filtered view to CSV. **(1) Base `DialogContent` max-height** ([src/components/ui/dialog.tsx](src/components/ui/dialog.tsx)) — appended `max-h-[calc(100dvh-2rem)] overflow-y-auto` to the base class string so EVERY dialog keeps a top/bottom gutter and scrolls its body internally on small screens. Callers that pass their own `max-h`/overflow (the two import-connector dialogs use `max-h-[85vh]` + an overflow-hidden flex column) keep winning via tailwind-merge. **(2) `sm:max-w` prefix sweep (7 sites)** — unprefixed `max-w-*` on a `DialogContent` resolves through tailwind-merge by **nuking** the base `max-w-[calc(100%-2rem)]` phone gutter, rendering those dialogs full-bleed at 390px. Prefixed every one so the width only applies at `sm`+ and the phone gutter survives: transactions delete + bulk-delete (`sm:max-w-sm`), portfolio + settings/investments dialogs (`sm:max-w-md`), split dialog (`sm:max-w-2xl`), transaction dialog (`sm:max-w-lg`), connector mapping + reconciliation (`sm:max-w-3xl`). **(3) Ride-alongs in owned files** — escaped the unescaped `'` in `portfolio/page.tsx` (`couldn&apos;t`, a prereq for the FINLYNQ-145 lint re-promotion) and swapped the 4 inline `new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0]` today-strings in `transaction-dialog.tsx` for the shared `todayISO()` ([src/lib/utils/date.ts](src/lib/utils/date.ts)). **(4) New `exportCsv` util** ([src/lib/csv-export.ts](src/lib/csv-export.ts)) — pure RFC-4180-correct CSV serialization + a browser download trigger, modeled on the reports `exportCSV` + settings/data `buildCsv`+`csvCell` WITHOUT touching those files (consolidation is a Tier-3 backlog item). A field is quoted iff it contains a comma, double-quote, CR, or LF; embedded quotes are doubled (`""`); rows joined with CRLF; a UTF-8 BOM is prepended by default (off via `{ bom: false }`) so Excel detects UTF-8. `exportCsv(rows, columns, filename, opts?)` takes a `{ header, accessor }` column descriptor; `serializeCsv`/`buildCsvString`/`csvField` are exported for tests + string-only callers. **(5) Export button on `/transactions`** — beside the filter bar; re-fetches `GET /api/transactions` with the SAME `buildTransactionQuery(filters, sortPref, colFilters, accounts, …)` the table uses (FINLYNQ-115 — never hand-rolls params) at page 0 + `limit: 100000`, then downloads `transactions-<today>.csv`. When a text/tag/encrypted-substring filter is active (mirrors the route's `postDecryptFilter`, which caps the candidate set at 1000 rows), an inline note states the export is capped at the first 1,000 matching transactions. Disabled while exporting or when the view is empty. SWR untouched; FINLYNQ-125 `RuleEditorDialog` sibling structure untouched; investment accounts stay hidden from the generic Add Transaction dialog in new-entry mode. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green · CSV quoting round-trip verified (comma + embedded `"` + newline payee → one quoted field, BOM present/absent per option). Files: `src/lib/csv-export.ts` (new), `src/components/ui/dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/_components/split-dialog.tsx`, `src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-mapping-dialog.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/import/components/connector-reconciliation-dialog.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(dashboard/reports/secondary): fetch hardening + reports skeleton + dark-mode & brand sweep + a11y; FIRE gate left dev-gated (FINLYNQ-143, 2026-06-11, dev).** Reliability + design-system sweep over the dashboard widgets, reports, and secondary pages. **(1) Fetch hardening** — `tax/page.tsx`, `settings/categorization`, dashboard `weekly-recap.tsx` / `insights-section.tsx` / `action-center.tsx`, and the four `reports/page.tsx` fetches now follow the `usePortfolioFormData` recipe: `r.ok` check, shape guard (`Array.isArray` / `typeof === "object"`), `.catch` to an error/empty state, and a `cancelled` flag so a failed API call degrades gracefully instead of rendering a blank/crashed widget. **(2) Reports first-paint** — added a `loading` flag set false in the trends `.finally()`; the 1378-line chart page now early-returns `<PageSkeleton variant="cards" />` until trends resolves, eliminating the blank-axes flash. Also escaped the unescaped `'` at ~780 (`you&apos;d` — prereq for the FINLYNQ-145 lint rule re-promotion) and swapped the two inline `new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0]` today-dates in reports + the opening-balance date in `accounts/page.tsx` for the shared `todayISO()` ([src/lib/utils/date.ts](src/lib/utils/date.ts)); the year-ago `past.toISOString()` in `getPresetRange` is intentionally NOT swapped. **(3) Dark-mode & brand sweep** — calendar today-cell `bg-blue-50`/`text-blue-600` → dark-aware `bg-primary/10 ring-primary/30` + `text-primary`; dashboard Net-Worth stat-card + insights "Category Trends" + weekly-recap orb/icon/bar-fill/budget-bar + calendar "Net for Month" + tax RRSP-calc icon + fire "Years to FIRE"/sensitivity-highlight/median-percentile all moved off pre-rebrand indigo onto `bg-primary/10 text-primary` / `var(--color-primary)`; added `dark:` pairs to the remaining `bg-*-100` icon chips on accounts (summary + section avatars + detail stat cards), tax (5 cards), calendar (income/bills), and fire (6 ResultCards + Coast-FIRE card + MC success badge). Chart fills stay sourced from `chart-colors.ts` / the `--color-primary` token; no chart math touched (`prepareTimeSeries` untouched, bar/pie/Sankey stay 0-anchored). **(4) chat/page.tsx full rebrand** — empty-state + header gradient orbs → solid `bg-primary`, user bubble + send button → `bg-primary` / `bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90`, assistant avatar + bubbles + loading dots + user avatar off `slate`/`indigo` onto `bg-muted`/`bg-primary/10` tokens (`grep -i 'indigo|violet|slate|purple'` → 0). **(5) aria-labels** on icon-only buttons (calendar month chevrons, chat send, weekly-recap expand toggle, action-center dismiss, accounts edit, categorization save/cancel/edit/delete). **(6) FIRE gate NOT promoted** — `/fire` + `/scenarios` stay `DevModeGuard`-gated and `mode:'dev'` in nav. **Decisive blocker:** `/api/fire`, `/api/fire/monte-carlo`, and `/api/scenarios` all call `requireDevMode(request)` ([src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts](src/lib/require-dev-mode.ts)), which returns **404** when dev mode is off — promoting only the page-level guard (as the ticket scoped) would ship a visible-but-broken page whose Calculate/Run POSTs 404 in prod mode. Making it work end-to-end would require also stripping the server gate from 3 API routes (out of group-6 scope), so the conservative fallback was taken: gates left in place, roadmap/landing copy untouched (FINLYNQ-140 already positions FIRE under "Up next"/"Building"). Web only.

- **fix(money-pages): res.ok + 423 handling, delete confirmations, loading/error states, copy-budgets-from-previous-month (FINLYNQ-142, 2026-06-11, dev).** Reliability sweep over the four money-management pages (goals / budgets / subscriptions / loans), all of which previously discarded user input on a failed save. **(1) New shared `ConfirmDialog`** ([src/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx](src/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx)) — a base-ui `<Dialog>` (render-prop, never `asChild`) mirroring the transactions delete-dialog styling (`AlertTriangle` + destructive title, outline Cancel / destructive Confirm, busy-state lock that blocks dismissal mid-delete). Wired into all **four** permanent deletes (goal, loan, subscription, budget) that previously fired on a single un-confirmed click. **(2) `res.ok` + 423 everywhere** — new pure client helper [src/lib/save-error.ts](src/lib/save-error.ts) (`parseSaveError(res, fallback)` + `DEK_LOCKED_MESSAGE`) centralizes the contract: **HTTP 423 (`requireEncryption` refused, no DEK) → the literal "Unlock your data to make changes"**, never a generic failure; everything else prefers the server's `{error}`/`{message}` body. Threaded through goals `GoalEditForm.handleSubmit`, budgets `handleSubmit`/`handleMoveMoney`/`handleApplyTemplate`/copy, subscriptions `handleSubmit`, loans `handleSubmit` — on failure the dialog **stays open**, the entered values are preserved, and the parsed reason renders inline above the submit button. Form errors via `useState` cleared with `""` (never `undefined`). **(3) Loading + error states** — added a `loading` flag to goals/budgets/subscriptions (loans already had one); render `PageSkeleton`/`LoansSkeleton` until first data, `ErrorState` (with working retry) on fetch failure, and gated every empty state on `!loading` so existing users no longer see a "Set your first…" flash. Hardened every bare fetch (`r.ok` guard + `Array.isArray` + `.catch` + `setLoading` in `.finally`). **Loans infinite-spinner fixed**: `setLoading(false)` moved to `.finally` (was success-only) and the loans/accounts fetches are now `r.ok`-guarded. **(4) `empty-state.tsx` rebrand** — both hand-rolled `bg-indigo-600` CTA branches replaced with the shared `<Button render={<Link/>}>` / `<Button onClick>` using primary tokens (visible on /accounts, /loans, transactions empty states). **(5) Copy budgets from previous month** — on a month with zero budgets, a prominent "Copy from &lt;prev month&gt;" button fetches `GET /api/budgets?month=<prev>` and POSTs each row's native `{categoryId, month, amount, currency}` to the existing **upserting** `POST /api/budgets` (reuses `upsertBudget` + `verifyOwnership` — **no API change**); button absent once the month has budgets. **(6) Ride-alongs in the four owned files** — dark-mode pairs on every badge map + summary-tile icon chip (were bare `bg-*-100`, washed out in dark mode); budgets healthy-state chip/bar swapped from indigo to brand `primary` tokens per ui-design.md; aria-labels on every icon-only button (4 deletes, goal edit/complete, subscription pause/resume/cancel/reminder, budget month-nav chevrons + template delete); removed 5 unused `Select` imports (budgets) + 1 unused `Progress` import (loans). **No SWR** (preserves the FINLYNQ-118 bespoke fetch/useState/useEffect pattern). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green. Files: `src/components/ui/confirm-dialog.tsx` (new), `src/lib/save-error.ts` (new), `src/components/empty-state.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/{goals,budgets,subscriptions,loans}/page.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(self-host): repair the one-command self-host path — crash-looping docker-compose, dead doc links, missing .env vars, foreign-domain OAuth issuer fallback (FINLYNQ-141, 2026-06-11, dev).** The advertised self-host flow was broken end-to-end. **(1) `docker-compose.yml` rewrite** — removed the NextAuth-era `NEXTAUTH_URL`/`NEXTAUTH_SECRET` (the app reads `PF_JWT_SECRET`, not NextAuth), set `APP_URL: http://localhost:3000`, and made the three secrets REQUIRED via `${VAR:?...}` interpolation (`PF_JWT_SECRET`, `PF_PEPPER` ≥32, `PF_STAGING_KEY` ≥32 — all module-load-throw in prod) so compose fails fast with a clear message instead of crash-looping the app container. Aligned the DB user/db/host across compose + `.env.example` (`pf`/`pf`/`db`), added a `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` override default + a commented `build: .` source-build fallback. No real secrets committed. **(2) `README.md`** — dropped the 404-ing `pf-app/` segment from the install curl URL (repo root IS `pf-app`), reworded the secrets note to the new `.env`-driven flow, repointed the dead `docs/architecture/mcp.md` + `docs/architecture/` + `docs/import-connectors.md` links (those live in the private junction, not the public repo) to `finlynq.com/mcp-guide` + live docs, and pointed the demo line at `finlynq.com/cloud?demo=1`. **(3) `.env.example`** — added commented entries (purpose + when-required) for `PF_DATABASE_URL` (precedence), `PF_PEPPER_V2`, `PF_PEPPER_TARGET_VERSION`, `PF_USER_ID`, `BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY_FILE`, `INBOUND_EMAIL_PROVIDER`, `FINLYNQ_INBOUND_SECRET`, `IMPORT_EMAIL_LOCALPART_PREFIX`; fixed the false "used by postgres service in docker-compose" comment. **(4) `docs/getting-started.md`** — `cd finlynq/pf-app` → `cd finlynq` (clone root IS the app), replaced the dead "see DEPLOY.md" pointer (DEPLOY.md isn't in the public repo) with the compose flow + `finlynq.com/self-hosted`, and documented the public unauthenticated `/api/healthz` endpoint for reverse-proxy / uptime monitors. **(5) `CONTRIBUTING.md` + `ci.yml`** — removed the dead `CLAUDE.md` pointer and the phantom `staging` branch row; dropped `staging` from `ci.yml`'s `pull_request` branch triggers. **(6) `docs/faq.md`** — the import landing surface is `/import` (email deep-links may open `/import/pending`), was pointing only at `/import/pending`. **(7) `src/lib/oauth.ts`** — `getIssuer()` no longer falls back to the foreign `finance.nextsoftwareconsulting.com` (which silently broke MCP connector setup for self-hosters and leaked a foreign domain); it now falls back to `http://localhost:3000` and `console.warn`s when `APP_URL` is unset under `NODE_ENV=production`. Replaced the three hand-rolled `result.rows ?? result ?? []` normalizations (`consumeAuthCode` / `validateOauthToken` / `getClient`) with the shared `normalizeDbRows<T>()` (CLAUDE.md DRY rule; wire shape unchanged — it's a superset of the hand-rolled form) and removed the unused `InvalidScopeError` import. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green · `docker compose config` fails closed without the three secrets (naming each) and succeeds with a test `.env`. **Operator-manual follow-up (cannot be done in code):** make the ghcr `finlynq` package Public so logged-out `docker pull ghcr.io/finlynq/finlynq:latest` works. **Known gap:** the static `nginx/landing/self-hosted.html` still references the old image org + a removed-SQLite setup (website/landing territory, FINLYNQ-140 domain — flagged as a follow-up, not touched here). Files: `docker-compose.yml`, `README.md`, `.env.example`, `docs/getting-started.md`, `docs/faq.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `src/lib/oauth.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(website): truth & conversion sweep — stale tool counts, dead footer link, demo CTAs, /cloud rebrand, SEO/AnalyticsConsent (FINLYNQ-140, 2026-06-11, dev).** Website-only credibility + conversion pass over the public marketing surface. **(1) Tool-count truth sweep** — the landing hero contradicted itself (102 / 91 / 90 within one page). Fixed every stale `91 tools` / `90 financial` / `91-tool` / `90 MCP tools` count to **102 HTTP / 93 stdio (v3.2.0)** across `landing-client.tsx` (hero bar, hero-meta tile, MCP section), `about/page.tsx` (Era FAQ — feeds FAQPage JSON-LD), `vs/alderfi/page.tsx` (tagline + FAQ), `blog/how-finlynq-encrypts-your-money`, and made the `README.md` features bullet count-free ("100+ MCP tools (HTTP & stdio)"). `git grep` for the stale-count regexes is now clean on `src/app`, `src/components/landing`, `README.md`. **(2) Footer fix** — the landing footer "API docs" link pointed at `/api-docs` (login-walled + DevModeGuard-gated, dead-ending every prospective dev); replaced with "MCP & API guide" → `/mcp-guide` and folded the duplicate plain "MCP guide" entry into it. **(3) Demo CTAs** — added "Try the live demo (no signup)" → `/try-demo?next=/dashboard` to the shared `vs/_components/VsPage.tsx` CTA box (covers all 8 vs pages), `vs/page.tsx`, `about/page.tsx`, the roadmap footer, the landing MCP CTA row + final CTA section, plus a "Just looking? One-click demo" line under the `/cloud` auth form. **(4) /cloud rebrand** — extracted the amber brand mark from `landing-client.tsx` into shared **`src/components/logo-mark.tsx`** (now imported by both landing + /cloud); replaced the literal "PF" placeholder block on `/cloud` with `<LogoMark>`; made the h1 tab-aware ("Create your free Finlynq account" for `?tab=register`, "Welcome back" for sign-in) + added the reassurance line "Free forever. AGPL v3. Encrypted with your password." under register. **(5) /about truth** — reworded the mobile FAQ + differentiators bullet to "Android app in final testing on Google Play; iOS to follow" + linked the mobile blog post; refreshed "Last updated" to 2026-06-11. **(6) Roadmap truth** — moved "in-app AI chat" out of the "Live now" MCP bullet (chat is dev-gated) into a new "Building now" item. **(7) AnalyticsConsent** — added the consent-gated GA banner to `/about`, `/roadmap`, `/mcp-guide`, the blog index, and both blog posts (traffic on the top SEO pages was previously unmeasurable). **(8) Landing nav** — added "Compare" (`/vs`) and "MCP guide" (`/mcp-guide`) cross-page links. **(9) /self-hosted** — dropped the 404-ing `pf-app/` path segment from the Docker Compose curl URL (the repo root IS `pf-app`, so the raw URL is `…/finlynq/main/docker-compose.yml`). No new public routes (all touched routes already in `site.ts` + middleware `isWebsite`); all JSON-LD stays through `<JsonLd>`; FAQ answers stay plain text; no em dashes in new copy. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · `next build` green (all touched routes compile). Files: `src/components/logo-mark.tsx` (new), `src/components/landing/landing-client.tsx`, `src/app/cloud/page.tsx`, `src/app/about/page.tsx`, `src/app/vs/_components/VsPage.tsx`, `src/app/vs/page.tsx`, `src/app/vs/alderfi/page.tsx`, `src/app/roadmap/page.tsx`, `src/app/self-hosted/page.tsx`, `src/app/blog/page.tsx`, `src/app/blog/finlynq-mobile-app/page.tsx`, `src/app/blog/how-finlynq-encrypts-your-money/page.tsx`, `src/app/mcp-guide/page.tsx`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(backfill): match FX conversion legs by cash-sleeve currency, not `transactions.currency`; add date-sort + general "Expense" override to the review page (2026-06-10, dev+prod).** Three changes to the transaction-canonicalization backfill, shipped to `dev` (`c3fa3f9`) and promoted to `main` (`079ba94`). **(1) FX leg matching (the load-bearing fix).** Two genuine FX legs on a multi-currency investment account couldn't be linked through the kind-override flow when one leg was manually entered in the account/base currency — e.g. a USD-cash-sleeve sell stored as `currency='CAD', amount=-12340.80` with the native USD in `entered_currency='USD'`/`quantity=-9000`. Both the candidate matcher and the converter keyed on `transactions.currency`, so the USD-sleeve leg and the CAD-sleeve leg both read `CAD`, looked same-currency, never appeared in each other's candidate list, and `convertExistingToFxPair` would have thrown `counterpart_currency_mismatch`. Now both key on the **cash-sleeve currency**: [counterpart-candidates/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/backfill/[runId]/counterpart-candidates/route.ts) left-joins the orphan's holding and the FX filter compares each leg's sleeve currency (`portfolio_holding_id → holdings.currency`, falling back to `transactions.currency` only for legs with no holding); [convertExistingToFxPair](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) gains `resolveFxLegSleeve` (resolves each leg's sleeve from its OWN holding, not a currency lookup) + `fxLegNativeMagnitude` (native amount from `quantity`, the cash-unit count — robust to a base-currency `amount`), validates the two **sleeve** currencies differ, and **normalizes the orphan's `currency`+`amount` onto its sleeve** so the result is a fully canonical `fx_from`/`fx_to` pair (`-9000 USD` ↔ `+12251.70 CAD`). **(2) General "Expense (general fee)" override.** The kind-override picker on a refused `orphan_stock_leg` gains a pair-less Expense entry that mirrors Interest: it **stays on the row's current holding** (no cash-sleeve move), needs **no related-holding pick**, and auto-categorizes as "Investment Fees" (the `fee` kind in `resolveOrCreateInvestmentIncomeCategory`) when no category is picked. It shares `kind='portfolio_expense'` with the existing holding-attributed entry; the two are disambiguated by `chosen_related_holding_id` being NULL (general) vs non-null (attributed). The UI option model now carries an `id` distinct from `kind` so two entries can share `portfolio_expense`; [apply.ts](src/lib/portfolio/backfill/apply.ts) narrows the `related_holding_missing` refusal to `portfolio_income` only and resolves the default fee category for the general variant. **No DB migration** (`portfolio_expense` was already a valid `chosen_kind`). **(3) Review-page date sort.** The left proposal list gains a Default/Date toggle; Date sorts by each proposal's earliest displaced-row date (ISO lexicographic, id tie-break). Component-state only. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13. Files: `src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/backfill/[runId]/counterpart-candidates/route.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/backfill/apply.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/backfill/[runId]/page.tsx`, `pf-app/plan/architecture/backfill.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(mcp): canonical `portfolio_*` write tools + block normal-transaction writes to investment accounts (MCP v3.2, 2026-06-10).** The HTTP MCP's investment-write surface was stale: it only exposed the legacy `record_trade` (buy/sell modeled as a same-account in-kind transfer via `createTransferPair`, predating the 2026-05-25 sign convention), so an AI assistant recording a trade produced differently-shaped rows than the web UI, and `record_transaction` would happily write a holding-bound row into an investment account, bypassing the lot-aware operations layer. **(1) 8 new HTTP-only tools** in [register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — `portfolio_buy` / `portfolio_sell` / `portfolio_swap` / `portfolio_transfer` / `portfolio_income_expense` / `portfolio_fx_conversion` / `portfolio_deposit` / `portfolio_withdrawal` — thin wrappers over the canonical domain layer [src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) (the SAME `recordBuy`/`recordSell`/… helpers the web forms + mobile drive through `/api/portfolio/operations/*`). They write the canonical lot-aware, sign-correct rows (stock leg +, cash leg −, sum 0; `*_cash_leg`/`fx_*`/`brokerage_*` kinds), resolve account by name (strict fuzzy via `resolveAccountStrict`) OR exact id and holdings by name/ticker OR id (account-scoped), call `invalidateUser` + `markSnapshotsDirty` after the write, and map domain errors (`CashSleeveNotFoundError`/`CurrencyMismatchError`/`HoldingNotFoundError`/`InvalidLinkPairError`, plus operations.ts guard errors) to friendly tool errors (mirrors `_helpers.ts:mapOperationError`). HTTP-only (they encrypt payee/note and need an unlocked DEK — stdio has none) and **CREATE-only** (edit-as-replace is `NextResponse`-coupled; edits stay on the web/REST path). **(2) Investment accounts are now fully refused** by `record_transaction` and `bulk_record_transactions` (HTTP) regardless of holding binding — refusal moved before holding resolution, with a message pointing at the `portfolio_*` tools; stdio `record_transaction` already refused investment accounts (its message updated to point at `portfolio_*`); stdio `bulk_record_transactions` remains refused entirely (Stream D). The domain `createTransaction` FK guard (`requireHoldingForInvestmentAccount`) is untouched, so REST/import paths that legitimately bind holdings are unaffected. Mirrors the web UI hiding investment accounts from the generic Add Transaction dialog in new-entry mode. **(3) `record_trade` removed** from both transports (HTTP + the stdio refuse-stub) — `portfolio_buy`/`portfolio_sell` replace it; `record_transfer` stays for plain cash transfers between non-investment accounts. `finlynq_help` (HTTP + stdio) re-cataloged. **Tool count now 102 HTTP / 93 stdio** (per the generated catalog — `record_trade` removed, 8 `portfolio_*` added). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · vitest: `oauth-scopes` + `mcp-record-transaction-parity` green, 4 new cases ([tests/api/mcp-portfolio-ops.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-portfolio-ops.test.ts): record_transaction refuses investment accts on the normal + dryRun paths and never calls createTransaction; portfolio_buy routes through `recordBuy` with `source='mcp_http'` + fires the cache/snapshot side effects; portfolio_buy refuses without a DEK). Files: `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`, `scripts/audit-invariants.ts`, `tests/oauth-scopes.test.ts`, `tests/api/{mcp-record-transaction-parity,mcp-portfolio-ops}.test.ts`, `src/lib/{transfer,bank-ledger}.ts`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts`, `mcp-server/{index,auto-annotations}.ts`, `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`, `docs/{faq,getting-started}.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`. **`serverInfo.version` bumped `3.1.0` → `3.2.0`** (stdio `mcp-server/index.ts` + HTTP `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`); the user-facing tool counts in `docs/faq.md` / `docs/getting-started.md` and the `auto-annotations.ts` header comment synced to **102 HTTP / 93 stdio**. Regenerated the public tool catalog [tools.generated.ts](src/app/mcp-guide/tools/tools.generated.ts) (it had drifted — the earlier hand-sync set 101/86 without re-running `generate-mcp-tool-catalog.mjs`, which actually emits 102 HTTP / 93 stdio = the count the live `/mcp-guide/tools` page renders) and synced the `/mcp-guide` `layout.tsx` metadata + the `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts` `serverInfo.description` blurb.

- **feat(loans): Loans & Debt v2 Phases 1–3+5 — payment-driven schedules, full frequency set, per-month interest accrual, lease residuals, account-linked balances (FINLYNQ-136, 2026-06-10, dev).** Evolves the dev-only Loans page from a term-only amortization calculator toward the ledger-integrated tracker. **Calculator core rewritten** ([src/lib/loan-calculator.ts](src/lib/loan-calculator.ts)) around a new options-based pure `buildLoanSchedule({ principal, annualRate, startDate, termMonths?, paymentAmount?, paymentFrequency?, extraPayment?, residualValue? })`: **(1) payment-driven mode** — given a payment, the schedule solves for the number of periods and the payoff/end date (term-driven mode unchanged; at least one of term/payment required); **(2) full frequency set** `weekly | biweekly | semi_monthly | monthly | quarterly | annual` (exported `PAYMENT_FREQUENCIES` + `PERIODS_PER_YEAR`; "bimonthly" deliberately excluded as ambiguous — biweekly + semi_monthly cover both readings; unknown legacy strings degrade to monthly). Per-period rate is now `annual/periodsPerYear` — this **fixes the v1 biweekly bug** that charged the MONTHLY rate every 14 days; **(3) per-calendar-month interest accrual** (`monthlyAccrual: [{month, interest}]`) — day-weighted allocation across periods that straddle month boundaries, the reportable per-month figure regardless of payment frequency; **(4) lease residual** — `residualValue` amortizes the balance down to the residual (balance at term end == residual, NOT 0), balloon-aware payment derivation; **(5) validation** — new `LoanValidationError` (mapped to HTTP 400 / MCP `err()`) refuses payment ≤ first-period interest ("never amortizes"), residual ≥ principal, no-term-and-no-payment, and >100-year payoffs. `monthlyPayment` keeps its legacy name (= payment per period) for every existing consumer; new `paymentPerPeriod` + `monthlyEquivalentPayment` (`payment·periodsPerYear/12`) normalize cross-frequency displays. Month stepping clamps to month-end (Jan 31 + 1mo = Feb 28/29, was overflowing to Mar 2-3) and date math is UTC-anchored (was TZ-dependent). The old positional `generateAmortizationSchedule` survives as a term-driven wrapper. **Account-linked balance:** `GET /api/loans` + MCP `list_loans` now read the outstanding balance from the linked account when it has ledger activity — `remainingBalance = |SUM(transactions.amount)|` (liability accounts carry negative balances), surfaced with `balanceSource: "account" | "projection"`, and the payoff date / periods remaining are **re-anchored from today's actual balance** via a payment-driven schedule (falling back to the pure projection when unlinked, no activity, or the payment no longer amortizes). **Schema** ([20260619_loans_v2.sql](scripts/migrations/20260619_loans_v2.sql)): `loans.residual_value DOUBLE PRECISION` (nullable) + `term_months DROP NOT NULL` (payment-driven loans have no fixed term; application layer enforces term-or-payment). Variable rates stay out of scope by design (a future rate-schedule table slots in additively). **Surfaces:** REST POST/PUT validate the (merged) row via the calculator and 400 with the human-readable reason; the `amortization` action accepts `paymentAmount`/`residualValue` and returns `monthlyAccrual`. Loans UI gains a Payment + Frequency + (lease-only) Residual/Buyout inputs (term placeholder "From payment" / payment placeholder "From term"), a 400-message surface in the dialog, a "· from account" badge on Remaining, frequency-labelled payment on cards, lease cards show "Term end" + residual, and the schedule view gains a **Monthly Interest** tab. MCP HTTP `add_loan`/`update_loan` accept `residual_value` + the 6-value frequency enum + optional `term_months` (refusing non-amortizing inputs with the validation message), `get_loan_amortization` (HTTP + stdio) honors stored `payment_amount`/`residual_value` and returns `monthlyAccrual`, `get_debt_payoff_plan` (HTTP + stdio) builds schedules payment-aware and excludes non-amortizing rows per-row instead of throwing. Deprecated `get_loans` SELECTs the new columns. **Deferred (tracked on FINLYNQ-136):** Phase 4 ledger posting (principal/interest split into `transactions`, auto-generate vs import-and-reconcile modes, balance reconciliation surface) — it carries the unresolved posting-trigger/dedup/categorization decisions; page stays behind `DevModeGuard`. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · vitest 43/43 (24 new calculator cases: payment-driven vs 30y reference, payment-below-interest + 100-year refusals, per-frequency period counts + per-period rate, accrual sums + straddle split, lease residual term-driven/payment-driven, month-end clamping; plus the 8 API tests fixed — they were failing at HEAD because the suite never mocked the `requireDevMode` gate). Files: `src/lib/loan-calculator.ts`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `scripts/migrations/20260619_loans_v2.sql`, `src/app/api/loans/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/loans/page.tsx`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`, `tests/loan-calculator.test.ts`, `tests/api/loans.test.ts`, `docs/loans.md` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(email-import): repair the FINLYNQ-138 retention sweep — `timestamptz < interval` runtime error (2026-06-10, dev).** The `email_inbox` retention purge ([cleanup.ts](src/lib/email-import/cleanup.ts)) threw at runtime on every startup-bootstrap + 30-min cron run: `operator does not exist: timestamp with time zone < interval`. **Root cause:** the predicate was written `received_at < ${now} - (window_days * INTERVAL '1 day')`, where `${now}` is a Drizzle bind param. Unlike the staged-imports / trash sweeps (which compare a known timestamptz column to the param, so the column anchors the param's type), here `now` sat inside a subtraction with no type anchor, so Postgres resolved `$N - interval` as `interval - interval` (→ `interval`) and the comparison became `timestamptz < interval` — no such operator. Because query #1 ran first and the throw propagated uncaught, **the staged_imports 14-day pending sweep and the 24-hour trash sweep never ran either** (collateral-blocked), so old raw emails / staged rows were never deleted — a privacy-retention regression. **Fix:** rewrote the predicate as `received_at + (window_days * INTERVAL '1 day') < ${now}::timestamptz` (the timestamptz column now types BOTH operands, matching the documented `received_at + window·INTERVAL` shape) and cast the `now` param `::timestamptz` explicitly. The statement is extracted into an exported pure `buildInboxRetentionPurge(now)` so its SQL shape is unit-testable without a DB. The staged_imports + trash sweeps were never themselves buggy (Drizzle `lt(column, now)` is type-safe) — fixing query #1 unblocks all three. Validated against the dev Postgres DB. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `next build` green · 5 new vitest cases ([tests/email-import/cleanup-sql.test.ts](tests/email-import/cleanup-sql.test.ts): now-cast-to-timestamptz, column-anchored interval-add, no bare-param compare / no param-subtraction, param order, junk-value COALESCE guard). Files: `src/lib/email-import/cleanup.ts`, `tests/email-import/cleanup-sql.test.ts`.

- **feat(admin): cross-user imported-email oversight screen (FINLYNQ-121, 2026-06-10, dev).** New operator screen at **`/admin/email-inbox`** + **`GET /api/admin/email-inbox`** to view inbound email-import rows (`email_inbox`) **across all users** — the cross-user counterpart to the per-user Email tab (`/import?tab=email`, Epic C1) and a sibling of `/admin/inbox` (incoming_emails) and `/admin/feedback`. Gated by `requireAdmin` + the managed-mode `getDialect()!=='postgres'` 403 guard (mirrors `/admin/feedback`; matches the surrounding admin-route convention — those routes hand-roll `requireAdmin` rather than using `apiHandler`, and there is no separate admin-access audit-log helper in this codebase). **⚠️ LOAD-BEARING ENCRYPTION CONSTRAINT — metadata-first:** `email_inbox.from_address`/`subject`/`body_text` are TWO-TIER encrypted; the screen ALWAYS surfaces metadata (`action`, `source_kind`, `parse_confidence`, `encryption_tier`, `received_at`, `message_id`, `matched_rule_id`, `recorded_transaction_id`, owning user) but shows a decrypted **from/subject preview ONLY for `service`-tier rows** (`sv1:`, operator-decryptable with `PF_STAGING_KEY`). **`user`-tier rows (`v1:`, owner DEK) stay REDACTED** — the admin has no DEK and CANNOT decrypt them (the same hard property as the feedback channel). The route's `adminPreview(tier, value)` helper ([src/app/api/admin/email-inbox/route.ts](src/app/api/admin/email-inbox/route.ts) lines 56–67) returns `null` for any non-`service` tier and try/catch-swallows a `decryptStaged` miss to **never leak ciphertext**; the row carries `redacted:true` so the UI renders a "🔒 user-encrypted" placeholder. `body_text` is never returned (no admin body-preview surface). **Filters:** `userId` / `action` (7-value lifecycle) / `sourceKind` (body|attachment) / `since` / `until` (date) + `limit`/`offset` pagination. **Per-user grouping:** a `byUser[]` pivot (computed over the action/source/date filters, independent of the `userId` row filter + pagination) with per-user `total` / `needs_review` backlog / `unparseable` count + `unparseableRate` — the operator's "what's stuck where" view. Bare-JSON response (admin screens read bare shapes). Nav link "Email Oversight" added under Admin Inbox. **No DB migration** (read-only oversight over existing `email_inbox`). Web only (mobile out of scope). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13. Files: `src/app/api/admin/email-inbox/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/admin/email-inbox/page.tsx`, `src/components/nav.tsx`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(email-import): user-configurable imported-email retention window (FINLYNQ-138, 2026-06-10, dev).** Each user can now choose how long Finlynq keeps the raw emails they forward to their import address before the cleanup sweep hard-deletes them. **Setting:** new `email_retention_days` key in the `settings` key/value table (**no migration**). Bounded `{7,30,60,90}` days; default `60` = unchanged legacy behavior for users who never set it. `GET`/`PUT /api/settings/email-retention` rejects out-of-range values AND any keep-forever sentinel with HTTP 400. **Sweep-time evaluation (NOT a re-stamp):** the cleanup sweep ([cleanup.ts](src/lib/email-import/cleanup.ts)) purges `email_inbox` via a single raw-SQL `DELETE … USING (users LEFT JOIN settings)` pass keyed on `received_at + window*INTERVAL`, reading the LIVE setting (`COALESCE` to 60d) — a settings change immediately governs all existing emails, and the stamped `expires_at` is never trusted as the source of truth. Sweep stays DEK-free. **Scope = raw email only:** `staged_imports`/`staged_transactions` keep their own SEPARATE fixed 14-day pending TTL, unchanged. Pure policy single-sourced in [src/lib/email-import/retention.ts](src/lib/email-import/retention.ts) (`parseRetentionDays` / `resolveRetentionDays` / `isInboxRowExpired` / `nextPurgeAt`). [store-email-inbox.ts](src/lib/email-import/store-email-inbox.ts) no longer claims the 60d stamp "matches the staged-import TTL" (it does not) and documents `expires_at` as advisory/display-only. **UI:** retention control in `/settings/import` → Email Import tab; the email inbox list shows a per-row "purges &lt;date&gt;" derived from the live window via `nextPurgeAt`. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 · 9 new vitest cases (`tests/email-retention.test.ts`). Files: `src/lib/email-import/{retention,cleanup,store-email-inbox}.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/email-retention/route.ts`, `src/app/api/import/email-inbox/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/inbox-email-tab.tsx`, `tests/email-retention.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(dashboard): rank Top Movers widget by absolute dollar value change, not percentage (FINLYNQ-127, 2026-06-10, dev).** The Top Gainers / Top Losers widget previously ranked holdings by `changePct` — surfacing a C$5/+5% holding above a C$500/+4% holding. Now ranks by `dayChangeDisplay` (per-holding day change in display currency): gainers sorted desc by `abs(dayChangeDisplay)`, losers sorted by most-negative first. Tie-break: equal `dayChangeDisplay` → symbol alphabetically ascending. Percentage is still displayed alongside the dollar change. 5 items per side unchanged. Comparator in `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` lines 797–802. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13.

- **feat(crypto): encrypt `bank_transactions.source_filenames` at rest (FINLYNQ-132, 2026-06-10, dev).** Closes the last plaintext PII leak on the bank ledger. `bank_transactions.source_filenames` (a `TEXT[]` of upload filenames) stored PLAINTEXT — filenames routinely carry bank name / account-number fragments / statement period, readable by a DB-dump-only attacker. FINLYNQ-120 (commit `da067c0`) explicitly left this column out of scope. **No DB migration** — each element is encrypted under the same per-row `encryption_tier` already on `bank_transactions` (`v1:` user-DEK via `encryptField`, `sv1:` `PF_STAGING_KEY` via `encryptStaged`), reusing the FINLYNQ-120 helper `encryptStagingMeta`/`decryptStagingMeta` ([src/lib/crypto/staging-metadata.ts](src/lib/crypto/staging-metadata.ts)). **Writer:** `upsertBankTransaction` ([src/lib/bank-ledger.ts](src/lib/bank-ledger.ts)) now encrypts the filename element before the `ARRAY[${…}]::TEXT[]` bind (keeping the bound-element pattern that dodges PG's malformed-array-literal 22P02); the `ON CONFLICT … array_append(EXCLUDED.source_filenames[1])` branch inherits the ciphertext automatically (it appends the already-encrypted INSERT value). **Readers (decrypt-before-surface, per-row tier branch, null-safe):** backup **export** ([data/export/route.ts](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts) `decryptBankLedgerRow`) decrypts each element to plaintext for cross-DEK portability (auth-tag failure / missing DEK drops the element + bumps the partial-export counter, never embeds `v1:`/`sv1:` ciphertext; service-tier decrypt wrapped in try/catch so one malformed element can't abort the export); backup **restore** ([data/import/route.ts](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts)) re-encrypts each plaintext element under the local user DEK (`isEncrypted` guards same-account double-wrap). No UI/MCP reader surfaces the column (the MCP `original_filename` reference is a writer-feeder, not a read). **Login sweep:** `upgradeBankLedgerEncryption` ([upgrade-staging-encryption.ts](src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts)) now also decrypts each service-tier filename under `PF_STAGING_KEY` and re-encrypts under the user DEK alongside payee/note/tags/accountName (legacy plaintext passes through `decryptStaged` unchanged). **New audit invariant #13 `source-filenames-encrypted`** (scoped to `bank-ledger.ts`) fails `npm run audit:invariants` if the `ARRAY[${…}]::TEXT[]` write-site loses its `encryptStagingMeta(` call — verified to bite (exit 1 naming the callsite) then reverted. `import_hash` (over plaintext payee) is untouched. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 13/13 (proved-bites). Files: `src/lib/bank-ledger.ts`, `src/app/api/data/export/route.ts`, `src/app/api/data/import/route.ts`, `src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts`, `scripts/audit-invariants.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `pf-app/docs/invariants.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(transactions): transfers no longer show "Balance Adjustment" category (FINLYNQ-131, 2026-06-10, dev).** Recording a transfer between two accounts surfaced its category as **"Balance Adjustment"** (or whatever the user's first `type='R'` category happened to be) instead of the canonical **"Transfer"**. **Root cause (WRITE path):** `resolveTransferCategoryId` in [src/lib/transfer.ts](src/lib/transfer.ts) — when no `type='R'` category matched the "Transfer" `name_lookup` HMAC, it fell back to `existing[0]`, the **lowest-id `type='R'` row**. The `20260509c_categories-type-T-to-R.sql` migration had retyped every legacy `type='T'` category to `type='R'`, so users can carry several unrelated `type='R'` rows (an imported "Balance Adjustment", "Non-Cash", broker reconciliation buckets); the fallback stamped the arbitrary first one onto every new transfer leg. The display layer was correct — it faithfully rendered whatever `category_id` the row carried — so this is purely a creation-path bug. **Fix:** both resolvers (`resolveTransferCategoryId` Drizzle/HTTP-web path + `resolveTransferCategoryIdViaSql` stdio path) now reuse a `type='R'` category **only when its `name_lookup` HMAC matches "Transfer"**, and **auto-create** the canonical "Transfer" category otherwise — never grabbing an unrelated `type='R'` row. The import-materialize path (`materializeBankRowAsTransfer`) delegates to `createTransferPair`, so it inherits the fix; all three surfaces (Transactions page, transaction detail dialog, MCP `search_transactions`) read the corrected `category_id`. Also removed a latent breakage in the stdio SQL variant: it SELECTed and INSERTed the plaintext `name` column that Stream D Phase 4 physically dropped. **Read-only / no migration:** existing transfer rows keep their current `category_id` untouched — only NEW transfers get the correct category. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green. Files: `src/lib/transfer.ts`, `CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(transactions): drill-through filters on summary values + URL-sync bug fix (FINLYNQ-130, 2026-06-10, dev).** Clicking a summary value now navigates to `/transactions` with the exact filters that produced it pre-applied. **Latent bug fixed first:** the transactions page ([src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx)) initialised filter state via `useState(urlParams.get(...))`, which only runs on mount — so a client-side drill-through *while already on `/transactions`* silently ignored the new params and kept stale filters. Replaced with a `useEffect([urlParams])` that re-syncs `filters` + the controlled `searchInput` and resets `page` to 0 on every navigation, so a drill **replaces** (not merges) the prior filters. **New single source of truth for the URL shape:** [src/lib/transactions/drill-url.ts](src/lib/transactions/drill-url.ts) — pure `buildTxDrillUrl(filters: Partial<TxFilters>)` appends only non-empty values to a `URLSearchParams` in caller insertion-order (ignores unrecognised keys), returns `/transactions` with no trailing `?` when empty. **Migrated** the 3 existing portfolio drill hrefs (holdings-table per-account + aggregate, holdings-by-account) to call it. **Surfaces wired:** dashboard **Monthly Income / Monthly Expenses** tiles (→ the displayed month's `startDate`/`endDate`, no category); budget category "spent" rows (→ `categoryId` + budget-month range, both traditional + envelope modes); **Spending Trends + Income Statement** category leaf rows in the Reports page (→ `categoryId` + report date range) — both render the same `trends` breakdown, so threaded `categoryId` through the `/api/reports/trends` breakdown (it already SELECTed `categories.id`; now carried through the per-name Map → client `BreakdownItem`, **category-mode only**; group-mode aggregates many categories so `categoryId` is null and no link renders). **Deliberately skipped** (noted on the DM item): **Sankey segments** (the shared `SankeyChart` SVG component has no per-node href/click API — wiring it is a component-API change that balloons scope) and **cash-flow forecast period totals** (projected future buckets from recurring transactions, not actual tx sums — no clean date-range→filter mapping). No new query params / schema changes (`TxFilters` already covers all 7). Web only. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · 6 new vitest cases ([tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts](tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts): empty → no `?`, both-params shape, empty/undefined skip, insertion order, URL-encoding, unrecognised-key guard). Files: `src/lib/transactions/drill-url.ts`, `src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/_components/{holdings-table,holdings-by-account}.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/budgets/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx`, `src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts`, `tests/lib/drill-url.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(charts): stacked top-10 member toggle on Net Worth / Income-vs-Expenses / Performance (FINLYNQ-129, 2026-06-09, dev).** Each of the three value-over-time charts gains a header toggle (next to the time-range buttons) that switches from the aggregate view (default, unchanged) to a **top-10 stacked-member view** — each member a `<Area stackId>` band, the tail past 10 collapsed into a signed **"Other"** residual so the **outer stack boundary equals the aggregate at every point**. Toggle is **component-state only** (resets on reload); a legend renders below the chart in stacked mode; all values via `formatCurrency`; distinct colours from [src/lib/chart-colors.ts](src/lib/chart-colors.ts) (`categories[]`, "Other" = the neutral slot). **New shared pure helper** [src/lib/chart-stack.ts](src/lib/chart-stack.ts) — `buildStackedSeries(points, { maxMembers })` ranks members by AVERAGE absolute contribution over the window (re-ranks on time-range change), emits Recharts rows keyed `m_<id>` + `__other` (each kept band seeded to 0 so a gap stays flat), and returns the ordered `legend` (key → name → colour). Builds ON FINLYNQ-128's `BreakdownMember` shape + `rankBreakdown`'s residual discipline — does NOT duplicate the breakdown computation. Shared legend [src/components/chart-stack-legend.tsx](src/components/chart-stack-legend.tsx). **Net Worth (`/dashboard`, "By account"):** reuses the per-account `breakdown[]` from FINLYNQ-128 — the route now also passes the FULL per-account `members[]` (with stable account ids) so the client stacks/re-ranks; built from the SAME downsampled points the aggregate plots (no X-grid shift on toggle). **Income vs Expenses (`/dashboard`, "By category"):** splits the dual-line chart into TWO vertically-stacked area charts (income / expenses), each from the FINLYNQ-128 per-(month,type) category breakdown. **Performance (`/portfolio`, "By holding (value)"):** stacks per-holding **market value $** (NOT per-holding TWRR — returns are non-additive) with the y-axis/label flipped to dollars. **Per-holding series — the part FINLYNQ-128 deferred:** `portfolio_snapshots` is per-ACCOUNT grain, so a per-holding daily series needs prices+FX per holding per day. Rather than a new pipeline, **extracted the existing per-holding pricing loop** out of `getHoldingsValueByAccount` into a shared `valueHoldingsAtDate` core + a new `getHoldingsValueByHolding(userId, dek, { asOfDate, accountId })` ([src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), `getHoldingsValueByAccount` now sums the same core — behaviour-identical), fed by a new **lazily-fetched** companion endpoint `GET /api/portfolio/performance/holdings` ([route](src/app/api/portfolio/performance/holdings/route.ts)) that samples the snapshot grid to ≤40 evenly-spaced days (always incl. today) and values each holding per day (`price_cache`/`fx_rates` amortize repeat days); fetched ONLY when the user toggles stacked mode, so the aggregate chart keeps using the cheap snapshot route. Σ(holdings) per day ties to the portfolio market value (stacked outer edge = aggregate). **Toggling back to aggregate restores the exact original view** (the aggregate path's markup is untouched; the stacked branch is additive). Web only (pf-mobile untouched). NOTE: the Income/Expenses chart on `/dashboard` is dev-mode-gated (pre-existing) — enable dev mode to see tc-3. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green · 8 new vitest cases for `buildStackedSeries` ([tests/chart-stack.test.ts](tests/chart-stack.test.ts): one-row-per-point, aggregate preserved with/without Other, signed-residual tie-out, average-contribution ranking, gap → flat 0 band, custom dateKey/otherLabel, id-keyed distinct same-name bands, empty). Files: `src/lib/chart-stack.ts`, `src/components/chart-stack-legend.tsx`, `src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/performance/holdings/route.ts`, `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/PerformanceChart.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/income-expense-chart.tsx`, `tests/chart-stack.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(charts): top-10 member breakdown in chart tooltips on hover (FINLYNQ-128, 2026-06-09, dev).** Hovering the **Net Worth Over Time** (`/dashboard`, per **account**) and **Income vs Expenses** (`/dashboard`, per **category**) charts now extends the existing tooltip with a ranked list of up to 10 contributors to that point's value, plus a single **"Other"** residual row when there are >10 members. Existing tooltip content (date / totals) is unchanged. **New shared, pure breakdown data layer** [src/lib/chart-breakdown.ts](src/lib/chart-breakdown.ts) — `rankBreakdown(members, { maxMembers })` ranks members desc by absolute contribution, collapses the tail past `maxMembers` into a signed "Other" residual (so top-N + Other re-sums to the grand total exactly), and drops zero-value members. **FINLYNQ-129 (stacked-member toggle) consumes the SAME breakdown payloads.** **Net Worth**: the pure `buildNetWorthHistory` core ([src/lib/net-worth-history.ts](src/lib/net-worth-history.ts)) now emits a per-account `breakdown[]` on every grid point (cash + investment passes merged; still name-free — no DEK in the core); `/api/net-worth-history` decrypts account names (`decryptName` + `safeAccountName` fallback) and ranks each point. The per-account chart hides the (redundant single-member) breakdown. **Income/Expenses**: new query `getIncomeExpenseByCategory` ([src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)) — a per-(month, type, category) variant of `getIncomeVsExpenses` with the same currency/reporting slicing; `/api/dashboard` converts each slice via `convertReportingSlice`, decrypts category names, and ranks per (month, type) into `incomeExpenseBreakdown`. **Shared tooltip UI** [src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx](src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx) — `TooltipBreakdownList` renders the ranked rows with `formatCurrency`, **height-capped + scrollable** (`max-h-40 overflow-y-auto`) and name-truncated so it never overflows a small viewport. The breakdown rides through `prepareTimeSeries` downsampling untouched (it keeps whole rows). **Performance per-holding (the third in-scope chart) is DEFERRED**: `portfolio_snapshots` has per-account grain only (no per-holding row), so a per-holding market-value-over-time series requires a substantial new endpoint (recomputing each holding's value at each historical date with prices + FX). Per the item's "phased follow-up may be needed" note, the Performance tooltip is left unchanged and the per-holding breakdown is a tracked follow-up. Web only (pf-mobile untouched); the breakdown is pre-fetched alongside the main chart payload (no per-hover fetch). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green · 7 new vitest cases for `rankBreakdown` ([tests/chart-breakdown.test.ts](tests/chart-breakdown.test.ts): sort, no-Other-at-≤10, tail-collapse, signed residual, zero-drop, custom label, empty) + the existing `buildNetWorthHistory` suite updated to assert the new `breakdown` field. Files: `src/lib/chart-breakdown.ts`, `src/components/chart-breakdown-list.tsx`, `src/lib/net-worth-history.ts`, `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/{chart-tooltip,income-expense-chart,types}.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `tests/{chart-breakdown,net-worth-history}.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **feat(rules): offer "Also create a rule" when categorizing a tx from reconcile/import (FINLYNQ-125, 2026-06-09, dev).** When a user materializes a bank row into the ledger from the account-anchored `/import` inbox lenses, they pick a category by hand — and the same payee lands uncategorized again on the next statement. The shared `TransactionDialog` ([transaction-dialog.tsx](src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx)) now offers an **opt-in** affordance: a "Also create a rule for next time" checkbox + a live `Payee contains "X" → <category>` helper line + a **"Customize…"** link. On save the tx + splits + reconcile link (`onSaved`) **always complete FIRST**, then (best-effort) the checkbox path POSTs a `payee contains "<payee>" → set category <chosen>` rule to the existing `POST /api/rules` (server Zod-validates + `verifyOwnership` + DEK-encrypts; client sends plaintext), and the "Customize…" path closes the main dialog and opens the existing `RuleEditorDialog` seeded with the same payee + category so power users can refine before creating. A rule POST failure NEVER unwinds the saved tx — it surfaces a soft amber `ruleNotice` and keeps the dialog open. Future-imports / re-apply only — no retroactive sweep (mirrors the `unresolved-categories-banner.tsx` create-rule-from-import precedent). **New pure helper** [src/lib/rules/build-payee-category-rule.ts](src/lib/rules/build-payee-category-rule.ts) — `buildPayeeCategoryRule(payee, categoryId)` builds the POST payload (name `Match "<payee.slice(0,100)>"` under the 120-char cap; condition value = full trimmed payee) via the **FINLYNQ-114 typed factory maps** in [rules/schema.ts](src/lib/rules/schema.ts) (`CONDITION_DEFAULTS.payee()` / `defaultActionForKind("set_category", …)`), never a hand-rolled `as unknown as` cast. **Gating:** the affordance renders ONLY when `offerRuleSuggestion && dialogMode==="transaction" && !editId && payee.trim() && categorySelected` — never in edit mode, transfer mode, or the generic `/transactions` dialog (new `offerRuleSuggestion?: boolean` prop, default off; wired `true` only from the 3 reconcile/import inbox tabs). The `RuleEditorDialog` is rendered as a **SIBLING** of the main `<Dialog>` (not nested) so it opens as the main one closes — nesting two base-ui dialogs triggers stacking bugs. Web only (pf-mobile untouched); zero new data fetching (the inbox tabs already pass `accounts`/`categories`/`holdings`). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green · 3 new vitest cases ([tests/lib/build-payee-category-rule.test.ts](tests/lib/build-payee-category-rule.test.ts): normal payee, trim, 300-char payee → name ≤120 + full-payee condition value + `Rule.safeParse(payload).success`). Files: `src/lib/rules/build-payee-category-rule.ts`, `src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx`, `src/components/inbox/{inbox-to-categorize-tab,inbox-to-approve-tab,inbox-reconcile-tab}.tsx`, `tests/lib/build-payee-category-rule.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **fix(import): reframe the /import Staging-tab balance banner into a bank-ledger staging calc, not a system-ledger duplicate of Reconcile (FINLYNQ-124, 2026-06-09, dev).** The Staging-tab banner (`ReconciliationCallout`) showed **"Statement says / Finlynq has now / After approval"** — a **system-ledger** projection (`SUM(transactions.amount)` / holdings value, computed server-side in [staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts)). In the detailed/manual flow "Send to bank ledger" writes ONLY to `bank_transactions`, **never** `transactions`, so projecting staged rows onto the system-ledger balance was meaningless, the third column went dead after send ("After approval" == "Finlynq has now"), and the whole thing duplicated the Reconcile tab's `BalanceSummaryCard`. **Reframed** to a true bank-ledger calc: **Statement says X · Bank ledger has Y · After sending N rows Z** — Y = the left pane's running total (latest-dated `dbRows.runningBalance`), Z = Y + the sendable delta over the right-pane checkboxes (3rd column renders **only when N ≥ 1**, so it stops being a dead duplicate once everything is sent), match (✓/Δ) compares the resulting bank-ledger balance to the statement. **New pure helper** [src/lib/import/bank-ledger-projection.ts](src/lib/import/bank-ledger-projection.ts) — `latestBankLedgerBalance(dbRows)` + `sendableDelta(rows, selected)` (`{count, delta}` over the SAME eligibility filter the old `liveProjection` + server `pendingDelta` used: `selected && dedupStatus!=='existing' && reconcileState∉{'skipped_duplicate','linked'}`). **TYPE-ONLY** import of `DbTransactionRow` keeps `@/db` out of the client bundle (a value import fails `next build` even though tsc passes). `ReconciliationCallout` swaps `currentBalance`/`projectedBalance` props for `bankLedgerBalance`+`sendCount`+`sendDelta`; [staged-review-surface.tsx](src/components/import/staged-review-surface.tsx) replaces the `liveProjection` memo with a `bankLedgerCalc` memo over `dbRows`/`detail.rows`/`selected`. **Server cleanup (free perf win):** dropped the now-dead `currentBalance`/`projectedBalance`/`pendingDelta` math (and the `getHoldingsValueByAccount` import, the `SUM(transactions)` query + `sql` import, and the `getRate` FX call) from the GET route — it now only resolves `boundAccountCurrency`; `StagedDetail.reconciliation` type trimmed to match ([_types.ts](src/app/(app)/import/pending/_types.ts)). The Reconcile tab's `BalanceSummaryCard` is untouched and stays independent. Display-only, never blocks send; same-currency assumption (cross-currency FX out of scope). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green · 11 new vitest cases ([tests/import/bank-ledger-projection.test.ts](tests/import/bank-ledger-projection.test.ts): latest-date pick across out-of-order rows, all-null runningBalance → null, eligibility filter, empty selection → {0,0}). Files: `src/lib/import/bank-ledger-projection.ts`, `src/components/staging/reconciliation-callout.tsx`, `src/components/import/staged-review-surface.tsx`, `src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/import/pending/_types.ts`, `tests/import/bank-ledger-projection.test.ts`.

- **fix(fx): report the same display-currency numbers on Weekly Recap / Spotlight / Insights / Forecast (FINLYNQ-123, 2026-06-09, dev).** Four web surfaces still summed raw native `amount` across mixed currencies with NO FX conversion (the same Phase-3 bug as the dashboard tiles, on the rest of the app), so a 100,000 CAD income showed **C$100,000** instead of **~$71,750** for a USD-display user, and a C$600 account read as "low balance" against a $500 USD bar. Fixed per the flow-vs-point-in-time split, reusing the Phase-3 single source of truth. **New shared helper `convertReportingSlice(row, displayCurrency, rateMap)`** ([src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts](src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts)) — extracts the dashboard route's inline `convertGroup` (now delegates to it): prefers the stored historical `reporting_amount` when `reporting_currency == displayCurrency`, else falls back to an on-the-fly current-rate `convertWithRateMap`. **(1) Weekly Recap (FLOW)** [weekly-recap.ts](src/lib/weekly-recap.ts) — spending / income / net-cash-flow / net-worth-change / budget "spent" now aggregate per `(currency, reporting_currency)` slice and convert via `reporting_amount`; notable-transaction amounts convert per-row (over-fetch 50 → sort by converted magnitude → top 5); subscription cost converts at the **current** rate (point-in-time). **(2) Spotlight low balances (POINT-IN-TIME)** [spotlight.ts](src/lib/spotlight.ts) `getLowBalances` — converts each account balance at the current rate (account currency added to the query + group) before the $500/$100 threshold check. **(3) Spending Insights (FLOW)** `getMonthlySpending` ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)) now returns per-`(month, category, currency, reporting_currency)` slices with both `SUM(amount)` and `SUM(reporting_amount)`; [insights/route.ts](src/app/api/insights/route.ts) converts each slice and collapses back per `(month, category)` before anomaly/trend detection — so FX swings between months no longer read as spending spikes. **(4) Cash-flow Forecast (POINT-IN-TIME)** [forecast/route.ts](src/app/api/forecast/route.ts) — converts each bank account's balance at the current rate before summing the forecast starting balance. **MCP Part B explicitly out of scope** (the four MCP flow handlers already convert correctly at the current rate; the historical-rate alignment is a descoped nice-to-have per the 2026-06-06 Owner comment) — `mcp-server/**` untouched. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green. Tests: `tests/api/{recap,spotlight,insights,forecast}.test.ts` all pass (12/12) — fixed 5 pre-existing stale failures (recap 3-arg call signature; insights dev-gate + FX mocks; forecast fx-service mock) and added a focused FX convert+collapse case to `insights.test.ts`. Files: `src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts`, `src/lib/weekly-recap.ts`, `src/lib/spotlight.ts`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/app/api/{dashboard,insights,forecast}/route.ts`, `tests/api/{recap,insights,forecast}.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md` + `pf-app/plan/invariants.md`.

- **feat(charts): unified chart-series util — tighten Y-axis + downsample long ranges (FINLYNQ-122, 2026-06-09, dev).** Value-over-time line/area charts anchored the Y-axis at $0 (a ~$208k portfolio line squashed into the top sliver) and rendered every daily point for 1Y/ALL ranges. Fixed through ONE shared, pure, unit-tested utility. **New `src/lib/chart-series.ts`** — `prepareTimeSeries<T>({ dateKey, valueKeys, maxPoints?, domainPadPct?, clampZeroFloor? })` returns `{ data, domain, spansZero, granularity }`. Tightens the domain to `[min − pad, max + pad]` instead of anchoring at 0; `clampZeroFloor` (default true) prevents a negative floor for all-positive series. Downsamples at >200 points by stepping daily → weekly (Monday-anchored ISO week) → monthly by span, keeping the **last observation per bucket** (end-of-period balance, never sum/average), force-keeping first + last row as endpoint anchors (live-hero invariant). `n ≤ 200` returns data verbatim so 1M/3M/6M stay pixel-identical. **Wired into 3 charts:** `PerformanceChart.tsx` (excludes `contribution` from domain; gates `<ReferenceLine y={0}>` behind `spansZero`), `net-worth-history-chart.tsx` (also covers per-account Balance Over Time; `useMemo`), `benchmark-chart.tsx` (`clampZeroFloor:false` — % returns go negative; domain-tighten is the win). **Bar/pie/Sankey/sparkline/projection charts are intentionally out of scope** — bar chart length encodes magnitude; anchoring at 0 is correct there. **42 vitest cases** (`tests/chart-series.test.ts`) covering all spec cases: passthrough, cap ≤202, granularity thresholds, last-in-bucket, endpoint preservation / live-hero invariant, no fabricated values, sorted output, domain padding, un-anchored, all-equal band, negatives/spansZero, clampZeroFloor true vs false, multi-series union, sparse nulls → no NaN, empty, single point. `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` passing. Accepting `maxPoints + 2` endpoint tolerance (the two extra are the load-bearing first/last anchors).

- **feat(staging): encrypt plaintext staging metadata — `staged_imports.{sample_rows,original_filename,subject,from_address}` + `bank_upload_batches.filename` (FINLYNQ-120, 2026-06-09, dev).** Several staging-surface columns held PLAINTEXT PII / bank identity readable by a DB-dump-only attacker: `sample_rows` (JSONB — raw statement rows, highest sensitivity), `original_filename` / `subject` / `from_address` (leak bank + account identity), and `bank_upload_batches.filename` (same leak, PERMANENT row). All are now encrypted in-place mirroring the PROVEN two-tier `staged_transactions` model: **service-tier** (`sv1:` under `PF_STAGING_KEY`, the email webhook with no DEK) upgraded to **user-tier** (`v1:` under the user's DEK) by the login sweep, with a new per-table `encryption_tier` column (CHECK `('service','user')`) that read paths branch on per-row. **New helper** [src/lib/crypto/staging-metadata.ts](src/lib/crypto/staging-metadata.ts) (`encryptStagingMeta`/`decryptStagingMeta` scalars + `encryptSampleRows`/`decryptSampleRows` JSONB-as-JSON-string-envelope; legacy plaintext rows pass through) delegating to the existing `encryptField`/`tryDecryptField` + `encryptStaged`/`decryptStaged` primitives — no new crypto. `staged_imports.headers` stays plaintext (column-names only). **Writers:** email path ([stage-email-import.ts](src/lib/email-import/stage-email-import.ts)) → service-tier; web upload ([staging/upload/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts)), simplified upload ([simplified-upload.ts](src/lib/import/simplified-upload.ts)), detailed approve ([staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts)) → user-tier. **Readers (decrypt-before-read):** staged list ([staged/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/route.ts), full-list branch upgraded to `requireEncryption`; count-only stays `requireAuth`), staged detail ([staged/[id]/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts) — feeds the unbound-template picker, so no client change), recent-uploads list ([uploads/route.ts](src/app/api/import/uploads/route.ts), upgraded to `requireEncryption`), backup export/restore ([data/export](src/app/api/data/export/route.ts) decrypts for portability, [data/import](src/app/api/data/import/route.ts) re-encrypts under the new DEK), and the MCP HTTP tools `list_staged_imports`/`get_staged_import`/`approve_staged_rows`/`reject_staged_import`. **Login sweep** [upgradeStagingEncryption](src/lib/email-import/upgrade-staging-encryption.ts) gained two passes converting existing plaintext/service rows to user-tier on next login (per-row try/catch + optimistic `WHERE encryption_tier='service'`). **New audit invariant #12 `staging-metadata-encrypted`** refuses any `staged_imports`/`bank_upload_batches` insert under `src/`+`mcp-server/` whose file lacks the encrypt helper (guard verified to fire then revert). Migration **`20260609_staging_metadata_encryption.sql`** (additive: ADD COLUMN `encryption_tier` + CHECK + index on both tables; NON-destructive — legacy plaintext is converted by the login sweep, not the migration). **Out of scope:** `bank_transactions.source_filenames` (TEXT[], plaintext — shipped as the FINLYNQ-132 follow-up), MCP on-disk uploads (TTL-cleaned), mobile. `tsc` clean · `audit:invariants` 12/12 · `next build` green.

- **refactor(codebase): DRY audit — extract shared hooks + utilities, remove ~1100 lines of duplication (2026-06-08, dev).** Full audit of copy-paste patterns across the codebase. **Shared React hooks (new):** [`useEditId`](src/lib/hooks/useEditId.ts) (URL `?editId` parsing, replaces 10+ inline copies); [`usePortfolioFormData`](src/lib/hooks/usePortfolioFormData.ts) (parallel accounts+holdings+categories fetch + edit-load useEffect, FINLYNQ-118 bespoke fetch/useState preserved — no SWR); [`useAccountHoldingSelection`](src/lib/hooks/useAccountHoldingSelection.ts) (investmentAccounts / selectedAccount / accountHoldings memos). All 8 portfolio operation forms (`BuyForm`, `SellForm`, `DepositForm`, `WithdrawalForm`, `SwapForm`, `TransferForm`, `IncomeExpenseForm`, `FxConversionForm`) migrated to these hooks. **Shared utilities (new/promoted):** [`todayISO()`](src/lib/utils/date.ts) (single export replacing 10+ inline `new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0]` copies); [`normalizeDbRows<T>()`](src/lib/db-utils.ts) (normalizes `pg` `{rows:[]}` vs raw array — replaces 3 independent implementations in `register-tools-pg.ts`, `investment-income-category.ts`, `dividends-category.ts`); `round2` promoted to `export const` in [`currency-conversion.ts`](src/lib/currency-conversion.ts) (replaces inline `Math.round(n*100)/100` in `fx-service.ts`); [`StringOp`](src/lib/schemas/rule-primitives.ts) shared Zod enum extracted from `rules/schema.ts` + `email-rules/schema.ts` (note: `AmountOp` intentionally NOT shared — email rules exclude `"eq"`). `tsc` clean · `lint` 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · `next build` green. All 8 portfolio forms + Rules page + Portfolio page + dividend income recording smoke-tested on dev with `demo@finlynq.com`. **CLAUDE.md updated** with conventions for all shared hooks/utilities and a DRY architecture principle. Commit: `fe51065`.

- **fix(email-import): record into the ACCOUNT currency (+ per-rule override) and add strict ledger-duplicate detection (2026-06-18, dev).** Two bugs on the email-to-transaction path. **(1) Currency:** `recordEmailInboxRow` ([process-pending-inbox.ts](src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts)) used the body-parsed currency (a bare `$` ⇒ USD), so an alert recorded into a CAD account stored **USD** — the `$4,057.73` vs `C$…` split users saw in the bank ledger. The recorded currency now defaults to the **target account's currency**, with an optional **rule-level override** (new nullable `email_import_rules.currency` column, [20260618_email_rule_currency.sql](scripts/migrations/20260618_email_rule_currency.sql); NULL ⇒ account currency; plaintext like `flip_sign`/`date_source`). Surfaced as a **Currency** picker in the email-rule dialog ([email-rule-dialog.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx), default "Account currency (XXX)") + threaded through `/api/email-rules` POST/PUT/GET + `load.ts` (`ActiveEmailRule.currency`) + the manual `/api/import/email-inbox/[id]` record PATCH. **(2) Dedup:** the record path only did exact-hash dedup (`date|account|amount|payee` vs `bank_transactions`), so the SAME alert delivered on two days (06-05 vs 06-06), or an alert vs a manually-entered "Payroll Deposit", produced different hashes and imported as duplicates. Added the **strict ledger-duplicate** check the reconcile engine already uses (amount ±$0.01 within a few days on the same account) as a second pass — pure, unit-tested [dedup.ts](src/lib/email-import/dedup.ts) (`findStrictLedgerDuplicate`, integer-cent compare, `EMAIL_DEDUP_DATE_TOLERANCE_DAYS=4`). On a strict hit the auto-sweep does **NOT** auto-create — it holds the row at `needs_review` (new `possibleDuplicates` counter); the Email tab shows an amber "Possible duplicate of #X — Record anyway" branch (re-PATCH with `force:true`, which bypasses only the fuzzy check, never the exact-hash one). `tsc` clean · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · `next build` green · 11 new vitest cases (`tests/email-dedup.test.ts`). Files: `src/lib/email-import/{process-pending-inbox,dedup}.ts`, `src/lib/email-rules/load.ts`, `src/app/api/email-rules/{route,[id]/route}.ts`, `src/app/api/import/email-inbox/[id]/route.ts`, `src/components/inbox/{email-rule-dialog,email-rules-manager,inbox-email-tab}.tsx`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `scripts/migrations/20260618_email_rule_currency.sql`.

- **chore(release): promote the 2026-06-04 import field-mapping + ledger-duplicate cohort to prod (2026-06-06).** PR [#282](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/282) merged `dev → main` (merge commit `68f56a8`); Deploy Production green. Promotes the 8-commit import/reconcile cohort documented below as "(2026-06-04, dev)" — now **dev + prod**: transfer rules in Auto-pilot/Approve-each, the per-account OFX payee-source (NAME/MEMO) + CSV mapping confirmation, the OFX/QFX preview-confirm dialog + prominent auto-vs-ask control, the "apply automatically" reset path, and ledger-duplicate flagging. Migration **`20260604_import_field_mapping.sql`** applied on `pf` by `deploy.sh` (additive/idempotent — `accounts.ofx_payee_source` + `accounts.csv_mapping_mode`). **Product behavior change now live for real users:** `csv_mapping_mode` defaults to `'confirm'`, so the first CSV upload to each existing account shows the column-mapping confirm dialog once (reset to silent via the account page → Import preferences, or Settings → Import). **Prod-verified** (finlynq.com demo): all accounts carry the new columns with valid defaults, `PATCH /api/accounts/[id]/import-prefs` → 200, `GET /api/settings/confirm-csv-mapping` → 200. The standalone `confirm_csv_mapping` per-user default lives in the schemaless `settings` table (no migration).
