- **feat(email-import): multi-condition email rules + body/payee/amount matching (2026-06-17, dev).** Email rules go from ONE match field (sender|subject) to an **AND group of conditions** over five fields: text **sender / subject / body / payee** (contains/exact/regex) + numeric **amount** (gt/lt/between). `body` matches the decrypted email body (text, or HTML stripped via the now-exported `htmlToText`); `payee`/`amount` match the parser's extracted candidate. **Widen-to-JSONB** (NOT a FINLYNQ-84 converge): new `email_import_rules.conditions JSONB` ([20260617_email_rule_conditions.sql](scripts/migrations/20260617_email_rule_conditions.sql) — adds the column, backfills existing flat rules into a 1-element `{all:[…]}` group with the encrypted `match_value` moved verbatim, drops the flat-tri `NOT NULL`); `conditions` is canonical, the flat `match_type/op/value` columns are a frozen read fallback (a `body`/`amount` condition can't fit the flat `match_type` CHECK, so writes always normalize to `conditions`). New [src/lib/email-rules/schema.ts](src/lib/email-rules/schema.ts) (`EmailCondition` z.union + `EmailConditionGroup` + `STRING_FIELDS` + `defaultEmailConditionForField`); [crypto.ts](src/lib/email-rules/crypto.ts) gains a conditions walker mirroring `rules/crypto.ts` `mapRuleStrings`/`asJsonArray` — encrypts text-field string values, leaves numeric amount plaintext (email rules are NOT in the login encryption sweep — encryption-at-write only, documented). Matcher [load.ts](src/lib/email-rules/load.ts) evaluates ALL conditions (AND; empty → no match), **amount compares `|amount|`** (parser signs are heuristic; users reason in magnitudes; `between` tolerates swapped bounds), and **caps the body haystack to 20KB before any regex** (ReDoS guard — body is attacker-controlled). The sweep ([process-pending-inbox.ts](src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts)) decodes the body + loads the staged candidate (amount/payee) to build the full match context; `recordEmailInboxRow` (single materialize path) is unchanged. API (`/api/email-rules` POST/GET + `[id]` PUT) accepts `conditions` (legacy flat tri still accepted as an input shim → normalized), encrypts string values, returns a `conditions` array. The "Create rule from this email" dialog ([email-rule-dialog.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx)) + the settings manager get a repeatable conditions editor (text + amount rows, field-change swaps to a typed default). `tsc`/`lint`(0 err)/`audit:invariants`(11/11)/`build` clean; 51 vitest cases (rewritten `tests/email-rules-match.test.ts` + new `tests/email-rules-crypto.test.ts`). Back-compat: pre-migration single-condition rules match exactly as before via the flat fallback.

- **fix(dashboard): FX-convert the income/expense + spending tiles to the display currency (currency rework Phase 3 follow-up, 2026-06-06, dev).** The Dashboard's **Monthly Income / Monthly Expenses** tiles, the income-vs-expenses sparklines, and the **spending-by-category** breakdown summed raw account-currency `amount` with no FX conversion — so a 100,000 CAD salary showed as **$100,000** for a USD-display user (the same bug as the reports, on the landing page; the dashboard's *net worth* was already current-rate converted, so it was internally inconsistent). `getIncomeVsExpenses` now returns per-`(month, type, currency, reporting_currency)` slices, and a new `getSpendingByCategoryWithReporting` does the same per category ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)); the dashboard route ([dashboard/route.ts](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts)) converts each slice to the display currency — preferring the stored historical `reporting_amount`, falling back to on-the-fly current-rate — and re-aggregates to the **same shapes the client already consumes** (`{month,type,total}` / `{categoryId,categoryNameCt,…,total}`). `getSpendingByCategory` is **left raw** for its budget-rollover internal caller (unchanged semantics). Net-worth conversion is unchanged (point-in-time current value). Verified on dev: the demo's Monthly Income went **$100,000.00 → $71,750.00** (100k CAD × 0.7175). `tsc` clean · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · `next build` green · lint 0 errors. Files: `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts`.

- **feat(reports): backfill reporting amounts on Dashboard load + a global recompute progress widget (currency rework Phase 3 follow-up, 2026-06-06, dev).** Two visibility/UX additions on top of the Phase-3 reporting amount. **(1) Dashboard trigger:** the dashboard route ([dashboard/route.ts](src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts)) now fires the guarded, DEK-free `selfHealReportingAmounts` so an existing user's stored reporting amounts backfill on the post-login landing page (was: only the four flow-report routes). The dashboard doesn't read `reporting_amount` itself — this is purely a proactive warm-up so the data is historical by the time Reports opens. **(2) Global progress widget:** new [`<ReportingRecomputeIndicator>`](src/components/reporting-recompute-indicator.tsx) mounted in the `(app)` shell ([layout.tsx](src/app/(app)/layout.tsx)) — a bottom-right pill that **polls continuously** (5s idle / 1.5s while active) so it reliably catches a recompute kicked from ANY source (a dashboard/reports self-heal or a Settings currency switch), showing "Recalculating reports… (done/total)" then "Reports updated to <ccy>" briefly on completion (the status route now returns `finishedAt` so the done-state only flashes ~8s, never stale). The first widget version polled once on a delay and raced the job start — fixed by the continuous poll. Verified on dev: NULLing the demo's 365 reporting rows then loading the dashboard repopulated them (365/365 → USD) and the pill showed "Reports updated to USD". Files: `src/components/reporting-recompute-indicator.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/layout.tsx`, `src/app/api/dashboard/route.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/reporting-currency/status/route.ts`.

- **fix(reports): Trends & YoY now convert to the display currency, at each transaction's HISTORICAL rate, STORED not recomputed (currency rework Phase 3, 2026-06-06, dev).** A 100,000 CAD income on a CAD account showed as `$100,000.00` on the Reports → "Income vs Expenses" chart for a USD-display user, because `/api/reports/trends` + `/api/reports/yoy` did `SUM(transactions.amount)` (account-currency) with **no FX conversion** (income-statement/balance-sheet DID convert, but at *today's* rate). Reverses the Phase-2 "reporting currency is computed at view time and not stored" decision ([schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts)). **Storage:** three nullable columns on `transactions` — `reporting_currency` / `reporting_amount` / `reporting_rate` ([20260606_tx_reporting_amount.sql](scripts/migrations/20260606_tx_reporting_amount.sql)) — where `reporting_amount` = `amount` converted to the user's display currency at **this row's `date`** historical rate, locked at write time (mirrors the `entered_fx_rate` trilogy). **Helper:** [src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts](src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts) — `computeReportingFields` (best-effort, never throws, NULL on unresolved rate; triangulates through USD via `getRateToUsdDetailed` like `convertToAccountCurrency`) + `recomputeReportingAmounts` (stale-scoped: grouped by distinct `(currency,date)`, only touches rows whose `reporting_currency` ≠ target or is NULL — so a currency SWITCH reprocesses all, self-heal/backfill touches only new rows, steady state is a no-op) + `selfHealReportingAmounts` (fire-and-forget on report load). **Reads:** trends / yoy / income-statement income+expense / tax-summary now `SUM(reporting_amount)` per `(currency, reporting_currency)` slice, **falling back** to on-the-fly current-rate conversion of `amount` for rows not yet stored in the current display currency (reports stay correct during/before backfill). **Balance-sheet intentionally stays current-rate** (a balance sheet is a point-in-time current value, not a historical-cost sum). **Writes:** `createTransaction` + `updateTransaction` ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)) lock the reporting amount at write time (covers REST + MCP-HTTP); all other paths (portfolio ops, stdio, reconcile, import) are backfilled incrementally by the stale-scoped self-heal. **Currency switch:** the display-currency `PUT` ([display-currency/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/display-currency/route.ts)) fires a guarded background recompute into the new currency; the Settings → General picker shows a **confirm dialog** then a **progress banner** polling `GET /api/settings/reporting-currency/status`. **Future-dated rows:** the `settle-future-fx` cron gained `settleFutureReportingRates()` to re-lock the historical rate once the date arrives. **Backfill:** [scripts/backfill-tx-reporting-amount.ts](scripts/backfill-tx-reporting-amount.ts) `<userId|--all>` (no DEK needed). Status table `reporting_recompute_status` drives the toast. `tsc` clean · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · `next build` green. **Known limitation:** the historical lock is on the account→display leg; mobile reports get the fix free (same endpoints) but the confirm-dialog/recompute UI is web-only (server recompute + self-heal keep mobile correct). Files: `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/fx/reporting-amount.ts`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/lib/cron/settle-future-fx.ts`, `src/app/api/reports/{trends,yoy,route}.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/display-currency/route.ts`, `src/app/api/settings/reporting-currency/status/route.ts`, `src/app/(app)/settings/general/page.tsx`, `scripts/migrations/20260606_tx_reporting_amount.sql`, `scripts/backfill-tx-reporting-amount.ts`.

- **feat(email-import): in-page rule creation + field mapping/transforms on the Email tab (2026-06-16, dev).** The Email tab of `/import` now (1) shows a **"What we identified"** panel for body emails — the parsed amount (+ outflow/inflow, "sign assumed", card last-4), payee, and date (+ "from received date" / "ambiguous DD/MM"), plus a **why-this-needs-review** list — driven by parser **signals** the (pure) `parseEmailBody` now returns (`detectedAmounts`/`multipleAmounts`/`dateAmbiguous`/`usedFallbackDate`/`signExplicit`/`last4`; additive, existing callers untouched), re-run at read by `GET /api/import/email-inbox/[id]` (no stored column); and (2) a **"Create rule from this email"** dialog pre-filled from the sender + a live "will record" preview that, on save, **creates the rule AND records this email through the same mapping**. **Email rules widened** (NOT converged onto the FINLYNQ-84 engine) with three transform knobs — `flip_sign` (reverse amount sign, 0 stays +0), `date_source` (`parsed`|`received`), `payee_override` (rename, user-DEK encrypted like `name`/`match_value`) — migration [20260616_email_rule_transforms.sql](scripts/migrations/20260616_email_rule_transforms.sql) (additive; `parsed` default = today's behavior). Transforms applied by the **pure, unit-tested** [apply-transform.ts](src/lib/email-import/apply-transform.ts) `applyEmailTransform()` (precedence: per-email override > rule transform > raw candidate) inside the **single materialize path** `recordEmailInboxRow` — BEFORE `checkGuards`/account-bound `generateImportHash`/`materialize`, so the bank ledger + dedup key on what's actually recorded (no new raw `transactions` writer; audit invariants intact). The auto-sweep passes the matched rule's transforms; the Email-tab `record` PATCH also accepts **per-email manual amount/date/payee** corrections (sent only when changed, kept ephemeral — never written back to staging). Shared [email-rule-dialog.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rule-dialog.tsx) (manager + from-email modes) replaces the inline form in [email-rules-manager.tsx](src/components/inbox/email-rules-manager.tsx). `tsc`/`lint`(0 errors)/`audit:invariants`(11/11)/`build` clean; 42 vitest cases (`tests/email-apply-transform.test.ts` + extended `tests/email-parse-body.test.ts`). **Known tradeoffs (documented):** a raw record then a later transform-rule yields a different hash → not a dup (the ledger reflects recorded values); flipping into the wrong sign for the category surfaces a `sign_category_mismatch` 400 (the from-email dialog still creates the rule, with a distinct notice).

- **chore(email-import): move import addresses off the root domain → `mail.finlynq.com` subdomain (2026-06-05, dev + prod).** Import addresses are now `import-<hex>@mail.finlynq.com` (prod) / `importdev-<hex>@mail.finlynq.com` (dev) instead of `@finlynq.com`, so the root `finlynq.com` MX is free for normal mail (feedback@/privacy@/info@) instead of being catch-all'd into the DevManager Mailpit box — the originally-planned subdomain design (`EMAIL_IMPORT_PLAN.md` C3). **No functional code change** — the domain is env-driven (`IMPORT_EMAIL_DOMAIN`, read once in [email-config/route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-config/route.ts)), the router matches on local-part prefix only ([address-router.ts](src/lib/email-import/address-router.ts)), and the DB authorizes by exact full-address match — so the only repo change is **stale `@finlynq.com` copy made domain-aware**: [bounce.ts](src/lib/email-import/bounce.ts) now derives the bounce-body example from `IMPORT_EMAIL_DOMAIN` + `importLocalpartPrefix()` (was the hardcoded, doubly-stale `import-<8chars>@finlynq.com` — wrong domain AND wrong hex count; also not on the self-smtp hot path since `discard` doesn't bounce), plus the `@finlynq.com` doc-comment examples in [import-address.ts](src/lib/email-import/import-address.ts) + [address-router.ts](src/lib/email-import/address-router.ts). **Server-side (applied via the `ssh` MCP, both envs):** `IMPORT_EMAIL_DOMAIN=mail.finlynq.com` set + live on dev (`pf-dev.service.d/mailpit.conf`) and prod (new `pf.service.d/import-domain.conf`; provider stays `resend` — self-smtp without the prod secret would 500); all stale `import_email`/`email_webhook_secret`/`email_webhook_dek` settings rows DELETED on both `pf_dev` (4) and `pf` (3) so users re-provision a fresh `…@mail.finlynq.com`. **Validated app-side on dev** (signed HMAC push to a provisioned `importdev-<hex>@mail.finlynq.com` → `category:"import"` + staged `email_inbox` row; bogus → `discard`). **Still external/pending:** DevManager must switch its recipient domain `finlynq.com`→`mail.finlynq.com`, and the root `finlynq.com` MX revert is DNS; prod self-smtp cutover (§7) still owns provider + prod secret. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/email-import/{bounce,import-address,address-router}.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md` + `HANDOVER_2026-06-05_EMAIL_PUSH_RELAY.md` (§10), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(email-import): env-specific import-address prefix + 2xx-discard for unknown import recipients (2026-06-05, dev).** DevManager's Mailpit relay is one catch-all for all `@finlynq.com` import mail and routes by local-part prefix to the right env: `import-<hex>` → prod, `importdev-<hex>` → dev. pf-app now matches: a new env var **`IMPORT_EMAIL_LOCALPART_PREFIX`** (default `import-`; dev sets `importdev-`) drives BOTH the address generator ([email-config/route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-config/route.ts)) AND the router's pre-filter regex ([address-router.ts](src/lib/email-import/address-router.ts)), via the shared helper [import-address.ts](src/lib/email-import/import-address.ts) (`importLocalpartPrefix()` / `importAddressRegex()` — `^<prefix>[a-f0-9]{8,64}$`). The hex token is unchanged (still `randomBytes(16)` = 32 hex) and prod leaves the env unset so existing `import-<hex>` addresses stay valid. The username reservation ([username.ts](src/lib/auth/username.ts)) now also blocks the active env prefix. **New `discard` route category:** an import-shaped address that matches NO user in this env (expired/rotated token, or spam to a guessed address the relay forwarded) now returns **2xx and writes nothing** — no `incoming_emails` row, no bounce, no admin-notify — so the relay deletes the Mailpit copy instead of looping (was: `trash` + bounce + admin-notify). Idempotent (re-push of the same `message_id` discards again, no-op). Non-import junk still routes `mailbox`/`trash` as before (the legacy resend 3-way path). tsc 0 · lint 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · 38 email unit tests (4 new: prefix/regex matrix) · `next build` green. **Server/cutover:** dev sets `IMPORT_EMAIL_LOCALPART_PREFIX=importdev-` in the `pf-dev` drop-in; **prod self-smtp is NOT yet deployed** — it needs the `dev → main` promotion + a `pf.service` drop-in with `INBOUND_EMAIL_PROVIDER=self-smtp` + prod's OWN `FINLYNQ_INBOUND_SECRET` (≠ dev) + `IMPORT_EMAIL_LOCALPART_PREFIX` left unset.

- **refactor(email-import): `self-smtp` provider reworked from Mailpit pull → DevManager HMAC push relay (2026-06-05, dev).** Supersedes the basic-auth Mailpit-pull design in the entry below per the DevManager architecture decision: DevManager stays the filtering middleman (recipient allowlist, attachment hard-deny, size caps, rate limit, best-effort SPF) on its own box, then **pushes a self-contained `NormalizedInboundEmail` JSON** (attachments inline base64) to `POST /api/import/email-webhook`, **HMAC-signed**. pf-app now holds **zero Mailpit credentials / network access** — it only verifies the signature and stores; DevManager owns the Mailpit DELETE (on our 2xx) and all retries (its ~2-min reconciliation sweep re-pushes anything we didn't 2xx). **Provider ([self-smtp.ts](src/lib/email-import/providers/self-smtp.ts)):** `verifyAuth` is now HMAC-SHA256 over ``` `${X-Mail-Timestamp}.${rawBody}` ``` with `FINLYNQ_INBOUND_SECRET` (`sha256=<hex>` header, constant-time compare, ±5-min freshness enforced app-side, 401 on mismatch/stale, 500 on missing/short secret); `parsePayload` maps the push payload (prefers the matched `recipient` so cc'd addresses don't spuriously hit the mailbox/trash router; attachments inline → no fetch); `fetchContent`/`deleteReceived` are no-ops. **Interface ([types.ts](src/lib/email-import/providers/types.ts)):** dropped `listPending`. **Removed** the Mailpit poll-backstop cron ([~~poll-mailpit.ts~~] + its `instrumentation.ts` wiring) — the push model has no mail store for the app to poll; DevManager owns reconciliation. **Webhook ([route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-webhook/route.ts)):** JSON body cap raised 10 MB → 20 MB (base64-inlined attachments inflate ~33%, so DevManager's 10 MB message → ~13.4 MB JSON; a 413 would loop the retry). **Env change:** the app side needs ONLY `INBOUND_EMAIL_PROVIDER=self-smtp` + `FINLYNQ_INBOUND_SECRET` (shared, identical on the DevManager box, ≥16 chars) — all `MAILPIT_API_URL/USER/PASS` + `MAILPIT_WEBHOOK_USER/PASS` are dropped. **Coordination:** DevManager's recipient `<domain>` must equal the app's `IMPORT_EMAIL_DOMAIN` (default `finlynq.com`) so the forwarded `recipient` matches `settings.import_email`. Default provider stays `resend` (safe no-op) until the coordinated cutover. tsc 0 · lint 0 errors · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · 34 email unit tests (7 new: HMAC verifyAuth + push-payload mapping) · `next build` green. Contract + cutover recipe in `EMAIL_IMPORT_HANDOVER.md` (§4/§5).

- **feat(email-import): Mailpit-backed email-to-transaction inbox — Epics B + C + A4-app (2026-06-05, dev).** Replaces the offline Resend inbound path with a self-hosted [Mailpit](https://mail.finlynq.com) receiver the app owns end-to-end (receive → view → process → delete). **Schema (B2):** [20260615_email_inbox.sql](scripts/migrations/20260615_email_inbox.sql) — `email_inbox` (per-user inbound email, two-tier `sv1:`→`v1:` encrypted from/subject/body, `action` lifecycle, `source_kind`, `staged_import` linkage; in `deleteAllUserDataTx`) + `email_import_rules` (sender/subject → account auto-record rules; `name`+`match_value` user-DEK encrypted like `transaction_rules`). NOT in `user-encrypted-registry.ts` (those are two-tier columns; the service→user flip lives in the sweep, not the plaintext→`v1` registry sweep). **Provider abstraction (B1):** `InboundEmailProvider` in [providers/](src/lib/email-import/providers/) — `resend.ts` (relocated svix verify + parse + attachment fetch; `deleteReceived` no-op) and `self-smtp.ts` (Mailpit basic-auth verify, summary parse, REST fetch body+parts, DELETE, `listPending`); `getInboundProvider()` selects by `INBOUND_EMAIL_PROVIDER` (default `resend` → byte-identical legacy). **Body parser (B3):** pure [parse-body.ts](src/lib/email-import/parse-body.ts). **Webhook ingest (B4):** [email-webhook/route.ts](src/app/api/import/email-webhook/route.ts) refactored to the provider + shared [ingest.ts](src/lib/email-import/ingest.ts); body emails parse a transaction, ALWAYS write a per-user `email_inbox` row (`storeEmailInbox`, idempotent on per-recipient `dedupe_key`), then delete from the provider. **DEK-bearing sweep (B5):** [process-pending-inbox.ts](src/lib/email-import/process-pending-inbox.ts) tier-upgrades, matches the top-priority email rule, recomputes the account-bound `import_hash`, dedups vs `bank_transactions`, and (mode=`auto` + category + guards) promotes via `upsertBankTransaction` + materializes `transactions` + primary `transaction_bank_links` + `invalidateUser` → `auto_recorded`; shared `recordEmailInboxRow` backs both the sweep and the manual click; triggered at login / MFA-verify / try-demo. **UI (C1/C2):** always-present account-agnostic **Email tab** in `/import` (`?tab=email`, sandboxed `srcDoc` iframe, inline Record/Discard) + `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/import/email-inbox[/id]`; **email-rules CRUD** (`/api/email-rules[/id]` + manager under `/settings/import`). **Poll backstop (A4-app):** [poll-mailpit.ts](src/lib/cron/poll-mailpit.ts) 5-min cron ingests+deletes any message a dropped webhook missed (no-op unless `self-smtp`). New env: `INBOUND_EMAIL_PROVIDER`, `MAILPIT_API_URL/USER/PASS`, `MAILPIT_WEBHOOK_USER/PASS`. tsc + lint(0) + `audit:invariants` 11/11 + 27 new unit tests + `next build` all green. Server-side A4 (`MP_WEBHOOK_URL` on the Mailpit box + dev env) + end-to-end validation tracked in `EMAIL_IMPORT_HANDOVER.md`.

- **feat(import): imported staged rows stay visible (highlighted) instead of vanishing; loaded-batch click re-opens the staging review (2026-06-05, dev).** On the staging two-pane review, "Send to bank ledger" used to **delete** the materialized `staged_transactions` (they disappeared from the file/right pane). Now the approve route ([staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts)) flips them to **`row_status='approved'` and KEEPS them**; the file pane ([file-pane.tsx](src/components/import/reconcile/file-pane.tsx)) renders `row_status='approved'` rows with an emerald highlight + **"imported"** badge, a disabled checkbox, and excludes them from select-all. The review stays open after a send (`StagedReviewSurface` approve handler refreshes detail + bank ledger in place instead of `closeDetail`, re-seeding the selection minus imported rows; `useBankLedger` gained a `reload`). An import is marked `status='approved'` (leaves the pending list) only once **every** row is approved (`row_status != 'approved'` count = 0), but its rows persist so the review is re-openable. The **"Loaded into the bank ledger"** click now re-opens that same staging two-pane review for the batch's source import (via the new `stagedImportId` on the uploads list, sourced from `bank_upload_batches.staged_import_id`) — one view, no separate dialog/screen; simplified/auto batches (no staged import) surface a "use the Reconcile tab" toast. Re-sending is safe: imported rows are unselectable client-side and `upsertBankTransaction` is idempotent. **Note:** rows imported under the OLD (delete) behavior are already gone — only imports sent after this change persist. tsc clean · build green · `audit:invariants` 11/11 · lint 0.

- **feat(import): per-template "Flip sign of amounts" option (2026-06-04, dev).** Import templates / column mappings gain an optional **`flipSign`** boolean that multiplies every parsed transaction amount by **-1** on import — for banks that export expenses as positive (or income as negative) against Finlynq's "negative = outflow" convention. **No migration:** the flag rides in the existing `import_templates.column_mapping` JSON blob, so it round-trips through the template POST/PUT routes + `deserializeTemplate` untouched. Added `flipSign?: boolean` to the canonical `ColumnMapping` ([import-templates.ts](src/lib/import-templates.ts)) and the Excel `ColumnMapping` ([excel-parser.ts](src/lib/excel-parser.ts)); applied in the **two functions every mapping-driven import funnels through** — `csvToRawTransactionsWithMapping` ([csv-parser.ts](src/lib/csv-parser.ts): covers CSV template / auto-match / column-mapping-dialog / email auto-match) and `extractExcelRows` ([excel-parser.ts](src/lib/excel-parser.ts): Excel dialog + email). Applied to **`amount` only** — never `quantity`, never the Balance anchor (an absolute running balance), and `0` stays `+0` (guarded against `-0`). UI: a **"Flip sign of amounts"** checkbox in the Import-options panel of [column-mapping-dialog.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx) (with a live-updating sample preview, preserved across skip-row re-detect), [save-template-dialog.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/components/save-template-dialog.tsx), and [edit-template-dialog.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/components/edit-template-dialog.tsx). The canonical CSV path (`csvToRawTransactions`) and OFX/QFX are unaffected (no template/mapping). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · csv-parser/csv-pipeline vitest 58/58 (4 new flip cases) · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run lint` 0 errors on touched files. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/{csv-parser,excel-parser,import-templates}.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/{column-mapping,save-template,edit-template}-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/tests/csv-parser.test.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(import): flag ledger duplicates in Auto-pilot + Approve-each instead of minting them (2026-06-04, dev).** Import dedup ran against `bank_transactions` (import_hash) + staging + fitId, but **never the `transactions` ledger** — so a row matching a transaction already in the ledger with no bank lineage (manually entered, or one whose bank row was deleted) was classified `new` and, in Auto-pilot, **silently auto-materialized into a duplicate** (Approve-each showed it as a fresh card whose Approve *created a new tx* even though it had a "match tx #N" line). Now a new **strict** duplicate signal — `ReconcileBankSnapshot.duplicateOfTransactionId`, computed in `computeReconcileForAccount`: an existing UNLINKED ledger tx matching by **exact `import_hash`, OR identical amount (±$0.01) within `dateToleranceDays`**, per-row and independent (deliberately NOT the loose ±$50-floor, greedily-assigned `suggestions` layer — that over-flags and a new row can lose the tx to an older one) — flags these as **possible duplicates of an existing transaction** rather than creating a second entry. **Auto-pilot** ([match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts) `applyRulesToBankRows`): before materializing, it reads that strict signal for the batch's bank rows and **refuses to auto-create** for any hit — the row stays a bank row with `skipReason='possible_ledger_duplicate'` + `duplicateOfTransactionId`; new `possibleDuplicates` count returned + surfaced in the upload response's `autoRule` stats. **Approve-each + Auto-pilot cards** ([row-card.tsx](src/components/inbox/row-card.tsx), [inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx), [inbox-to-categorize-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-to-categorize-tab.tsx)): a flagged row shows an amber **"Possible duplicate of an existing transaction · payee · date · amount"** warning with **"Link to existing"** (POST `/api/reconcile/links` — links the bank row to the matched tx, no new entry) + **"Keep separate"** (the old approve/categorize path). The approve tab's broken `kind:"match"` suggestion (which created a duplicate on Approve) is replaced by this; **"Accept all suggested" now excludes possible duplicates** so the bulk path can't mint them. Manual mode was already safe (its two-pane shows these as suggestions to link). Match strictness follows the per-account reconcile thresholds (tunable at `/settings/reconciliation`); the scan is best-effort (a failure never blocks the upload). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/{row-card,inbox-to-approve-tab,inbox-to-categorize-tab}.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(import): give "apply automatically" a reset path (2026-06-04, dev).** Gap from the field-mapping work: once an account was set to **Apply automatically**, the upload preview never reappeared AND the two-layer logic makes a per-account `auto` override the global Settings toggle — so there was **no way to switch it back to "ask me first."** Two reset surfaces added (both PATCH the existing `/api/accounts/[id]/import-prefs`, no schema change): (1) a new **"Import preferences" card on the account page** ([/accounts/[id]](src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx), next to the Reconciliation-mode picker) via [ImportPrefsPicker](src/components/inbox/import-prefs-picker.tsx) — confirm-vs-auto + OFX Name/Memo radios that save immediately with a "Saved" badge; (2) an inline **"This account imports automatically · Switch to confirm-first"** notice in the upload drawer ([upload-drawer.tsx](src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx)) shown when the account is in `auto`, so the reset is right where the user notices the missing preview. The misleading "re-enable in Settings → Import" copy in both confirm dialogs now correctly points to the account page → Import preferences. `Account` type on the page + `getAccounts` already carry `csvMappingMode`/`ofxPayeeSource`. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/components/inbox/import-prefs-picker.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/{column-mapping-dialog,ofx-confirm-dialog}.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): OFX/QFX preview-confirm dialog + prominent auto-vs-ask control (2026-06-04, dev).** Follow-up to the import field-mapping work below, from user feedback: (1) the OFX/QFX choice was a bare Name/Memo radio on the upload card, but it should mirror the CSV flow — load the file into a **preview view** showing how rows will land in staging, with the field choice + a confirm step; (2) the per-account "apply automatically vs ask me first" control was a buried confirm-only checkbox that wasn't discoverable. **§A — OFX/QFX preview:** the upload route now gates bank/CC OFX/QFX the same way CSV is gated (the per-account `csv_mapping_mode` + per-user `confirm_csv_mapping` — **one setting governs both file types**): on a 'confirm' account it returns a 422 `ofx-confirm` with the parsed rows (raw NAME + MEMO + amount/date/type/fitId) + statement snapshot instead of staging. New [OfxConfirmDialog](src/app/(app)/import/components/ofx-confirm-dialog.tsx) (wide, `sm:max-w-4xl`) renders a read-only preview table (Date / Payee / Note / Type / Amount) with a single live **"Payee comes from: Name / Memo"** toggle that swaps payee↔note across all rows client-side (no re-upload per toggle), plus the statement balance/row-count. Confirming re-uploads with `confirmedImport=1` + the chosen `payeeSource` → stages (then the account's reconcile policy applies as usual). The old card radio was replaced with a one-line hint pointing at the preview. Investment statements are never gated (synthesized payees, no NAME/MEMO choice). **§B — prominent control:** the buried confirm-only "Don't ask again" checkbox in [ColumnMappingDialog](src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx) is now a clear **"On future uploads to this account: ○ Ask me to confirm first / ○ Apply this mapping automatically"** radio shown in BOTH the needs-mapping and confirm flows; the choice is a true per-account setting (the drawer PATCHes `csv_mapping_mode` to `confirm`/`auto` on every confirm, not just on opt-out). The OFX dialog carries the same radio. Both dialogs are **wider with more fields** — the CSV sample preview gained Note + Currency columns (Date/Account/Payee/Note/Currency/Amount, 5 rows). Settings → Import relabeled to "Confirm field mapping before importing" (covers CSV + OFX). No schema/migration change (reuses the two columns from the entry below). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/ofx-confirm-dialog.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): per-account OFX payee-source (NAME vs MEMO) + CSV mapping confirmation (2026-06-04, dev).** The upload pipeline silently picked which source field maps to which canonical column, with no way to see or veto it. Two fixes, both persisted **per account** on `accounts` (migration [20260604_import_field_mapping.sql](scripts/migrations/20260604_import_field_mapping.sql), idempotent additive columns + CHECK constraints). **§A — OFX/QFX payee source.** Bank/CC statements hardcoded `<NAME>`→payee, `<MEMO>`→note, but many banks bury the merchant in `<MEMO>` and put a generic type label in `<NAME>`. [ofx-parser.ts](src/lib/ofx-parser.ts) `parseStatement`/`parseOfx` now take a `payeeSource: "name"|"memo"` knob (default `"name"` = today's behavior; `"memo"` flips payee↔note); threaded through [parsers/ofx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/ofx.ts) `parseOfxToCanonical(raw, format, {payeeSource})` + [qfx.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/qfx.ts). Investment statements are unaffected (synthesized payees). New `accounts.ofx_payee_source` (default `'name'`); the [upload route](src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts) reads it (per-upload form override wins), passes it to the parser, and echoes the resolved value back; the [upload drawer](src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx)/[upload-card](src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx) show a "Payee from: Name / Memo" radio for `.ofx`/`.qfx` files that PATCHes the account via new [/api/accounts/[id]/import-prefs](src/app/api/accounts/[id]/import-prefs/route.ts). **§B — CSV mapping confirmation.** [csv-pipeline.ts](src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts) auto-applied a detected mapping in steps 2/3/3.5 and only prompted when nothing matched; a wrong guess (e.g. binding `Memo` over `Description` as payee) floods the ledger. New `confirmAutoMapping` flag (default `false` → `/api/import/preview` and other callers unaffected) makes those three steps return a `kind:"auto-detected"` result `{mapping, source, templateId?, headers, sampleRows, rowCount}` instead of committing; the upload route returns a 422 `csv-confirm-mapping` that the drawer handles by opening the existing `ColumnMappingDialog` pre-filled, with a "Don't ask again for this account" checkbox. Gated by a **two-layer** decision mirroring §A: per-account `accounts.csv_mapping_mode ∈ {'confirm','auto'}` (default `'confirm'`) is the override; the per-user default lives in `settings.confirm_csv_mapping` (default ON) via new [/api/settings/confirm-csv-mapping](src/app/api/settings/confirm-csv-mapping/route.ts) + a Settings → Import toggle. `import_hash` is unaffected (payee-source is chosen at parse time, before hashing; confirm runs before staging — no row hashed twice). Mobile parity deferred. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green · `npx vitest run tests/ofx-parser.test.ts tests/csv-pipeline.test.ts` **40/40**. Files: the migration + 2 new API routes above, `pf-app/src/lib/ofx-parser.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/external-import/parsers/{ofx,qfx,csv-pipeline}.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/{staging/upload,preview}/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/reconcile/upload-card.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/column-mapping-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/{settings/import,import}/page.tsx`, `pf-app/tests/{ofx-parser,csv-pipeline}.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(reconcile): transfer rules auto-apply in Auto-pilot + one-click approve-as-transfer in Approve-each (2026-06-04, dev).** Transfer rules (`create_transfer` action, no `set_category`) only fired in **Manual** mode — the recent materialize-dialog fix (`fc823a3`) wired them into Manual only; Auto-pilot dropped them with `no_set_category_action` and Approve-each showed "no match — needs your decision". New shared helper [materializeBankRowAsTransfer](src/lib/reconcile/materialize-transfer.ts) extracts the Manual two-step write (create pair + primary-link the source leg) so BOTH modes reuse one chokepoint — **no new transfer write-site**, so the `link_id` four-check (audit invariant #8) keeps holding through `createTransferPair`. Typed guards return `{ok:false,code}`: `transfer_self` / `transfer_inflow` / `transfer_investment_dest` / `transfer_dest_not_found`. **Scope (mirrors the Manual fix):** outflow rows only (`amount < 0`) — the bank row links to the transfer's source (debit) leg; inflow transfer-rule rows are a documented manual-handling skip; investment destinations refused (a rule can't supply the holding/qty). **Auto-pilot** ([match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts) `applyRulesToBankRows`): the `categoryId == null` branch now computes `pickTransferDestFromActions(...)` and, when `autoMaterialize`, writes the pair via the helper with `source='auto_rule'` (else keeps the `no_set_category_action` skip). **Approve-each** — [approve route](src/app/api/bank-transactions/[bankId]/approve/route.ts) makes `categoryId` optional, adds `transferDestAccountId` with a `.refine()` requiring **exactly one** of the two, and on the transfer branch writes via the helper with `source='manual'` (maps codes → 400, `transfer_dest_not_found` → 404); [inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx) surfaces a `transfer` suggestion (only when `suggestedCategoryId == null`, `suggestedTransferAccountId != null`, `amount < 0`, dest real + non-investment + distinct) wired into both `onApprove` + accept-all, and [row-card.tsx](src/components/inbox/row-card.tsx) renders "transfer to {dest}" (the `suggestedTransferAccountId` field was already built by `computeReconcileForAccount` and consumed by the Manual tab). Mobile parity deferred (note in `plan/mobile-updates-todo.md`). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/reconcile/materialize-transfer.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/bank-transactions/[bankId]/approve/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/row-card.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(reconcile): populate the Transfer tab + apply transfer rules + auto-link when materializing a bank row (2026-06-04 dev → 2026-06-06 prod).** On `/import` → Reconcile, "+ Create" (materialize a bank-ledger row into the ledger) opens the shared [TransactionDialog](src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx). Three problems, all fixed and verified live on dev.finlynq.com via the demo. **(A) Transfer tab was blank.** `onMaterialize` ([inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx](src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx)) only built a `transaction-prefill` (Transaction-tab) initial state; the dialog's `transaction-prefill` branch calls `resetToCreateDefaults()` (which resets `transferForm` to today + empty) then seeds only `form`, so switching to the Transfer tab wiped the bank row's date/amount/From-account. The `transaction-prefill` initial state gained an optional `transferSeed`; `onMaterialize` now always seeds it (From = bank account, date, amount = `|amount|`, note = payee) so switching tabs keeps the context. **(B) A `create_transfer` rule never affected this path.** Reconcile rules fed `set_category` only (`pickCategoryFromActions`). [match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts) now also surfaces `bankTransactions[].suggestedTransferAccountId` via the new `pickTransferDestFromActions` (first `create_transfer` action's `destAccountId`, self-transfers dropped); when present (and the dest is a real, non-investment, distinct account) `onMaterialize` sets `transferSeed.toAccountId` and the dialog opens **directly in Transfer mode** with the destination pre-filled. **Gated to outflow rows (`amount < 0`)** so the bank row links to the on-account debit leg (the "e-Tfr to …" case); inflow rows stay in Transaction mode with the Transfer tab still seeded. The pair is still written through `createTransferPair` (four-check `link_id` intact); no new write-sites. **(C) Materialized transfer left the bank row unlinked.** `/api/transactions/transfer` returns the debit leg as `fromTransactionId`, but the dialog's `onSaved` recovery read `fromTxId`/`id` (neither exists) → `debitTxId` fell back to `0` → the reconcile auto-link POST no-oped (the row only fuzzy-matched afterward, needing a manual Accept). Reads `fromTransactionId` first now, so the bank row auto-links to the debit leg on materialize (status → `linked`), matching the Transaction-tab behavior. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · live on dev.finlynq.com — Transfer tab carries date/amount/From; a seeded "payee contains e-Tfr → create transfer → Chequing" rule opened the materialize dialog in Transfer mode with To=Chequing pre-filled; "Create Transfer" produced a correct ±C$5,000 linked pair and the bank row flipped to `linked`. Three commits on `dev`: `fc823a3` (A+B) + `044f1f6` (auto-link) + `f34c1e5` (changelog). Files: `pf-app/src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/inbox/inbox-reconcile-tab.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/plan/reconciliation.md`, `pf-app/plan/transaction-rules-v2.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`. **Promoted to prod 2026-06-06** via `dev → main` merge `e365e7c` — Deploy Production green (no migration), and **prod-verified** on finlynq.com (demo): `/import` → Manual-review lens → Reconcile → "+ Create" on the `e-Tfr…` bank row → Transfer tab carries Date `2026-06-02` / Amount `5000` / From `Savings · USD` / note (To empty — prod demo has no transfer rule).
- **feat(import): consolidate the four money-in surfaces into a single account-anchored `/import` (2026-06-04, dev).** Closes the long-parked roadmap item #1 ([plan/import-reconcile-roadmap.md](plan/import-reconcile-roadmap.md)) + the Inbox-V4 "`/inbox` → cutover" future-work note. The app exposed **four overlapping surfaces** — `/import` (upload hub), `/import/pending` (staged review), `/reconcile` (bank↔ledger link), `/inbox` (account-anchored lens/policy, not even in nav). They're now **one surface at `/import`**, account-anchored, labelled "Import". **Zero API/server-logic changes** — the approve flow, encryption-tier branching, and half-pair enforcement are all server-side and untouched; the separate mobile inbox hits APIs (not these web routes) and is unaffected. Shipped + dev-validated across 7 commits (`2558d52` → `83eca55`, + balance/cleanup follow-ups). **Phase 1 (`2558d52`)** — account-agnostic import MANAGEMENT (CSV templates / WealthPosition connector / email-import address) moved to a new [/settings/import](src/app/(app)/settings/import/page.tsx), lifted verbatim (`TemplateManager`/`ConnectorTab` + email block). **Phase 2 (`281b257`)** — the `/inbox` upload drawer ([src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx](src/components/inbox/upload-drawer.tsx)) was a no-op `router.push("/import")`; it now uploads **in place** (reuses `ReconcileUploadCard` with a new optional `lockedAccount` prop + `ColumnMappingDialog`, POSTs to `/api/import/staging/upload` with the pre-selected account, handles the 422 csv-needs-mapping flow), then refreshes the policy-appropriate tab via a `reloadKey` threaded into each tab body's `key`. **Phase 3a (`ebd8d06`)** — the advanced importer the drawer can't do (IBKR FlexQuery XML + multi-account investment-statement binding, issue #64) relocated to a "Investment statements" tab on `/settings/import` via new [investment-statement-importer.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/components/investment-statement-importer.tsx). **Phase 3b (`66963f1`)** — the swap: the `/inbox` page body moved into [import/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx) (retitled "Import", dropped the v4 badge, added `?tab=` URL support); the old hub was preserved at `/import/classic` for testing; [nav.tsx](src/components/nav.tsx) collapsed the two tool links into one "Import" entry (removed the standalone "Reconcile" link + unused `Link2`); [next.config.ts](next.config.ts) redirects `/inbox` + `/reconcile` + `/import/reconcile` → `/import` (query strings preserved). **Balance-validation restoration (`fc23370` + `e309411`)** — the consolidation surfaced two pre-existing gaps: (a) the at-upload OFX balance snapshot (the old OFX preview showed it) is back as a `statement` summary the upload route returns + the drawer renders; (b) the rich collapsible per-day `BalanceWarningBanner` (Date / Prior anchor / Expected / Bank says / Δ) now ALSO fires for existing-bank-ledger drift (Calculated≠Loaded), not just staged-file anchors — mapped from `dbRows` into `BalanceWarning[]`, gated to not double-banner; plus a stale `/import/reconcile` link fix. **Phase 5 (`83eca55`)** — the detailed staged-review two-pane is now **embedded** in the `/import` Staging tab instead of deep-linking out: the whole `/import/pending` orchestration was extracted to a shared [staged-review-surface.tsx](src/components/import/staged-review-surface.tsx) with an `embedded`/`accountScope` mode (route mode keeps URL-driven `?id=`/`?account=` byte-for-byte; embedded mode is local-state + account-scoped), rendered by both the thin route page and the inbox Staging tab; `/api/import/staged` + `StagedRow` gained `boundAccountId` so the embedded list filters by account without the old N+1 binding-resolution hack (deleted). **Phase 6 (this commit)** — investment-account lens suppression (Auto-pilot / Approve-each disabled in [lens-chip.tsx](src/components/inbox/lens-chip.tsx) for `isInvestment` accounts, since their commit routes refuse server-side); **deleted** the now-redirect-shadowed `reconcile/page.tsx` + `import/reconcile/page.tsx` + the tested `import/classic/page.tsx` backup + the orphaned legacy direct-execute preview dialogs `ofx-preview.tsx` + `template-picker-dialog.tsx` (`import-preview-dialog.tsx` KEPT — still used by the WealthPosition connector flow); added an `/import/classic` → `/import` redirect for old bookmarks. Post-merge the only page routes under these paths are `/import` + `/import/pending`. **Verification (every phase):** `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green · live click-through on dev.finlynq.com via the demo (upload drawer, embedded staging review, redirects, rich drift banner, investment lens). Still `dev`-only; prod promotion (`dev → main`) pending. Files: see the per-phase commits above + workspace `CLAUDE.md` + `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **ci(deploy): make the dev-deploy git switch robust against regenerated-file drift (2026-06-04).** The `Deploy Dev` workflow ([.github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml](.github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml)) runs `deploy.sh --skip-pull`, so it does its own `git fetch` + branch-switch in the workflow. Its pre-checkout sweep was `git checkout -- package-lock.json src/app/mcp-guide/tools/tools.generated.ts 2>/dev/null || true` — but `tools.generated.ts` is **no longer a tracked file** (it's gitignored/generated), so the unmatched pathspec made `git checkout --` abort the WHOLE operation (a single bad pathspec aborts the lot), leaving `package-lock.json` (dirtied every deploy by `npm install`) uncleaned. The next `git checkout -B dev origin/dev` then wedged on *"Your local changes to package-lock.json would be overwritten by checkout. Aborting"* — the dev clone stuck on the old HEAD, serving stale code. Replaced the fragile targeted sweep with `git checkout -f -B dev origin/dev` + `git reset --hard origin/dev`, which discards any tracked working-tree drift (untracked files like `.env`/`node_modules`/`.next` preserved) — mirroring the proven stash+reset pattern `deploy.sh` already uses on its non-`--skip-pull` (prod) path. The prod workflow ([deploy-prod.yml](.github/workflows/deploy-prod.yml)) was already robust (it delegates all git handling to `deploy.sh`), so no prod change was needed. Files: `pf-app/.github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(lint): burn down + re-promote the 7 quick-win FINLYNQ-112 ESLint baseline rules to `error` (FINLYNQ-119, 2026-06-04).** FINLYNQ-112 baselined 12 rules (+ a 13th, `preserve-manual-memoization`) to `warn` so the blocking CI lint step could land while gating only on errors. This burns down + re-promotes the **7 quick-win / small-count rules** — they are removed from the baseline-downgrade block in [eslint.config.mjs](eslint.config.mjs) and now run at their default `error` severity with **zero violations repo-wide**. The 5 large backlogs (`@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports`, `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect`, `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any`, `react/display-name`, `react/no-unescaped-entities`) + `react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization` stay `warn` as separate follow-up PRs. **Per-rule fixes (12 source files, behaviour-preserving):** `prefer-const` (3 — `let`→`const` auto-fix in `mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts` + `src/app/api/transactions/reconciliation/route.ts`); `@typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment` (2 — added `-- <reason>` descriptions to the bad-shape `@ts-expect-error`s in `tests/crypto-spot-cache.test.ts`); `@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages` (1 — `<a href>`→`next/link` `<Link>` in `src/app/(app)/settings/data/page.tsx`); **`react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` (2 — a real latent bug):** two `useCallback`s in `src/components/sankey-chart.tsx` sat AFTER the empty-data early `return`, so an empty→non-empty render flipped the hook count — moved both above the early return (reading names off source `incomeData`/`expenseData`, identical to the layout nodes); `react-hooks/immutability` (7 — `window.location.href =`→`.assign()` in `src/app/oauth/authorize/page.tsx`; `.map`-closure `let` reassignment in `sankey-chart.tsx` rewritten as explicit loops; use-before-declare callback reorders in `inbox-to-approve-tab.tsx` / `inbox-to-categorize-tab.tsx` / `transactions/transaction-dialog.tsx`, which also let two stale `eslint-disable react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` comments be removed by adding the proper deps); `react-hooks/static-components` (14 — hoisted `SortIcon` in `portfolio/_components/holdings-table.tsx` + `Spinner` in `staging/staged-row-editor.tsx` to module scope, passing the previously-closed-over state as props). `use-memo` had 0 occurrences (already clean). **No new `eslint-disable` / no new downgrade was added** (tc-2). **Verification:** `npm run lint` exit 0 (0 errors, 334 warnings — all from the 5 remaining baselined rules + next defaults) · `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green. Files: the 12 source files above + `eslint.config.mjs` (baseline comment + block) + workspace `CLAUDE.md` + this `CHANGELOG.md`.
- **refactor: extract portfolio + import/pending god-components into hooks + sub-components (FINLYNQ-118, FINLYNQ-111 Phases 3-4, 2026-06-04).** The deferred tail of FINLYNQ-111 — the two busiest, most stateful client screens — extracted behaviour-preservingly, mirroring the Phase-2 transactions split. **NO data-fetching library** (that's FINLYNQ-115); the bespoke `fetch / useState / useEffect / finally` pattern is kept verbatim. Two commits on `dev`: `4b43346` (phase 3) + `fcd8563` (phase 4). **Phase 3 — portfolio** ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx)): **1996 → 593 lines (~70%)**. New `portfolio/_types.ts` (types + color constants + `clientCanonicalKey`), `portfolio/_hooks/use-portfolio.ts` (`usePortfolioOverview(currency)` + `useBenchmarks(period, enabled)`), and `portfolio/_components/` (`portfolio-ui` — tooltips/`ChangeBadge`/`DayChange`/skeleton; `csv`; `allocation-charts`; `benchmark-chart`; `etf-xray-card`; `holdings-by-account`; `holdings-table`). The page keeps page-level view state + the derived memos (`filteredHoldings` / `holdingsByCanonicalKey` / `accountGroups`) with their `(x ?? "").localeCompare` null-guards intact, the hero/P&L/top-movers blocks, and the holding edit/create dialog. **Phase 4 — import/pending** ([src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx)): **1796 → 1180 lines (~34%)**. New `import/pending/_types.ts` (`StagedRow`/`ParsedAnchorRow`/`StagedDetail` + `daysUntil`/`shiftDays`), `import/pending/_hooks/use-staged.ts` (`useStagedImports` / `useStagedDetail` / `useBankLedger`), and `import/pending/_components/` (`staged-list-view`, `reconcile-header` + the re-apply modal, `bank-pane` — DbPane `rowActions`, `staged-pane` — FilePane `rowActions` + suggestions header, `unbound-picker-pane`). The hooks own load-on-mount/-change + the data state; the page keeps the tightly-coupled orchestration (`openId` / `selected` / the match-action callbacks / the click-to-highlight neighborhoods) verbatim, threading state + setters through — exactly the FINLYNQ-111 split. `safeName` null-guards retained at the moved `accountOptions` callsite; approve / apply-rules / per-row-delete / link / flag behaviour unchanged. **Verification (both phases):** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors (only FINLYNQ-112 baselined warnings; the `set-state-in-effect` findings in the new files are verbatim-moved pre-existing patterns) · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green · `npm test` 77 failed / 1040 passed (unchanged at the documented baseline; no new file in the failure set) · no `swr`/`useSWR` import in either page's tree. Plan: [plan/finlynq-111-extract-god-components.md](plan/finlynq-111-extract-god-components.md) (Phases 3-4). Files: the 18 new `_types`/`_hooks`/`_components` files above + the two slimmed `page.tsx`, plus workspace `CLAUDE.md` + `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **refactor(api): migrate `notifications` + `holding-accounts` routes to apiHandler (FINLYNQ-116, increment 1, 2026-06-04).** First route group beyond the FINLYNQ-107 portfolio/operations proof-of-pattern down the [docs/api-handler-rollout.md](docs/api-handler-rollout.md) checklist. Picked the **lowest-contract-risk group** per the item body (internal/admin, no-mobile-consumer first): `src/app/api/notifications/route.ts` (GET + POST) + `src/app/api/holding-accounts/route.ts` (GET + POST + PUT + DELETE). **Both migrated in raw/compat mode (`raw: true`)** — auth + body-validation + error handling are now centralized in [`apiHandler`](src/lib/api-handler.ts) while the wire shape stays **byte-identical**, so there is **zero blank-screen risk**. Per the load-bearing rail (CLAUDE.md / FINLYNQ-107), every route's consumers were grepped first: **`notifications` has ZERO callsites** in `src/` or `mobile/src/`; **`holding-accounts` is consumed ONLY by the web `settings/holding-accounts` page** (bare-array GET + `res.ok`-only success checks + bare `{ error }` failure reads), **no mobile consumer**. `holding-accounts` POST/PUT use `apiHandler`'s `body` Zod option (bad JSON / invalid body → bare 400); their in-handler ownership / duplicate-409 / not-found-404 / last-pairing-409 guards return their own `NextResponse`, passed through verbatim by the wrapper. `notifications` POST self-dispatches on `body.action` (multiple shapes) so it keeps its own validation (no `body` schema). New `tests/api/holding-accounts.test.ts` (8 cases) pins the bare contract (GET bare array; POST 201 bare row / 400 invalid body / 409 duplicate; DELETE 409 last-pairing / 400 missing params; notifications GET bare `{ notifications, unreadCount }`; 401 unauth). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` 0 errors (only FINLYNQ-112 baselined warnings) · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run build` green (both routes compiled `ƒ /api/holding-accounts`, `ƒ /api/notifications`) · `npx vitest run tests/api/holding-accounts.test.ts tests/api-handler.test.ts` **23/23** + no NEW failures vs the ~77 baseline. Checklist updated (~113 → ~111 remaining; both struck from the list). NOT a bulk sweep — one group per increment. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/notifications/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/holding-accounts/route.ts`, `pf-app/tests/api/holding-accounts.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/docs/api-handler-rollout.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(transactions): adopt SWR behind the FINLYNQ-111 data hooks (FINLYNQ-115, 2026-06-03).** Spun out of FINLYNQ-111, which extracted the transactions god-component into custom data hooks (`useTransactions` / `useLookups` / `useTxColumnPrefs` / `useTxSortPref` / `useTxFilterPrefs`) but deliberately deferred the data-layer bet. This item adopts **SWR** behind those hooks — the **decision is RESOLVED = SWR** (not TanStack; revisit only if mutation ergonomics become the bottleneck) and the scope is **bounded to the transactions hooks ONLY** (the portfolio + import/pending god-components stay bespoke until **FINLYNQ-118**; NOT a repo-wide sweep). **Added `swr@^2.4.1`** (surgically — `package-lock.json` got the 14-line swr subtree by hand to dodge the Windows `npm install` Linux-optional-dep stripping gotcha; `@next/swc-linux-*` 12→12 + `lightningcss-linux-*` 15→15 byte-stable, zero deletions). **New shared seam [src/lib/swr/](src/lib/swr/)** — the single documented fetcher + key convention so new screens follow one pattern: `jsonFetcher` (throws `FetchError` on `!res.ok` → populates SWR `error`; for the list, whose old hook did an unconditional `r.json()`) + `softJsonFetcher(fallback)` (resolves a default on `!ok`/error, never throws; for the lookups + pref GETs whose old hook did `r.ok ? r.json() : <default>`) — both read **BARE JSON** (the `{success,data}` envelope is MCP-only); `swrKey(path)` for static-path keys; and `swrListOptions` which **tunes request volume to no-noisier-than-before** (`revalidateOnFocus:false` + `revalidateOnReconnect:false` + `shouldRetryOnError:false` — the old hooks did none of those — plus `dedupingInterval:2000` to collapse the uncoordinated parallel lookup fetches, `revalidateIfStale:true` + `keepPreviousData:true` for the nav-back background-revalidate win with no full-screen reload flash). README at [src/lib/swr/README.md](src/lib/swr/README.md). **Options are passed PER-HOOK, not via a global `<SWRConfig>`**, so the blast radius stays scoped to the transactions hooks (the shared `(app)` layout is untouched). **Hooks reimplemented on `useSWR` behind their EXACT FINLYNQ-111 signatures** (consuming `page.tsx` unchanged): `useTransactions` keeps `{ txns, total, loading, limit, loadTxns }` — cache key is the request URL built by the pure, unit-tested `buildTransactionQuery(...)` (same `{data,total}` unwrap, issue #59), `loading = isLoading || isValidating` (matches the old set-true-on-every-fetch), and `loadTxns` is now a bound `mutate()` (same imperative-refetch call sites after create/edit/delete + the split dialog `onSaved`); `useLookups` keeps `{ accounts, categories, holdings }` (3 soft-`[]` SWR reads, now de-duped/cached); the three pref hooks keep their `{ state, setters, helpers }` signatures — SWR owns the GET-on-mount, its result seeds the editable `useState` ONCE on first arrival (a guard ref so a background revalidate can't clobber unsaved local edits), and the **debounced PUT-on-change (400ms) + the legacy `pf-tx-cols-v1` localStorage migration are kept verbatim**. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` exit 0 · `npm run lint` exit 0 (the 3 new `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` findings in the seed effects are the same rule baselined to `warn` by FINLYNQ-112 — non-blocking, no NEW error rules) · `npm run build` exit 0 (`/transactions` compiled) · `npm test` **77 failed / 1032 passed / 13 skipped** — failed count **identical to the documented baseline** (verified by stash-diffing the two hook files: 77/1032 with and without the change, none of the 77 reference any touched file), and `tests/build-transaction-query.test.ts` stays **21/21** green. Files: `pf-app/package.json`, `pf-app/package-lock.json`, `pf-app/src/lib/swr/{fetcher,config,index}.ts` (new) + `pf-app/src/lib/swr/README.md` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/transactions/_hooks/{use-transactions,use-tx-prefs}.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(backfill): release the paired kind-override kinds + flag non-investment rows in investment accounts (2026-06-03).** Two changes to `/settings/backfill`. **(A) Non-investment flag** — the snapshot loads investment-account rows only, so a row with `portfolio_holding_id IS NULL` violates the `is_investment ⇒ references a holding` invariant (a mis-filed expense/income/transfer). New planner **Pass 2.9** ([src/lib/portfolio/backfill/planner.ts](src/lib/portfolio/backfill/planner.ts), before the Pass-3 safety net) flags these as `refusalReason:'non_investment_in_investment_account'` instead of letting them fall into the generic `unmatched_candidate`. The coverage endpoint ([coverage/route.ts](src/app/api/settings/backfill/coverage/route.ts)) adds a `nonInvestmentRows` metric (total + per-account, mirroring the `missingLots` block); the review UI gains a "Not an investment transaction" heading + `REFUSAL_EXPLANATIONS` entry + a coverage card + per-account "Not inv." column. **(B) Paired kind-override** — the `(soon)` paired override kinds on a refused `orphan_stock_leg` are now released. New converters in [src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) — the ONLY legal home for paired `kind` literals (audit invariant #8): `convertExistingToBuySellPair` (Buy/Sell, same-account, **both** `synth_new` + `link_existing`, `trade_link_id`; pure `normalizeBuySellLegs` core), `convertExistingToBrokeragePair` / `convertExistingToFxPair` / `convertExistingToInKindTransferPair` (**`link_existing` only**, `link_id`). Each UPDATEs the orphan in place (preserving id/created_at/import_hash/bank lineage) + synthesizes (`source='backfill_synth'`) or re-tags the user-picked counterpart; lot replay is the caller's job post-commit. **Apply** ([apply.ts](src/lib/portfolio/backfill/apply.ts)): `applyOrphanOverride`'s non-pair-less branch dispatches to `applyPairedOverride` (buy/sell) / `applyCrossAccountOverride` (brokerage/fx/transfer) — snapshot orphan (+ counterpart for link) → convert in one tx → flip status → replay (`applyLotEffectsForTx` per-row for buy/sell + brokerage; `applyLotEffectsForLinkPair` for fx/transfer). **Two latent undo bugs fixed:** the restore patch now restores `quantity`/`portfolioHoldingId`/`relatedHoldingId` (was amount/kind/links only → income/expense + paired overrides half-reverted on undo); the undo sibling-walk now follows `link_id` too (was `trade_link_id` only → a synth `link_id` counterpart was orphaned on undo). **New endpoint** `GET /api/settings/backfill/[runId]/counterpart-candidates?proposalId=&kind=` ranks unmatched counterpart rows per family (buysell=same-account cash; brokerage=other-account null-holding; fx=same-account different-currency; transfer=other-account same-holding), excluding the orphan + already-paired/canonical rows. **Load-bearing decision:** an investment-account orphan can only be the investment-side leg, and the `20260609` migration has no counterpart-account/currency column, so cross-account kinds (brokerage/fx/transfer) are **`link_existing`-only** (synth would have nowhere to record the target account/currency); Buy/Sell (same-account) keep both modes. **UI** ([[runId]/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/backfill/[runId]/page.tsx)): enables Buy/Sell (synth + link) + the 6 investment-side cross-account kinds (link-only, synth radio disabled; the 2 external-leg brokerage kinds are omitted from the picker entirely — a backfill orphan is always on an investment account, so it can never BE the bank-side leg), a counterpart-mode toggle, a candidate picker fed by the endpoint, a two-leg WILL-BECOME for Buy/Sell + a pairing note for cross-account. New refusal codes: `ambiguous_orphan_for_override`, `counterpart_{missing,not_found,is_orphan,account_mismatch,currency_mismatch,already_linked,same_account,holding_mismatch}`, `cash_sleeve_missing`, `synth_not_supported_cross_account`, `orphan_{not_stock_leg,not_cash_sleeve_leg,zero_qty}`. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npx vitest run tests/portfolio/backfill-convert-buysell.test.ts tests/backfill-planner.test.ts` **38/38** (normalize signs + DB-free converter validation across all four converters + 2 Pass-2.9 planner cases). No migration. **Deferred:** a WILL-BECOME parity unit test (client mirror lives in page.tsx) + DB-backed apply/undo round-trip (needs a test Postgres) → covered by on-dev end-to-end verification. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/portfolio/backfill/planner.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/settings/backfill/coverage/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/settings/backfill/[runId]/counterpart-candidates/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/portfolio/backfill/apply.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/backfill/[runId]/page.tsx`, `pf-app/tests/portfolio/backfill-convert-buysell.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/tests/backfill-planner.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/plan/architecture/backfill.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **ci(hardening): add a blocking ESLint step + promote `audit:invariants` from advisory to blocking (FINLYNQ-112, 2026-06-03).** CODE_REVIEW.md §5. `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s `validate` job (runs on `pull_request` → main/staging/dev only — it does NOT gate the `deploy-dev.yml` dev deploy, so the blast radius is future PRs) gains a `Lint` step (`npm run lint`) after Type check, and the `audit:invariants` step drops `continue-on-error: true` (it passes 11/11 on HEAD, so it's now genuinely blocking). **ESLint baseline (config-only, zero code change):** `npm run lint` was exiting 1 with **178 errors / 249 warnings**. Rather than mass-`--fix` 178 errors across the repo (behaviour risk, out of scope), the **12 currently-firing error rules** are downgraded `error → warn` in a new baseline override block in [eslint.config.mjs](eslint.config.mjs): `@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports` (60), `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` (38), `@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any` (36), `react/display-name` (23), `react/no-unescaped-entities` (7), `prefer-const` (3), `react-hooks/static-components` (3), `react-hooks/use-memo` (2), `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` (2), `@typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment` (2), `@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages` (1), `react-hooks/immutability` (1). Result: `npm run lint` exits **0** (0 errors / 427 warnings) so the new step lands blocking-on-errors today. **Every rule NOT in that list keeps its current severity, so the step still catches any FUTURE new-rule error** — its teeth grow as the backlog is burned down and rules re-promoted to `error` (follow-up tracked in DevManager). **Prettier deliberately skipped** — no `.prettierrc` exists and a `prettier --check` would flag the entire repo without buy-in (the item explicitly says "optionally" + "Don't add Prettier if it would churn the whole repo without buy-in"). Config-only: `tsc --noEmit` clean, full test suite unchanged vs baseline. Synthetic-violation proof: a throwaway raw `INSERT INTO transactions` under `src/app/api/transactions/` made `npm run audit:invariants` exit 1 (`[FAIL] sign-vs-category`); deleting it returned exit 0 (`ALL INVARIANTS PASS`).
- **refactor(transactions): extract the fat client component into a pure query builder + hooks + sub-components (FINLYNQ-111 Phases 1-2, 2026-06-03).** CODE_REVIEW.md §A2 — behaviour-preserving maintainability split of `src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx` (**2063 → 1021 lines, ~50% reduction**). Scope narrowed at Todo review to the transactions page ONLY; the portfolio + import/pending extractions (Phases 3-4) are split to **FINLYNQ-118** (`postponed`). **Phase 1 — pure query builder:** the ~80-line inline param-builder inside `loadTxns` (page lines :784-856) is now `buildTransactionQuery(filters, sort, colFilters, accounts, page): URLSearchParams` at [src/lib/transactions/build-query.ts](src/lib/transactions/build-query.ts) — pure, no React/fetch, takes `accounts` as an explicit arg so the `accountType` enum filter resolves type→account-ids while staying testable. **Byte-identical to the original inline code:** same param keys, same conditional order, the same `accountType`→ids resolution, and the load-bearing rule that the `date` column filter only sets `startDate`/`endDate` when the top-bar quick filter hasn't already. New [tests/build-transaction-query.test.ts](tests/build-transaction-query.test.ts) (21 golden-value cases, hand-derived from the ORIGINAL inline behaviour) pins date/text/numeric/enum (incl. `accountType`→ids + the no-match→emit-nothing case) + sort + pagination + the date-col-filter precedence rule. **Phase 2 — hooks + sub-components (mechanical, behaviour-preserving move):** shared types moved to `_types.ts` (Transaction/LinkedSibling/Account/Category/Holding/ColFilterShape/SortPref/ColumnPref). Five data hooks in `_hooks/`: `useLookups` (accounts/categories/holdings mount triplet), `useTxColumnPrefs` (column layout + legacy `pf-tx-cols-v1` localStorage migration + debounced PUT), `useTxSortPref` (header sort cycle + debounced PUT), `useTxFilterPrefs` (per-column filters + debounced PUT), `useTransactions` (txns/total/loading + `loadTxns`; `page` stays page-level state so the sort/filter hooks reset it to 0 on change via an `onChange` callback — verbatim with the old inline `setPage(0)`). Three components in `_components/`: `<TransactionTable>` (header sort/filter/drag + body cell-render switch), `<ColumnFilterPopover>` (moved verbatim), `<SplitBadge>`. Each hook moves the EXISTING state + effects verbatim — same deps, fetch URLs, setState, debounce timings; no UX/fetch-timing/state-shape change. The two `set-state-in-effect` eslint findings in the new files pre-date this change (verbatim-moved from the original page, lines 226 + 867 on `dev`). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean (`ignoreBuildErrors:false`) · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run lint` no NEW errors of this change's making · the new build-query suite 21/21 green · full `npm test` **77 failed / 1012 passed / 13 skipped** (failed unchanged at the 77 baseline — passed rose by the +21 new tests; none of the 77 failures reference any new file). Commits: Phase 1 `f0769ed`, Phase 2 `4cd4b77`. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/transactions/build-query.ts` (new), `pf-app/tests/build-transaction-query.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/transactions/_types.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/transactions/_hooks/{use-tx-prefs,use-transactions}.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/transactions/_components/{transaction-table,column-filter-popover,split-badge}.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **refactor(types): eliminate risky `as any` clusters — db-proxy execute, rule Condition/Action unions, JSONB read boundary (FINLYNQ-114, 2026-06-03).** CODE_REVIEW.md §A5 — typing-only, runtime behavior + rule matching byte-identical. Five clusters retyped, before→after: **(1) `src/lib/api-auth.ts` 5×→0** `(db as any).execute(...)`. The `db` proxy is already typed `DrizzleDb` (= `NodePgDatabase`), which exposes a typed `execute(query: SQLWrapper | string)` — the `as any` casts were pure noise defeating the checker on the AUTH write path (a wrong query shape now type-errors). No adapter-interface change needed. **(2) `src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx` 12×→0** `as unknown as Partial<Condition|Action>`. The field/kind-switch defaults now come from typed factory maps in [src/lib/rules/schema.ts](src/lib/rules/schema.ts) — `CONDITION_DEFAULTS`/`ACTION_DEFAULTS` + `defaultConditionForField(field, accountId?)`/`defaultActionForKind(kind, id?)`, each entry returning the exact discriminated-union member via `Extract<Action, {kind:K}>`. A wrong field on a variant is now a compile error at the factory, not a silent hole. `blankCondition`/`blankAction` route through the same factories. **Behavior note:** the editor seeds BLANK placeholders (`to:""`, `categoryId:0`) the user fills before submit — intentionally not yet Zod-valid; objects are byte-identical to what the casts produced (pinned by test). **(3) `src/lib/rules/crypto.ts` 4×→0** `as unknown[]` over JSONB crossing the encryption boundary. New `asJsonArray(value)` does a permissive `z.array(z.unknown()).safeParse` with a fallback to `[]` — it **NEVER throws** (the per-element `isObj()` guards still pass non-object survivors through), so a parse miss degrades to today's behavior and the decrypt-before-match boundary can't break the rules engine on legacy/partial/encrypted rule shapes. **(4) `src/app/api/mcp/route.ts` 2×→0** `(server as any).tool` scope-injection monkey-patch — replaced with a single cast through a named `ScopeFilterableServer` interface (`tool(name, ...args): RegisteredTool | undefined`). **(5) `src/lib/transfer.ts` 11×→1** inline `pool as unknown as SqlPool` casts hoisted to one `asSqlPool(pool)` helper. **Out of scope, deliberately left:** the in-place op/value `... as Partial<Condition>` patch casts in rule-editor-dialog (25, a different narrowing pattern, not the named 12), the `registerPgTools(server, db as any, …)` pg-compat-shim handle (MCP shim boundary, not the tool-scope cluster), and the correct `globalThis` HMR singleton casts (`db/index.ts:7`, `dek-cache.ts:43`). **Convention established:** rule `Condition`/`Action` defaults are constructed via the typed factory maps in `rules/schema.ts`, never ad-hoc casts. **Tests:** new [tests/rules-factory-and-crypto-boundary.test.ts](tests/rules-factory-and-crypto-boundary.test.ts) (9 cases) pins the factories to their exact pre-refactor shapes + proves the crypto Zod boundary never throws on malformed/legacy/null JSONB. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · auth + rules suites green · full `npm test` **77 failed / 990 passed / 13 skipped** (the only over-77 delta-file is the pre-existing flaky bcrypt-timing `tests/auth/security-b6.test.ts` — passes/fails across runs independent of this change; no previously-passing test FILE regressed) · re-grep confirms 0 targeted casts remain. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/api-auth.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/rules/schema.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/src/lib/rules/crypto.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/mcp/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/transfer.ts`, `pf-app/tests/rules-factory-and-crypto-boundary.test.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **chore(hygiene): dead-code removal + crypto-symbol dedup + stale-doc fix (FINLYNQ-113, 2026-06-03).** Three independent CODE_REVIEW.md §A6/§5 quick wins, all behavior-preserving. **(a) Dead code** — deleted [src/components/google-analytics.tsx](src/components/google-analytics.tsx) (a `GoogleAnalytics` component with ZERO importers — the live GA loader is the inline `GoogleAnalyticsScripts` in `analytics-consent.tsx`, not this file) and the dev-gated `<NetWorthChart>` from the dashboard ([src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx)): removed its import + its sole render site inside the `{devMode && …}` block, then `git rm`'d the now-orphaned [src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/net-worth-chart.tsx](src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/net-worth-chart.tsx). The CORRECT, unconditional `<NetWorthHistoryChart>` stays; `netWorthData` is preserved (still feeds the hero sparkline + net-worth delta); `useDevMode`/`devMode` stay (still gate the other dev-only charts). **(b) Crypto-symbol dedup** — three identical hardcoded 38-coin `CRYPTO_SYMBOLS` sets + their base-extracting `isCrypto`/`isCryptoSymbol` helpers (in [holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts), [/api/portfolio/overview](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts), [/api/portfolio/crypto](src/app/api/portfolio/crypto/route.ts)) collapsed to ONE canonical source: new `CRYPTO_HOLDING_SYMBOLS` + `isCryptoSymbol(symbol)` (strips the `BTC-USD`→`BTC` quote-suffix, same as the locals) in [src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts](src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts). **Union-coverage check:** all three local lists were byte-identical (same 38 coins) so no coin had to be ADDED — the canonical set is exactly that union, eliminating the silent mis-pricing risk where one copy could drift. Distinct from the FX-rate `isCryptoCurrency()` (BTC/ETH/USDC/USDT *currencies*) which is unchanged — the new helper classifies *holding symbols* for CoinGecko market-value pricing. The two divergent `isCurrencyCodeSymbol` copies (holdings-value: supported-set only; overview: supported-set + user `active_currencies`) were also hoisted to a canonical `isCurrencyCodeSymbol(sym, extraCodes?)` — holdings-value passes nothing, overview passes `activeCurrencies` (renamed its closure to `symbolIsCash`), preserving each call site's exact behavior. **(c) Doc drift** — workspace `CLAUDE.md` (untracked, NOT in this commit) said `typescript.ignoreBuildErrors: true` in three places (Commands block, CI/CD principle, Known Issues) but `next.config.ts:52` is `false` (FINLYNQ-11 flipped it); fixed all three. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npm run lint` no NEW errors in touched files (0 errors) · full `npm test` **77 failed / 982 passed / 13 skipped** (unchanged from baseline — the two portfolio-aggregator suites in the failure set are pre-existing `DATABASE_URL`-required infra skips, not regressions) · re-grep confirms zero `google-analytics` importers, single canonical crypto set, no live `NetWorthChart` refs. Files: deleted `pf-app/src/components/google-analytics.tsx` + `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/net-worth-chart.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/lib/fx/supported-currencies.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/portfolio/crypto/route.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.

- **refactor(mcp): route MCP HTTP transaction writes through the domain layer (FINLYNQ-108, 2026-06-03).** Closes CODE_REVIEW.md §A1/§A7 (the third increment): REST writes go through [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) `createTransaction`, but MCP **HTTP** issued **raw** `INSERT INTO transactions` in `record_transaction` ([mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) ~:2935), `bulk_record_transactions` (~:3410), and the `record_trade` **fee leg** (~:4287). So each write site re-asserted the load-bearing invariants by hand — which is exactly why `scripts/audit-invariants.ts` (FINLYNQ-48) has to grep-backstop them.
