All three now call the SAME `createTransaction(userId, data, dek)` helper the REST `POST /api/transactions` route uses, making the audit trio (`source`/`created_at`/`updated_at`), the investment-account FK guard, and sign-vs-category **structural** (defined once) instead of policed-by-grep. **Why this is byte-identical, not a behavior change:** in the HTTP MCP context the `db` handle passed to `registerPgTools` IS `@/db` ([src/app/api/mcp/route.ts:179](src/app/api/mcp/route.ts)) — the very module-level Drizzle proxy `createTransaction` writes through — so the row is produced from the same connection with the same columns. **Field mapping (raw INSERT col → createTransaction arg):** `user_id`→1st arg; `date`→`date`; `account_id`→`accountId`; `category_id`(nullable)→`categoryId`; `currency`/`amount`(roundMoney)/`entered_*`→same; `payee`/`note`/`tags`→passed ALREADY-ENCRYPTED (mirrors REST `encryptTxWrite`, `v1:` ciphertext); `portfolio_holding_id`→`portfolioHoldingId`; `quantity`→`quantity`; `source='mcp_http'`→`source`; `trade_link_id`→`tradeLinkId`. **Minimal helper extension (REST-compatible widening only):** `createTransaction`'s `data` type now accepts `categoryId: number | null` (MCP writes uncategorized rows), nullable `payee`/`note`/`tags` (cold-DEK ternary fallback), and a new `tradeLinkId?: string | null` (issue #96 pair linker; REST never passes it → column defaults NULL, REST behavior unchanged). **invalidateUser + markSnapshotsDirty preserved verbatim:** `record_transaction` still fires `invalidateUserTxCache` + the per-row lot wiring (`applyLotEffectsForTx`) + `markSnapshotsDirty` for investment rows AFTER the helper returns; `bulk_record_transactions` still has NO per-row lot wiring / snapshot-dirty (it never did — the `lots-write-hook` baseline exception is unchanged), so omitting them keeps behavior exact. **Out of scope (documented):** the staging-import transfer-pair INSERT (~:10890, `source='import'`, two-row `link_id`/`import_hash`/`fit_id`/`bank_transaction_id` write — not a `record_transaction`-class write) stays raw; stdio (`register-core-tools.ts`) untouched (sibling FINLYNQ-110). Tool input/output contracts unchanged. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS (sign-vs-category write-sites 13→12, invalidate-after-mcp-tx-mutation 36→35 — fewer raw INSERTs, the invariants are now structural) · `rg "INSERT INTO transactions" mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts` leaves only the out-of-scope import transfer-pair · new [tests/api/mcp-record-transaction-parity.test.ts](tests/api/mcp-record-transaction-parity.test.ts) 4/4 (mock-based: asserts the handler hands `createTransaction` a source-stamped, currency-rounded, encrypted-payee payload + `invalidateUser` fires + sign-vs-category warn-but-allow + dryRun skips the write + per-row bulk parity) · full `npm test` failed count unchanged at **77** (no regression; verified by stash-diffing the affected `transactions.test.ts`/`mcp-http-smoke.test.ts` baseline). Files: `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/queries.ts`, `pf-app/tests/api/mcp-record-transaction-parity.test.ts` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **refactor(api): introduce `apiHandler` wrapper + migrate `/api/portfolio/operations/*` (FINLYNQ-107, 2026-06-03).** Closes CODE_REVIEW.md §A4 (the FIRST increment): capable route helpers existed but were adopted in **0 / 164** routes (`safeRoute` in [src/lib/validate.ts](src/lib/validate.ts), `apiSuccess`/`apiError` in [src/lib/api-response.ts](src/lib/api-response.ts)) while ~121 routes hand-rolled the same auth → try → `validateBody` → catch → `safeErrorMessage`/status quartet. New [src/lib/api-handler.ts](src/lib/api-handler.ts) `apiHandler({ auth, body, raw?, mapError?, fallbackMessage? }, handler)` folds the four concerns: **(1)** auth gating (`requireAuth` → 401 / `requireEncryption` → 423), **(2)** body parse/validate via `validateBody` → 400, **(3)** catch → optional `mapError` (e.g. `mapOperationError`) first, then `safeErrorMessage` + `logApiError`, honouring `AppError.status` (vs a bare 500), **(4)** the `apiSuccess` `{ success: true, data }` envelope. **Load-bearing two-mode design** — the envelope is the default, but `raw: true` is a per-route opt-out where the handler returns its own `NextResponse` verbatim AND the catch branch stays bare (`{ error }`). This is mandatory because **the `/api/portfolio/operations/*` POST routes are consumed BARE** by the web portfolio forms (`BuyForm` reads `data.code`/`data.error`/`data.blockingClosureTxIds` on error, `res.ok` on success) and the mobile `postPortfolioOperation` ([mobile/src/api/client.ts](mobile/src/api/client.ts) — bare `{ id, ... }` on 2xx, bare structured error `{ code, currency, blockingClosureTxIds }` on 4xx). **Consumer-check finding:** flipping these to the envelope would blank client screens (the documented mobile REST-envelope-mismatch failure mode), so all **8 POST routes** (buy/sell/swap/transfer/deposit/withdrawal/fx-conversion/income-expense) were migrated through `apiHandler` in **raw/compat mode** — centralizing auth + validation + error mapping WITHOUT changing the wire shape (each route dropped its ~35-line hand-rolled skeleton to a ~20-line config + handler). `markSnapshotsDirty` is preserved on every route (audit invariant #11 still 8/8). The `load/route.ts` GET is left as-is (already `{ success, data }`-enveloped AND consumed as `res.data`). **tc-2 adjustment:** the non-primary tc-2 originally asserted a migrated operations route returns a `{ success: true, data }` envelope — but that would BREAK the bare consumers, so per the item's own "Don't change response shape … without checking consumers" guard, tc-2 now asserts the route's ACTUAL safe contract (bare `{ id }` 201 + bare structured error preserved, auth+validation+error centralized) with the envelope proven on the wrapper unit test instead; the primary tc-1 is unchanged. The broad envelope rollout of the remaining ~113 try/catch routes is the deferred follow-up **FINLYNQ-116** (`postponed`), enumerated with per-route bare-vs-safe classification in the new checklist [docs/api-handler-rollout.md](docs/api-handler-rollout.md). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · new [tests/api-handler.test.ts](tests/api-handler.test.ts) 15/15 (envelope, raw mode, 400 on bad body/JSON, 401/423 auth, `AppError.status` honoured, `mapError` short-circuit) + [tests/api/portfolio-operations-buy.test.ts](tests/api/portfolio-operations-buy.test.ts) 4/4 (201 bare `{id}`, bare 400, bare structured domain error, 423 no-DEK) · full `npm test` failed count unchanged at **77** (no regression; the 77 are the pre-existing documented failures). Files: `pf-app/src/lib/api-handler.ts` (new), the 8 `pf-app/src/app/api/portfolio/operations/{buy,sell,swap,transfer,deposit,withdrawal,fx-conversion,income-expense}/route.ts`, `pf-app/tests/api-handler.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/tests/api/portfolio-operations-buy.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/docs/api-handler-rollout.md` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **test(portfolio) + refactor(portfolio): cross-aggregator parity net + single-source issue-#128 cash-leg skip (FINLYNQ-106, 2026-06-03).** Closes CODE_REVIEW.md §A3: portfolio holdings/cost-basis aggregation is implemented three ways — `getHoldingsValueByAccount` ([src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts)), the inline reimplementation in the `/api/portfolio/overview` GET handler ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)), and the raw-`sql` `aggregateHoldings`/`accumulate` in [mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts](mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) — and they had **silently diverged**: the overview route + MCP `accumulate` carried the issue-#128 paired cash-leg skip (`kind IN ('buy_cash_leg','sell_cash_leg') OR (trade_link_id IS NOT NULL AND amount = 0)`); `holdings-value.ts` did NOT. Two deliverables, behavior-preserving: **(1)** the skip predicate is now defined in **one** place — new [src/lib/portfolio/aggregation-predicates.ts](src/lib/portfolio/aggregation-predicates.ts) exports `cashLegSkipSql()` (drizzle `sql` fragment, imported by both SQL aggregators) + `isCashLegRow()` (pure-JS form, imported by the MCP `accumulate()`) + `CASH_LEG_KINDS`, all derived from one literal rule. `holdings-value.ts` + `overview/route.ts` now build their `SUM(CASE … AND NOT <skip>)` from the shared fragment (no duplicated inline predicate string); MCP `accumulate()` calls `isCashLegRow(...)` instead of its inline `isPairedCashLeg`. **(2)** New DB-free parity vitest [tests/portfolio/aggregator-parity.test.ts](tests/portfolio/aggregator-parity.test.ts) drives ONE multi-currency fixture (paired buy + sell cash legs on a USD cash sleeve, a dividend, a sell) through all three aggregators and asserts equal per-stock-holding qty/costBasis/marketValue. **Aligning `holdings-value.ts` to the #128 behavior is a verified no-op for reported numbers** — a Phase-2 cash leg lands on the cash *sleeve* (kind=buy_cash_leg/sell_cash_leg → portfolio_holding_id = the sleeve), never on a stock holding, so the skip changes nothing for stock-holding qty/cost; for cash sleeves (priced at 1) the zeroed cost tally falls back to the identical market value. The divergence is only observable in the MCP realized-gain path, which the test's tc-2 pins (red→green proof: disabling the skip in production `accumulate()` flips the sleeve from 0 to a phantom buy of 1000). `tsc --noEmit` clean · `audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · the new suite is green; the existing DB-backed FINLYNQ-49/65 suites are untouched.

- **feat(onboarding) + chore(merge): USD "Load sample data" seed + side EUR/CAD accounts; `mobile-dev` → `dev` (2026-06-03).** Closes the "Related surface NOT touched" note on the currency entry below — `POST /api/onboarding/sample-data` (the rich demo-grade "Load sample data" generator the mobile button hits, [src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts](src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts)) hardcoded **CAD** throughout, so under the new USD default it rendered every amount FX-converted (ugly non-round numbers). Re-denominated the whole seed to **USD**: the 5 core accounts (Chequing / Savings / Visa Rewards / Brokerage / TFSA), all regular transactions, USD cash sleeves, dividends, goals, loans, subscriptions, budgets. Swapped the 3 **CAD-listed TFSA ETFs** (VFV.TO / XEQT.TO / VAB.TO) for **USD-listed equivalents** (SPY / QQQ / BND) with believable qty/cost — the per-holding dividend rows + the TFSA trim-sell were re-keyed to match. Added two **side foreign-currency cash accounts** — `EUR Wallet` (EUR) + `CAD Account` (CAD), each with ~4 recent transactions (net-positive balances) — so the seed exercises multi-currency display + net-worth FX conversion (**7 accounts** total). The regular-tx insert is now **currency-aware per account** (new `acctCurrencyById` map) instead of a single hardcoded `"CAD"` literal; buys/sells/deposits still route through `recordBuy`/`recordSell`/`recordBrokerageDeposit` (audit invariant #8 intact). The obsolete thin pre-`8057348` copy of this route still on `mobile-dev` was reverted (web routes deploy from `dev`, not the mobile branch). **Then merged `mobile-dev` → `dev`** (merge commit `28c09eb`): brings 18 mobile-parity commits (portfolio / reports / reconcile-inbox / settings / splits / create-flows / dual-currency / onboarding-prompt + the EAS-free gradle Android build + Play-publish workflow) onto the web integration branch. The merge was **dry-run conflict-free** — `shared/types.ts` (changed on both sides) auto-merged on non-overlapping additions; the only other web-tree files from `mobile-dev` were `docs/mobile-setup.md` + `.github/workflows/mobile-build-gradle.yml`; dev's rich USD seed route was untouched (mobile-dev never modified it). **Verification:** full `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors on both the seed commit (`cb93cbc`) and the merged tree · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · Deploy-Dev (build + SSH deploy to dev.finlynq.com + verify) green on both pushes · Mobile-CI (tsc + jest) green on the merge. Still `dev`-only; prod promotion (`dev → main`) pending. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts`, merge `28c09eb`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(currency): custom `C$`/`$` dollar-family symbols + USD as the new default currency (2026-06-03).** Two coupled changes. **(1) Symbols** — money used to render through `Intl.NumberFormat("en-CA", { style: "currency" })`, which prints CAD as the unmarked `$1,234.56` and USD as the clunky `US$1,234.56` (the inverse of the convention most users expect). `Intl` *cannot* emit `C$` for CAD under any locale/`currencyDisplay` combo (only `$` / `CA$` / `CAD`), so [src/lib/currency.ts](src/lib/currency.ts) `formatCurrency` now formats the "dollar family" as a plain `Intl` decimal with a hand-picked symbol prefix: **USD → `$`, CAD → `C$`, AUD → `A$`, NZD → `NZ$`, HKD → `HK$`, SGD → `S$`, MXN → `MX$`** (`-C$500.10` puts the minus before the symbol). Every non-dollar currency keeps its native `Intl` symbol (EUR → €, GBP → £, JPY → ¥). `formatCurrency` gained an `opts?: { decimals?: number }` param (mirrors mobile) so the three scattered duplicate formatters — `health-info-dialog.tsx` (`undefined` locale, 0 decimals), `reconcile/balance-summary-card.tsx` + `staging/balance-warning-banner.tsx` (both `en-US` + `narrowSymbol`, which is why they showed plain `$` for *both* CAD & USD) — now delegate to the shared function for consistent `C$`/`$`. Mobile mirrors the map in [mobile/src/lib/format.ts](mobile/src/lib/format.ts) and the four screen-local duplicate formatters (Dashboard/Budgets/Import/TransactionDetail) were collapsed onto it. **(2) Default currency → USD** — new users had no `settings.display_currency` row at signup, so the de-facto default came from two fallbacks now flipped `"CAD"` → `"USD"`: `getDisplayCurrency()` ([src/lib/fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts)) and the `/api/auth/session` `displayCurrency` seed ([route.ts](src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts)). `users.base_currency` already defaulted to `"USD"`, so the accounting basis now aligns with display. **Note:** existing users who never explicitly chose a display currency also flip from the CAD fallback to USD (their stored data is unchanged — only the conversion target). **(3) Demo seed** — [scripts/seed-demo.ts](scripts/seed-demo.ts) was a uniform single-currency CAD dataset with no `display_currency` setting, so under the new USD default it would render every amount FX-converted (ugly non-round numbers). Re-denominated the whole seed CAD → USD (accounts, transactions, holdings, cash sleeve, budgets) — keeps the round amounts, stays internally consistent (single shared cash sleeve), and incidentally fixes VTI/VOO/VXUS/AAPL which were mislabeled CAD. The two `.TO` tickers (VCN.TO/VAB.TO) are now cosmetically USD-labeled (the demo was never currency-accurate on holdings; a true multi-currency demo is a separate follow-up). **Verification:** web `npx tsc --noEmit` clean + `npx vitest run tests/currency.test.ts` 17/17 (added exact-symbol locks for C$/`$`/A$/NZ$/S$, negative-sign placement, decimals option, and €/£ passthrough); mobile `npx tsc --noEmit` clean + `npx jest` 183/183 (on `mobile-dev`). **Shipped to `dev` + verified live (2026-06-03):** web committed `0247e83` and deployed to dev.finlynq.com, then validated via Chrome on the demo account — native CAD balances render `C$3,117.28`, USD display totals render `$18,199.55`, and the dashboard chart reads "USD · current-rate FX" (both symbols + the default flip confirmed). The mobile mirror was committed on `mobile-dev` `89c40f1` (mobile-CI tsc + jest green); both web + mobile changes were then folded into `dev` via merge `28c09eb` (pushed to `origin/dev`). **Prod promotion pending** — the USD default reaches real mobile users only after `dev → main` (the mobile app points at `finlynq.com`), and the `C$`/`$` symbols reach phones only with a new mobile build (next is versionCode 12 / app.json 1.0.5), both tracked in workspace [HANDOVER_2026-06-03_MOBILE_CURRENCY.md](../HANDOVER_2026-06-03_MOBILE_CURRENCY.md). **Related surface NOT touched:** `/api/onboarding/sample-data` ("Load sample data") still seeds CAD — it shares the same new-USD-default ugliness and is flagged for the separate mobile-seeding pass. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/currency.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/health-info-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/reconcile/balance-summary-card.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/staging/balance-warning-banner.tsx`, `pf-app/src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/auth/session/route.ts`, `pf-app/scripts/seed-demo.ts`, `pf-app/mobile/src/lib/format.ts`, `pf-app/mobile/src/screens/{Dashboard,Budgets,Import,TransactionDetail}Screen.tsx`, `pf-app/tests/currency.test.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **feat(net-worth): store historical-FX per-account CASH balances for "Net Worth Over Time" (2026-06-13 migration, shipped `dev` 2026-06-02).** Closes the documented `fxApproximation` gap from `plan/net-worth-cash-snapshots.md`. The chart was a hybrid: investment accounts read stored `portfolio_snapshots` (already translated at each day's historical FX rate), but cash/liability accounts were computed live from `transactions` and converted with a **single current-rate map** — so a USD cash balance from 2023 was shown in CAD at *today's* rate and drifted every load. The cash side now reads its own stored per-account daily snapshots, translated at each day's historical rate, consistent with the investment side. **Key lever:** cash balance = cumulative `SUM(transactions.amount)` + cached FX, which needs **no DEK** (unlike investment, which needs the DEK to decrypt holding symbols for pricing). So cash snapshots are built/rebuilt by a real background cron + a DEK-free chart-load self-heal. **Phase 1 — watermark** ([20260613_cash_snapshot_meta.sql](scripts/migrations/20260613_cash_snapshot_meta.sql) + `portfolioCashSnapshotMeta` in [schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts)): per-user `portfolio_cash_snapshot_meta(user_id PK, tx_max_updated, tx_count, built_through, built_at)` — `portfolio_snapshots.created_at` isn't bumped on re-UPSERT so it can't signal "rebuilt since"; this row is the fingerprint instead (`tx_count` is load-bearing — a DELETE leaves max-updated untouched, only the count reveals it). `source='cash'` needs no migration (`portfolio_snapshots.source` is free-text, no CHECK). **Phase 2 — queries** ([queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts)): `getCashDailyDeltasByAccount` (per-account per-day delta, `is_investment=false`+`archived=false`), `getCashSnapshotsInRange` (cash mirror of `getInvestmentSnapshotsInRange` — `is_investment=false`+`archived=false`+`source='cash'`, never the NULL aggregate), `getCashTxFingerprint` (`GREATEST(MAX(created_at),MAX(updated_at))` + `COUNT(*)` over cash txns). **Phase 3 — builder** ([cash-builder.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts) + [cash-meta.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-meta.ts)): DEK-free `buildCashSnapshots` does one cumulative walk per account, folding the pre-window prefix into day 1 and writing `mv = cumulativeNative × getRate(accCcy, reporting, day)` via the **verbatim COALESCE-target UPSERT from builder.ts** (`source='cash'`, `cost_basis=mv`, `net_contribution=0`, per-row `gaps_filled`); **NEVER writes the NULL aggregate** (investment-only — it feeds /portfolio TWRR/MWRR); **skips any account with a `portfolio_holdings`/`holding_accounts` row** (Risk 1 — avoids the unique-index source-collision with `buildDailySnapshot` on a misconfigured `is_investment=false`-with-holdings account, e.g. the old demo Brokerage); clamps the start UP to the earliest cash tx (no leading zero rows) and prewarms FX per-day concurrently. Pure unit-tested `isCashStale(live, meta, today)` is the freshness linchpin. **Phase 4 — wire** (DEK-free): [rebuild.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts) builds cash over FULL history after the investment walk (decoupled from the investment `from`, which may be a recent dirty-marker date — a partial cash build would wrongly claim the watermark fresh), guarded by a parallel `__pfCashRebuildInFlight` set (`tryBeginCashRebuild`/`endCashRebuild`); the [cron](src/lib/cron/portfolio-snapshots.ts) gains a per-user **incremental** cash pass broadened to ALL cash users — fresh users roll only today's row forward + bump the watermark; stale users get a bounded 90-day refresh that **deliberately leaves the watermark stale** so the full-history chart-load self-heal owns deep/back-dated edits (no silent hiding); the [route](src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts) adds a DEK-free cash self-heal that fires when `isCashStale`. **Phase 5 — read switch + pure refactor**: the route reads `getCashSnapshotsInRange("1900-01-01", today)` + builds a live cash override from `getAccountBalances` (today substitution, matches the hero); [net-worth-history.ts](src/lib/net-worth-history.ts) drops `cashDeltas`/the current-rate cumulative fold and adds `cashSnapshots` + `liveCashByAccount`, using the **same per-account carry-forward machinery as the investment side** (a single `sumPassForDay` helper now drives both passes). `convertWithRateMap` is kept (passthrough when stored ccy == display ccy; re-bases at current rate only on a post-storage display-currency switch — the same documented value-chart discontinuity the investment side has), so `fxApproximation` stays `true`. **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · `npx vitest run tests/net-worth-history.test.ts tests/cash-snapshot-staleness.test.ts` 20/20 (rewritten cash cases pass `cashSnapshots` + new live-cash-today / CAD-stored-display-USD re-FX / combined-both-overridden cases + 9 `isCashStale` cases: insert/edit/delete/new-day/missing-meta/fresh/null-watermark/equal-instant). Migration auto-applies on deploy. Files: `scripts/migrations/20260613_cash_snapshot_meta.sql` (new), `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-builder.ts` (new), `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/cash-meta.ts` (new), `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts`, `src/lib/cron/portfolio-snapshots.ts`, `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `src/lib/net-worth-history.ts`, `tests/net-worth-history.test.ts`, `tests/cash-snapshot-staleness.test.ts` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`. plan/net-worth-cash-snapshots.md.

- **perf+feat(crypto): cache-first + historical crypto pricing for net-worth snapshots (2026-06-02, `dev`).** Closes `plan/crypto-price-caching-fix.md`. Stock/ETF (Yahoo) + FX prices were already DB-cached cache-first via `price_cache`/`fx_rates`; crypto (CoinGecko) was the exception, with two problems the new "Net Worth Over Time" rebuild/self-heal exercises heavily. **Fix #1 — cache-read-first (commit `b10554f`):** `getCryptoPrices()` always hit CoinGecko and only *wrote* `price_cache`, never read it, so a 184-day snapshot rebuild re-fetched the same crypto price ~184× (slow + rate-limit risk). New `getCryptoSpotPrices({coinId, symbol}[])` in [src/lib/crypto-service.ts](src/lib/crypto-service.ts) — the crypto analogue of `fetchMultipleQuotes`: bulk-reads today's `price_cache` (`readCryptoCacheBulk`), live-fetches only the misses via `getCryptoPrices` (which writes the cache), merges. Cache hits return `price` only (rich `change24h`/`marketCap`/`image` aren't stored — same lossiness as the stock cache path), so the two RICH single-shot consumers (`/api/portfolio/overview` day-change/marketCap/logo at lines ~457-463, `/api/portfolio/crypto`) stay on the live `getCryptoPrices` — untouched, **zero regression**. Only the price-only callers were rerouted: [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) (the rebuild hot path) + `fetchCryptoRateToUsd` in [src/lib/fx-service.ts](src/lib/fx-service.ts). Live misses are re-keyed to the caller's symbol so symbol-keyed lookups resolve even on the MATIC/POL→`matic-network` collision. **Fix #2 — historical crypto pricing (commit `552801f`):** `getHoldingsValueByAccount` valued crypto at TODAY's price for every historical snapshot day (the crypto branch ignored `asOfDate`), so the chart's crypto slice was flat-at-today while stocks/ETFs were historically accurate via `fetchMultipleQuotesAtDate`. New `getCryptoPricesAtDate(coins, date)` — reads `price_cache` for `date`; for misses fetches the whole `[date, today]` window per coin in ONE `market_chart?days=N` call, bulk-writes the strictly-past days into `price_cache` (`CRYPTO:<SYM>`, immutable, pre-filtered against existing rows since the `(symbol,date)` index is non-unique, **never writes today** — the live path owns it), re-reads. Pure `bucketDailyCryptoPrices` (last-of-day wins, skips today/future) is exported + unit-tested. The [src/lib/holdings-value.ts](src/lib/holdings-value.ts) crypto branch now mirrors the stock branch (`isToday ? getCryptoSpotPrices(...) : getCryptoPricesAtDate(..., asOfDate)`) and flows into stored snapshots via `buildDailySnapshot(asOfDate=date)`. Because the rebuild walks OLDEST-first, the oldest day's miss fetches the full window once and every later day is a cache hit → **~1 historical call per coin**; dates beyond the free-tier ~365-day limit (or an API failure) fall back to the live spot price (never worse than before). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run build` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 · `tests/crypto-spot-cache.test.ts` 9/9 (5 `splitCryptoCacheHits` + 4 `bucketDailyCryptoPrices`). Both deployed to `dev` + verified end-to-end on dev.finlynq.com via the demo: rebuild `POST /api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild` → 200 ("Rebuilt 184 days"), no console errors, `/portfolio` BTC day-change intact (−5.38% — rich path unregressed), and a read-only SSH `psql` confirmed `price_cache` now holds **183 distinct daily `CRYPTO:BTC` prices** ($86,192–$134,689 CAD, varying day-to-day — not flat-at-today). Still on `dev`; prod promotion pending. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/crypto-service.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/holdings-value.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/fx-service.ts`, `pf-app/tests/crypto-spot-cache.test.ts` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.

- **fix(seed-demo): flag the demo Brokerage as `is_investment=true` (2026-06-02).** The demo seed created the "Brokerage" account (group "Investments", 8 holdings, ~$28.5k market value) but the accounts INSERT never set `is_investment`, so it defaulted to `false`. The dashboard/accounts net-worth math keys on the `is_investment` boolean (NOT `group`), so the demo's net worth treated the brokerage as cash — counting its $9,200 cash-flow basis instead of its $28.5k holdings value — and the "Net Worth Over Time" chart showed no investment line. Added `isInvestment?` to `AccountSeed`, set `isInvestment: true` on Brokerage, and included `is_investment` in the INSERT. (The `/api/onboarding/sample-data` "Load sample data" route already did this correctly for its Brokerage + TFSA.) **Re-seeded the dev demo + verified:** net worth jumped $1,474.25 → **$21,899.07**, and the "Net Worth Over Time" chart's investment line self-healed on first view (DEK-bearing rebuild, `hasInvestmentData:true`, latest point == hero). Applies to prod on the next nightly demo reset (`finlynq-demo-reset.service`, 03:00 UTC). Files: `scripts/seed-demo.ts`.

- **feat(dashboard+accounts): Net Worth & Account Balance Over Time chart + snapshot auto-rebuild (2026-06-02).** Adds a daily, period-selectable (6M / 1Y / ALL) "Net Worth Over Time" area chart to the dashboard (always visible, after the ROW 2 metric cards) and the same chart per account as "Balance Over Time" on `/accounts/[id]`. Unlike the dev-gated `NetWorthChart` (pure `SUM(transactions.amount)` per month, which is wrong for investment accounts whose value is `holdings.value`), this is an **accurate merged series**: cash/liability accounts computed live from `transactions`, investment accounts read from the stored daily `portfolio_snapshots`, and **TODAY substituted by the live holdings aggregator so the latest point matches the dashboard hero net-worth number exactly**. **Part A (chart):** pure unit-testable core `buildNetWorthHistory` ([src/lib/net-worth-history.ts](src/lib/net-worth-history.ts), 9 vitest cases — cash carry-forward, nearest-snapshot-before, multi-ccy FX, today override, empty→`hasInvestmentData:false`, 6m/1y grid length); two queries `getCashDailyDeltas` (innerJoin accounts, `is_investment=false` + `archived=false`, per-day per-ccy deltas over ALL history so the window's first day carries the full prior cumulative) + `getInvestmentSnapshotsInRange` (per-account rows only, never the NULL aggregate) in [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts); route `GET /api/net-worth-history?period=&accountId=` ([route.ts](src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts)) mirroring the dashboard head (requireAuth → getDEK → getDisplayCurrency → getRateMap), restricting the live override to the SAME non-archived investment account set the hero sums; component [net-worth-history-chart.tsx](src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx) reusing the `NetWorthChart` gradient/axes with a 6M/1Y/ALL picker. FX uses the current-rate approximation (matches the existing chart; historical-rate cash FX is an explicit follow-up). **Part B (snapshot freshness):** the nightly snapshot cron is forward-only, so a back-dated investment edit leaves history stale. **Load-bearing constraint:** post Stream D Phase 4 holding symbols are ENCRYPTED, so pricing a holding needs the session DEK to decrypt the symbol — a DEK-less background job (cron) would mis-value stock holdings as $1/unit. So auto-rebuild is **DEK-bearing**, never a blind cron. Added (1) a shared `rebuildPortfolioSnapshots` lib ([rebuild.ts](src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild.ts), extracted from `backfill-portfolio-snapshots.ts` which now calls it) with a per-user in-flight guard (`tryBeginRebuild`/`endRebuild`); (2) a synchronous manual `POST /api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild` behind a "Rebuild investment history" button on **Settings → Investments** + the chart's empty-state ([rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx](src/components/portfolio/rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx)) — gated by `requireEncryption` (**423 if no DEK** so it never writes garbage); (3) a `portfolio_snapshot_dirty(user_id PK, from_date, marked_at)` work-queue (migration [20260612_portfolio_snapshot_dirty.sql](scripts/migrations/20260612_portfolio_snapshot_dirty.sql) + Drizzle def), stamped via `markSnapshotsDirty(userId, fromDate)` (LEAST-coalescing, swallows its own errors) co-located with `invalidateUser` on every investment write (all 8 `/api/portfolio/operations/*` routes + `/api/transactions` POST/PUT/DELETE gated on investment rows + MCP `record_trade`/`record_transaction`); (4) a **DEK-bearing self-heal on chart load** — `GET /api/net-worth-history`, which has the session DEK, fires a fire-and-forget rebuild (in-flight guarded, clears the dirty row on success) when a dirty marker exists OR the user has live investments but no snapshots yet (initial backfill on first view). **There is intentionally NO background drain cron** (it had no DEK → would write $1/unit garbage and clobber good snapshots). The latest chart point is always correct regardless of snapshot freshness (it's the live holdings aggregator with the request DEK). `getInvestmentSnapshotsInRange` is `accounts.is_investment = true`-filtered so the cash pass (`is_investment=false`) and snapshot pass stay disjoint — without it a misconfigured `is_investment=false`-account-with-holdings (e.g. the demo's Brokerage) would be double-counted after a rebuild. **Audit invariant #11 `investment-write-marks-snapshots-dirty`** (scoped to `src/app/api/portfolio/operations/`) backstops that every op route co-locating `invalidateUser` also calls `markSnapshotsDirty` — 8/8 PASS. **Two pre-existing snapshot-builder bugs fixed in `buildDailySnapshot` (surfaced by the rebuild button):** (a) the per-account INSERT used Drizzle `onConflictDoUpdate` targeting bare `(user_id, snap_date, account_id)`, but the unique index is the EXPRESSION index `(user_id, snap_date, COALESCE(account_id, -1))` — Postgres rejected the ON CONFLICT ("no matching constraint"), which threw on the first per-account row and aborted the whole build, so **NO snapshots were ever written** (the long-standing "No snapshots yet" state). Rewrote it as raw SQL with the COALESCE conflict target, mirroring the aggregate insert. (b) Once (a) lets the write succeed, a DEK-less caller would write `$1/unit` garbage (encrypted symbols can't be priced) and clobber good snapshots — so `buildDailySnapshot` now **no-ops without a DEK**, making the nightly `runSnapshotsCron` + the backfill script inert (they couldn't price correctly anyway). Snapshots are built only by the DEK-bearing manual button + chart-load self-heal. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean · `npm run audit:invariants` 11/11 PASS · new vitest suite 8/8 · `npm run build` exit 0. Verified on dev via the demo: dashboard + per-account charts render, latest point == hero ($1,474.25) to the cent; manual rebuild surfaced + fixed the UPSERT bug. plan/net-worth-over-time.md. Files: `src/lib/net-worth-history.ts`, `tests/net-worth-history.test.ts`, `src/lib/queries.ts`, `src/app/api/net-worth-history/route.ts`, `src/components/net-worth-history-chart.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/rebuild-snapshots-button.tsx`, `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/{builder,dirty,rebuild}.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/snapshots/rebuild/route.ts`, `src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `scripts/migrations/20260612_portfolio_snapshot_dirty.sql`, `src/instrumentation.ts`, `scripts/backfill-portfolio-snapshots.ts`, `scripts/audit-invariants.ts`, `src/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/settings/investments/page.tsx`, the 8 `src/app/api/portfolio/operations/*/route.ts`, `src/app/api/transactions/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`.

- **feat(portfolio): show per-holding day-change dollar value inline beside the % (2026-06-02).** Follow-up to the FINLYNQ-92 `chartPreviousClose` fix below. The per-holding day change previously rendered only a percentage (`<ChangeBadge>`) in Top Gainers / Top Losers and the per-account "Day Chg" column, while only the "Day Change" hero card showed a dollar figure. Now every per-holding day-change shows the dollar amount inline next to the percentage (e.g. `↑2.07% +$43.89`), color-matched, in the display currency. **API** ([src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts)) adds `dayChangeDisplay` per holding = `(change-per-unit × qty)` FX-converted to the display currency — computed identically to each holding's contribution to `summary.dayChangeDisplay`, so the per-holding dollars reconcile to the hero total; `null` when there's no live change (mirrors the `holdingsWithChange` filter). **UI** ([src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/portfolio/page.tsx)) adds a `DayChange` component (% badge + inline dollar) replacing the three per-holding `<ChangeBadge value={h.changePct} />` spots; `EnrichedHolding` gains `dayChangeDisplay`. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run audit:invariants` 10/10 PASS. Verified on dev via the demo account (Top Gainers/Losers + per-account Day Chg column). Commits `0993387` (initial, dollar stacked below) + `33774a5` (moved inline beside the %). Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx`.
- **feat(onboarding): rich "Load sample data" seed — portfolio, dividends, goals, loans, subscriptions, budgets (2026-06-02).** `POST /api/onboarding/sample-data` ([src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts](src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts)) previously seeded only 12 categories + a single Chequing account + ~3 months of basic transactions — leaving the mobile Portfolio/Dividends/Goals screens empty after a one-tap "Load sample data". The route now generates a full, demo-grade dataset for the calling user (encrypted with their session DEK), so the mobile **and** web testing surfaces all light up: **5 accounts** (Chequing, Savings, Visa Rewards liability, plus `is_investment=true` **Brokerage** + **TFSA**), **15 categories** (incl. the load-bearing **Dividends** income category the dividend report resolves by HMAC name lookup), **~6 months** of believable cash-flow on the non-investment accounts (salary, rent, utilities, groceries, dining, transit, fuel, subscriptions, entertainment, shopping, savings/CC transfers), a **funded portfolio** (cash sleeves + brokerage deposits, **8 holdings** incl. VTI/VOO/VXUS/AAPL/BTC/VFV.TO/XEQT.TO/VAB.TO, **8 buys + 2 sells** for realized-gain/lot-closure coverage, **12 cash dividends** across holdings & quarters), **3 goals**, **2 loans** (auto + student), **5 subscriptions**, and **6 current-month budgets**. Routed buys/sells/deposits through the canonical `recordBuy`/`recordSell`/`recordBrokerageDeposit` helpers in [operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) so cash-leg pairing + inline lot/closure wiring come for free (no separate `buildLotsForUser` pass) and invariant #8 stays satisfied. **Idempotent:** regular transactions dedup on a deterministic `import_hash` (pseudo-random jitter, not `Math.random`); the portfolio block is gated on "no existing holdings"; goals/loans/subscriptions gated on "none exist"; budgets skip already-budgeted categories — so re-running never duplicates. Each enrichment block is independently try/caught into a `warnings[]` array so one failing block degrades gracefully instead of 500-ing the whole seed. Response keeps the top-level `transactionsCreated` field the mobile client reads and adds a `summary` + optional `warnings`. The mobile app needs **no change** — its existing "Load sample data" button (`endpoints.loadSampleData`) calls this route; the richer data ships when `dev` deploys to the web backend. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run audit:invariants` 10/10 PASS. File: `pf-app/src/app/api/onboarding/sample-data/route.ts`.

- **fix(portfolio): fall back to `meta.chartPreviousClose` when Yahoo omits `previousClose` on closed markets (FINLYNQ-92 follow-up, 2026-06-02).** The FINLYNQ-92 day-change badge (commit `8bc8e02`) was broken for every stock/ETF: each `price_cache` row written while the regular US session was closed got `previous_close = NULL`, so `deriveDayChange()` short-circuited to `0/0`. Root cause — Yahoo's `/v8/finance/chart` API **omits `meta.previousClose` whenever the session is closed** (weekends, exchange holidays, AND pre-/after-hours), returning only `meta.chartPreviousClose`. `fetchQuoteLive` read `meta.previousClose ?? null` only. This bit on weekdays too: the demo's nightly reset/first fetch ran pre-market, cached NULL rows, and those rows are sticky for the whole calendar day. Crypto badges were unaffected because `/api/portfolio/overview` reads crypto change live from CoinGecko, bypassing `price_cache`. **Symptoms:** every stock day-change badge rendered `--`, the "Top Gainers" card disappeared and "Top Losers" showed only crypto, and the "Day Change" hero reflected only the crypto sleeve's move (all stocks contributed $0). **Fix** ([pf-app/src/lib/price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts)) — extracted a pure exported `resolvePreviousClose(meta)` helper = `meta?.previousClose ?? meta?.chartPreviousClose ?? null` and called it in `fetchQuoteLive`; `deriveDayChange` exported for testing. For a `range=1d` request the two Yahoo fields are identical in-session, so weekday/in-session behavior is unchanged and the closed-market gap closes. No new migration (the `20260522_price-cache-previous-close.sql` column already exists); the deploy is paired with a one-shot `DELETE FROM price_cache WHERE date=today AND previous_close IS NULL` so today's sticky NULL rows re-fetch under the new code. **Tests:** new [tests/price-service.test.ts](tests/price-service.test.ts) — 7 cases covering previousClose-present (prefers it), previousClose-absent+chartPreviousClose-present (falls back, non-zero change), both-absent (degrades to 0/0), and the `0`-close edge (`??` not `||`). `npx vitest run tests/price-service.test.ts` 7/7 PASS; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/price-service.ts`, `pf-app/tests/price-service.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`. Hand-off: workspace [HANDOVER_2026-06-02_STOCK_DAYCHANGE_WEEKEND_FALLBACK.md](../HANDOVER_2026-06-02_STOCK_DAYCHANGE_WEEKEND_FALLBACK.md).
- **feat(feedback): two-way reply threads — admin ↔ user follow-up channel (2026-06-02, web-only on `dev`).** Extends the one-way feedback feature so the admin can reply to a user's feedback and the user can reply back. **In-app only** (no reply emails either direction): the user is notified via a new "Your feedback" nav badge; the admin via an admin-unread dot on `/admin/feedback`. **Data model** — new `feedback_messages` table (`id, feedback_id FK→feedback ON DELETE CASCADE, author_role 'user'|'admin', author_id, body, created_at`, plaintext, same rationale as `feedback.message`) + two read-tracking columns `feedback.user_last_read_at` / `admin_last_read_at`. The original submission stays the immutable thread SEED in `feedback.message` — **NOT backfilled into `feedback_messages`** (admin-unread keys on `author_role='user'` rows, so a backfilled seed would make every thread look like an unread reply). Unread = a message from the *other* role newer than that side's `*_last_read_at` (NULL → epoch); posting a reply bumps the author's own `*_last_read_at`. Migration [scripts/migrations/20260611_feedback_threads.sql](scripts/migrations/20260611_feedback_threads.sql) (purely additive `IF NOT EXISTS`, sorts after `20260610_*`). **Routes** (all reuse existing guards; ownership → 404 not 403): user `GET /api/feedback` (bare-array thread summaries w/ `unread`), `GET /api/feedback/[id]` (omits `adminNote`), `POST /api/feedback/[id]/read`, `POST /api/feedback/[id]/reply` (distinct rate-limit bucket `feedback-reply:` 30/hr vs submit's `feedback:` 10/hr); admin `GET /api/admin/feedback/[id]` (marks `admin_last_read_at`, includes `adminNote` + submitter identity), `POST /api/admin/feedback/[id]/reply` (audit action `feedback_replied` added to `AdminAuditAction` — no SQL, `admin_audit.action` is free-form text; soft-promotes `new→triaged` only, never auto-resolve/reopen). Admin list `GET /api/admin/feedback` gains `replyCount` + `adminUnread`. Shared helper [src/lib/feedback/thread.ts](src/lib/feedback/thread.ts) (`buildThreadSummary` owns the unread predicate; `toFeedbackMessage`). **UI** — new user page [src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx) (thread cards → Dialog with message bubbles + reply box, marks read on open); new "Your feedback" nav item with an unread badge mirroring `/whats-new` ([nav.tsx](src/components/nav.tsx): `feedbackUnread` state + generalized `unreadFor(href)` helper); [src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx) gains a thread/reply Dialog + per-card admin-unread dot, with the inline note relabeled "Admin note (private)" vs the user-visible reply. Shared types `FeedbackMessage` / `FeedbackThreadSummary` / `FeedbackThread` + `FeedbackStatus` in `shared/types.ts`. **Mobile parity deferred** to a separate `mobile-dev` commit (the API + types here are what mobile will consume). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run audit:invariants` 10/10 PASS. Lands on `dev` alongside the un-promoted `20260610_*` feedback/announcements migrations — promote together. Files: `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260611_feedback_threads.sql` (new), `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/shared/types.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/feedback/thread.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/admin-audit.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/feedback/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/feedback/[id]/{route,read/route,reply/route}.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/api/admin/feedback/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/admin/feedback/[id]/reply/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/feedback/page.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/admin/feedback/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/nav.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`. Hand-off: workspace [HANDOVER_2026-06-01_ANNOUNCEMENTS_FEEDBACK.md](../HANDOVER_2026-06-01_ANNOUNCEMENTS_FEEDBACK.md) "Two-way reply threads" section.
- **feat(crypto): encrypt free-text notes + transaction-rule sensitive fields at rest (2026-06-01).** Closes the plaintext-at-rest gaps from `plan/encryption-plaintext-gaps.md`. Before this, the encrypted scope was only `transactions.{payee,note,tags}`, `transaction_splits.{note,description,tags}`, and the Stream D name columns — every other free-text column (notes + rule patterns) was plaintext, so a DB-only leak could read it (and it caused the mobile cold-DEK symptom: payees stayed readable because legacy/stdio rows were plaintext, needing no key). **(0) Shared helpers** — `encryptOptional`/`decryptOptional` ([encrypted-columns.ts](src/lib/crypto/encrypted-columns.ts)), the `USER_ENCRYPTED_COLUMNS` registry ([user-encrypted-registry.ts](src/lib/crypto/user-encrypted-registry.ts)), and `encryptRuleFields`/`decryptRuleFields`/`ruleHasPlaintext` ([rules/crypto.ts](src/lib/rules/crypto.ts)). **(1-2) Note columns** — `loans.note`, `goals.note`, `snapshots.note`, `subscriptions.notes`, `fx_overrides.note`, `contribution_room.note`, `recurring_transactions.{payee,note}`, plus MCP-write-only `accounts.note`/`categories.note` encrypt on write + decrypt on read across the 5 REST routes + MCP HTTP add/update/list tools. **(3-4) Rules** — rule `name` + payee/note/tags condition values + `rename_payee.to`/`set_tags.tags` encrypted on write (REST `/api/rules`, MCP `create_rule`/`update_rule`, staged inline create-rule) + display, and **decrypted before every matcher callsite**: the linchpin `loadActiveRulesForReconcile(userId, dek)` in [match-engine.ts](src/lib/reconcile/match-engine.ts) (DEK param added; stale "JSONB not encrypted" comment corrected), plus apply-to-staged-batch, import-pipeline, MCP `apply_rules_to_uncategorized`/`suggest_transaction_details`. Currency codes / FK ids / ops / amounts / dates stay plaintext so matching + FK guards still work; a wrong/null DEK yields a non-matching needle, not a crash. **(5) Login sweep** — `upgradeUserFieldEncryption` ([upgrade-user-fields.ts](src/lib/crypto/upgrade-user-fields.ts)) runs every login (fire-and-forget at login / mfa-verify / try-demo) and converts remaining legacy/stdio/email-import plaintext to `v1:`; idempotent, double-encrypt-guarded, never touches `import_hash`/`name_lookup`. **(6) Guardrail** — audit invariant #10 `note-columns-encrypted` (stdio `register-core-tools` baseline-excepted — no DEK, sweep cleans up). stdio MCP can't match encrypted rules (no DEK) — parked until a self-hosted server-held key. **Verification:** `tsc --noEmit` clean, `audit:invariants` 10/10 PASS, 69 vitest cases pass (4 new test files + 45 existing matcher cases), `npm run build` clean. **Shipped to `dev` (`04a1fdf`+`8dd0516`) then promoted to prod 2026-06-02 (full `dev→main` merge `047d314`).** Validated on both envs via the demo account + SSH read-only: the login sweep flips seeded plaintext notes/rules to `v1:` at rest on login while reads serve decrypted, and an encrypted rule still fires auto-categorization. Real-user prod plaintext backlog (72 payees / 18 notes / 1 fx note / 3 rule names) clears as each user next logs in.
- **fix(deploy): atomic `.next` swap to avoid an rm race with the live service (2026-06-01).** Re-applies the fix originally proposed in [PR #202](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/pull/202) (which went stale, ~245 commits behind, and was closed in favour of this). The running pf-dev/pf service writes runtime fetch-cache files into `.next/standalone/.next/cache/fetch-cache/` on every outbound HTTP fetch (FX rates, Yahoo prices, etc.). `deploy.sh`'s in-place `rm -rf .next` could fail with `Directory not empty` when new fetch-cache entries appeared mid-traversal — after already deleting `server.js`, leaving the env serving 500 on every page until the next successful deploy. Fix in [deploy.sh](deploy.sh): swap `rm -rf .next` for `mv .next .next.old.$$ && rm -rf .next.old.$$`. The `mv` is atomic and the running service keeps its open inodes through the rename, so the `rm` operates on a detached tree nobody is writing to. Adds a belt-and-suspenders sweep of orphaned `.next.old.*` trees from a deploy that died between the mv and the rm.
- **refactor(mobile): align the React Native (Expo) companion app to the current account-login backend (2026-05-29).** The `pf-app/mobile/` app was stale against the backend and partially broken. **(1) Removed the dead self-hosted passphrase/unlock path.** [mobile/src/api/client.ts](mobile/src/api/client.ts) dropped `getUnlockStatus`/`unlock`/`lock` (the `/api/auth/unlock` endpoint was removed server-side) and [useAuth.ts](mobile/src/hooks/useAuth.ts) dropped the `serverMode`/passphrase/`needsSetup` machinery. `GET /api/auth/session` is now the single identity source — the new top-level `getSession()` client helper validates a stored session JWT (sent as a Bearer token) on launch. **(2) Fixed the auth payloads.** Login now sends `{ identifier, password }` (username OR email) instead of `{ email, password }`; register sends `{ username, email?, password, displayName?, acknowledgeNoRecovery? }` (username required, email optional recovery channel, no-recovery acknowledgement when email is blank) — matching `/cloud` on the web. Password placeholder corrected from "8+" to "at least 12 characters" per [password-policy.ts](src/lib/auth/password-policy.ts). **(3) Collapsed the two-mode selector.** With both hosted and self-hosted backends now using account login, the `ModeSelectScreen` (self-hosted vs cloud) was deleted; the app boots straight into a single **set-server-URL + sign-in** flow ([LoginScreen.tsx](mobile/src/screens/LoginScreen.tsx) gained a collapsible Server field for first-run, defaulting to `https://finlynq.com`). The passphrase `UnlockScreen` was replaced with a biometric-only `LockScreen` (Face ID / fingerprint gate over a held session token; no passphrase). **(4) Lifted `useAuth` into a shared `AuthProvider` context** ([App.tsx](mobile/App.tsx)) so the navigator and the Settings screen read one instance — previously each got an independent hook, so signing out from Settings never returned the app to the login screen. Auto-lock only re-locks when biometrics are enabled (otherwise it would strand the user). **(5) Rebranded "PF" → "Finlynq"** across screens, About, biometric prompt, and `app.json` `name`. **Aligned to** [docs/mobile-setup.md](docs/mobile-setup.md) (account-login model, no passphrase), which was updated to note the sign-in-screen Server field + the auto-lock/biometric coupling. Tests rewritten (`apiClient`, `navigation`, `SettingsScreen`; `UnlockScreen.test` → `LockScreen.test`). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npx jest` 9 suites / 81 tests pass. Mobile is a client-only app and is NOT part of the web CI/CD deploy pipeline. Files: `mobile/App.tsx`, `mobile/app.json`, `mobile/src/api/client.ts`, `mobile/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`, `mobile/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`, `mobile/src/screens/{LoginScreen,LockScreen,SettingsScreen}.tsx` (LockScreen new; `UnlockScreen.tsx` + `ModeSelectScreen.tsx` deleted), `mobile/src/__tests__/{apiClient,navigation,SettingsScreen,LockScreen}.test.*` (LockScreen new; UnlockScreen test deleted), `shared/types.ts` (`UnlockStatus` → `SessionInfo` + `RegisterPayload`), `pf-app/docs/mobile-setup.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(portfolio): Phase 5c cash-sleeve lot tracking + 3 follow-up cleanups (2026-05-26, commit `d8bc935`).** Closes the four open items from [HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PORTFOLIO_OPS_FOLLOWUPS.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PORTFOLIO_OPS_FOLLOWUPS.md). **(1) Issue #128 aggregator skip — Phase 2 update.** Five callsites (REST overview, MCP `accumulate()`, MCP `analyze_holding`, snapshot builder, performance contributions reader) updated their cash-leg skip predicate from `tradeLinkId IS NOT NULL AND amount = 0` to `kind IN ('buy_cash_leg', 'sell_cash_leg') OR (tradeLinkId IS NOT NULL AND amount = 0)`. Phase 2 cash legs carry `qty != 0, amount != 0` so the original predicate no longer matched — they were phantom-counting as buys (sell_cash_leg, qty>0) or sells (buy_cash_leg, qty<0) on the cash sleeve. Applied symmetrically to BOTH the buy- and sell-side branches (previously only sell had the guard). Legacy half of the predicate stays for pre-Phase-2 backfilled rows where `kind` is NULL. 2 new Phase 2 test cases (tc-5, tc-6) added to `tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts`. **(2) Short-position UI badges.** `/portfolio` renders a red `Short` badge + rose-coloured Total Qty for net-negative holdings (main table + per-account + by-account views). `/portfolio/realized-gains` adds `CLOSE_KIND_META` icon+badge map: `short_open` → `ArrowDownLeft`/rose, `short_close` → `ArrowUpLeft`/amber, `swap_out` → `RefreshCw`/sky, `fx_conversion` → `Coins`/violet/label `"Currency"`. **(3) base-ui `<Select>` first-click polish.** All 8 portfolio forms now pass `alignItemWithTrigger={false} side="bottom"` on `SelectContent` — popup opens below the trigger instead of stacking on top, eliminates the "first-click revert" caused by `itemPress` re-firing on the already-selected option. **(4) Cash-sleeve lot tracking (Phase 5c).** New module `src/lib/portfolio/lots/cash-hooks.ts` (`openCashLotHook`, `closeCashLotsHook`, `inferCashCloseKind`). Per-inflow model: lots open at `costPerShare=1` in sleeve currency; FIFO outflows close them with `proceedsPerShare=1`. In-sleeve realized gain always 0; FX gain in base currency comes from `augmentWithBaseCurrency()`'s historical FX lookups at openDate vs closeDate. `LotContext` extended with `isCashHoldingById`; `applyLotEffectsForTx` routes `is_cash=true` holdings to cash hooks (replaces the prior `_cash_leg` skip). `applyLotEffectsForLinkPair` for FX-conversion pairs writes `closeKind='fx_conversion'` on source + a fresh lot on dest. Six `operations.ts` recordX paths wired their cash legs (Buy/Sell, Income/Expense, Brokerage Deposit/Withdrawal, FX fee). `reverseLotsForDeleteHook` continues to work via `openTxId` / `closeTxId` lookups — cash + stock lots share the same tables. Pre-Phase-5c historical cash-sleeve activity has no lots; closures on those sleeves log `[portfolio.lots.cash]` shortfall warnings until a backfill script ships (design state in [HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PHASE5C_AND_BACKFILL_PLAN.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PHASE5C_AND_BACKFILL_PLAN.md)). **Verification:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run audit:invariants` 8/8 PASS (`portfolio-ops-kind-via-operations` count 14 → 19), `npx vitest run tests/cash-hooks-pure.test.ts` 4/4 PASS. Demo user re-seeded on dev (262 txs, 8 lots, 1 closure, 1 cash sleeve). Files: `src/lib/portfolio/lots/cash-hooks.ts` (new), `tests/cash-hooks-pure.test.ts` (new), `src/lib/portfolio/lots/write-hooks.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/lots/fx-conversion.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/snapshots/builder.ts`, `src/lib/portfolio/performance/contributions.ts`, `src/app/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts`, `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/page.tsx`, `src/app/(app)/portfolio/realized-gains/page.tsx`, `src/components/portfolio/forms/{Buy,Sell,Swap,Withdrawal,Deposit,FxConversion,Transfer,IncomeExpense}Form.tsx`, `tests/helpers/portfolio-fixtures.ts`, `tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts`. Hand-off: [HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PHASE5C_AND_BACKFILL_PLAN.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-26_PHASE5C_AND_BACKFILL_PLAN.md).

- **fix(portfolio): sign convention + cascade delete + edit-as-replace + add-tx dropdown — Phase 2 follow-up 5-bug-fix sweep (2026-05-25).** User-reported issues after first Phase 2 verification round. (1) **Sign convention** ([operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts)) — stock leg `amount` now POSITIVE (book value of acquisition for a Buy, depletion for a Sell); cash leg `amount` carries the cash effect (negative for Buy, positive for Sell). Sum across both legs = 0 (internal swap). Pre-fix the stock leg was displaying as e.g. "AAPL: -$2000" in the ledger which confused users — Buy is a positive acquisition event. Lot engine uses `Math.abs()` so cost-basis / realized-gain math is unchanged. Added `kind` field to `TxRowForLots` + guard in `applyLotEffectsForTx` so post-sign-flip cash-leg rows (`kind: "buy_cash_leg" | "sell_cash_leg"`) don't get misclassified by the qty-sign dispatch and spam `closeLotsForSellHook` shortfall warnings. **Existing wrong-signed rows on dev are NOT migrated** per user direction — only new transactions use the new convention. (2) **Cascade delete** ([/api/transactions](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts) DELETE) — builds the full set of siblings sharing `trade_link_id` (buy/sell cash-leg pairs) or `link_id` (in-kind transfers, FX conversions) and deletes them in a single pass. Edit guard runs against every row in the set; blocking-tx ids exclude rows already in the delete set so the message stays actionable. Response carries `{success, deletedIds[], cascaded}`. (3) **Add Transaction split button** ([transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx)) — main click opens the quick Transaction dialog (unchanged); chevron opens a dropdown listing Transaction / Transfer / ─── / Buy / Sell / Swap / In-kind / Income / FX. Portfolio items navigate to `/portfolio/new?op=<key>`. (4) **Edit-click routing** (`startEdit`) — clicking Edit on a row with `kind` in the portfolio-ops set routes to `/portfolio/new?op=<op>&editId=N` instead of opening the generic dialog (which would silently break the pair). For `*_cash_leg` and `fx_*` / `in_kind_*` siblings, the editor resolves back to the primary leg via `trade_link_id` / `link_id` on the load endpoint. (5) **Edit-as-replace** in the 6 forms + new load endpoint: new [GET /api/portfolio/operations/load?id=N](src/app/api/portfolio/operations/load/route.ts) returns the operation-shaped data the form needs to prefill (decrypted payee/note/tags), dispatching by `kind` to the right shape. All 6 operation POST routes accept an optional `editId` — when present, runs new `cascadeDeleteForReplace` shared helper at [operations/_helpers.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/operations/_helpers.ts) which cascade-deletes the existing pair (with edit-guard refusal returning 409 + `blockingClosureTxIds[]`) before creating the new pair; response carries `replaced: <editId>`. BuyForm / SellForm / IncomeExpenseForm / TransferForm / FxConversionForm detect `?editId=N` in URL, fetch + prefill, swap card title to "Edit `<Op>`" + button to "Save edit", surface 409 `portfolio_edit_blocked` with the blocking tx ids as deep links. SwapForm is notice-only — "Swap edits aren't supported via this form — delete the original sell + buy separately" (swaps are two unlinked op pairs internally). **Surface additions**: `transactions.kind` + `tradeLinkId` exposed on GET `/api/transactions` so the client can branch on them for edit routing. **Verified on dev (demo user)**: Buy 10 AAPL × $2000 USD → stock leg `qty=+10, amount=+$2000`; cash leg `qty=-2000, amount=-$2000`; DELETE one leg removes both (`deletedIds: [39488,39489]`); Add Transaction dropdown visible; Edit click on sell row → `/portfolio/new?op=sell&editId=39486` with "Edit Sell" + prefilled fields + "Save edit"; POST with `editId` returns `{replaced, ...newIds}` and old pair gone. `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors; `npm run audit:invariants` 8/8 PASS; `npm run build` green. Commit `ba98a61` — 20 files changed, +1383/-100. New file: `src/app/api/portfolio/operations/load/route.ts`. Hand-off: workspace [HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P2.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P2.md) "Phase 2 follow-up: 5-bug-fix sweep" section.
- **feat(portfolio): GET /api/portfolio/lots — unblock SellForm LotPicker (Phase 2 follow-up, 2026-05-25).** New [/api/portfolio/lots](src/app/api/portfolio/lots/route.ts) endpoint lists lot rows for a holding so the Phase 2 SellForm's `LotPicker` (which previously fell through to FIFO with a "coming soon" notice) starts populating with real lots. Request: `GET /api/portfolio/lots?holdingId=N[&accountId=M][&openOnly=1]`. Response: `{success: true, data: {lots: LotRow[]}}` where each LotRow carries `lotId / holdingId / accountId / openTxId / openDate / qtyOriginal / qtyRemaining / qty (alias of qtyRemaining for picker compat) / costPerShare / costBasis (precomputed) / currency / origin / status / parentLotId`. `openOnly=1` filters to `status='open' AND qty_remaining > 0`. Rows returned in FIFO order (open_date ASC, id ASC) so the picker matches the engine's depletion order. Auth: `requireAuth` (no DEK — lot rows carry no encrypted fields); cross-tenant scoping via userId predicate. Verified on dev with demo user: `GET /api/portfolio/lots?holdingId=561&openOnly=1` returns the open VOO lot with qty=6 (4 sold across prior closures), costPerShare=$480, costBasis=$2880, origin=backfill. `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors; `npm run audit:invariants` 8/8 PASS; `npm run build` green. Commit `814d004`. File: `pf-app/src/app/api/portfolio/lots/route.ts` (new, 140 LOC).
- **feat(portfolio): operation forms + API routes + cash-sleeve UI + edit guard — Phase 2 of the portfolio-ops refactor (2026-05-25).** Wires Phase 1's six operation helpers (commit `54af299`) into the user-facing flow. Six new thin-shell API routes under [/api/portfolio/operations/](src/app/api/portfolio/operations/) (`buy`, `sell`, `swap`, `transfer`, `income-expense`, `fx-conversion`) — each `requireEncryption` + Zod-validates + delegates to the matching helper in [src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts](src/lib/portfolio/operations.ts) + `invalidateUserTxCache` + maps domain errors to structured 4xx via [_helpers.ts](src/app/api/portfolio/operations/_helpers.ts) (`CashSleeveNotFoundError` → 400 with `accountId/currency`, `CurrencyMismatchError` → 400, `HoldingNotFoundError` → 404, `InvalidLinkPairError` → 400). Cash-sleeve provisioning at [/api/portfolio/holdings/cash-sleeve](src/app/api/portfolio/holdings/cash-sleeve/route.ts) — POST creates a per-currency sleeve (`is_cash=true` + dual-writes `holding_accounts`); DELETE refuses with 409 + `transactionCount` when any transaction references the sleeve; partial unique index enforces one sleeve per `(account, currency)`. UI: new [/portfolio/new](src/app/(app)/portfolio/new/page.tsx) chooser page (Suspense-wrapped per Next 16) + 6 form components at [src/components/portfolio/forms/](src/components/portfolio/forms/) (`BuyForm` 465 LOC, `SellForm` 509, `LotPicker` 184, `SwapForm` 512, `TransferForm` 412, `IncomeExpenseForm` 506, `FxConversionForm` 523 — ~3.3K LOC total). Deep-link via `?op=<key>`. Forms fetch `/api/accounts` + `/api/portfolio`, filter accounts to `isInvestment=true`, filter holdings by `accountId` + `isCash`, resolve the matching cash sleeve client-side and prompt the user to create one when missing. SellForm has an optional `LotPicker` that falls through to FIFO gracefully when `/api/portfolio/lots` doesn't exist (it doesn't yet). Account-detail page ([accounts/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx)) gains a "Cash sleeves" panel — list per-currency sleeves with tx counts, "Add sleeve" dialog (currency picker from `SUPPORTED_FIAT_CURRENCIES`, defaults to account currency), delete button disabled when sleeve has transactions. Generic Add Transaction + Transfer dialogs ([transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx)) now show a yellow advisory banner when the picked account(s) are investment, pointing users to `/portfolio/new` — banner is non-blocking, existing flows still work for back-compat (full strip is a follow-up once `/portfolio/new` proves stable). Edit-guard wiring on [/api/transactions](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts) PUT + DELETE returns 409 + `code: "portfolio_edit_blocked"` + `blockingClosureTxIds[]` when the row opens a lot with downstream closures (sell or transfer-out); delete-confirm dialog surfaces blocking ids as deep links so the user can navigate + delete dependents first. `getOrCreateCashHolding` in [investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) now tags new sleeves with `isCash: true` going forward (Phase 1 migration backfilled existing rows). `getPortfolioHoldings` in [queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) exposes `isCash` on GET so client forms can filter sleeves vs securities without a separate fetch. One-off backfill script [scripts/backfill-buy-sell-cash-legs.ts](scripts/backfill-buy-sell-cash-legs.ts) (531 LOC) mints the paired `buy_cash_leg`/`sell_cash_leg` siblings for legacy single-row buys/sells — uses raw `pg.Pool` (no Drizzle bootstrap), idempotent, `--dry-run`, `--user-id=<uuid>`, per-user `BEGIN…COMMIT`, handles NULL `trade_link_id` (mints UUID + updates source row's `updated_at`), missing cash sleeve (auto-creates + dual-writes pairing), legacy `is_cash=false` sleeves (flips to true), `source='backup_restore'` on cash legs; **NOT yet applied to dev** — separate manual application after Phase 2 deploys clean. Audit invariant #8 (`portfolio-ops-kind-via-operations`) gains a documented exception for the backfill script (raw-SQL one-off, TypeScript union-type annotation false-positive). Lot-selection enum is `"FIFO" | "HIFO" | "SPECIFIC"` (HIFO = Highest-In-First-Out — Phase 1 hand-off incorrectly mentioned LIFO; corrected in Phase 2 docs + the API route's Zod schema). `npm run audit:invariants` 8/8 PASS; `npx tsc --noEmit` 0 errors; `npm run build` green. Files (23 changed in pf-app, +4805/-13): the API routes + forms + `/portfolio/new/page.tsx` + helpers above + [accounts/[id]/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/[id]/page.tsx) + [transactions/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/transactions/page.tsx) + [transactions/route.ts](src/app/api/transactions/route.ts) + [investment-account.ts](src/lib/investment-account.ts) + [queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) + [audit-invariants.ts](scripts/audit-invariants.ts) + [backfill-buy-sell-cash-legs.ts](scripts/backfill-buy-sell-cash-legs.ts) + this CHANGELOG + workspace `CLAUDE.md` + `HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P2.md`. Hand-offs: workspace [HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P1.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P1.md) (Phase 1) + [HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P2.md](../HANDOVER_2026-05-25_PORTFOLIO_OPS_P2.md) (Phase 2). Commits: `54af299` (Phase 1) + `56956d3` (Phase 2).
- **refactor(sign-category): convert hard reject to advisory warning across REST + MCP + import (FINLYNQ-97, 2026-05-23).** The sign-vs-category check from issue #212 is no longer a gate — it still fires on every tx-write callsite but the result is surfaced as an advisory message and the row lands. Pre-FINLYNQ-97 the validator was hard-rejecting legitimate cases like a refund booked against the original Groceries expense category (`+50` against an `E`-type row), forcing users to either flip the sign (wrong semantically) or pick a different category (wrong audit trail). Six caller groups touched: (1) **REST** `/api/transactions` POST/PUT — drops the `instanceof SignCategoryMismatchError → 400` branches; validator runs at the route boundary (POST inline; PUT computes post-merge amount/category by reading the existing row when the patch is partial); non-null result attaches a top-level `warning: string` to the 200/201 body. (2) **`src/lib/queries.ts` `createTransaction` / `updateTransaction`** — drops the inline validator calls + throws; `updateTransaction`'s `needsCurrent` predicate simplifies to only the investment-account FK pre-read; `dek` param kept on both for callsite compat (now unused — `void dek`). (3) **HTTP MCP `record_transaction`** ([register-tools-pg.ts](pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts) line 2839) — swaps `return err(sErr.message)` for `warnings.push(sErr.message)` after the existing `deriveTxWriteWarnings` array is declared. (4) **HTTP MCP `bulk_record_transactions`** (line 3290) — per-row `results.push({success:false, code:"sign_category_mismatch"})` becomes per-row `rowWarnings.push(sErr.message)`; row still inserts, `results[i].success` stays `true`. (5) **HTTP MCP `update_transaction`** (line 3672) — `return err` becomes `signWarnUpdate = sErr.message`; appended to `data.warnings[]` on the success envelope. (6) **Stdio MCP `record_transaction` + `update_transaction`** — introduce `const warnings: string[] = []` at the top of each handler; swap `return sqliteErr(sErr.message)` for `warnings.push(sErr.message)`; surface `warnings` in `data` envelope (no DEK → message degrades to `category #<id>`). (7) **Import pipeline** ([import-pipeline.ts](pf-app/src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) line 717) — deletes the `signRejected` splice block so mis-signed rows materialize; the per-row push into `importErrors[]` is reworded as `"Warning: Row N — category Y (type Z) and amount sign (X) disagree; imported anyway. Flip the sign in the editor if this was an error."`. The validator helpers (`validateSignVsCategory` / `validateSignVsCategoryById` / `getCategoryTypeMap`) and `SignCategoryMismatchError` class stay exported with byte-identical behavior — only callers change. File header rewritten on [sign-category-invariant.ts](pf-app/src/lib/transactions/sign-category-invariant.ts) to describe the helper as advisory with a transport-by-transport surface map. `SignCategoryMismatchError` import dropped from `register-tools-pg.ts` (line 100) and `route.ts` (line 12); `validateSignVsCategoryById` import added to the REST route. **Audit invariant**: `scripts/audit-invariants.ts` BASELINE_EXCEPTIONS gets `src/lib/queries.ts:sign-vs-category` because the validator now lives at the route boundary — the grep at the inner helper would otherwise fail. The other 12 audited callsites still call `validateSignVsCategory` (the grep doesn't care whether the result throws or pushes warnings). Workspace `CLAUDE.md` "Sign-vs-category invariant" load-bearing gotcha bullet rewritten to describe the advisory surface. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0 (13 callsites OK); `npm run build` green; `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/transactions/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/queries.ts`, `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `pf-app/mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/import-pipeline.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/transactions/sign-category-invariant.ts`, `pf-app/scripts/audit-invariants.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(health-score): shared calculator + info dialog + restore Age of Money (FINLYNQ-94, 2026-05-23).** Single source of truth for the financial-health score lives at [pf-app/src/lib/financial-health.ts](src/lib/financial-health.ts) (`calculateFinancialHealth({ db, userId, dek, reportingCurrency })`). Promoted from the inline MCP tool body at `register-tools-pg.ts:1962-2258` (issue #235) and extended with the 6th component the MCP version had dropped: **Age of Money**. Both `/api/health-score` and `get_financial_health_score` are now thin wrappers (~25 lines each) around the calculator. Component weights pinned at current API values — Savings Rate 0.25 / Debt-to-Income 0.20 / Emergency Fund 0.15 / Net Worth Trend 0.15 / Budget Adherence 0.15 / Age of Money 0.10 (sum = 1.0). Issue #235 features preserved: (a) un-rounded sub-component accumulation + single round at the end; (b) liquid-assets `is_investment` + cash-group whitelist; (c) NW Trend `{direction, magnitudePct, descriptor}` structured detail under new `detailRich` field; (d) excluded components renormalize across kept ones; (e) DTI uses trailing-12-month on both sides. New `excludedComponents[]` / `reportingCurrency` / `totals` fields on `HealthData` are passed through to the dashboard. Dashboard's Financial Health card gains a `(?)` `Info`-icon button that opens [HealthInfoDialog](src/app/%28app%29/dashboard/_components/health-info-dialog.tsx) — 6 per-component blocks with what/formula/note + Overall Score intro + the user's interpolated inputs (income 3m, expenses 3m, avg monthly expenses, income 12m, debt payments 12m, total liabilities, liquid assets, net worth today / 90d ago, age of money days + trend). Stdio MCP doesn't expose `get_financial_health_score` today — no stdio change needed. Score-shift caveat for the rollover: any user whose oldest tx is < 60 days back will now see Net Worth Trend EXCLUDED (was scored neutral-50 in the old REST impl), and Age of Money may shift the composite by up to ±10 points if it was previously included but is now excluded (or vice-versa) on first read. Tests: new [tests/lib/financial-health.test.ts](tests/lib/financial-health.test.ts) covers 5 load-bearing branches (budget-excluded-when-no-budgets, NW-flat-when-<60d-history, multi-currency CAD+USD, liquid-assets `is_investment`+CASH_GROUPS, AoM excluded when ageInDays=0) + weight-sum + renorm via an in-process Drizzle-SQL chunk-matcher (real `finlynq_test` Postgres not currently set up in `.env`). [tests/api/health-score.test.ts](tests/api/health-score.test.ts) rewritten to mock at the calculator boundary instead of duplicating route math. MCP tool count unchanged (91 HTTP / 87 stdio — refactor not surface change). `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0 (no write-site changes; calculator is read-only); `npm run build` green; `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0; `npx vitest run tests/lib/financial-health.test.ts tests/api/health-score.test.ts` 13/13 passing. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/financial-health.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/api/health-score/route.ts`, `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/health-info-dialog.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/health-score-card.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/dashboard/_components/types.ts`, `pf-app/tests/lib/financial-health.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/tests/api/health-score.test.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(reports): coerce BIGINT count to Number to prevent string concat in transactions column (FINLYNQ-93, 2026-05-23).** Postgres returns `COUNT(*)` as a JS string (node-postgres maps BIGINT → string to avoid precision loss); summing across SQL group rows with `entry.count += row.count` coerced the running total to a string and concatenated digits (e.g. `0 + "5" + "2" + "3" + "1"` rendered as a long digit string in the Reports page Transactions column). Three-file targeted fix wraps each accumulator with `Number()`: (1) [pf-app/src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts](src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts) `entry.count += Number(row.count)`; (2) [pf-app/src/app/api/reports/route.ts](src/app/api/reports/route.ts) `existing.count += Number(row.count)` plus defense-in-depth `count: Number(row.count)` on the sibling map-init branch; (3) [pf-app/src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx) group-level rollup `group.items.reduce((s, i) => s + Number(i.count), 0)`. Intentionally NOT globalized via `pg.types.setTypeParser(20, ...)` — targeted scope per the FINLYNQ-93 body; other call-sites read `row.count` through helpers that already coerce or compare. Per-item `{item.count}` JSX renders are untouched (`"5"` and `5` render identically). `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green; `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/reports/trends/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/reports/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/reports/page.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(portfolio): persist previousClose in price_cache so stock day-change survives cache hits (FINLYNQ-92, 2026-05-23).** `/portfolio` was showing live day-change badges for crypto (which is live-fetched each render) but `0` for every stock the moment the `price_cache` row for today existed — the read helpers hardcoded `change: 0, changePct: 0` because the table didn't carry the prior close. Three-file fix: (1) `priceCache` Drizzle table in [pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts](src/db/schema-pg.ts) gains a nullable `previousClose: doublePrecision("previous_close")`; (2) new tracked migration [pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260522_price-cache-previous-close.sql](scripts/migrations/20260522_price-cache-previous-close.sql) adds the column with `IF NOT EXISTS` (additive + nullable — picked up automatically by deploy.sh's `schema_migrations` runner; safety scan permits because no destructive intent); (3) [pf-app/src/lib/price-service.ts](src/lib/price-service.ts) — `QuoteResult` gains `previousClose?: number | null`; `fetchQuoteLive` extracts `meta.previousClose` from Yahoo and threads it through; new internal `deriveDayChange(price, previousClose)` helper computes `{change, changePct}` with a null/zero guard (back-compat for pre-migration rows + divide-by-zero safety); `readPriceCache` / `readPriceCacheBulk` route through `deriveDayChange` instead of hardcoding zeros; `writePriceCache` accepts `previousClose: number | null = null` and persists it on insert; both `fetchQuote` (single) and `fetchMultipleQuotes` (bulk) forward `live.previousClose ?? null` to the writer; `fetchQuoteAtDate` writes `previousClose: null` because historical bars don't carry a prior-day reference (the bar BEFORE `chosen` would be needed and isn't currently fetched — the day-change badge already doesn't render for historical date queries). **Downstream untouched** — `/api/portfolio/overview/route.ts` already maps `q.change` / `q.changePct` into per-holding fields and `/portfolio/page.tsx`'s `<ChangeBadge>` already consumes them; the moment today's stale rows roll over to tomorrow's date the new column populates and badges start surfacing real values. Crypto path is out of scope (already live-fetched per render, doesn't touch price_cache). `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0 (no `transactions` write-site touched); `npm run build` green; `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. Files: `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260522_price-cache-previous-close.sql` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/price-service.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **style(accounts): compact /accounts page rows + section spacing (FINLYNQ-91, 2026-05-23).** Pure presentational compaction of [pf-app/src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx) — no API/DB/schema changes, no new components. Row height drops from ~64px to ~44px (~30% denser): currency `Badge` and the optional `Archived` badge are now inline siblings of the account name `<p>` inside a single flex row (removed the inner `<div className="min-w-0">` wrapper + the nested `<div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 mt-0.5">` two-line layout); link padding `py-2.5 px-3` → `py-1.5 px-3`; avatar `h-8 w-8` → `h-7 w-7`. Section cards (`<Card key={group}>`) switched to the existing `size="sm"` variant in [pf-app/src/components/ui/card.tsx](src/components/ui/card.tsx) — drops gap from `gap-4 py-4` to `gap-3 py-3` and CardContent/Header padding from `px-4` to `px-3`; the inline `pb-2` on the CardHeader is gone (`size="sm"` covers it). `renderSection` outer `space-y-4` → `space-y-2`; page-root `space-y-6` → `space-y-4` (both empty-state and main return blocks); two-column grid `gap-8` → `gap-4`. Summary cards (Total Assets / Total Liabilities) get `pt-4 pb-3` → `pt-3 pb-3`, icon wrapper `h-8 w-8 ... mb-2` → `h-7 w-7 ... mb-1.5`, amount `mt-0.5` → `mt-0`. **Untouched**: `h1` title, hover-only edit pencil (`h-7 w-7`), `CardTitle` group label, `Show archived` / `Create Account` buttons, Create/Edit dialogs. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/accounts/page.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
