- **docs(rules): shared rule editor subsection (FINLYNQ-90 closeout, 2026-05-23).** Appended a "Shared rule editor" subsection to [pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md](docs/transaction-rules-v2.md) covering the new shared `RuleEditorDialog` component at `src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx`: the URL-agnostic `onSubmit` callback contract, inline error rendering, lazy-fetch behavior at the banner call-site, the seed-from-row pattern (payee/contains condition + `Match "<payee>"` name prefill, capped to 100 chars), the two call-sites table (settings full-CRUD vs banner staged-batch), the FINLYNQ-89 Combobox + dropdown-order inheritance, and a don't-touch list (no client-side side-effect-action pre-filter, no URL bake-in, no `onRuleApplied` contract change). Change log table updated with the 3 FINLYNQ-90 phase rows. Workspace `CLAUDE.md` Doc index unchanged — `transaction-rules-v2.md` was already listed. Doc-only deliverable; no schema, no code touched. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0. Files: `pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): shared rule editor in unresolved-categories banner (FINLYNQ-90 phase 3, 2026-05-23).** Rewrote [pf-app/src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx](src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx) to open the shared `RuleEditorDialog` per-row instead of the pre-FINLYNQ-84 inline 3-field form (matchType / matchValue / categoryId). The banner now exposes the full v2 surface in the reconciliation context: multi-condition AND group (7 field kinds × per-field ops), 7 action kinds (5 pure + 2 side-effect), priority + isActive, live preview. Per-row "Create rule" click seeds the dialog with `{field:"payee", op:"contains", value: rowPayee}` and `name: \`Match "${payee.slice(0,100)}"\`` (capped to stay inside `transaction_rules.name`'s 120-char limit). Submit POSTs the v2 payload to the existing `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule` endpoint (which has accepted both legacy and v2 since FINLYNQ-84); endpoint refusal surfaces inline in the dialog (existing AlertTriangle banner) and dialog stays open. **Lazy-fetch**: the 3 FK option lists (`/api/categories`, `/api/accounts`, `/api/portfolio`) are NOT fetched on banner mount — they fire on FIRST per-row "Create rule" click and cache in component state. Dismissing the banner without ever clicking "Create rule" triggers zero fetches (load-bearing: banner renders on every approve-time refusal). FINLYNQ-88 already removed the `side_effect_action_disallowed` refusal on the staged `/create-rule` endpoint, so side-effect actions added via the new editor are now applied; the dialog shows them without pre-filter per the FINLYNQ-90 plan's "server is the authority" decision. Removed: legacy `openFormForRowId` / `matchValue` / `matchType` / `categoryId` state, the inline form JSX, the per-row dynamic `<Select>` blocks, and the `ensureCategoriesLoaded` helper. Banner-parent props contract unchanged (`stagedImportId` / `rowIds` / `payees` / `onRuleApplied` / `onDismiss`) — `/import/pending` integration untouched. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **refactor(rules): settings page consumes shared RuleEditorDialog (FINLYNQ-90 phase 2, 2026-05-23).** Replaced the in-file `RuleEditor` / `ConditionRow` / `ActionRow` / `CONDITION_FIELDS` / `ACTION_KINDS` / `blankCondition` / `blankAction` definitions in [pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/rules/page.tsx) with an import of `RuleEditorDialog` from `@/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog`. Page file shrank from 765 → 309 lines (-456). Kept `summarizeConditions` / `summarizeActions` / `describeCondition` / `describeAction` in the page — they're used by the rule-list card rendering, not the editor. `<RuleEditor>` JSX replaced with `<RuleEditorDialog>` + an `onSubmit` callback that POSTs/PUTs `/api/rules` (id added on PUT). New helper `ruleRowToSeed(rule)` adapts `RuleRow` → `RuleSeed` for the dialog prop. UX is identical to post-FINLYNQ-89: same condition/action editing, same Combobox for category/account/holding, same `useDropdownOrder("category")` sort, same live preview, same error inline rendering. Imports cleaned: no more `Dialog`, `DialogContent`, `DialogFooter`, `DialogHeader`, `DialogTitle`, `DialogTrigger`, `Input`, `Label`, `Select*`, `Combobox`, `ComboboxItemShape`, `useDropdownOrder`, `ChevronUp`, `ChevronDown`, `computePureActionPatch`, `useMemo` — they live in the shared component now. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **refactor(rules): extract shared RuleEditorDialog from settings page (FINLYNQ-90 phase 1, 2026-05-23).** New shared component at [pf-app/src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx](src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx) lifted verbatim from `/settings/rules/page.tsx`: `RuleEditorDialog` (renamed from `RuleEditor`), `ConditionRow`, `ActionRow`, `CONDITION_FIELDS`, `ACTION_KINDS`, `blankCondition`, `blankAction`. New file exports type aliases (`Category`, `Account`, `Holding`, `RuleSeed`, `RuleEditorPayload`, `SubmitResult`, `RuleEditorDialogProps`) for the two future call-sites. **Load-bearing decoupling**: the editor NEVER bakes in a URL — the caller's `onSubmit` callback owns the fetch and returns `{ ok, error? }`. On `{ ok: false }` the editor renders the error inline (existing AlertTriangle banner) and stays open. Without this, the two call-sites (settings page + reconciliation banner) cannot share the form. Editor also gained `initialName` / `initialConditions` / `initialActions` / `initialPriority` / `initialIsActive` props so fresh-dialog callers can seed the form (banner call-site will use `initialConditions=[{field:"payee",op:"contains",value:rowPayee}]` + `initialName` from the row's payee). Preserves the FINLYNQ-89 Combobox + `useDropdownOrder("category")` swap. Phase 1 deliverable only — the settings page still uses its in-file `RuleEditor`; phase 2 swaps it. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/components/rules/rule-editor-dialog.tsx` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(settings): replace FK Select with searchable Combobox in rules editor (FINLYNQ-89, 2026-05-22).** Five FK `<Select>` blocks in [pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/settings/rules/page.tsx) swapped for the existing `<Combobox>` primitive (`@/components/ui/combobox`): `ConditionRow` `account is/is_not` value + `ActionRow` `set_category` / `set_account` / `set_portfolio_holding` / `create_transfer` (destination account). Combobox computes its trigger label from `items.find(it => it.value === value)?.label`, ships a search input, and matches the existing `/budgets` + `/transactions` pattern — picking a category now shows "Group — Name" instead of the raw integer id, and any list ≥ ~30 entries is now searchable. Category options sorted via `useDropdownOrder("category")` (the existing DropdownOrderProvider wraps the `(app)` layout); accounts + holdings keep canonical API order. Operator-dropdown `<Select>` blocks (`contains` / `gt`/`lt`/`eq` / `is`/`is_not` / `weekday`/`day_of_month`/`between`) intentionally untouched — those are fixed enums that don't benefit from search. Imports added: `Combobox`, `ComboboxItemShape`, `useDropdownOrder`. No API / schema / write-path changes — pure UI swap. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **docs(rules): rule application on staged batches living doc (FINLYNQ-88 phase 5 closeout, 2026-05-22).** Extended [pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md](docs/transaction-rules-v2.md) with a new "Rule application on staged batches" section covering: when rules apply (upload time over new rows; manual Re-apply over the whole batch; inline-create-rule scoped to `onlyRuleId`); the 6 load-bearing invariants enforced inside `applyRulesToStagedBatch` (`import_hash` byte-identical, `encryption_tier` tier-preserve, `reconcile_state` skip-linked/skipped_duplicate, `link_id`/`trade_link_id` approve-time-only, cross-tenant FK guards, sign-vs-category skip-just-that-action); Re-apply button semantics + confirmation modal text; the tier-preservation decode/encode pattern. Roadmap items #1 (rule-driven `create_transfer` wired through staging-approve) and #2 (`set_account` side-effect runner via staged-batch path) marked SHIPPED in the same doc. Workspace [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) "Auto-categorize rules are JSONB conditions + actions" gotcha block updated to (a) KEEP the `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` refusal (committed-rows surface unchanged), (b) REMOVE the pre-FINLYNQ-88 inline-create-rule refusal mention, (c) ADD a new bullet about staged-batch persistence + Re-apply semantics + the 6 helper invariants. Doc-index entry for transaction-rules-v2.md expanded to mention the new section. CHANGELOG.md ph-5 entry. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0. Doc-only deliverable; no schema, no code touched. Files: `pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): Re-apply rules button on /import/pending + banner hint (FINLYNQ-88 phase 4, 2026-05-22).** New **"Re-apply rules"** button in the two-pane page header at [pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx](src/app/%28app%29/import/pending/page.tsx) next to the existing Discard/Import actions. Click opens a confirmation modal (Dialog primitive) with the wording: *"This re-applies all active rules to every row in this batch. It may overwrite manual edits to payee, category, tags, type, or account on matched rows. Rows you've already linked to existing transactions and rows marked as duplicates are skipped."* On confirm → POST `/api/import/staged/[id]/apply-rules` → toast `"N rows updated by rules"` → refetches staged detail so the row table renders the new state (renamed payees, flipped `tx_type`, target_account_id, etc.). Errors surface in the same toast pattern. `unresolved-categories-banner.tsx` gains a hint line: *"If you added a transfer or account rule recently, click **Re-apply rules** at the top of the page."* — surfaces when at least one row is unresolved at approve refusal so the user knows where to look. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): /apply-rules endpoint + inline create-rule refactor (FINLYNQ-88 phase 3, 2026-05-22).** New `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/apply-rules` at [pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/apply-rules/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/%5Bid%5D/apply-rules/route.ts) — manual "Re-apply rules" button on `/import/pending`; reuses `requireEncryption` + ownership/status checks (404 cross-tenant, 409 non-pending), no body, calls `applyRulesToStagedBatch(db, userId, dek, id)` over the entire batch and returns `{ success: true, data: { rowsTouched, matches: Array<{rowId, ruleId}> } }`. Inline `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule` REFACTORED — the `ruleHasSideEffects` refusal (`code: 'side_effect_action_disallowed'`) is GONE; the bespoke walk-and-patch loop (which only handled `set_category` + plaintext re-encrypt) is REPLACED with `applyRulesToStagedBatch(db, userId, dek, id, { onlyRuleId: ruleId! })`. `onlyRuleId` scopes the apply pass to the just-created rule so re-running this endpoint doesn't blow away other rules' effects on user-edited rows. Response shape unchanged — `updatedRowIds` derived from `result.matches.map(m => m.rowId)`. Behavior change: rules whose actions include `set_account` or `create_transfer` no longer 400 here; matched rows flip `tx_type='R'` + `target_account_id` (create_transfer) or `account_id` + `account_name_ct` (set_account) inside the helper's tier-preserving UPDATE. `link_id` minting stays server-side at approve time via `createTransferPair` per the four-check rule. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/apply-rules/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): apply active rules at staging-upload time (FINLYNQ-88 phase 2, 2026-05-22).** `POST /api/import/staging/upload` now invokes `applyRulesToStagedBatch(tx, userId, dek, stagedImportId)` inside the same DB transaction as the row INSERT — so the user lands on `/import/pending` with rule effects already visible (renamed payees, flipped `tx_type='R'` + `target_account_id` for `create_transfer` rules, reassigned account for `set_account` rules, etc.). Scope: the entire new batch (no merge-append path post the 2026-05-22 two-ledger refactor — re-uploads produce a fresh batch with `skipped_duplicate` markers on collision, which the helper skips by construction). Helper failures are caught at the route boundary: upload still succeeds, `counts.ruleApplied` is set to `null`, and a `ruleApplyWarning: <message>` field appears in the response so the client can surface a soft warning toast. Successful runs surface `counts.ruleApplied: <rowsTouched>` (0 when nothing matched). The existing approve-time invariants (load-bearing per CLAUDE.md "Two-ledger import model" + the FINLYNQ-88 phase 1 helper header) compose naturally: `import_hash` is computed at row INSERT from plaintext payee; the helper that fires immediately after never touches it; the bank-ledger probe + `skipped_duplicate` marker happen BEFORE rule application; `encryption_tier='user'` (set at upload) is preserved through every rule-driven re-encrypt. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green. Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(rules): applyRulesToStagedBatch helper for staged-batch rule application (FINLYNQ-88 phase 1, 2026-05-22).** New helper at [pf-app/src/lib/rules/apply-to-staged-batch.ts](src/lib/rules/apply-to-staged-batch.ts) that walks active v2 rules over `staged_transactions` rows and mutates them in place — covers the 7 v2 action kinds (pure: `set_category` / `set_tags` / `rename_payee` / `set_entered_currency` / `set_portfolio_holding`; side-effect: `set_account` / `create_transfer`). Foundation for FINLYNQ-88's wiring at upload time + manual "Re-apply rules" button + inline `/create-rule` refactor (phases 2-4). **Load-bearing invariants asserted in the file header + enforced in code**: (1) `import_hash` NEVER recomputed even on `rename_payee`; (2) `encryption_tier` NEVER flipped — text columns re-encrypted at the row's existing tier (tryDecryptField/encryptField for `user`, decryptStaged/encryptStaged for `service`); (3) `reconcile_state IN ('linked', 'skipped_duplicate')` rows SKIPPED entirely (user already actioned them); (4) `link_id` / `trade_link_id` NEVER touched — minted at approve time by `createTransferPair`; (5) Cross-tenant FK guards via 3 batched ownership SELECTs across categories/accounts/holdings, with un-owned-FK actions silently SKIPPED at apply time; (6) Sign-vs-category mismatch on `set_category` SKIPS that action only (other actions on the same rule still fire) per user decision 2026-05-22. `set_account` to investment account assigns `defaultHoldingForInvestmentAccount` when `portfolio_holding_id` is currently null AND no `set_portfolio_holding` action fires on the same rule (preserves user/parser-supplied holding ids per triage default 2026-05-22). `create_transfer` skips when the row is already `R`/`T` OR has any pairing field set (`peer_staged_id` / `target_account_id`) — no overwrite of user pairing. Phase 1 is helper-only; no callers wired yet. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0. Files: `pf-app/src/lib/rules/apply-to-staged-batch.ts` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **chore(deploy): fix recurring SSH-deploy failures from regen-file drift on dev VPS (2026-05-21).** The dev VPS's local clone permanently has uncommitted modifications to `src/app/mcp-guide/tools/tools.generated.ts` and `package-lock.json` because every deploy's `npm install` + `npm run build` regenerates them. The GH Actions deploy-dev workflow's `git checkout -B dev origin/dev` step was aborting on the local-mod conflict (most recently on the FINLYNQ-84 tail-commit deploy at run [26225315462](https://github.com/finlynq/finlynq/actions/runs/26225315462)). Two-part fix: (a) [.gitignore](.gitignore) now ignores + de-tracks `tools.generated.ts` (auto-regenerated from `mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts` via `scripts/generate-mcp-tool-catalog.mjs` on every build — never edited by hand). (b) [.github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml](.github/workflows/deploy-dev.yml) now runs `git checkout -- package-lock.json src/app/mcp-guide/tools/tools.generated.ts` BEFORE the `git checkout -B dev origin/dev` step, so any lingering local mods get discarded before the branch sync. `deploy-prod.yml` was already safe (no `git checkout -B`, relies on `deploy.sh`'s stash-recovery). 2026-05-21 session review surfaced this as recurring infra debt.
- **fix(rules): collapse Condition schema to z.union (FINLYNQ-84 cycle 2)** — Zod v4 discriminatedUnion rejects duplicate discriminator values; the original schema had `field:"amount"` ×2 and `field:"date"` ×3. Switched to top-level `z.union` over the 8 leaf condition schemas; validation behavior unchanged, error messages slightly less directed.
- **FINLYNQ-83 phase 3**: hashed the one framework-injected inline `<style>` block Next.js bakes into `_global-error.html` + the SSR not-found bundle (222-byte CSS with `next-error-h1` rule). Hash `'sha256-ybR9Y4T3awQNBE4FheACcusKsc8nree1t2vQRl3tP7w='` lives in a new `FRAMEWORK_STYLE_HASHES` const in [pf-app/src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) and is appended to the Report-Only `style-src` (so `/not-found` / `/error` no longer surface as violations). Adjacent `LIBRARY_STYLE_HASHES` const seeded empty + commented with phase-4 instructions — phase 4 will populate it after ≥7 days of prod telemetry. Enforcing CSP unchanged (still `'unsafe-inline'` until phase 5).
- **FINLYNQ-83 phase 2**: migrated all 59 functional `style={{ ... }}` callsites across the 11 user-facing files cataloged in the plan to CSP-safe alternatives. Static colors / widths / sizes moved to Tailwind atomic classes (analytics-consent banner; landing-page bar-viz heights → new `.bars i.hNN` classes in `landing.css`; landing-page margins / fonts / link colors → new `.fl-landing` utility classes). Dynamic values (computed percentages on progress bars, runtime dot colors, tooltip x/y coordinates) moved to **ref-callback / useEffect-based `element.style.setProperty`** mutations — CSP only inspects HTML-rendered `style="..."` attributes at parse time, not JS-set element.style mutations later. New shared component at [pf-app/src/components/csp-safe-bar.tsx](src/components/csp-safe-bar.tsx) exports three helpers: `<CspSafeBar>` (percent-width progress bar with a static Tailwind color), `<CspSafeColorBar>` (percent-width + per-instance bg color), `<ColorDot>` (small colored dot). Health-score ring dropped redundant framer-motion `style={{ strokeDashoffset }}` since `initial` + `animate` already cover the same state (and `useMotionValue` + `useSpring` were unused after the removal). After this commit `grep 'style={{'` reports 0 functional uses across `pf-app/src/` — only 2 in middleware.ts JSDoc comments and 1 in csp-safe-bar.tsx JSDoc remain. `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` + `npm run audit:invariants` all green.
- **FINLYNQ-83 phase 1**: new `POST /api/csp-report` endpoint at [pf-app/src/app/api/csp-report/route.ts](src/app/api/csp-report/route.ts) accepts CSP Level 2 (`{ "csp-report": ... }`) AND Reporting API (`[{ body: ... }]`) shapes, logs structured `[csp-report]` JSON to stdout → journalctl, returns 204 on malformed body (no 500). Middleware at [pf-app/src/middleware.ts](src/middleware.ts) adds `report-uri /api/csp-report; report-to csp-endpoint` to BOTH the enforcing and Report-Only CSP directives plus a `Report-To: {"group":"csp-endpoint",...}` response header. Sink is intentionally unauthenticated — anonymous landing-page visitors (highest-volume Recharts/framer source) must POST. This wires the inventory channel for the Phase 3/4 framework + library style-hash extraction. Phases 2-3 land in this drain; Phases 4-5 deferred pending ≥7d prod telemetry.
- **FINLYNQ-67**: `pf-app/docs/architecture/mcp.md` forward-links the stdio-MCP-write tradeoff doc; the stdio refusal message returned by `streamDRefuse()` in `register-core-tools.ts` now cites the doc filename so users hitting the refusal land on the explanation.
- **FINLYNQ-85**: `/import` template manager — pencil now opens a full edit dialog (name, default account, isDefault, all 8 column mappings) instead of inline-renaming only the name. Refetch templates list on isDefault flip to keep the badge in sync after server-side clear-others.
- **docs(rules): transaction rules v2 living doc + workspace CLAUDE.md updates + side-effect runner skeleton (FINLYNQ-84 phase 6 closeout, 2026-05-21).** New living feature doc at [pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md](docs/transaction-rules-v2.md) covering the whole FINLYNQ-84 surface: schema contract, Zod surface, REST/MCP/staged endpoints + their dual-shape support, matcher algorithm, pure-action patcher, side-effect runner status, component inventory, load-bearing rules, test-plan mapping (which scenarios are unit-tested vs manual-only), 8-item roadmap. Cross-linked from workspace `CLAUDE.md` Doc index. **Workspace `CLAUDE.md` "Auto-categorize SQL filters on..." load-bearing gotcha** REPLACED with the FINLYNQ-84 entry — covers the JSONB shape, the side-effect-action gating on `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` (HTTP + stdio surface refusal with `skipped[]` / `reason: 'requires_staging'`), the `decryptNameish`-before-`fuzzyFind` invariant preserved for the legacy MCP shorthand path, `link_id`/`trade_link_id` server-mint rule, the destructive migration's loose path, the TRUNCATE wipe-and-replace user decision, and the stdio `autoCategory` narrowed scope. **Side-effect runner skeleton** at [pf-app/src/lib/rules/execute.ts](src/lib/rules/execute.ts) — `executeSideEffectActions(actions, txnRow, ctx)` exposed for FUTURE callers (a `/test-rule-on-this-row` surface, an explicit MCP tool, etc.). Today `set_account` and `create_transfer` branches throw "not wired into a caller yet — use createTransferPair directly" — intentional: the staging-approve materialization classifier is the canonical wiring for `create_transfer` (mints `link_id` server-side inside the four-check transfer-pair commit), and `set_account` needs investment-account-aware UPDATE which we want callers to opt-into knowingly. The skeleton documents the deferred-shape so future PRs can drop in the concrete impl without re-litigating the contract. **What did NOT ship in phase 6** vs the plan: (a) the plan's E2E suite at `tests/e2e/rules-v2.test.ts` — covered by the unit-test rewrite shipped in phase 1-3 (`tests/auto-categorize.test.ts` + `tests/api/rules.test.ts` + `tests/staged-approve-unresolved-gate.test.ts`) plus manual dev verification; (b) rule-driven `create_transfer` action wired through staging-approve's materialization classifier — flagged as roadmap item #1 in the living doc. **Deploy gate**: the destructive SQL migration is STILL manual — operator must apply [pf-app/scripts/migrate-finlynq-84-rules-v2.sql](scripts/migrate-finlynq-84-rules-v2.sql) per env via psql AFTER the code bundle is live, per the code-FIRST-then-SQL rule. Until applied, any rule-read path against the old schema columns will 500 (acceptable for dev given the TRUNCATE wipe-and-replace strategy). `npm run audit:invariants` + `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` all green. Files: `pf-app/docs/transaction-rules-v2.md` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/rules/execute.ts` (side-effect runner skeleton), workspace `CLAUDE.md` (Doc-index entry + auto-categorize gotcha rewrite).
- **feat(rules): new `/settings/rules` page + delete categorization rules card (FINLYNQ-84 phase 5, 2026-05-21).** New dedicated rules sub-page at [pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx) with full multi-condition + multi-action editor. The legacy rules UI inside [pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/categorization/page.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/categorization/page.tsx) (the entire `<Card>` block + `rules` state + `ruleForm` state + `ruleFormErrors` + `matchTypeOptions` + `handleSaveRule` + `handleToggleRule` + `handleEditRule` + `handleDeleteRule` + `handleTestRule` + the test box, ~335 lines) is gone. The categorization page now manages categories ONLY; the header description points at /settings/rules. Settings layout nav at [pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx](src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx) gains a `{ href: "/settings/rules", label: "Rules", icon: Zap }` entry after Categorization. **Editor surface**: name + priority + isActive toggle, multi-condition list (every 7 field/op combo wired — payee/note/tags string ops, amount gt/lt/eq/between, account is/is_not, currency is/is_not, date weekday/day_of_month/between), multi-action list with up/down reorder (every 7 action kind wired — set_category, set_tags, rename_payee, set_account, set_entered_currency, set_portfolio_holding, create_transfer). Side-effect actions (`set_account`, `create_transfer`) are visually marked with an amber left border + "(approve-time only)" suffix in the action picker to telegraph that they don't fire from the apply_rules_to_uncategorized path. **Live preview panel**: runs `computePureActionPatch` client-side over a user-typed sample {payee, amount} input + shows the patch diff as compact JSON; updates in a single render. **Plain-English summaries** on the rule list — "When: payee contains 'Whole Foods' AND amount between 50-500", "Then: set category → Groceries, set tags → 'food'" — built from the conditions + actions + the GET /api/rules `actionFKNames` map (no N+1 lookups). FK option lists (categories / accounts / holdings) fetched once on page mount. `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` green; `/settings/rules` appears in the route table. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/rules/page.tsx` (new, ~470 lines), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/categorization/page.tsx` (rules card + state + handlers deleted), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/layout.tsx` (Zap icon import + nav entry).
- **feat(rules): MCP HTTP + stdio rule tools rewired to v2 JSONB shape (FINLYNQ-84 phase 4, 2026-05-21).** All rule-related MCP tools now read/write the new JSONB conditions+actions columns instead of the legacy flat columns. **HTTP-side**: `list_rules` returns conditions+actions verbatim + a batched `actionFKNames` map (categories/accounts/holdings) for human-readable rendering; `create_rule` keeps the legacy shorthand (match_payee + assign_category + rename_to? + assign_tags?) and SYNTHESIZES the v2 shape internally — `decryptNameish` before `fuzzyFind` invariant preserved (issue #214); `update_rule` accepts EITHER legacy shorthand (auto-synthesized into a full conditions+actions replacement) OR the v2 fields (`conditions` / `actions`) — mixing the two on one call is rejected; `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` walks JSONB rules + REFUSES rules whose actions contain `set_account` or `create_transfer` (surfaced in `skipped[]` with `reason: 'requires_staging'` — silent balance corruption risk if applied to a committed row); `suggest_transaction_details` rewired to probe JSONB conditions; `preview_delete_category` + `delete_category` ruleCount queries use Postgres JSONB `@>` containment instead of `WHERE assign_category_id = ...`. **Stdio-side**: `get_transaction_rules` + `list_rules` return JSONB shape; `create_rule` synthesizes v2 from the shorthand (assign_category_id required — stdio can't decrypt category names); `update_rule` matches HTTP's dual-shape pattern; `apply_rules_to_uncategorized` parses JSONB + refuses side-effect actions with the same `skipped[]` surface; `autoCategory()` helper at module top (used by `record_transaction` / `bulk_record_transactions`) restricts to **payee-only conditions + set_category actions** — any other condition kind or action makes the rule ineligible at write-time (intentional narrow stdio scope; full-fidelity rule firing is HTTP-only); top-level `matchesRule()` rewritten to the v2 shape so the stdio matcher is field-shape-correct across all callers. **The import-preview rule-coverage block on MCP HTTP** (the `import_preview` flow's "Category coverage via the active rule set" loop) already shipped in phase 1-3; this commit covers the remaining 8 HTTP + 5 stdio tool surfaces. `npm run build` + `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run audit:invariants` all green. Files: `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `pf-app/mcp-server/register-core-tools.ts`.
- **feat(rules): transaction rules v2 schema + engine + REST/staging (FINLYNQ-84 phases 1-3, 2026-05-21).** Combined Phases 1-3 of FINLYNQ-84 (multi-condition rule matching + richer actions + dedicated /settings/rules page). **Phases 1-3 combined** because [next.config.ts](next.config.ts) has `typescript.ignoreBuildErrors: false` since FINLYNQ-11 (2026-05-17) so a schema-only Phase 1 would have broken the build until consumers caught up. The new `transaction_rules` shape swaps 6 narrow columns (`match_field`, `match_type`, `match_value`, `assign_category_id`, `assign_tags`, `rename_to`) for two JSONB columns: `conditions` (AND-only group of typed conditions across 7 fields × per-field ops — `payee/note/tags` string ops, `amount` gt/lt/eq/between, `account` is/is_not, `currency` is/is_not, `date` weekday/day_of_month/between) and `actions` (typed array over 7 action kinds: `set_category`, `set_tags`, `rename_payee`, `set_account`, `set_entered_currency`, `set_portfolio_holding`, `create_transfer`). Plus `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ` + index on `(user_id, is_active, priority DESC)`. **Migration is destructive** (TRUNCATE + DROP COLUMN + ADD COLUMN) and lives at the LOOSE path [scripts/migrate-finlynq-84-rules-v2.sql](scripts/migrate-finlynq-84-rules-v2.sql) — **overriding the plan's first draft** which had it in the auto-run `scripts/migrations/` dir. Per workspace CLAUDE.md "Destructive migrations need the manual code-FIRST-then-SQL flow," the loose dir is correct so deploy.sh's auto-runner skips it. Manual sequence per env documented in [docs/migrations.md](docs/migrations.md). TRUNCATE on apply per user decision 2026-05-21 (no backfill; users re-enter rules on first /settings/rules visit). Zod discriminated unions at [src/lib/rules/schema.ts](src/lib/rules/schema.ts) (Condition, ConditionGroup, Action, Rule + `collectActionFKs()` + `ruleHasSideEffects()` helpers). Engine rewrite at [src/lib/auto-categorize.ts](src/lib/auto-categorize.ts) (`matchesRule()` AND-folds over `conditions.all[]`; per-field UTC date evaluators). Pure action patcher at [src/lib/rules/execute.ts](src/lib/rules/execute.ts) (`computePureActionPatch()`; `executeSideEffectActions()` stubbed for phase 6). REST `/api/rules` POST/PUT accept the new shape with `verifyOwnership` on every FK referenced inside conditions+actions; GET decrypts FK display names in a batch for UI summaries. Staged inline-create-rule at [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts) accepts BOTH legacy shorthand AND advanced shape; side-effect actions refused here with `code: 'side_effect_action_disallowed'`. Approve gate at [src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts](src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts) routes through `computePureActionPatch(actions).categoryId` so rules with only `rename_payee`/`set_tags` no longer count as resolving. Import pipeline at [src/lib/import-pipeline.ts](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) applies pure actions only (accountId null at pipeline stage — RawTransaction carries name string, not id). MCP HTTP `import_preview`'s rule-coverage block updated. **Remaining: the 8 other MCP rule tools (`list_rules`/`create_rule`/`update_rule`/`delete_rule`/`test_rule`/`reorder_rules`/`apply_rules_to_uncategorized`/`suggest_transaction_details` on HTTP + stdio mirrors) still use legacy column names in raw SQL queries** — they ship in FINLYNQ-84 phase 4 BEFORE the destructive SQL migration runs. Tests rewritten for the new shape: `tests/auto-categorize.test.ts` (every field/op combo + AND-fold + priority-DESC), `tests/staged-approve-unresolved-gate.test.ts`, `tests/api/rules.test.ts`, `tests/api/authz-ownership.test.ts`. `npm run audit:invariants` exits 0; `npm run build` green; `npx tsc --noEmit` green. **Deploy sequence (DEV)**: code pushes via CI → SQL migration applied MANUALLY by operator afterwards (see migration playbook). Files: `pf-app/scripts/migrate-finlynq-84-rules-v2.sql` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/rules/schema.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/rules/execute.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/auto-categorize.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/import-pipeline.ts`, `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/rules/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts`, `pf-app/docs/migrations.md`, plus test files.
- **feat(import): template-picker step + dark-mode preview polish + duplicate match detail (FINLYNQ-86, 2026-05-21).** Two-commit cohort on the classic `/import` upload flow. Replaces the post-upload "This looks like X (Y% match). Re-import using this template?" banner — which fired *after* the server had already auto-applied the same template, so the **Apply** button was a no-op — with a pre-upload [TemplatePickerDialog](src/app/(app)/import/components/template-picker-dialog.tsx) the user actually chooses from. CSV uploads with saved templates open the picker first; headers are extracted client-side from the first 64KB (no extra round-trip), templates scored via existing `scoreTemplateMatch(fileHeaders, templateHeaders)`, sorted desc, top hit ≥80% marked **Best match** and preselected; **Auto-detect (no template)** option always rendered at the bottom. **Server-side**: new `skipAutoMatchTemplate` opt on [parseCsvWithFallback](src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts) suppresses **step 3** (auto-match saved template by header overlap) entirely when set; [/api/import/preview](src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts) sets it from a new `noTemplate=1` form field — the client sends it when the user explicitly picked "Auto-detect" so step 3 doesn't silently re-apply the template the user just declined. Default `false` so every existing caller (staging upload, reconcile) keeps its current behavior. [ImportPreviewDialog](src/app/(app)/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx) gets a `← Change template` footer button (renders only when the parent passes `onChangeTemplate` — i.e., the dialog was opened via the picker) that pops back to the picker for the same file. **Preview dialog dark-mode pass**: `sm:max-w-3xl` / `80vh` → `sm:max-w-5xl` / `90vh`; every light-only Tailwind color (`bg-amber-50`, `bg-orange-50`, `text-orange-900`, `bg-rose-50`, `text-amber-800`, `text-rose-700`) replaced with semi-transparent backgrounds (`bg-amber-500/10`) + dark-aware text variants (`text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300`); `opacity-50` on rows dropped (flattened contrast on dark theme — dim state now uses `text-muted-foreground`); expanded probable-duplicate detail gets a `border-l-2 border-orange-500/50` left accent + brighter font weight on the "Score 0.70 · ±7%" line. **Exact-duplicate match detail surfaced**: clicking a `Duplicate` badge now expands a detail block mirroring the Probable-dup shape — matched txn id (amber font), existing date + amount, and the match basis ("Exact match · bank-provided fitId" for OFX/QFX rows matched via `transactions.fit_id`, "Exact match · date + account + amount + payee" for CSV/email rows matched via `transactions.import_hash`); when the row isn't being force-imported, the detail hints "check the box above to import anyway". **`PreviewResult` contract widened**: new `duplicateMatches: ExactDuplicateMatch[]` field on [PreviewResult](src/lib/import-pipeline.ts) parallel to the existing `probableDuplicates: DuplicateMatch[]`, each entry `{ rowIndex, matchBasis: "fit_id" | "import_hash", matchedTx: { id, date, amount, source } }`. **Two new lookup helpers in [import-hash.ts](src/lib/import-hash.ts)**: `findDuplicateMatches(hashes, userId)` returns `Map<hash, { id, date, amount, source }>`, `findFitIdMatches(fitIds, userId)` is the fitId-keyed sibling; both pick the **lowest-id match per key** on collision so the surfaced txn id is stable across re-runs. The set-returning `checkDuplicates` and `checkFitIdDuplicates` helpers stay unchanged for the four other callers (`executeImport`, csv-parser, staging upload, reconcile) — no contract change there. **Shipped on dev** as commits `ec8277c` (template picker + dialog polish, deploy run 26217120440) and `7dc42e9` (duplicate match detail, deploy run 26218014657); manual UI smoke against https://dev.finlynq.com passed. **Out of scope**: equivalent UX on the `/import/pending` two-pane staging surface (different code path); MCP exposure of `duplicateMatches[]` (the 7 staging-review tools don't surface exact-duplicate metadata yet either); OFX/QFX/PDF/Excel preview dialogs (no template concept on those paths). Workspace `CLAUDE.md` "Load-bearing gotchas" gained two bullets covering the picker-then-preview contract + the `duplicateMatches[]` surface. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/template-picker-dialog.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/components/import-preview-dialog.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/preview/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/external-import/parsers/csv-pipeline.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/import-hash.ts`, `pf-app/src/lib/import-pipeline.ts`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, workspace `CHANGELOG.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **docs(reconciliation): living feature doc for `/import/pending` two-pane surface (FINLYNQ-56 closeout).** New `pf-app/docs/reconciliation.md` captures the as-shipped surface (what it does, URL state, endpoints, auto-match algorithm, component inventory, live-balance predicate, load-bearing rules) plus a roadmap of 8 follow-up items the same page will likely pick up: persist `auto_suggested` on staged rows (today the matcher's output is ephemeral, recomputed per GET — persisting would let the UI show "X new suggestions since you last visited" and let the matcher tune threshold without re-running every batch's pairs); multi-account batch support (today the matcher + AccountSelector assume one account per batch via `boundAccountId`); flag visibility off `/import/pending` (the `transaction_reconciliation_flags` rows persist past staging but only surface on the reconciliation page — adding a badge/filter to `/transactions` closes the loop); MCP exposure for reconciliation (reasonable additions: `link_staged_to_transaction`, `skip_staged_row`, `unskip_staged_row`, `flag_transaction_missing`, `unflag_transaction` — HTTP-only); persistent reject for auto-match (today reject is page-state-local; if users complain about repeated suggestions, add a `staged_transaction_rejected_matches` table); bulk actions on FilePane (multi-select skip/link for heavy users with 100+ row statements); currency-aware match (today same-currency required; cross-currency match via FX hop is possible with stale-FX warning); confidence-threshold tuning via telemetry (count exact vs fuzzy accepts/rejects per release). Cross-linked from workspace `CLAUDE.md` Doc index and from `pf-app/docs/architecture/database.md` "Reader / writer routes (FINLYNQ-56)" subsection. The plan doc at `pf-app/docs/two-pane-reconciliation-plan.md` stays as the implementation-ready plan (frozen at ship time); the new reconciliation.md is the LIVING doc going forward. Doc-only deliverable; no schema, no code, no tests touched. Files: `pf-app/docs/reconciliation.md` (new), `pf-app/docs/architecture/database.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): live balance recompute + polish for two-pane reconciliation (FINLYNQ-56 Phases 4 + 5).** Phase 4 + Phase 5 wrapped into a single commit since Phase 4's predicate work landed during Phase 2's rebuild. **Phase 4 refactor**: extracted the inline IIFE-based `liveProjection` computation into a `useMemo` keyed on `[detail, selected]` so the projection only recomputes when state actually changes, not on every parent render. Predicate unchanged: SELECTED rows minus rows where `dedupStatus='existing'` (already in the live balance) OR `reconcileState='skipped_duplicate'` (will be de-queued) OR `reconcileState='linked'` (target tx is already in the live balance). The ≤500ms target is met by virtue of React's synchronous render after `setDetail` — every match/skip/flag/link/unlink action updates `detail.rows` via the PATCH response, the memo re-runs, and the `<ReconciliationCallout>` props change in the same React tick. No fetch round-trip on the projection path. **Phase 5 polish**: 423 (session locked) responses on `PATCH /api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]` and `GET /api/transactions/reconciliation` now surface a specific "Your session needs to be unlocked. Reload and sign in again." toast instead of the generic error message — meaningful when a deploy restart drops the DEK cache mid-session. Other error paths (PATCH 400 invalid enum, 404 cross-tenant `linkedTransactionId`, POST/DELETE flag errors, DB-rows fetch failures) already surfaced via the toast helper without a status-specific message; left as-is to avoid bloating the surface. **Empty states** verified — DbPane shows "No transactions in the ±7-day window for this account" when `rows.length === 0 && !loading`; FilePane shows "No staged rows for this account" when `rows.length === 0`; SuggestionsGroup returns null when `suggestions.length === 0` (no zero-state card — Phase 1's matcher returns `[]` for batches without candidates, the FilePane just renders without the header). All three empty states ship inline; no new component. **CLAUDE.md update** — extended the "Staged-transactions reads MUST branch on encryption_tier per row" load-bearing gotcha with the FINLYNQ-56 PATCH addition (the new `reconcileState` + `linkedTransactionId` fields don't touch `import_hash` or `encryption_tier` defensively; half-pair transfer rule deferred to approve time per the user decision documented in the commit body). **docs/architecture/database.md** — added a "Reader / writer routes (FINLYNQ-56)" subsection under the existing FINLYNQ-55 columns documentation, pointing at the three endpoints (PATCH staged-row, GET transactions/reconciliation, POST/DELETE reconciliation-flag) plus the approve-endpoint linked-bucket extension. Verified with `npm run build` (clean) and `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — all 6). **What's NOT in this commit**: the human-walked tc-1 + tc-2 + tc-4 verification runs (against `dev.finlynq.com` — these need a live database, fixture rows, and a stopwatch); the Jest tc-3 code test (the existing approve-side half-pair classifier + the new Phase 3 linked-bucket refusal both satisfy tc-3's "selecting only one of two peer-linked transfer rows still errors out" assertion — code-level verification by reading the approve route is the documented evidence). Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/docs/architecture/database.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): match actions for two-pane reconciliation (FINLYNQ-56 Phase 3).** All four match actions land on `/import/pending` two-pane UI plus the approve-endpoint extension that de-queues linked rows. **Auto-match accept**: clicking the `Link` button on a `<SuggestionsGroup>` card PATCHes the staged row to `reconcileState='linked'` + `linkedTransactionId=<N>` and updates the matched DB row's `linkedStagedRowId` back-reference in local state — the "linked to staged #X" indicator on the DB row appears within the 500ms target without a fetch. **Auto-match reject**: hides the suggestion locally for the lifetime of the page state (per sub-item FINLYNQ-71 — intentionally NOT persisted; matcher re-runs on every GET). The `rejectedSuggestions: Set<string>` keys on `<stagedRowId>:<transactionId>`. **Manual link**: clicking `Link` on a staged row enters `linkMode = { stagedRowId }`; a banner appears ("Pick a transaction on the left pane to link to staged row #X") and DB pane's `rowActions` flip to render a `Pick` button per row (disabled on rows already linked to a different staged row). Clicking `Pick` PATCHes the same shape as accept, then clears linkMode. **Unlink**: PATCHes `reconcileState='unmatched'` + `linkedTransactionId=null` and clears the DB row's back-ref. **Skip / un-skip**: PATCHes `reconcileState='skipped_duplicate'` (skip — also removes from the approve selection set) or `'unmatched'` (un-skip; selection is up to the user since un-skipping doesn't auto-re-check the box). **Mark missing / un-mark**: POSTs/DELETEs the `/api/transactions/[id]/reconciliation-flag` endpoint with `flag_kind: 'missing_from_statement'`; updates the DB row's `reconciliationFlag` field in local state. New `<SuggestionsGroup>` component at `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/suggestions-group.tsx` renders the auto-match cards pinned via the FilePane's `header` slot — one card per (staged, db) candidate with confidence badge (exact = emerald, fuzzy = amber), payee + date + amount on both sides, and Accept / Reject buttons. Multi-candidate cases surface all (per the Phase 1 matcher's "surface all" decision); the user picks one and the rest fall out of the list as their staged row flips to 'linked'. The four `rowActions` slots (skip/link buttons on FilePane rows, flag/pick buttons on DbPane rows) are tiny icon-only buttons with `title` tooltips so they don't dominate the row. **Approve endpoint extension** (`pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`): rows where `reconcile_state='linked'` are split out into a `linkedRows` bucket BEFORE the existing classification (peerPairs / targetTransfers / cashRows) and added to `materializedRowIds` at cleanup — they get DELETEd from `staged_transactions` without an INSERT into `transactions` (the live row already exists). Half-pair transfer enforcement on linked rows: a `tx_type='R'` row whose `peer_staged_id` is set but whose peer is NOT also `'linked'` is refused with `HTTP 400 { code: 'half_pair_link' }` BEFORE any cleanup, preserving the existing approve-side guarantee that transfer pairs commit atomically. No `invalidateUserTxCache` for the linked bucket (we didn't INSERT into `transactions`, so the per-user payee cache is unchanged). Response shape gains `linked: <count>` alongside `imported` and `skippedDuplicates`. Total accounts for ALL selected rows including linked (`allSelected.length`, not `selected.length`) so the caller can see how many rows the user actioned vs how many materialized into `transactions`. Type extension: `RowActions` slot on FilePane and DbPane (Phase 2 already plumbed the prop shape; Phase 3 just passes a callback). Verified with `npm run build` (clean — no new TS errors) and `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — all 6). **Strict scope**: Phase 4 wires the live "After approval" callout recompute (currently the page already excludes linked + skipped rows from the projection — Phase 4 just verifies the ≤500ms target); Phase 5 lands empty states + error toasts + the human-walked tc-1 against dev. Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/suggestions-group.tsx` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): two-pane UI shell for /import/pending (FINLYNQ-56 Phase 2).** Wholesale restructure of `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`. The page now has two modes: **list view** (when `?id=` is absent) shows the pending-batch cards as before, and **two-pane reconciliation view** (when `?id=` is set) replaces the previous max-w-4xl Dialog with a full-page side-by-side layout — the dialog wasn't wide enough to host two real panes. URL state: `?id=<batchId>&account=<accountId>`; both fields update via `history.replaceState` so closing + reopening the tab restores selection without polluting back/forward. Default account = first option in the derived list (from staged-row `accountName` strings resolved against the loaded `/api/accounts` catalog, with `staged.boundAccountId` as the fallback when per-row resolution returns nothing — the common OFX/QFX case where rows don't carry an explicit account name). **Four new presentational components** under `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/`: `account-selector.tsx` (renders a non-interactive label for single-account batches and a `<Select>` for multi-account; emits change events upward, parent owns persistence), `two-pane-layout.tsx` (lg+ side-by-side, narrower viewports stack with DB pane on top; each pane scrolls internally), `file-pane.tsx` (right pane — staged rows with new `<RowBadge>` next to the per-row `<Badge>` for `tx_type`; supports optional `rowActions` and `header` slots for Phase 3 wiring of skip/link buttons + SuggestionsGroup; keeps the existing `<StagedRowEditor>` expansion verbatim), `db-pane.tsx` (left pane — DB rows from `GET /api/transactions/reconciliation` with a `linked to staged #X` indicator when `linkedStagedRowId != null` and a `missing from statement` indicator when the flag back-reference is present), plus a small `row-badge.tsx` helper that maps the four `reconcile_state` values to colored pill badges (linked → emerald, auto_suggested → sky, skipped_duplicate → amber, unmatched → null/no badge — the file is intentionally a no-op for the default state, not a "neutral" badge cluttering every row). **DB-pane fetch wiring**: a `useEffect` fires `GET /api/transactions/reconciliation?accountId=&from=&to=` whenever `accountId` or the computed `dbWindow` changes; window is `staged.dateRangeStart - 7d` to `staged.dateRangeEnd + 7d`, falling back to min/max of staged-row dates for pre-FINLYNQ-58 batches with NULL ranges (matches the GET staged-detail endpoint's matcher window — Phase 1). Cleanup on unmount via a `cancelled` ref-style guard prevents a late response from a stale account from clobbering the current account's rows. **Existing flows preserved verbatim**: `<ReconciliationCallout>` still renders when `statementBalance != null` (extended live-delta predicate now also excludes `reconcile_state IN ('linked', 'skipped_duplicate')` — load-bearing for Phase 4's "After approval" recompute correctness), `<UnresolvedCategoriesBanner>` still surfaces on approve refusal with `code='unresolved_categories'`, `<StagedRowEditor>` still autosaves on blur via the existing PATCH endpoint (no signature change), approve/discard buttons + selection checkbox semantics unchanged. **Type extension**: `StagedEditableRow` in `pf-app/src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx` gained an optional `linkedTransactionId?: number | null` mirroring the Phase 1 PATCH response shape — non-null iff `reconcileState === 'linked'`. **Strict scope**: no Phase 3 actions wired yet (FilePane + DbPane accept optional `rowActions` slots but none are passed; SuggestionsGroup not built); no approve-endpoint extension for `'linked'` rows yet (Phase 3 routes them to a new de-queue-only bucket); no E2E human walk-through yet (Phase 5 wires tc-1/tc-4 against dev). Verified with `npm run build` (clean). Files: `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/account-selector.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/two-pane-layout.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/file-pane.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/db-pane.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/components/import/reconcile/row-badge.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): backend for two-pane reconciliation on /import/pending (FINLYNQ-56 Phase 1).** Backend-only commit; UI phases ship separately. **PATCH `/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]`** extended with two optional fields — `reconcileState` (enum `unmatched | auto_suggested | linked | skipped_duplicate`) and `linkedTransactionId` (nullable integer). Server validates cross-field consistency: `reconcileState='linked'` requires non-null `linkedTransactionId` AND the referenced `transactions.id` must belong to the same user (cross-tenant returns 404, never 403 — no existence disclosure); `reconcileState !== 'linked'` forces `linkedTransactionId = null` on the write (no orphan FK reuse on a future state flip). Half-pair transfer enforcement stays at approve time per user decision (2026-05-20) — PATCH-side post-state validation would deadlock the UI's two sequential PATCHes for paired legs; the existing approve-side classifier in `approve/route.ts` lines 231–290 already refuses half-pair, which is what tc-3 verifies. `import_hash` is NEVER recomputed by these new fields (the PATCH's `update` builder excludes it defensively per CLAUDE.md "import_hash always over plaintext payee"); `encryption_tier` is preserved per row per CLAUDE.md "Do NOT flip per-row encryption_tier mid-edit". The response shape (`shapeRowResponse`) now also returns `linkedTransactionId` so the client sees the post-PATCH value. **New `POST /api/transactions/[id]/reconciliation-flag`** — inserts a `transaction_reconciliation_flags` row (FINLYNQ-55 table) with `flag_kind: 'missing_from_statement'` and an optional free-text note; returns 201 with the new row id. Companion `DELETE` is fully idempotent — first call returns `data: { removed: 1 }`, second returns `data: { removed: 0 }`. Both use `requireEncryption()` for surface uniformity (the table itself is plaintext but the flag/unflag intent is gated behind a logged-in DEK-equipped session). Optional `?flag_kind=...` query param on DELETE is forward-compat plumbing (today's only kind is `'missing_from_statement'`). Cross-tenant 404 on the parent `transactions` row. **New `GET /api/transactions/reconciliation?accountId=<int>&from=<YYYY-MM-DD>&to=<YYYY-MM-DD>`** — powers the left pane of the two-pane UI. Returns user-scoped `transactions` rows for one account in a date window, with decoded payee/note/category-name (via `decryptTxRows` + `decryptName`) and per-row enrichment for back-references (`linkedStagedRowId` from `staged_transactions.linked_transaction_id`) + flags (latest `transaction_reconciliation_flags` row, or null). Per user decision (2026-05-20) the endpoint uses `requireEncryption()` (decoded names; no soft-fallback) — the user is mid-import so they already have a DEK, and `'—'` rows would defeat the reconcile surface. Cross-tenant `accountId` returns 404. `txType` is derived from the joined category's `type` column ('E'/'I'/'R'/'T'); null when uncategorized. **New `src/lib/import/auto-match.ts`** — pure helper `findAutoMatches({ staged, db })` returning `{ stagedRowId, transactionId, confidence: 'exact' | 'fuzzy' }[]` for matched pairs. Match window per user decision (2026-05-20): same `accountId`, same `currency`, `|Δamount| ≤ 0.01`, `|Δdate| ≤ 1 day`. `'exact'` = bit-identical date AND amount; `'fuzzy'` = within tolerance but not exact. Cross-currency rows stay unmatched by design. Multi-candidate cases surface ALL — the UI's SuggestionsGroup renders each as a separate accept/reject pair. Staged rows already at `reconcile_state IN ('linked', 'skipped_duplicate')` skipped; DB rows already referenced by any `staged_transactions.linked_transaction_id` excluded from candidates (avoids surfacing the same DB row to two staged rows). **GET `/api/import/staged/[id]`** extended with a new `suggestedMatches: { stagedRowId, transactionId, confidence }[]` response field. Computed inline after the per-tier decode loop — only when `boundAccountId` is set (the matcher requires an accountId scope) and the batch has at least one row. Window: `staged.dateRangeStart - 7d` to `staged.dateRangeEnd + 7d`, falling back to min/max of staged-row dates for pre-FINLYNQ-58 rows with NULL `dateRangeStart/End`. Single round-trip per pane render (matcher is in-memory pure). Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean — strict typecheck on production build per FINLYNQ-11), `npm run build` (clean — both new routes registered: `/api/transactions/reconciliation`, `/api/transactions/[id]/reconciliation-flag`), `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — `staged_transactions` is out of the audit's 6-table scope; new endpoints don't touch the in-scope tables). **Strict scope** — no UI changes (Phase 2+ ship separately); approve endpoint NOT touched yet (Phase 3 adds the "de-queue only" bucket for `reconcile_state='linked'` rows); no MCP tool wiring; no migration (FINLYNQ-55 already shipped the columns + table); no test fixtures (Phase 5 wires tc-1/tc-2/tc-4 against dev). Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/transactions/[id]/reconciliation-flag/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/api/transactions/reconciliation/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/import/auto-match.ts` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **feat(import): overlapping-upload merge prompt + already-imported marker (FINLYNQ-58, F-53E).** Two F-53E behaviours land together; both depend on the FINLYNQ-54 date-range plumbing + FINLYNQ-55 `reconcile_state` column. (1) **Merge prompt** — when the authenticated user uploads a statement for an account that already has a pending `staged_imports` row whose `[date_range_start, date_range_end]` overlaps the new upload's parsed date span, `POST /api/import/staging/upload` now returns `{ success: true, data: { mergeCandidate: { stagedImportId, dateRangeStart, dateRangeEnd, rowCount, originalFilename } } }` BEFORE inserting anything (HTTP 200; no DB write). The reconcile-page modal (`pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx`) renders three options: **Merge** re-fires the upload with `action=merge` + `mergeIntoStagedImportId=<id>` → appends non-collision rows to the existing batch, drops in-batch `import_hash` collisions silently (surfaced as a count), bumps `totalRowCount` + `duplicateCount`, and widens `date_range_*` to encompass the new rows; **Create new batch** re-fires with `action=new` → bypasses overlap detection; **Cancel** discards the upload entirely without inserting. Load-bearing per CLAUDE.md / item body: import_hash is NOT recomputed on merged rows, the existing `staged_imports.created_at` + parser knobs are NOT mutated, cross-tenant merge refused via the `user_id` + `id` guard (404 with no information leak), merge into `status != 'pending'` refused (409). (2) **Already-imported marker** — every newly-staged row's `import_hash` is probed against `transactions.import_hash` for the same user (uses the new `idx_transactions_user_import_hash` index); hits land at `staged_transactions.reconcile_state = 'skipped_duplicate'` at INSERT time. The approve endpoint (`/api/import/staged/[id]/approve`) now default-excludes `skipped_duplicate` rows when `rowIds` is omitted; when `rowIds` IS provided, the explicit list is honoured verbatim so the user can manually re-check a marked row to override (CLAUDE.md "Do NOT silently flip skipped_duplicate back to unmatched if the user re-uploads"). UI: row table at `/import/pending` shows an amber "already imported" badge + line-through styling for `skipped_duplicate` rows; default selection excludes them. Statement-balance projection in `GET /api/import/staged/[id]` also excludes `skipped_duplicate` rows so "After approval" doesn't double-count rows the user won't import. Schema: new migration `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260520_finlynq-58-date-range-and-import-hash-index.sql` adds `staged_imports.date_range_start` + `staged_imports.date_range_end` (both `TEXT`, nullable; pre-migration rows take NULL and skip overlap detection cleanly) and a partial composite index `idx_transactions_user_import_hash ON transactions (user_id, import_hash) WHERE import_hash IS NOT NULL` for the per-upload probe. Drizzle schema in `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts` mirrors. Migration auto-applies on next dev deploy via the `schema_migrations` tracker. Pure-function regression coverage in `pf-app/tests/import-staging-merge.test.ts` (11 tests, all pass): 7 cases for the overlap predicate (covers right-edge touch, full-contain, fully-disjoint, NULL-range skip), 2 for `import_hash` byte-identity across merge vs fresh-batch (load-bearing — dedup keys on the ingest-time hash; recomputing would break cross-source dedup), 2 for the cross-tenant guard (same `null` return for "wrong tenant" and "no such id" — no information leak). Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean), `npm run build` (clean), `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — staged_transactions is out of the audit's 6-table scope; the new INSERT branch reuses existing helpers). **Strict scope** — no MCP-side merge support yet (staging MCP tools at `list_staged_imports` / `get_staged_import` / `list_staged_transactions` are still single-batch oriented); HTTP-only flow today. The `account` parameter on staging-merge is required (overlap-detection skips when `accountId` is null — CSV without a bound account routes through the per-row review queue in `/import/pending` as before). Files: `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260520_finlynq-58-date-range-and-import-hash-index.sql` (new), `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staging/upload/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/reconcile/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/staging/staged-row-editor.tsx`, `pf-app/tests/import-staging-merge.test.ts` (new), workspace `CLAUDE.md`. Test plan: tc-3 (cross-tenant merge refused) + tc-4 (no-recompute-on-merge) coverage shipped; tc-1 (merge prompt on overlap) + tc-2 (already-imported marker) still pending verifier exercise against `dev.finlynq.com`.
- **docs(plan): two-pane reconciliation design (FINLYNQ-56).** New `pf-app/docs/two-pane-reconciliation-plan.md` capturing the F-53C decomposition before any code lands. Five phases (backend → UI shell → match actions → live balance → polish), each with file/endpoint inventory, acceptance bullets, and effort estimates (`≤30m` to `half-day`). Anchors against the 8 existing DevManager sub-items (FINLYNQ-68..75) — the plan IS the decomposition; no new sub-items filed. Five open questions surfaced (auto-match scoring threshold being the load-bearing one — strict-vs-tolerance equality is exposed on the API response, so re-running existing batches' suggestions later means deciding now). Cross-cutting `Don't` rules copied verbatim from CLAUDE.md + the item body (no `import_hash` recompute, no tier flip mid-edit, no `flagged_missing` in `reconcile_state`, no sign-vs-category skip, no half-pair-transfer bypass). Note: chose `docs/two-pane-reconciliation-plan.md` over the dispatcher-default `plan/two-pane-reconciliation.md` because `plan/` is `.gitignore`d at the repo level (matches `docs/plan/` too) — git-tracked location keeps the plan in CI history. Phase 1 NOT shipped this cycle because open question #1 changes the API contract for suggested matches — defer until the user picks the threshold. Files: `pf-app/docs/two-pane-reconciliation-plan.md` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(rules): accept null for empty optional fields in rule-creation form (FINLYNQ-66).** The Transaction Rules form at `pf-app/src/app/(app)/settings/categorization/page.tsx` serializes empty optional fields as `null` (`assignTags: ruleForm.assignTags || null`, same for `renameTo` and `assignCategoryId`). The Zod `postSchema` / `putSchema` in `pf-app/src/app/api/rules/route.ts` declared these as `z.string().optional()` / `z.number().optional()`, which permits `string | undefined` but rejects `null`, so every submit with empty Tags + a filled Rename To (or any combo with at least one empty optional) 400'd with `Invalid input: expected string, received null` and no rule was created. Switched the optional optional-input fields (`assignCategoryId`, `assignTags`, `renameTo`, `priority` on POST; `assignCategoryId`, `assignTags`, `renameTo` on PUT) to `z.<type>().nullish()` so the schema accepts `null` / `undefined` / missing uniformly. The downstream INSERT already normalized `assignTags || null` and `renameTo || null` to NULL, so no handler change was needed. The PUT schema's required-shape fields (`name`, `matchField`, `matchType`, `matchValue`, `isActive`, `priority`) stay at `.optional()` because the handler's `updates.name.trim()` / `updates.matchValue.trim()` would crash on a null arrival — those four are only ever sent as strings or omitted by the form. **Scope:** the `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule` endpoint (FINLYNQ-57) was audited and is unaffected — it doesn't share this schema and its body shape (`matchField` / `matchType` / `matchValue` / `assignCategoryId`) doesn't include the nullable tag/rename fields. Added four FINLYNQ-66 regression cases to `pf-app/tests/api/rules.test.ts` covering the exact repro payload (`assignCategoryId: null, assignTags: null, renameTo: "sas", priority: 1`) plus the three sibling shapes (`assignTags: ""`, `assignTags` omitted, `assignTags: null + renameTo: null`); all four PASS post-fix and would 400 against the pre-fix schema. The one pre-existing failure in `rules.test.ts` (the GET test asserts `categoryName` field absence) is out of scope and untouched. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run audit:invariants` PASS. **No DB migration; runtime-bundle-only fix — prod needs a rebuild for the new schema to take effect.** Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/rules/route.ts`, `pf-app/tests/api/rules.test.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`.
- **fix(tests): FINLYNQ-65 tc-4 synthetic-regression cash-leg fixture (cycle 2).** The original `pf-app/tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts` shipped on commit `82877e0` set the sell-pair variant's cash leg to `quantity: 0, amount: 0` — but the issue #128 skip predicate (`tradeLinkId != null && amt === 0`) lives **inside** the `qty < 0` branch in `accumulate()` (`pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts:1112-1131`), so a `qty=0` cash leg never enters the branch at all and the predicate is never evaluated. Verifier confirmed (FINLYNQ-65 bounce comment, 2026-05-20): temporarily disabling the predicate (`if (false && tradeLinkId != null && amt === 0)`) did NOT cause any test to fail — defeating the synthetic-regression marker. Fix: changed the cash-leg fixture to `quantity: -5, amount: 0, trade_link_id=sellLinkId` so the row enters the qty<0 branch where the predicate gates the skip. tc-3 assertions tightened to `sell_qty=5 / sell_amount=750 / realizedGain=250` (rather than relying on `nvda.sell_qty=5` matching by coincidence) so each carries an explicit pre/post-removal diff: with predicate present the cash leg is skipped → assertions pass; with predicate removed the cash leg adds 5 to `sell_qty` (→ 10), `abs(755)` to `sell_amount` (→ 1505), flipping realized gain to 505. tc-4 is rewritten as a documentation marker that names the exact `expect(...).toBeCloseTo(...)` calls in tc-3 sell-pair variant that flip when the predicate is removed, plus calls out the conjunctivity caveat (disjunctive `||` would silently skip legitimate cash withdrawals). The buy-pair variant (tc-3 first case) **keeps its cash-leg `quantity: 0`** because the SUT's stock-leg/cash-leg LEFT JOIN requires `COALESCE(cash.quantity, 0) = 0` to pair them up — that JOIN is exercised by the buy branch's `r.cash_entered_amount` lookup, which the sell branch deliberately doesn't use. Synthetic-regression empirically verified (FINLYNQ-65 cycle 2, 2026-05-20): predicate-disabled run failed tc-3 sell-pair variant with `expected 10 to be close to 5` on `sell_qty` (cash leg added its 5 shares); predicate-restored run passes all 5. **Test-only change** — `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts` was NOT modified (the aggregator was already correct; only the test fixture exercised the wrong shape). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; no schema impact; no migration. Files: `pf-app/tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts`, `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md` (one-line update under "Portfolio aggregator" load-bearing gotcha).
- **test(portfolio): aggregator coverage for dividends (#84) + realized-gain sell-branch skip (#128) (FINLYNQ-65, follow-up to FINLYNQ-49).** Closes the two coverage gaps the FINLYNQ-49 regression suite explicitly deferred. `getHoldingsValueByAccount()` (the library aggregator FINLYNQ-49 targeted) doesn't compute `dividendsReceived` or realized gain, so neither the issue #84 category-id classification nor the issue #128 realized-gain phantom-loss assertion could be tested against it. Per the FINLYNQ-65 body, taking the **surgical export-and-test path** (NOT the end-to-end REST handler invocation harness): export `aggregateHoldings` from `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts` (single `async function aggregateHoldings` → `export async function aggregateHoldings`) and call it directly from a sibling vitest suite. No production aggregator code changed — only the function visibility. Five new tests in `pf-app/tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts` against the same real-Postgres harness (`tests/helpers/portfolio-fixtures.ts`) FINLYNQ-49 shipped: **tc-1** (dividend reinvestment `qty>0 + amt<0 + category=Dividends` contributes to both `buy_qty` AND `dividends`), **tc-2** (withholding-tax row `qty=0 + amt<0 + category=Dividends` is NOT silently dropped — sum across both rows is preserved with sign), **tc-3 buy-pair variant** (buy-pair with no subsequent sell → `sell_qty=0`, realized gain=0), **tc-3 sell-pair variant** (sell-pair with paired cash leg `qty=0+amount=0+trade_link_id` → cash leg is skipped from the qty<0 branch, realized gain = $250 not phantom loss), **tc-4** (synthetic-regression-marker documenting the load-bearing conjunctive predicate `trade_link_id IS NOT NULL AND amount = 0` per CLAUDE.md "Portfolio aggregator"; tc-3 above is the live regression catch — removing the skip would flip its assertion). All 5 PASS in 842ms against `finlynq_test`. Tests use the FINLYNQ-49 helpers (`createTestUser`, `createAccount`, `createCategory`, `createHolding`, `recordTransaction`, `seedFxRate`) verbatim — no new fixture surface added. Run with `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://devmanager:dev@localhost:55432/finlynq_test npx vitest run tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts`; runs alongside the FINLYNQ-49 suite cleanly when invoked with `--no-file-parallelism` (the shared real-Postgres harness can't run parallel `TRUNCATE` cycles — pre-existing constraint, not new). FINLYNQ-49's `describe.skip("#84 ...")` placeholder stays in place because the brief explicitly left "if unsure, leave existing `.skip` alone". `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; `npm run audit:invariants` PASS (no write-site touched). Files: `pf-app/mcp-server/register-tools-pg.ts` (one-word export change), `pf-app/tests/portfolio-aggregator-dividends-and-sellskip.test.ts` (new), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`.
- **feat(webhooks): schema migration for `webhooks` + `webhook_deliveries` tables (FINLYNQ-60, parent FINLYNQ-43).** First sub-item of the FINLYNQ-43 decomposition (F-43A) — schema-only foundation that F-43B (worker, FINLYNQ-61), F-43C (tx-write wiring, FINLYNQ-62), F-43D (UI, FINLYNQ-63), and F-43E all depend on. New migration `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260520_finlynq-60-webhooks.sql` (additive, idempotent, picked up by `deploy.sh` via the `schema_migrations` tracker) creates two tables. `webhooks(id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), user_id TEXT NN REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, url TEXT NN, secret TEXT NN, event_filter TEXT[] NN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NN DEFAULT NOW(), last_failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL)` with a CHECK constraint on `event_filter` (`array_length > 0 AND <@ ARRAY[<v1 list>]`) — every array element must be in the v1 closed list documented in `pf-app/docs/architecture/webhook-events.md` (`transaction.created` / `transaction.updated` / `transaction.deleted` / `transfer.created` / `import.approved`). `webhook_deliveries(id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), webhook_id UUID NN REFERENCES webhooks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, event TEXT NN, payload_hash TEXT NN, status_code INTEGER NULL, attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NN DEFAULT NOW())` with a CHECK constraint on `event` enumerating the same five strings — drift between the two CHECK lists or vs. webhook-events.md is a contract breach. Both PKs use `gen_random_uuid()` (built-in to Postgres 13+; Finlynq runs on PG 16, no extension needed). `user_id` is `TEXT` not `UUID` because `users.id` is `text("id")` across the schema (UUID-as-text); the FK column type must match the referenced PK or the FK won't validate. `webhooks.secret` is intentionally plaintext (NOT envelope-encrypted under user DEK) because the delivery worker fires async from background jobs (cron, retry queue) where the user DEK isn't in scope — the secret is a row-scoped HMAC key, not user-derived data, and rotation is via revoke-and-recreate per the webhook-events.md "Don't" section. Indexes: `(user_id)` and `(user_id, created_at DESC)` on `webhooks` for the list-and-sort-desc UI path; `(webhook_id, attempted_at DESC)` on `webhook_deliveries` for the per-webhook "recent deliveries" pane. FK CASCADE on both `webhooks.user_id → users(id)` and `webhook_deliveries.webhook_id → webhooks(id)` is load-bearing for the wipe-account flow (CLAUDE.md "Wipe-account is single-transaction + user_id-only filters") — deleting a user cleans up dependent rows automatically without the wipe endpoint touching either table. CHECK constraints guard via `pg_constraint` lookup since Postgres has no `ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS` (mirrors `20260506_staging_unified_columns.sql` and `20260520_finlynq-55-reconcile-state.sql`). Drizzle schema additions in `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts` mirror the SQL: `webhooks` uses `uuid("id").primaryKey().default(sql\`gen_random_uuid()\`)`, `text("user_id").references(() => users.id, { onDelete: "cascade" })`, `text("event_filter").array().notNull()`, and `webhookDeliveries` similarly mirrors. **Strict scope** — no worker, no UI, no MCP tool registration, no tx-write callsite wiring, no `enqueueWebhookDelivery` helper. F-43B/C/D ship those separately. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — webhooks tables are out of the 6 in-scope Stream D tables). No `db:push` run locally — the migration auto-applies on next dev deploy via the runner in `deploy.sh`. Files: `pf-app/scripts/migrations/20260520_finlynq-60-webhooks.sql` (new), `pf-app/src/db/schema-pg.ts`, `pf-app/docs/architecture/database.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`. Test plan: tc-1-primary (migration applies + CHECK rejects unknown event) verified structurally — verifier will exercise against `finlynq_test`; tc-2 (FK cascades on user delete) verified structurally via the migration text + Drizzle schema's `onDelete: "cascade"`; tc-3 (Drizzle typecheck) PASS via `npx tsc --noEmit`.
- **feat(import): reject unresolved-category rows on staging approval + inline rule-creation flow (FINLYNQ-57).** Approval (`POST /api/import/staged/[id]/approve`) now refuses to materialize any selected row whose decoded category name is empty AND whose plaintext payee doesn't match any active `transaction_rules` row, returning `HTTP 400 { success: false, code: "unresolved_categories", data: { rowIds, payees } }` before classification / `executeImport`. Transfers (`tx_type='R'`) and true-ups (`tx_type='T'`) are exempt by construction — `createTransferPair()` resolves the type='R' category server-side and true-ups don't need one. The gate inlines a per-tier decode (`decryptStaged` for `encryption_tier='service'` rows, `tryDecryptField(dek, ...)` for `'user'` rows) so it composes cleanly with the existing two-tier staging-encryption surface (CLAUDE.md "Staged-transactions reads MUST branch on `encryption_tier` per row"). Match-probe goes through the shared `matchesRule` helper now exported from `src/lib/auto-categorize.ts` so the gate and the import-pipeline's auto-categorize pass share one matcher. New endpoint `POST /api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule` accepts `{ matchField, matchType, matchValue, assignCategoryId }`, INSERTs into `transaction_rules` with synthesized `name` (NOT NULL) + ISO `created_at` + `is_active: true` (BOOLEAN per the 2026-05-18 FINLYNQ-12 migration), then walks the CURRENT staged batch only — uncategorized non-transfer rows whose decoded payee matches the new rule get their `category` column (the encrypted NAME, not a FK) re-encrypted under the row's existing tier and updated in-place. Historical `transactions` rows are untouched (per the FINLYNQ-57 acceptance criteria: "applies to current batch only"). `import_hash` is NEVER recomputed by this UPDATE (load-bearing per CLAUDE.md). `decryptNameish`-before-`fuzzyFind` is sidestepped by accepting `assignCategoryId` exact-id directly (no fuzzy name resolution needed — the UI builds the picker from `/api/categories`). UI: new `pf-app/src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx` rendered above the row table in `/import/pending` when the approve handler sees `code: 'unresolved_categories'` — lists affected rows by payee with a per-row "Create rule" inline form (match type + match value + category Select). Approve handler now branches on `data.code` and stashes the unresolved set; `refreshDetail()` re-fetches the staged batch after a rule applies and filters the banner row list against the refreshed `category` column so the count drops in lockstep. Per-row category assignment without a rule still flows through the existing PATCH `/api/import/staged/[id]/rows/[rowId]` (NOT re-implemented). HTTP only — stdio MCP has no DEK on the staging surface and is out of scope. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean), `npm run audit:invariants` (PASS — gate uses `matchesRule` pure; create-rule uses `decryptField` not `fuzzyFind`), `npx vitest run tests/staged-approve-unresolved-gate.test.ts` (8 PASS — including tc-2 transfers-exempt). Files: `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/approve/route.ts`, `pf-app/src/app/api/import/staged/[id]/create-rule/route.ts` (new), `pf-app/src/app/(app)/import/pending/page.tsx`, `pf-app/src/components/staging/unresolved-categories-banner.tsx` (new), `pf-app/src/lib/auto-categorize.ts` (export `matchesRule`), `pf-app/tests/staged-approve-unresolved-gate.test.ts` (new — covers tc-2 transfers-exempt + 7 sibling cases), `pf-app/CHANGELOG.md`, workspace `CLAUDE.md`. No DB migration — `transaction_rules` schema is unchanged.
- **feat(import): staged_transactions reconciliation-decision columns + transaction_reconciliation_flags table (FINLYNQ-55).** Schema-only scaffolding for the F-53C two-pane reconciliation UI ahead of the UI itself, so each FINLYNQ-53 sub-item can ship independently without a code-FIRST-then-SQL conflict.
